type: ignore[has-type]\\n826:             raise\\n827:         except (\\n828:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\\n829:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\\n830:             exc.SkipFrame,\\n831:         ):\\n832:             raise\\n833:         except Exception:\\n834:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n835:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n836:             raise\\n837:         finally:\\n838:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n839: \\n840:         output = tracer.output\\n841:         assert output is not None\\n842:         assert output.output_instructions\\n843:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n844:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n845:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n846:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\\n847:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\\n848: \\n849:     @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\\\"compile_inner\\\")\\n850:     def compile_inner(\\n851:         code: CodeType,\\n852:         one_graph: bool,\\n853:         hooks: Hooks,\\n854:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\\n855:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n856:         with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:\\n857:             stack.enter_context(\\n858:                 torch._dynamo.callback_handler.install_callbacks(\\n859:                     CallbackTrigger.DYNAMO, str(CompileContext.current_compile_id())\\n860:                 )\\n861:             )\\n862:             stack.enter_context(CompileTimeInstructionCounter.record())\\n863:             return _compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\\n864: \\n865:         return (\\n866:             ConvertFrameReturn()\\n867:         )  #   "content": "{\"content\":\"780:     )\\n781: \\n782:     # Only nonlocal defs here please!\\n783:     # Time spent compiling this frame before restarting or failing analysis\\n784:     dynamo_time_before_restart: float = 0.0\\n785:     output: Optional[OutputGraph] = None\\n786:     tracer: Optional[InstructionTranslator] = None\\n787: \\n788:     tf_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode] = (\\n789:         torch.overrides._get_current_function_mode_stack()\\n790:     )\\n791: \\n792:     @preserve_global_state\\n793:     def transform(\\n794:         instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, object]\\n795:     ) -> None:\\n796:         nonlocal output\\n797:         nonlocal tracer\\n798:         speculation_log.restart()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n799:         exn_vt_stack = ExceptionStack()\\n800:         tracer = InstructionTranslator(\\n801:             instructions,\\n802:             code,\\n803:             locals,\\n804:             globals,\\n805:             builtins,\\n806:             closure,\\n807:             tf_mode_stack,\\n808:             code_options,\\n809:             compiler_fn,\\n810:             one_graph,\\n811:             export,\\n812:             export_constraints,\\n813:             frame_state=frame_state,\\n814:             speculation_log=speculation_log,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n815:             exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\\n816:             distributed_state=distributed_state,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n817:             package=package,\\n818:         )\\n819: \\n820:         try:\\n821:             tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\\n822:             with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\\n823:                 tracer.run()\\n824:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\\n825:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n826:             raise\\n827:         except (\\n828:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\\n829:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\\n830:             exc.SkipFrame,\\n831:         ):\\n832:             raise\\n833:         except Exception:\\n834:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n835:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n836:             raise\\n837:         finally:\\n838:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n839: \\n840:         output = tracer.output\\n841:         assert output is not None\\n842:         assert output.output_instructions\\n843:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n844:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n845:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n846: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL780-845\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "780:     )\n781: \n782:     # Only nonlocal defs here please!\n783:     # Time spent compiling this frame before restarting or failing analysis\n784:     dynamo_time_before_restart: float = 0.0\n785:     output: Optional[OutputGraph] = None\n786:     tracer: Optional[InstructionTranslator] = None\n787: \n788:     tf_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode] = (\n789:         torch.overrides._get_current_function_mode_stack()\n790:     )\n791: \n792:     @preserve_global_state\n793:     def transform(\n794:         instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, object]\n795:     ) -> None:\n796:         nonlocal output\n797:         nonlocal tracer\n798:         speculation_log.restart()  # type: ignore[has-type]\n799:         exn_vt_stack = ExceptionStack()\n800:         tracer = InstructionTranslator(\n801:             instructions,\n802:             code,\n803:             locals,\n804:             globals,\n805:             builtins,\n806:             closure,\n807:             tf_mode_stack,\n808:             code_options,\n809:             compiler_fn,\n810:             one_graph,\n811:             export,\n812:             export_constraints,\n813:             frame_state=frame_state,\n814:             speculation_log=speculation_log,  # type: ignore[has-type]\n815:             exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\n816:             distributed_state=distributed_state,  # type: ignore[has-type]\n817:             package=package,\n818:         )\n819: \n820:         try:\n821:             tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\n822:             with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\n823:                 tracer.run()\n824:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\n825:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\n826:             raise\n827:         except (\n828:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\n829:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\n830:             exc.SkipFrame,\n831:         ):\n832:             raise\n833:         except Exception:\n834:             if translation_validation_enabled():\n835:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\n836:             raise\n837:         finally:\n838:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\n839: \n840:         output = tracer.output\n841:         assert output is not None\n842:         assert output.output_instructions\n843:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\n844:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\n845:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\n846: ",

{
  "endLine": 480,

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"440: \\n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n447: \\n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n451:             #\\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\\n455:             # the program execution.\\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\\n462:         )\\n463: \\n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\\n467: \\n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\\n473:                 export=self.export,\\n474:             )\\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\\n479:         )\\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\\n481: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL440-480\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "440: \n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n447: \n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n451:             #\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n455:             # the program execution.\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\n462:         )\n463: \n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\n467: \n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n473:                 export=self.export,\n474:             )\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n479:         )\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\n481: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL440-480"
  }
}

get File:lineno code from stack_trace\\n297: def _parse_stack_trace(stack_trace: str):\\n298:     if stack_trace is None:\\n299:         return None\\n300:     pattern = re.compile(r\\\"^File \\\\\\\"(.+)\\\\\\\", line (\\\\d+), in (.+)$\\\")\\n301:     lines = stack_trace.strip().split(\\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n302:     # stacktrace should have innermost frame last, so we\\n303:     # iterate backwards to find the first line that starts\\n304:     # with 'File '\\n305:     for idx in range(len(lines) - 2, -1, -1):\\n306:         line = lines[idx].strip()\\n307:         matches = pattern.match(line)\\n308:         if matches:\\n309:             file = matches.group(1)\\n310:             lineno = matches.group(2)\\n311: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/graph.py%3FL250-310\"}",

Modules that are passed into torch._dynamo optimized functions\\n5199:         # will normally be held onto through the generated GraphModule,\\n5200:         # which contains the modules. remove the reference in this backend\\n5201:         # and test that no additional references are being held.\\n5202:         class NoLeakBackend:\\n5203:             def __call__(self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs):\\n5204:                 gm.mod = None\\n5205: \\n5206:                 def foo(*args, **kwargs):\\n5207:                     return (1,)\\n5208: \\n5209:                 return foo\\n5210: \\n5211:         no_leak_backend = NoLeakBackend()\\n5212: \\n5213:         @torch.compile(backend=no_leak_backend)\\n5214:         def foo(mod, x):\\n5215:             return mod(x)\\n5216: \\n5217:         foo(mod, x)\\n5218:         del mod\\n5219:         del x\\n5220:         self.assertIsNone(mod_ref(), None)\\n5221:         self.assertIsNone(mod_weight_ref(), None)\\n5222: \\n5223:     @skipIfWindows(msg=\\\"TODO: (xuhancn) conform, AssertionError: False is not true\\\")\\n5224:     def test_release_scope_memory(self):\\n5225:         def inner(y):\\n5226:             y\\n5227: \\n5228:         inner = torch.compile(inner, backend=\\\"eager\\\")\\n5229: \\n5230:         p_ref = None\\n5231: \\n5232:         x = torch.randn((10, 10))\\n5233:         inner(x)\\n5234: \\n5235:         p_ref = weakref.ref(x)\\n5236:         self.assertTrue(p_ref() is not None)\\n5237:         del x\\n5238:         self.assertTrue(p_ref() is None)\\n5239: \\n5240:     def test_update_locals_and_stack_uses_shared_cache(self):\\n5241:         def fn(x):\\n5242:             perm = [0, 3, 5]\\n5243:             perm = list(range(min(perm))) + perm\\n5244:             perm.extend(i for i in range(x.dim()) if i not in perm)\\n5245:             return perm\\n5246: \\n5247:         x = torch.rand([2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2])\\n5248:         res1 = fn(x)\\n5249:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\\n5250:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\\n5251:         res2 = opt_fn(x)\\n5252:         self.assertTrue(same(res1, res2))\\n5253: \\n5254:     def test_side_effects_codegen_update_mutated(self):\\n5255:         # codegen to update mutated variables with side effect\\n5256:         # should after stack value's codegen\\n5257:         def f1(x):\\n5258:             alist = [x]\\n5259:             alist.append(x + 1)\\n5260:             alist[0].sum().item()  # Modules that are passed into torch._dynamo optimized functions\n5199:         # will normally be held onto through the generated GraphModule,\n5200:         # which contains the modules. remove the reference in this backend\n5201:         # and test that no additional references are being held.\n5202:         class NoLeakBackend:\n5203:             def __call__(self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs):\n5204:                 gm.mod = None\n5205: \n5206:                 def foo(*args, **kwargs):\n5207:                     return (1,)\n5208: \n5209:                 return foo\n5210: \n5211:         no_leak_backend = NoLeakBackend()\n5212: \n5213:         @torch.compile(backend=no_leak_backend)\n5214:         def foo(mod, x):\n5215:             return mod(x)\n5216: \n5217:         foo(mod, x)\n5218:         del mod\n5219:         del x\n5220:         self.assertIsNone(mod_ref(), None)\n5221:         self.assertIsNone(mod_weight_ref(), None)\n5222: \n5223:     @skipIfWindows(msg=\"TODO: (xuhancn) conform, AssertionError: False is not true\")\n5224:     def test_release_scope_memory(self):\n5225:         def inner(y):\n5226:             y\n5227: \n5228:         inner = torch.compile(inner, backend=\"eager\")\n5229: \n5230:         p_ref = None\n5231: \n5232:         x = torch.randn((10, 10))\n5233:         inner(x)\n5234: \n5235:         p_ref = weakref.ref(x)\n5236:         self.assertTrue(p_ref() is not None)\n5237:         del x\n5238:         self.assertTrue(p_ref() is None)\n5239: \n5240:     def test_update_locals_and_stack_uses_shared_cache(self):\n5241:         def fn(x):\n5242:             perm = [0, 3, 5]\n5243:             perm = list(range(min(perm))) + perm\n5244:             perm.extend(i for i in range(x.dim()) if i not in perm)\n5245:             return perm\n5246: \n5247:         x = torch.rand([2, 2, 2, 2, 2, 2])\n5248:         res1 = fn(x)\n5249:         cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n5250:         opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n5251:         res2 = opt_fn(x)\n5252:         self.assertTrue(same(res1, res2))\n5253: \n5254:     def test_side_effects_codegen_update_mutated(self):\n5255:         # Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"3564: \\n3565: class ShapeEnv:\\n3566:     # This is a wrapper over the actual __init__ function.\\n3567:     #\\n3568:     # Where to add a new constructor parameter to ShapeEnv?\\n3569:     # =====================================================\\n3570:     # This __init__ function should be used only for parameters related to event recording.\\n3571:     # These are parameters that we don't wish to pass down the road to new ShapeEnv instances\\n3572:     # created from replaying events.\\n3573:     #\\n3574:     # If you wish to add a parameter to the constructor of ShapeEnv, unrelated to event\\n3575:     # recording, do so in the _init function.\\n3576:     def __init__(\\n3577:         self,\\n3578:         *,\\n3579:         should_record_events: Optional[bool] = None,\\n3580:         tracked_fakes: Optional[list[Any]] = None,\\n3581:         **kwargs: Any,\\n3582:     ) -> None:\\n3583:         self._init(**kwargs)\\n3584: \\n3585:         # Disable event recording when replaying.\\n3586:         kwargs[\\\"should_record_events\\\"] = False\\n3587: \\n3588:         from torch.fx.experimental.validator import translation_validation_enabled\\n3589: \\n3590:         self._translation_validation_enabled = translation_validation_enabled()\\n3591: \\n3592:         # If not specified, enable event recording if both:\\n3593:         #   - Translation validation is on\\n3594:         #   - Translation validation bisection is not disabled\\n3595:         self.should_record_events = (\\n3596:             should_record_events\\n3597:             if should_record_events is not None\\n3598:             else (\\n3599:                 self._translation_validation_enabled\\n3600:                 and not config.translation_validation_no_bisect\\n3601:             )\\n3602:         )\\n3603: \\n3604:         # Enable event recording check if both:\\n3605:         #   - It should record events\\n3606:         #   - The recording check is enabled\\n3607:         self.check_recorded_events = (\\n3608:             self.should_record_events and config.check_shape_env_recorded_events\\n3609:         )\\n3610: \\n3611:         # This will make sure we only record the top-level function call.\\n3612:         self.is_recording = False\\n3613:         # Keep track of the list of tracked fakes.\\n3614:         self.tracked_fakes = tracked_fakes\\n3615:         # List of events for reconstructing ShapeEnv at arbitrary points in time.\\n3616:         self.events: list[ShapeEnvEvent] = (\\n3617:             [ShapeEnvEvent(ShapeEnv, kwargs=kwargs)]\\n3618:             if self.should_record_events\\n3619:             else []\\n3620:         )\\n3621: \\n3622:         # FakeTensor per-ShapeEnv operation cache. This is used for caching\\n3623:         # operations that contain symbolic shapes which have guards on the\\n3624:         # ShapeEnv (so are ShapeEnv-dependent).\\n3625:         #\\n3626:         # NOTE: It's important that SymNodes in this cache have their ShapeEnv\\n3627:         # stripped otherwise you end up with cycles which can only be cleaned\\n3628:         # with the GC.\\n3629:         self.fake_tensor_cache: dict[\\n3630:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheKey,\\n3631:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheEntry,\\n3632:         ] = {}\\n3633: \\n3634:     # Pro-tip: if you add new field to ShapeEnv, this affects some accept\\n3635:     # tests.  Accept their output with:\\n3636:     #\\n3637:     #   EXPECTTEST_ACCEPT=1 python test/dynamo/test_dynamic_shapes.py -k test_shape_env_equal\\n3638:     #\\n3639:     def _init(\\n3640:         self,\\n3641:         *,\\n3642:         allow_scalar_outputs: bool = True,\\n3643:         allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops: bool = True,\\n3644:         # NB: These are legacy configuration that help us make good choices\\n3645:         # when the constraint/dynamic dims are not explicitly passed to us.\\n3646:         # Ideally we will fix all call sites to be explicit and not have\\n3647:         # implicit choices, but this apparently was pretty involved.\\n3648:         assume_static_by_default: bool = False,\\n3649:         # Note - On 0/1 specialization\\n3650:         #\\n3651: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py%3FL3564-3650\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "3564: \n3565: class ShapeEnv:\n3566:     # This is a wrapper over the actual __init__ function.\n3567:     #\n3568:     # Where to add a new constructor parameter to ShapeEnv?\n3569:     # =====================================================\n3570:     # This __init__ function should be used only for parameters related to event recording.\n3571:     # These are parameters that we don't wish to pass down the road to new ShapeEnv instances\n3572:     # created from replaying events.\n3573:     #\n3574:     # If you wish to add a parameter to the constructor of ShapeEnv, unrelated to event\n3575:     # recording, do so in the _init function.\n3576:     def __init__(\n3577:         self,\n3578:         *,\n3579:         should_record_events: Optional[bool] = None,\n3580:         tracked_fakes: Optional[list[Any]] = None,\n3581:         **kwargs: Any,\n3582:     ) -> None:\n3583:         self._init(**kwargs)\n3584: \n3585:         # Disable event recording when replaying.\n3586:         kwargs[\"should_record_events\"] = False\n3587: \n3588:         from torch.fx.experimental.validator import translation_validation_enabled\n3589: \n3590:         self._translation_validation_enabled = translation_validation_enabled()\n3591: \n3592:         # If not specified, enable event recording if both:\n3593:         #   - Translation validation is on\n3594:         #   - Translation validation bisection is not disabled\n3595:         self.should_record_events = (\n3596:             should_record_events\n3597:             if should_record_events is not None\n3598:             else (\n3599:                 self._translation_validation_enabled\n3600:                 and not config.translation_validation_no_bisect\n3601:             )\n3602:         )\n3603: \n3604:         # Enable event recording check if both:\n3605:         #   - It should record events\n3606:         #   - The recording check is enabled\n3607:         self.check_recorded_events = (\n3608:             self.should_record_events and config.check_shape_env_recorded_events\n3609:         )\n3610: \n3611:         # This will make sure we only record the top-level function call.\n3612:         self.is_recording = False\n3613:         # Keep track of the list of tracked fakes.\n3614:         self.tracked_fakes = tracked_fakes\n3615:         # List of events for reconstructing ShapeEnv at arbitrary points in time.\n3616:         self.events: list[ShapeEnvEvent] = (\n3617:             [ShapeEnvEvent(ShapeEnv, kwargs=kwargs)]\n3618:             if self.should_record_events\n3619:             else []\n3620:         )\n3621: \n3622:         # FakeTensor per-ShapeEnv operation cache. This is used for caching\n3623:         # operations that contain symbolic shapes which have guards on the\n3624:         # ShapeEnv (so are ShapeEnv-dependent).\n3625:         #\n3626:         # NOTE: It's important that SymNodes in this cache have their ShapeEnv\n3627:         # stripped otherwise you end up with cycles which can only be cleaned\n3628:         # with the GC.\n3629:         self.fake_tensor_cache: dict[\n3630:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheKey,\n3631:             torch._subclasses.fake_tensor._DispatchCacheEntry,\n3632:         ] = {}\n3633: \n3634:     # Pro-tip: if you add new field to ShapeEnv, this affects some accept\n3635:     # tests.  Accept their output with:\n3636:     #\n3637:     #   EXPECTTEST_ACCEPT=1 python test/dynamo/test_dynamic_shapes.py -k test_shape_env_equal\n3638:     #\n3639:     def _init(\n3640:         self,\n3641:         *,\n3642:         allow_scalar_outputs: bool = True,\n3643:         allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops: bool = True,\n3644:         # NB: These are legacy configuration that help us make good choices\n3645:         # when the constraint/dynamic dims are not explicitly passed to us.\n3646:         # Ideally we will fix all call sites to be explicit and not have\n3647:         # implicit choices, but this apparently was pretty involved.\n3648:         assume_static_by_default: bool = False,\n3649:         # Note - On 0/1 specialization\n3650:         #\n3651: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/experimental/symbolic_shapes.py%3FL3564-3650"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"800:             wrap_stmts = \\\"\\\\n\\\".join([f'{wrap_name}(\\\"{name}\\\")' for name in wrapped_fns])\\n801:         else:\\n802:             wrap_stmts = \\\"\\\"\\n803: \\n804:         if self._body_transformer:\\n805:             body = self._body_transformer(body)\\n806: \\n807:         for name, value in self.additional_globals():\\n808:             add_global(name, value)\\n809: \\n810:         prologue = self._call_method_with_signature_check(\\n811:             self.gen_fn_def,\\n812:             free_vars,\\n813:             maybe_return_annotation[0],\\n814:             expanded_def=expanded_def,\\n815:         )\\n816: \\n817:         # remove counter and generate lineno to node index mapping\\n818:         lineno_map: dict[int, Optional[int]] = {}\\n819:         prologue_len = prologue.count(\\\"\\\\n\\\") + 1\\n820:         new_lines: list[str] = []\\n821:         cur_idx = None\\n822:         for line in \\\"\\\".join(body).split(\\\"\\\\n\\\"):\\n823:             counter = _counter_regexp.search(line)\\n824:             if counter is not None:\\n825:                 cur_idx = int(counter.group(1))\\n826:             else:\\n827:                 lineno_map[len(new_lines) + prologue_len] = cur_idx\\n828:                 new_lines.append(line)\\n829: \\n830:         code = \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(new_lines).lstrip(\\\"\\\\n\\\")\\n831:         code = \\\"\\\\n\\\".join(\\\"    \\\" + line for line in code.split(\\\"\\\\n\\\"))\\n832: \\n833:         fn_code = f\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n834: {wrap_stmts}\\n835: \\n836: {prologue}\\n837: {code}\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n838:         return PythonCode(fn_code, globals_, _lineno_map=lineno_map)\\n839: \\n840: \\n841: # Ideally, we'd like to refactor all of the pytree logic into this codegen\\n842: # class. Unfortunately, there are 3 areas we currently need extra logic in FX.\\n843: # 1. In the initial symbolic trace, the pytree logic is tied up with `concrete_args`.\\n844: # 2. In the FX graph, we need to access 2 attributes - in_spec and out_spec.\\n845: #    Since we can't access .graph within the FX forward, we need to copy the attribute to the module.\\n846: # 3. We currently can't register the pytree imports with `add_global` - not sure why.\\n847: class _PyTreeCodeGen(CodeGen):\\n848:     def __init__(self, pytree_info: _PyTreeInfo):\\n849:         super().__init__()\\n850:         self.pytree_info: _PyTreeInfo = pytree_info\\n851: \\n852:     def process_inputs(self, *inputs: Any) -> Any:\\n853:         flat_args = pytree.arg_tree_leaves(*inputs)\\n854:         return flat_args\\n855: \\n856:     def process_outputs(self, out: Any) -> Any:\\n857:         if self.pytree_info is None or self.pytree_info.out_spec is None:\\n858:             return out\\n859:         if not isinstance(out, (list, tuple)):\\n860:             out = [out]\\n861: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/graph.py%3FL800-860\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "800:             wrap_stmts = \"\\n\".join([f'{wrap_name}(\"{name}\")' for name in wrapped_fns])\n801:         else:\n802:             wrap_stmts = \"\"\n803: \n804:         if self._body_transformer:\n805:             body = self._body_transformer(body)\n806: \n807:         for name, value in self.additional_globals():\n808:             add_global(name, value)\n809: \n810:         prologue = self._call_method_with_signature_check(\n811:             self.gen_fn_def,\n812:             free_vars,\n813:             maybe_return_annotation[0],\n814:             expanded_def=expanded_def,\n815:         )\n816: \n817:         # remove counter and generate lineno to node index mapping\n818:         lineno_map: dict[int, Optional[int]] = {}\n819:         prologue_len = prologue.count(\"\\n\") + 1\n820:         new_lines: list[str] = []\n821:         cur_idx = None\n822:         for line in \"\".join(body).split(\"\\n\"):\n823:             counter = _counter_regexp.search(line)\n824:             if counter is not None:\n825:                 cur_idx = int(counter.group(1))\n826:             else:\n827:                 lineno_map[len(new_lines) + prologue_len] = cur_idx\n828:                 new_lines.append(line)\n829: \n830:         code = \"\\n\".join(new_lines).lstrip(\"\\n\")\n831:         code = \"\\n\".join(\"    \" + line for line in code.split(\"\\n\"))\n832: \n833:         fn_code = f\"\"\"\n834: {wrap_stmts}\n835: \n836: {prologue}\n837: {code}\"\"\"\n838:         return PythonCode(fn_code, globals_, _lineno_map=lineno_map)\n839: \n840: \n841: # Ideally, we'd like to refactor all of the pytree logic into this codegen\n842: # class. Unfortunately, there are 3 areas we currently need extra logic in FX.\n843: # 1. In the initial symbolic trace, the pytree logic is tied up with `concrete_args`.\n844: # 2. In the FX graph, we need to access 2 attributes - in_spec and out_spec.\n845: #    Since we can't access .graph within the FX forward, we need to copy the attribute to the module.\n846: # 3. We currently can't register the pytree imports with `add_global` - not sure why.\n847: class _PyTreeCodeGen(CodeGen):\n848:     def __init__(self, pytree_info: _PyTreeInfo):\n849:         super().__init__()\n850:         self.pytree_info: _PyTreeInfo = pytree_info\n851: \n852:     def process_inputs(self, *inputs: Any) -> Any:\n853:         flat_args = pytree.arg_tree_leaves(*inputs)\n854:         return flat_args\n855: \n856:     def process_outputs(self, out: Any) -> Any:\n857:         if self.pytree_info is None or self.pytree_info.out_spec is None:\n858:             return out\n859:         if not isinstance(out, (list, tuple)):\n860:             out = [out]\n861: ",

  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1246:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\\n1247:     def erase_node(self, to_erase: Node) -> None:\\n1248:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1249:         Erases a ``Node`` from the ``Graph``. Throws an exception if\\n1250:         there are still users of that node in the ``Graph``.\\n1251: \\n1252:         Args:\\n1253: \\n1254:             to_erase (Node): The ``Node`` to erase from the ``Graph``.\\n1255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1256:         if len(to_erase.users) > 0:\\n1257:             raise RuntimeError(\\n1258:                 f\\\"Tried to erase Node {to_erase} but it still had {len(to_erase.users)} \\\"\\n1259:                 f\\\"users in the graph: {to_erase.users}!\\\"\\n1260:             )\\n1261:         if to_erase.graph != self:\\n1262:             raise RuntimeError(f\\\"Attempting to remove {to_erase} from wrong graph!\\\")\\n1263:         if to_erase._erased:\\n1264:             warnings.warn(f\\\"erase_node({to_erase}) on an already erased node\\\")\\n1265:             return\\n1266: \\n1267:         if (\\n1268:             self.owning_module is not None\\n1269:             and getattr(self.owning_module, \\\"_erase_node_hooks\\\", None) is not None\\n1270:         ):\\n1271:             for f in self.owning_module._erase_node_hooks:\\n1272:                 f(to_erase)\\n1273: \\n1274:         self._find_nodes_lookup_table.remove(to_erase)\\n1275:         to_erase._remove_from_list()\\n1276:         to_erase._erased = True  # iterators may retain handles to erased nodes\\n1277:         self._len -= 1\\n1278: \\n1279:         # Null out this Node's argument nodes so that the Nodes referred to\\n1280:         # can update their ``users`` accordingly\\n1281:         to_erase._update_args_kwargs(\\n1282:             map_arg(to_erase._args, lambda n: None),\\n1283:             map_arg(to_erase._kwargs, lambda n: None),\\n1284:         )\\n1285: \\n1286:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\\n1287:     def inserting_before(self, n: Optional[Node] = None):\\n1288:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Set the point at which create_node and companion methods will insert into the graph.\\n1289:         When used within a 'with' statement, this will temporary set the insert point and\\n1290:         then restore it when the with statement exits::\\n1291: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/graph.py%3FL1246-1290\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1246:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\n1247:     def erase_node(self, to_erase: Node) -> None:\n1248:         \"\"\"\n1249:         Erases a ``Node`` from the ``Graph``. Throws an exception if\n1250:         there are still users of that node in the ``Graph``.\n1251: \n1252:         Args:\n1253: \n1254:             to_erase (Node): The ``Node`` to erase from the ``Graph``.\n1255:         \"\"\"\n1256:         if len(to_erase.users) > 0:\n1257:             raise RuntimeError(\n1258:                 f\"Tried to erase Node {to_erase} but it still had {len(to_erase.users)} \"\n1259:                 f\"users in the graph: {to_erase.users}!\"\n1260:             )\n1261:         if to_erase.graph != self:\n1262:             raise RuntimeError(f\"Attempting to remove {to_erase} from wrong graph!\")\n1263:         if to_erase._erased:\n1264:             warnings.warn(f\"erase_node({to_erase}) on an already erased node\")\n1265:             return\n1266: \n1267:         if (\n1268:             self.owning_module is not None\n1269:             and getattr(self.owning_module, \"_erase_node_hooks\", None) is not None\n1270:         ):\n1271:             for f in self.owning_module._erase_node_hooks:\n1272:                 f(to_erase)\n1273: \n1274:         self._find_nodes_lookup_table.remove(to_erase)\n1275:         to_erase._remove_from_list()\n1276:         to_erase._erased = True  #   "content": "{\"content\":\"996: @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\\n997: class Graph:\\n998:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n999:     ``Graph`` is the main data structure used in the FX Intermediate Representation.\\n1000:     It consists of a series of ``Node`` s, each representing callsites (or other\\n1001:     syntactic constructs). The list of ``Node`` s, taken together, constitute a\\n1002:     valid Python function.\\n1003: \\n1004:     For example, the following code\\n1005: \\n1006:     .. code-block:: python\\n1007: \\n1008:         import torch\\n1009:         import torch.fx\\n1010: \\n1011: \\n1012:         class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\\n1013:             def __init__(self):\\n1014:                 super().__init__()\\n1015:                 self.param = torch.nn.Parameter(torch.rand(3, 4))\\n1016:                 self.linear = torch.nn.Linear(4, 5)\\n1017: \\n1018:             def forward(self, x):\\n1019:                 return torch.topk(\\n1020:                     torch.sum(self.linear(x + self.linear.weight).relu(), dim=-1), 3\\n1021:                 )\\n1022: \\n1023: \\n1024:         m = MyModule()\\n1025:         gm = torch.fx.symbolic_trace(m)\\n1026: \\n1027:     Will produce the following Graph::\\n1028: \\n1029:         print(gm.graph)\\n1030: \\n1031:     .. code-block:: text\\n1032: \\n1033:         graph(x):\\n1034:             %linear_weight : [num_users=1] = self.linear.weight\\n1035:             %add_1 : [num_users=1] = call_function[target=operator.add](args = (%x, %linear_weight), kwargs = {})\\n1036:             %linear_1 : [num_users=1] = call_module[target=linear](args = (%add_1,), kwargs = {})\\n1037:             %relu_1 : [num_users=1] = %topk_1 : [num_users=1] = call_function[target=torch.topk](args = (%sum_1, 3), kwargs = {})\\n1040:             return topk_1\\n1041: \\n1042:     For the semantics of operations represented in the Compared to test_linear_module_free, the linear\\n11539:         # layer is not the code object that is directly compiled.\\n11540: \\n11541: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_misc.py%3FL11490-11540\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "11490:         def finalize():\n11491:             nonlocal cleared\n11492:             cleared = True\n11493: \n11494:         def run():\n11495:             mod, inp = model_inp_ctr()\n11496:             weakref.finalize(weakref_watch(mod), finalize)\n11497:             torch.compile(mod, backend=\"eager\")(inp)\n11498: \n11499:         run()\n11500:         gc.collect()\n11501:         self.assertTrue(cleared)\n11502: \n11503:     def test_custom_module_free(self):\n11504:         \"\"\"Test that a model is freed when it goes out of scope\"\"\"\n11505: \n11506:         class Mod(torch.nn.Module):\n11507:             def __init__(self) -> None:\n11508:                 super(Mod, self).__init__()\n11509:                 self.fc = torch.nn.Linear(100, 100)\n11510: \n11511:             def forward(self, out):\n11512:                 return self.fc(out)\n11513: \n11514:         self._test_compile_model_free(\n11515:             lambda: (Mod(), torch.randn(100, 100)),\n11516:             lambda mod: mod.fc,\n11517:         )\n11518: \n11519:     def test_sequential_module_free(self):\n11520:         self._test_compile_model_free(\n11521:             lambda: (\n11522:                 torch.nn.Sequential(\n11523:                     torch.nn.Linear(100, 100),\n11524:                     torch.nn.ReLU(),\n11525:                 ),\n11526:                 torch.randn(100, 100),\n11527:             ),\n11528:             lambda mod: mod[0],\n11529:         )\n11530: \n11531:     def test_linear_module_free(self):\n11532:         self._test_compile_model_free(\n11533:             lambda: (torch.nn.Linear(100, 100), torch.randn(100, 100)),\n11534:             lambda mod: mod,\n11535:         )\n11536: \n11537:     def test_outside_linear_module_free(self):\n11538:         # Compared to test_linear_module_free, the linear\n11539:         # layer is not the code object that is directly compiled.\n11540: \n11541: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_misc.py%3FL11490-11540"
  }

            "endLine": 501,

            "content": "519:   // To handle this, we do the following:\n520:   //   1. inlineInterfaceCalls:\n521:   //     a. inline the graph, and in the process record all interfaces\n522:   //     b. simultaneously, check (throw error) for disallowed SetAttr calls.\n523:   //   2. call reassignInterfaceTypes, which reassigns interface types to their\n524:   //      concrete types. If restart reason is None just log the type of the exception\\n912:                 restart_reasons.add(e.restart_reason or str(type(e)))\\n913:                 # We now have a new \\\"last attempt\\\", reset the clock\\n914:                 last_attempt_start_time = time.time()\\n915:                 if attempt > 100:\\n916:                     unimplemented_v2(\\n917:                         gb_type=\\\"Excessive RestartAnalysis() calls\\\",\\n918:                         context=\\\"\\\",\\n919:                         explanation=\\\"Dynamo attempted to trace the same frame 100+ times. \\\"\\n920:                         \\\"Giving up on compiling as the compile time tradeoff is likely not \\\"\\n921:                         \\\"worth the performance gain.\\\",\\n922:                         hints=[],\\n923:                     )\\n924:             except exc.SkipFrame as e:\\n925:                 if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\\n926:                     TensorifyState.clear()\\n927:                 log.debug(\\n928:                     \\\"Skipping frame %s %s \\\\\\n929:                     %s %s\\\",\\n930:                     e,\\n931:                     code.co_name,\\n932:                     code.co_filename,\\n933:                     code.co_firstlineno,\\n934:                 )\\n935:                 if one_graph or _is_error_on_graph_break(tracer):\\n936:                     log.debug(\\n937:                         \\\"No graph captured with one_graph=True or error_on_graph_break=True\\\"\\n938:                     )\\n939:                 return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n940: \\n941:         assert distributed_state is None or distributed_state.all_states is not None, (  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n942:             \\\"compiler collective wasn't run before compilation completed\\\"\\n943:         )\\n944: \\n945:         assert out_code is not None\\n946:         log_bytecode(\\n947:             \\\"MODIFIED BYTECODE\\\",\\n948:             code.co_name,\\n949:             code.co_filename,\\n950:             code.co_firstlineno,\\n951:             out_code,\\n952:         )\\n953: \\n954:         for idx, hook in enumerate(_bytecode_hooks.values()):\\n955:             with dynamo_timed(f\\\"bytecode_hooks_{idx}\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\\n956:                 hook_output = hook(code, out_code)\\n957:                 if hook_output is not None:\\n958:                     out_code = hook_output\\n959: \\n960:         orig_code_map[out_code] = code\\n961:         output_codes.add(out_code)\\n962:         dynamo_time_before_restart = last_attempt_start_time - start_time\\n963:         assert output is not None\\n964: \\n965:         # Tests for new code objects.\\n966:         # The rationale for these tests can be found in torch/csrc/dynamo/eval_frame.c\\n967:         # Only test once the code object is created.\\n968:         # They are not tested during runtime.\\n969: \\n970:         def count_args(code: CodeType) -> int:\\n971:             import inspect\\n972: \\n973:             return (\\n974:                 code.co_argcount\\n975:                 + code.co_kwonlyargcount\\n976:                 + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARARGS)\\n977:                 + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARKEYWORDS)\\n978:             )\\n979: \\n980:         assert out_code is not None\\n981: \\n982:         total_argcount_old = count_args(code)\\n983:         total_argcount_new = count_args(out_code)\\n984:         msg = \\\"arg mismatch: \\\"\\n985:         msg += f\\\"old code object has args {code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]}, \\\"\\n986:         msg += f\\\"new code object has args {out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]}\\\"\\n987:         assert (\\n988:             code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]\\n989:             == out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]\\n990:         ), msg\\n991: \\n992:         msg = \\\"free var mismatch: \\\"\\n993:         msg += f\\\"old code object has free var {code.co_freevars}, \\\"\\n994:         msg += f\\\"new code object has free var {out_code.co_freevars}\\\"\\n995:         assert code.co_freevars == out_code.co_freevars, msg\\n996: \\n997:         msg = \\\"cell var mismatch: \\\"\\n998:         msg += f\\\"old code object has cell var {code.co_cellvars}, \\\"\\n999:         msg += f\\\"new code object has cell var {out_code.co_cellvars}\\\"\\n1000:         assert code.co_cellvars == out_code.co_cellvars, msg\\n1001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL870-1000\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "870:     def _compile_inner(\n871:         code: CodeType,\n872:         one_graph: bool,\n873:         hooks: Hooks,\n874:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\n875:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n876:         nonlocal dynamo_time_before_restart\n877:         last_attempt_start_time = start_time = time.time()\n878: \n879:         def log_bytecode(\n880:             prefix: str, name: str, filename: str, line_no: int, code: CodeType\n881:         ) -> None:\n882:             if bytecode_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\n883:                 bytecode_log.debug(\n884:                     format_bytecode(prefix, name, filename, line_no, code)\n885:                 )\n886: \n887:         log_bytecode(\n888:             \"ORIGINAL BYTECODE\",\n889:             code.co_name,\n890:             code.co_filename,\n891:             code.co_firstlineno,\n892:             code,\n893:         )\n894: \n895:         out_code = None\n896:         for attempt in itertools.count():\n897:             CompileContext.get().attempt = attempt\n898:             try:\n899:                 with dynamo_timed(\n900:                     f\"compile_attempt_{attempt}\", log_pt2_compile_event=True\n901:                 ):\n902:                     out_code = transform_code_object(code, transform)\n903:                 break\n904:             except exc.RestartAnalysis as e:\n905:                 if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\n906:                     TensorifyState.clear()\n907:                 log.info(\n908:                     \"Restarting analysis due to %s\",\n909:                     LazyString(format_traceback_short, e.__traceback__),\n910:                 )\n911:                 # node index.\\n229:     _lineno_map: Optional[dict[int, Optional[int]]]\\n230: \\n231: \\n232: def _format_target(base: str, target: str) -> str:\\n233:     elems = target.split(\\\".\\\")\\n234:     r = base\\n235:     for e in elems:\\n236:         if not e.isidentifier():\\n237:             r = f'getattr({r}, \\\"{e}\\\")'\\n238:         else:\\n239:             r = f\\\"{r}.{e}\\\"\\n240:     return r\\n241: \\n242: \\n243: class _InsertPoint:\\n244:     def __init__(self, graph, new_insert):\\n245:         self.graph = graph\\n246:         self.orig_insert, graph._insert = graph._insert, new_insert\\n247: \\n248:     def __enter__(self):\\n249:         pass\\n250: \\n251:     def __exit__(self, type, value, tb):\\n252:         self.graph._insert = self.orig_insert\\n253: \\n254: \\n255: class _node_list:\\n256: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/graph.py%3FL200-255\"}",

\\\"call_function\\\":\\n309:             if not callable(target):\\n310:                 raise ValueError(\\n311:                     f\\\"Node [graph = {graph}, name = '{name}'] target {target} has the linked list of Nodes.\\n352: \\n353:         Returns:\\n354: \\n355:             The next ``Node`` in the linked list of Nodes.\\n356:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n357:         return self._next\\n358: Returns:\\n365: \\n366:             The previous ``Node`` in the linked list of Nodes.\\n367:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n368:         return self._prev\\n369: \\n370:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\\n371:     def prepend(self, x: \\\"Node\\\") -> None:\\n372:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n373:         Insert x before this node in the list of nodes in the graph. Example::\\n374: \\n375:             Before: p -> self\\n376:                     bx -> x -> ax\\n377:             After:  p -> x -> self\\n378:                     bx -> ax\\n379: \\n380:         Args:\\n381:             x (Node): The node to put before this node. Must be a member of the same graph.\\n382:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n383:         assert self.graph == x.graph, \\\"Attempting to move a Node into a different Graph\\\"\\n384:         if self == x:\\n385:             log.debug(\\n386:                 \\\"Trying to prepend a node to itself. This behavior has no effect on the graph.\\\"\\n387:             )\\n388:             return\\n389:         x._remove_from_list()\\n390:         p = self._prev\\n391:         p._next, x._prev = x, p\\n392:         x._next, self._prev = self, x\\n393: \\n394:         # compute x._sort_key\\n395:         psk = x._prev._sort_key\\n396:         nsk = x._next._sort_key\\n397:         if len(psk) > len(nsk):\\n398:             idx: int\\n399:             *prefix, idx = psk[: len(nsk) + 1]\\n400:             x._sort_key = (*prefix, idx + 1)\\n401: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/node.py%3FL300-400\"}",

isinstance(target, str):\n317:                 raise ValueError(\n318:                     f\"Node [graph = {graph}, name = '{name}'] target {target} has type {torch.typename(target)} \"\n319:                     \"but a str is expected\"\n320:                 )\n321:         super().__init__(graph, name, op, target, return_type)\n322:         self._update_args_kwargs(args, kwargs)\n323: \n324:     def __getstate__(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n325:         return {\n326:             **self.__dict__,\n327:             \"graph\": self.graph,\n328:             \"name\": self.name,\n329:             \"op\": self.op,\n330:             \"target\": self.target,\n331:             \"type\": self.target,\n332:             \"_sort_key\": self._sort_key,\n333:             \"_args\": self._args,\n334:             \"_kwargs\": self._kwargs,\n335:             \"_erased\": self._erased,\n336:             \"_prev\": self._prev,\n337:             \"_next\": self._next,\n338:             \"_input_nodes\": self._input_nodes,\n339:             \"users\": self.users,\n340:             \"_repr_fn\": self._repr_fn,\n341:             \"meta\": self.meta,\n342:         }\n343: \n344:     def __setstate__(self, state: dict[str, Any]) -> None:\n345:         for k, v in state.items():\n346:             setattr(self, k, v)\n347: \n348:     @property\n349:     def next(self) -> \"Node\":\n350:         \"\"\"\n351:         Returns the next ``Node`` in the linked list of Nodes.\n352: \n353:         Returns:\n354: \n355:             The next ``Node`` in the linked list of Nodes.\n356:         \"\"\"\n357:         return self._next\n358: \n359:     @property\n360:     def prev(self) -> \"Node\":\n361:         \"\"\"\n362:         Returns the previous ``Node`` in the linked list of Nodes.\n363: \n364:         Returns:\n365: \n366:             The previous ``Node`` in the linked list of Nodes.\n367:         \"\"\"\n368:         return self._prev\n369: \n370:     @compatibility(is_backward_compatible=True)\n371:     def prepend(self, x: \"Node\") -> None:\n372:         \"\"\"\n373:         Insert x before this node in the list of nodes in the graph. Example::\n374: \n375:             Before: p -> self\n376:                     bx -> x -> ax\n377:             After:  p -> x -> self\n378:                     bx -> ax\n379: \n380:         Args:\n381:             x (Node): The node to put before this node. Must be a member of the same graph.\n382:         \"\"\"\n383:         assert self.graph == x.graph, \"Attempting to move a Node into a different Graph\"\n384:         if self == x:\n385:             log.debug(\n386:                 \"Trying to prepend a node to itself. This behavior has no effect on the graph.\"\n387:             )\n388:             return\n389:         x._remove_from_list()\n390:         p = self._prev\n391:         p._next, x._prev = x, p\n392:         x._next, self._prev = self, x\n393: \n394:         # compute x._sort_key\n395:         psk = x._prev._sort_key\n396:         nsk = x._next._sort_key\n397:         if len(psk) > len(nsk):\n398:             idx: int\n399:             *prefix, idx = psk[: len(nsk) + 1]\n400:             x._sort_key = (*prefix, idx + 1)\n401: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/fx/node.py%3FL300-400"
  }
}

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n2234: \\n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2236:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n2237:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n2248: \\n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n2251: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2228-2250\"}",

type: ignore[assignment]\\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n2234: \\n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2236:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n2237:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n2248: \\n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n2251:     ) -> None:\\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n2253: \\n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n2255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2256:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n2257:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2258:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n2260: \\n2261: \\n2262: err_epilogue = (\\n2263:     \\\"With the current config, we will graph break \\\"\\n2264:     \\\"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \\\"\\n2265:     \\\"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \\\"\\n2266: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2227-2265\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2227: \n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\n2234: \n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2236:             if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n2237:                 del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\n2248: \n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n2251:     ) -> None:\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n2253: \n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n2255:         \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n2256:         if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n2257:             return node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2258:         assert node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\n2260: \n2261: \n2262: err_epilogue = (\n2263:     \"With the current config, we will graph break \"\n2264:     \"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \"\n2265:     \"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \"\n2266: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2227-2265"
  }

78. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 50,
  "path": "torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
  "startLine": 1
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: This module implements TorchDynamo's core frame conversion functionality, transforming Python\\n3: frames into FX graphs. It handles:\\n4: \\n5: - Frame analysis and bytecode transformation\\n6: - Guard creation and management for dynamic behaviors\\n7: - Cache management for recompilation\\n8: - Error handling and fallback mechanisms\\n9: \\n10: Key classes:\\n11: - ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling\\n12: - ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic\\n13: - Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion\\n14: - CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic\\n15: \\n16: The conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations\\n17: through torch.compile() and related systems.\\n18: \\n19: NOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.\\n20: By going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,\\n21: which is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.\\n22: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n23: \\n24: from __future__ import annotations\\n25: \\n26: import collections\\n27: import contextlib\\n28: import cProfile\\n29: import dis\\n30: import functools\\n31: import gc\\n32: import itertools\\n33: import logging\\n34: import os\\n35: import pstats\\n36: import random\\n37: import subprocess\\n38: import sys\\n39: import threading\\n40: import time\\n41: import traceback\\n42: import typing\\n43: import weakref\\n44: from dataclasses import dataclass\\n45: from pathlib import Path\\n46: from types import CellType, CodeType, FunctionType, ModuleType\\n47: from typing import Any, Callable, Optional, TypeVar, Union\\n48: from typing_extensions import ParamSpec\\n49: from weakref import ReferenceType\\n50: \\n51: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL1-50\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: This module implements TorchDynamo's core frame conversion functionality, transforming Python\n3: frames into FX graphs. It handles:\n4: \n5: - Frame analysis and bytecode transformation\n6: - Guard creation and management for dynamic behaviors\n7: - Cache management for recompilation\n8: - Error handling and fallback mechanisms\n9: \n10: Key classes:\n11: - ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling\n12: - ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic\n13: - Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion\n14: - CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic\n15: \n16: The conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations\n17: through torch.compile() and related systems.\n18: \n19: NOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.\n20: By going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,\n21: which is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: from __future__ import annotations\n25: \n26: import collections\n27: import contextlib\n28: import cProfile\n29: import dis\n30: import functools\n31: import gc\n32: import itertools\n33: import logging\n34: import os\n35: import pstats\n36: import random\n37: import subprocess\n38: import sys\n39: import threading\n40: import time\n41: import traceback\n42: import typing\n43: import weakref\n44: from dataclasses import dataclass\n45: from pathlib import Path\n46: from types import CellType, CodeType, FunctionType, ModuleType\n47: from typing import Any, Callable, Optional, TypeVar, Union\n48: from typing_extensions import ParamSpec\n49: from weakref import ReferenceType\n50: \n51: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL1-50"
  }
}
79. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "python3.11 -c \"\nimport urllib.request\nimport json\n\n# Try Sourcegraph GraphQL API\nquery = '''\nquery {\n  repository(name: \\\"github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1\\\") {\n    commit(rev: \\\"HEAD\\\") {\n      blob(path: \\\"torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py\\\") {\n        content\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n'''\n\ndata = json.dumps({'query': query}).encode()\nreq = urllib.request.Request(\n    'https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/.api/graphql',\n    data=data,\n    headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'}\n)\ntry:\n    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=30) as resp:\n        result = json.loads(resp.read())\n        if 'data' in result and result['data']:\n            content = result['data']['repository']['commit']['blob']['content']\n            print('SUCCESS: got', len(content), 'chars')\n        else:\n            print('FAILED:', json.dumps(result)[:500])\nexcept Exception as e:\n    print('ERROR:', e)\n\" 2>&1",
  "description": "Try Sourcegraph GraphQL API to fetch file content",
  "timeout": 30000
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,

}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: This module implements TorchDynamo's core frame conversion functionality, transforming Python\\n3: frames into FX graphs. It handles:\\n4: \\n5: - Frame analysis and bytecode transformation\\n6: - Guard creation and management for dynamic behaviors\\n7: - Cache management for recompilation\\n8: - Error handling and fallback mechanisms\\n9: \\n10: Key classes:\\n11: - ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling\\n12: - ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic\\n13: - Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion\\n14: - CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic\\n15: \\n16: The conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations\\n17: through torch.compile() and related systems.\\n18: \\n19: NOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.\\n20: By going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,\\n21: which is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.\\n22:     "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: This module implements TorchDynamo's core frame conversion functionality, transforming Python\n3: frames into FX graphs. mechanisms\n9: \n10: Key classes:\n11: - ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling\n12: - ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic\n13: - Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion\n14: - CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic\n15: \n16: The conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations\n17: through torch.compile() and related systems.\n18: \n19: NOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.\n20: By going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,\n21: which is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.\n22: \"\"\"\n23: \n24: from __future__ import Input
{
  "endLine": 500,
  "path": "torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"200:             self.seen.append(obj)\\n201:             self.seen_ids.add(idx)\\n202: \\n203:     def __contains__(self, item: CodeType) -> bool:\\n204:         return id(item) in self.seen_ids\\n205: \\n206:     def clear(self) -> None:\\n207:         self.seen.clear()\\n208:         self.seen_ids.clear()\\n209: \\n210: \\n211: input_codes = Tracker()\\n212: output_codes = Tracker()\\n213: \\n214: initial_global_state: Optional[GlobalStateGuard] = None\\n215: \\n216: \\n217: @functools.wraps(original_forward_from_src)\\n218: def fx_forward_from_src_skip_result(\\n219:     src: str, globals: dict[str, Any], co_fields: Optional[dict[str, str]] = None\\n220: ) -> FunctionType:\\n221:     # we monkey patch FX to prevent infinite loop of trying to convert\\n222:     # our generated code\\n223:     result = original_forward_from_src(src, globals, co_fields)\\n224:     skip_code(result.__code__)\\n225:     return result\\n226: \\n227: \\n228: def log_dynamo_start(code: CodeType, skip: int = 0) -> list[str]:\\n229:     convert_frame_intern = structured.intern_string(__file__)\\n230:     # Extract and filter the stack\\n231:     stack = list(\\n232:         itertools.takewhile(\\n233:             lambda f: f[\\\"filename\\\"] != convert_frame_intern,\\n234:             structured.from_traceback(\\n235:                 CapturedTraceback.extract(skip=4 + skip).summary()\\n236:             ),\\n237:         )\\n238:     ) + [\\n239:         {\\n240:             \\\"line\\\": code.co_firstlineno,\\n241:             \\\"name\\\": code.co_name,\\n242:             \\\"filename\\\": structured.intern_string(code.co_filename),\\n243:         }\\n244:     ]\\n245:     # Initialize the ChromiumEventLogger on start\\n246:     torch._logging.trace_structured(\\n247:         \\\"dynamo_start\\\",\\n248:         lambda: {\\\"stack\\\": stack},\\n249:     )\\n250: \\n251:     stack_strings = [\\n252:         f\\\"Line: {frame['line']}, Name: {frame['name']}, Filename: {frame['filename']}\\\"\\n253:         for frame in stack\\n254:     ]\\n255:     return stack_strings\\n256: \\n257: \\n258: def preserve_global_state(fn: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\\n259:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n260:     Context manager to:\\n261:         1) Save/restore torch.is_grad_enabled() state\\n262:         2) Save/restore python random state\\n263:         3) Save/restore torch random state\\n264:         4) Monkey patch torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src\\n265:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n266: \\n267:     @functools.wraps(fn)\\n268:     def _fn(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:\\n269:         guards = GlobalStateGuard()\\n270:         prior_grad_mode = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n271: \\n272:         # Just in case we get left in a bad dispatch state we want to restore\\n273:         # it. This can happen because the dispatch bits aren't a true\\n274:         # stack/counter - so we can't just increment/decrement them as we enter\\n275:         # and leave.\\n276:         with (\\n277:             torch._C._PreserveDispatchKeyGuard(),\\n278:             maybe_disable_inference_mode(),\\n279:             maybe_disable_inference_mode_for_fake_prop(),\\n280:         ):\\n281:             prior_inference_mode = torch.is_inference_mode_enabled()\\n282:             prior_deterministic = torch.are_deterministic_algorithms_enabled()\\n283:             prior_warn_only = torch.is_deterministic_algorithms_warn_only_enabled()\\n284:             prior_mobile_allocator_state = (\\n285:                 torch._C._is_default_mobile_cpu_allocator_set()\\n286:             )\\n287:             py_rng_state = random.getstate()\\n288:             prior_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()\\n289:             torch_rng_state = torch.random.get_rng_state()\\n290:             cuda_rng_state = None\\n291:             if torch.cuda.is_available():\\n292:                 cuda_rng_state = torch.cuda.get_rng_state()\\n293:             cuda_matmul_fp32_prec = torch._C._get_fp32_precision_getter(\\n294:                 \\\"cuda\\\", \\\"matmul\\\"\\n295:             )\\n296:             prior_fwd_from_src = torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src\\n297:             torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src = fx_forward_from_src_skip_result\\n298:             cleanup = setup_compile_debug()\\n299:             exit_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\\n300:             exit_stack.enter_context(\\n301:                 torch.fx._symbolic_trace._maybe_revert_all_patches()\\n302:             )\\n303:             exit_stack.enter_context(torch_function_mode_stack_state_mgr)\\n304:             try:\\n305:                 return fn(*args, **kwargs)\\n306:             finally:\\n307:                 cleanup.close()\\n308:                 assert torch._C._len_torch_function_stack() == 0, (\\n309:                     \\\"Torch function mode stack state changed while dynamo tracing, please report a bug\\\"\\n310:                 )\\n311:                 exit_stack.close()\\n312:                 torch._C._set_grad_enabled(prior_grad_mode)\\n313:                 torch.autograd.grad_mode._enter_inference_mode(prior_inference_mode)\\n314:                 torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(\\n315:                     prior_deterministic, warn_only=prior_warn_only\\n316:                 )\\n317:                 random.setstate(py_rng_state)\\n318:                 torch.random.set_rng_state(torch_rng_state)\\n319:                 torch.set_default_dtype(prior_dtype)\\n320:                 curr_mobile_allocator_state = (\\n321:                     torch._C._is_default_mobile_cpu_allocator_set()\\n322:                 )\\n323:                 if prior_mobile_allocator_state != curr_mobile_allocator_state:\\n324:                     torch._C._unset_default_mobile_cpu_allocator()\\n325:                 if cuda_rng_state is not None:\\n326:                     torch.cuda.set_rng_state(cuda_rng_state)\\n327:                 torch._C._set_fp32_precision_setter(\\n328:                     \\\"cuda\\\", \\\"matmul\\\", cuda_matmul_fp32_prec\\n329:                 )\\n330:                 torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src = prior_fwd_from_src\\n331:                 assert guards.check(), (\\n332:                     f\\\"Global {guards.reason()}state changed while dynamo tracing, please report a bug\\\"\\n333:                 )\\n334: \\n335:     _fn._torchdynamo_orig_backend = fn  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n336:     return _fn\\n337: \\n338: \\n339: @TorchPatcher.suppress_torch_distributed_warnings\\n340: def has_tensor_in_frame(frame: DynamoFrameType) -> bool:\\n341:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Check if the frame has torch.* related bits\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n342:     # Check if the function was decorated using torch._dynamo.optimize\\n343:     if frame.f_code in always_optimize_code_objects:\\n344:         return True\\n345: \\n346:     # Check if there is global import of torch.*\\n347:     for co_name in frame.f_code.co_names:\\n348:         if co_name in frame.f_globals:\\n349:             obj = frame.f_globals[co_name]\\n350:             if isinstance(obj, ModuleType) and (\\n351:                 obj.__name__.startswith(\\\"torch.\\\") or obj is torch\\n352:             ):\\n353:                 return True\\n354:             # ... or a global import of numpy.*\\n355:             if np and config.trace_numpy and (obj is np or is_numpy(obj)):\\n356:                 return True\\n357: \\n358:     seen_ids: dict[int, bool] = {}\\n359: \\n360:     def has_tensor(obj: object) -> bool:\\n361:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Recursively check if the obj has a tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n362:         obj_id = id(obj)\\n363:         if obj_id in seen_ids:\\n364:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n365:         seen_ids[obj_id] = False\\n366: \\n367:         if isinstance(obj, (torch.Tensor, torch.nn.Module)) or (\\n368:             istype(obj, type) and issubclass(obj, torch.nn.Module)\\n369:         ):\\n370:             seen_ids[obj_id] = True\\n371:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n372:         elif (\\n373:             config.trace_numpy\\n374:             and np\\n375:             and (istype(obj, np.ndarray) or isinstance(obj, np.generic))\\n376:         ):\\n377:             seen_ids[obj_id] = True\\n378:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n379:         elif istype(obj, (list, tuple)):\\n380: during runtime. So, make a\\n384:             # copy of values to avoid issues like \\\"RuntimeError: dictionary\\n385:             # changed size during iteration\\\"\\n386:             values = list(obj.values())\\n387:             seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(v) for v in values)\\n388:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n389:         elif istype(obj, (str, int, float, type(None), bool)):\\n390:             seen_ids[obj_id] = False\\n391:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n392:         elif is_namedtuple(obj) and hasattr(obj, \\\"_fields\\\"):\\n393:             seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(getattr(obj, v)) for v in obj._fields)\\n394:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\\n395:         else:\\n396:             # if config.debug:\\n397:             #     print(\\n398:             #         f\\\"Assuming that object of type {type(obj)} does not have a tensor\\\"\\n399:             #     )\\n400:             return False\\n401: \\n402:     # Check if the passed arguments are of type Tensor\\n403:     for value in frame.f_locals.values():\\n404:         if has_tensor(value):\\n405:             return True\\n406: \\n407:     log.debug(\\n408: \\n432: \\n433: FRAME_COUNTER = 0\\n434: FRAME_COMPILE_COUNTER: typing.Counter[Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]] = (\\n435:     collections.Counter()\\n436: )\\n437: \\n438: \\n439: def maybe_cprofile(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\\n440:     if config.cprofile:\\n441:         return cprofile_wrapper(func)\\n442:     return func\\n443: \\n444: \\n445: def cprofile_wrapper(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\\n446:     @functools.wraps(func)\\n447:     def profile_wrapper(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:\\n448:         trace_id = CompileContext.current_trace_id()\\n449:         assert trace_id, \\\"Trace id is None\\\"\\n450:         profile_path = Path(\\n451:             f\\\"/tmp/{func.__name__}_{str(trace_id).replace('/', '_')}.profile\\\"\\n452:         )\\n453:         prof = cProfile.Profile()\\n454:         try:\\n455:             prof.enable()\\n456:             start_ts = time.time()\\n457:             retval = prof.runcall(func, *args, **kwargs)\\n458:             profile_latency = time.time() - start_ts\\n459:             prof.disable()\\n460:         except ValueError:\\n461:             log.exception(\\\"failed to enable cProfile\\\")\\n462:             profile_latency = 0\\n463:             retval = func(*args, **kwargs)\\n464:         log.warning(\\n465:             \\\"### Cprofile for %s trace id [%s] took %.3f seconds ###\\\",\\n466:             func.__name__,\\n467:             trace_id,\\n468:             profile_latency,\\n469:         )\\n470:         ps = pstats.Stats(prof)\\n471:         try:\\n472:             prof.dump_stats(profile_path)\\n473:         except OSError:\\n474:             log.exception(\\\"Cannot write to %s\\\", profile_path)\\n475: subprocess.Popen(\\n479:                 [\\n480:                     \\\"gprof2dot\\\",\\n481:                     \\\"-f\\\",\\n482:                     \\\"pstats\\\",\\n483: \\\"-Tsvg\\\", \\\"-o\\\", str(svg_path)],\\n492:                 stdin=gprof2dot_process.stdout,\\n493:             )\\n494:             log.warning(\\\"Generated SVG from profile at %s\\\",
