    "content": "200:             self.seen.append(obj)\n201:             self.seen_ids.add(idx)\n202: \n203:     def __contains__(self, item: CodeType) -> bool:\n204:         return id(item) in self.seen_ids\n205: \n206:     def clear(self) -> None:\n207:         self.seen.clear()\n208:         self.seen_ids.clear()\n209: \n210: \n211: input_codes = Tracker()\n212: output_codes = Tracker()\n213: \n214: initial_global_state: Optional[GlobalStateGuard] = None\n215: \n216: \n217: @functools.wraps(original_forward_from_src)\n218: def fx_forward_from_src_skip_result(\n219:     src: str, globals: dict[str, Any], co_fields: Optional[dict[str, str]] = None\n220: ) -> FunctionType:\n221:     # we monkey patch FX to prevent infinite loop of trying to convert\n222:     # our generated code\n223:     result = original_forward_from_src(src, globals, co_fields)\n224:     skip_code(result.__code__)\n225:     return result\n226: \n227: \n228: def log_dynamo_start(code: CodeType, skip: int = 0) -> list[str]:\n229:     convert_frame_intern = structured.intern_string(__file__)\n230:     # Extract and filter the stack\n231:     stack = list(\n232:         itertools.takewhile(\n233:             lambda f: f[\"filename\"] != convert_frame_intern,\n234:             structured.from_traceback(\n235:                 CapturedTraceback.extract(skip=4 + skip).summary()\n236:             ),\n237:         )\n238:     ) + [\n239:         {\n240:             \"line\": code.co_firstlineno,\n241:             \"name\": code.co_name,\n242:             \"filename\": structured.intern_string(code.co_filename),\n243:         }\n244:     ]\n245:     # Initialize the ChromiumEventLogger on start\n246:     torch._logging.trace_structured(\n247:         \"dynamo_start\",\n248:         lambda: {\"stack\": stack},\n249:     )\n250: \n251:     stack_strings = [\n252:         f\"Line: {frame['line']}, Name: {frame['name']}, Filename: {frame['filename']}\"\n253:         for frame in stack\n254:     ]\n255:     return stack_strings\n256: \n257: \n258: def preserve_global_state(fn: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\n259:     \"\"\"\n260:     Context manager to:\n261:         1) Save/restore torch.is_grad_enabled() state\n262:         2) Save/restore python random state\n263:         3) Save/restore torch random state\n264:         4) Monkey patch torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src\n265:     \"\"\"\n266: \n267:     @functools.wraps(fn)\n268:     def _fn(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:\n269:         guards = GlobalStateGuard()\n270:         prior_grad_mode = torch.is_grad_enabled()\n271: \n272:         # Just in case we get left in a bad dispatch state we want to restore\n273:         # it. This can happen because the dispatch bits aren't a true\n274:         # stack/counter - so we can't just increment/decrement them as we enter\n275:         # and leave.\n276:         with (\n277:             torch._C._PreserveDispatchKeyGuard(),\n278:             maybe_disable_inference_mode(),\n279:             maybe_disable_inference_mode_for_fake_prop(),\n280:         ):\n281:             prior_inference_mode = torch.is_inference_mode_enabled()\n282:             prior_deterministic = torch.are_deterministic_algorithms_enabled()\n283:             prior_warn_only = torch.is_deterministic_algorithms_warn_only_enabled()\n284:             prior_mobile_allocator_state = (\n285:                 torch._C._is_default_mobile_cpu_allocator_set()\n286:             )\n287:             py_rng_state = random.getstate()\n288:             prior_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()\n289:             torch_rng_state = torch.random.get_rng_state()\n290:             cuda_rng_state = None\n291:             if torch.cuda.is_available():\n292:                 cuda_rng_state = torch.cuda.get_rng_state()\n293:             cuda_matmul_fp32_prec = torch._C._get_fp32_precision_getter(\n294:                 \"cuda\", \"matmul\"\n295:             )\n296:             prior_fwd_from_src = torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src\n297:             torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src = fx_forward_from_src_skip_result\n298:             cleanup = setup_compile_debug()\n299:             exit_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n300:             exit_stack.enter_context(\n301:                 torch.fx._symbolic_trace._maybe_revert_all_patches()\n302:             )\n303:             exit_stack.enter_context(torch_function_mode_stack_state_mgr)\n304:             try:\n305:                 return fn(*args, **kwargs)\n306:             finally:\n307:                 cleanup.close()\n308:                 assert torch._C._len_torch_function_stack() == 0, (\n309:                     \"Torch function mode stack state changed while dynamo tracing, please report a bug\"\n310:                 )\n311:                 exit_stack.close()\n312:                 torch._C._set_grad_enabled(prior_grad_mode)\n313:                 torch.autograd.grad_mode._enter_inference_mode(prior_inference_mode)\n314:                 torch.use_deterministic_algorithms(\n315:                     prior_deterministic, warn_only=prior_warn_only\n316:                 )\n317:                 random.setstate(py_rng_state)\n318:                 torch.random.set_rng_state(torch_rng_state)\n319:                 torch.set_default_dtype(prior_dtype)\n320:                 curr_mobile_allocator_state = (\n321:                     torch._C._is_default_mobile_cpu_allocator_set()\n322:                 )\n323:                 if prior_mobile_allocator_state != curr_mobile_allocator_state:\n324:                     torch._C._unset_default_mobile_cpu_allocator()\n325:                 if cuda_rng_state is not None:\n326:                     torch.cuda.set_rng_state(cuda_rng_state)\n327:                 torch._C._set_fp32_precision_setter(\n328:                     \"cuda\", \"matmul\", cuda_matmul_fp32_prec\n329:                 )\n330:                 torch.fx.graph_module._forward_from_src = prior_fwd_from_src\n331:                 assert guards.check(), (\n332:                     f\"Global {guards.reason()}state changed while dynamo tracing, please report a bug\"\n333:                 )\n334: \n335:     _fn._torchdynamo_orig_backend = fn  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n336:     return _fn\n337: \n338: \n339: @TorchPatcher.suppress_torch_distributed_warnings\n340: def has_tensor_in_frame(frame: DynamoFrameType) -> bool:\n341:     \"\"\"Check if the frame has torch.* related bits\"\"\"\n342:     # Check if the function was decorated using torch._dynamo.optimize\n343:     if frame.f_code in always_optimize_code_objects:\n344:         return True\n345: \n346:     # Check if there is global import of torch.*\n347:     for co_name in frame.f_code.co_names:\n348:         if co_name in frame.f_globals:\n349:             obj = frame.f_globals[co_name]\n350:             if isinstance(obj, ModuleType) and (\n351:                 obj.__name__.startswith(\"torch.\") or obj is torch\n352:             ):\n353:                 return True\n354:             # ... or a global import of numpy.*\n355:             if np and config.trace_numpy and (obj is np or is_numpy(obj)):\n356:                 return True\n357: \n358:     seen_ids: dict[int, bool] = {}\n359: \n360:     def has_tensor(obj: object) -> bool:\n361:         \"\"\"Recursively check if the obj has a tensor\"\"\"\n362:         obj_id = id(obj)\n363:         if obj_id in seen_ids:\n364:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\n365:         seen_ids[obj_id] = False\n366: \n367:         if isinstance(obj, (torch.Tensor, torch.nn.Module)) or (\n368:             istype(obj, type) and issubclass(obj, torch.nn.Module)\n369:         ):\n370:             seen_ids[obj_id] = True\n371:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\n372:         elif (\n373:             config.trace_numpy\n374:             and np\n375:             and (istype(obj, np.ndarray) or isinstance(obj, np.generic))\n376:         ):\n377:             seen_ids[obj_id] = True\n378:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\n379:         elif istype(obj, (list, tuple)):\n380:             seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(v) for v in obj)\n381:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\n382:         elif istype(obj, dict):\n383:             # Some packages like pytest can be updated during runtime. So, make a\n384:             # copy of values to avoid issues like \"RuntimeError: dictionary\n385:             # changed size during iteration\"\n386:             values = list(obj.values())\n387:             seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(v) for v in values)\n388:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\n389:         elif istype(obj, (str, int, float, type(None), bool)):\n390:             seen_ids[obj_id] = False\n391:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\n392:         elif is_namedtuple(obj) and hasattr(obj, \"_fields\"):\n393:             seen_ids[obj_id] = any(has_tensor(getattr(obj, v)) for v in obj._fields)\n394:             return seen_ids[obj_id]\n395:         else:\n396:             # if config.debug:\n397:             #     print(\n398:             #         f\"Assuming that object of type {type(obj)} does not have a tensor\"\n399:             #     )\n400:             return False\n401: \n402:     # Check if the passed arguments are of type Tensor\n403:     for value in frame.f_locals.values():\n404:         if has_tensor(value):\n405:             return True\n406: \n407:     log.debug(\n408:         \"skipping because no torch.* %s \\\n409:             %s %s\",\n410:         frame.f_code.co_name,\n411:         frame.f_code.co_filename,\n412:         frame.f_code.co_firstlineno,\n413:     )\n414: \n415:     return False\n416: \n417: \n418: def exception_handler(\n419:     e: Exception,\n420:     code: CodeType,\n421:     frame: Optional[DynamoFrameType] = None,\n422:     export: bool = False,\n423: ) -> None:\n424:     record_filename = None\n425:     if hasattr(e, \"exec_record\"):\n426:         record_filename = gen_record_file_name(e, code)\n427:         write_record_to_file(record_filename, e.exec_record)\n428:         e.record_filename = record_filename  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n429: \n430:     augment_exc_message(e, export=export)\n431: \n432: \n433: FRAME_COUNTER = 0\n434: FRAME_COMPILE_COUNTER: typing.Counter[Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]] = (\n435:     collections.Counter()\n436: )\n437: \n438: \n439: def maybe_cprofile(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\n440:     if config.cprofile:\n441:         return cprofile_wrapper(func)\n442:     return func\n443: \n444: \n445: def cprofile_wrapper(func: Callable[_P, _T]) -> Callable[_P, _T]:\n446:     @functools.wraps(func)\n447:     def profile_wrapper(*args: _P.args, **kwargs: _P.kwargs) -> _T:\n448:         trace_id = CompileContext.current_trace_id()\n449:         assert trace_id, \"Trace id is None\"\n450:         profile_path = Path(\n451:             f\"/tmp/{func.__name__}_{str(trace_id).replace('/', '_')}.profile\"\n452:         )\n453:         prof = cProfile.Profile()\n454:         try:\n455:             prof.enable()\n456:             start_ts = time.time()\n457:             retval = prof.runcall(func, *args, **kwargs)\n458:             profile_latency = time.time() - start_ts\n459:             prof.disable()\n460:         except ValueError:\n461:             log.exception(\"failed to enable cProfile\")\n462:             profile_latency = 0\n463:             retval = func(*args, **kwargs)\n464:         log.warning(\n465:             \"### Cprofile for %s trace id [%s] took %.3f seconds ###\",\n466:             func.__name__,\n467:             trace_id,\n468: \"-o\", str(svg_path)],\n492:                 stdin=gprof2dot_process.stdout,\n493:             )\n494:             log.warning(\"Generated SVG from profile at %s\", svg_path)\n495:         except FileNotFoundError:\n496:             log.warning(\n497:                 \"Failed to generate SVG from profile -- dumping stats instead.\"\n498:                 \"Try installing gprof2dot and dot for a better visualization\"\n499:             )\n500:             ps.sort_stats(pstats.SortKey.TIME).print_stats(20)\n501: ",

  "content": "{\"content\":\"500:             ps.sort_stats(pstats.SortKey.TIME).print_stats(20)\\n501:             ps.sort_stats(pstats.SortKey.CUMULATIVE).print_stats(20)\\n502: \\n503:         if manifold_link := maybe_upload_prof_stats_to_manifold(\\n504:             str(profile_path)\\n505:         ):  # fb-only\\n506:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\\n507:                 \\\"link\\\",\\n508:                 lambda: {\\\"name\\\": \\\"cprofile_manifold_url\\\", \\\"url\\\": manifold_link},\\n509:             )\\n510:         return retval\\n511: \\n512:     return profile_wrapper\\n513: \\n514: \\n515: @dataclass\\n516: class ConvertFrameBox:\\n517:     error_on_graph_break: Optional[bool] = None\\n518: \\n519: \\n520: def _is_error_on_graph_break(tx: Optional[InstructionTranslator]) -> bool:\\n521:     if tx is None:\\n522:         return _get_error_on_graph_break()\\n523:     return tx.error_on_graph_break\\n524: \\n525: \\n526: def get_compile_id(\\n527:     frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\\n528: ) -> CompileId:\\n529:     global FRAME_COUNTER\\n530:     if \\\"_id\\\" not in frame_state:\\n531:         frame_state[\\\"_id\\\"] = FRAME_COUNTER\\n532:         FRAME_COUNTER += 1\\n533:     frame_id = frame_state[\\\"_id\\\"]\\n534:     assert isinstance(frame_id, int)\\n535: \\n536:     frame_compile_id = FRAME_COMPILE_COUNTER[frame_id]\\n537:     FRAME_COMPILE_COUNTER[frame_id] += 1\\n538: \\n539:     compiled_autograd_id = None\\n540:     if prior := CompileContext.current_compile_id():\\n541:         compiled_autograd_id = prior.compiled_autograd_id\\n542:     return CompileId(\\n543:         compiled_autograd_id=compiled_autograd_id,\\n544:         frame_id=frame_id,\\n545:         frame_compile_id=frame_compile_id,\\n546:     )\\n547: \\n548: \\n549: class ConvertFrameAssert:\\n550:     def __init__(\\n551:         self,\\n552:         compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\\n553:         one_graph: bool = True,\\n554:         export: bool = False,\\n555:         export_constraints: Optional[typing.Never] = None,\\n556:         package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\\n557:     ) -> None:\\n558:         # assert export_constraints is None\\n559:         reset_graph_break_dup_checker()\\n560:         self._torchdynamo_orig_backend = compiler_fn\\n561:         self._one_graph = one_graph\\n562:         self._export = export\\n563:         self._export_constraints = export_constraints\\n564:         self._package = package\\n565:         self._box = ConvertFrameBox()\\n566: \\n567:     @property\\n568:     def _clone_with_backend(self) -> Callable[[CompilerFn], ConvertFrameAssert]:\\n569:         return lambda backend: convert_frame_assert(\\n570:             backend,\\n571:             self._one_graph,\\n572:             self._export,\\n573:             self._export_constraints,\\n574:         )\\n575: \\n576:     def __call__(\\n577:         self,\\n578:         frame: DynamoFrameType,\\n579:         cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\\n580:         hooks: Hooks,\\n581:         frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\\n582:         *,\\n583:         skip: int = 0,\\n584:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n585:         increment_frame()\\n586:         code = frame.f_code\\n587: \\n588:         cache_size = compute_cache_size(frame, cache_entry)\\n589:         input_codes.add(code)\\n590:         if code in output_codes:\\n591:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n592:         if (\\n593:             os.environ.get(\\\"TORCHDYNAMO_DEBUG_FUNCTION\\\")\\n594:             and os.environ.get(\\\"TORCHDYNAMO_DEBUG_FUNCTION\\\") != code.co_name\\n595:         ):\\n596:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n597:         if code.co_name == \\\"<genexpr>\\\" and code.co_filename.endswith(\\n598:             (\\n599:                 \\\"transformers/file_utils.py\\\",\\n600:                 \\\"transformers/utils/generic.py\\\",\\n601:                 \\\"diffusers/utils/outputs.py\\\",\\n602:             )\\n603:         ):\\n604:             # not needed, but cleans up torchbench error stats\\n605:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n606:         if code.co_name == \\\"__setattr__\\\":\\n607:             # setattr could be tricky to handle generally,\\n608:             # but also not likely useful to compile- skip the whole frame\\n609:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n610:         if code.co_name == \\\"__init__\\\" and code.co_filename.startswith(\\n611:             os.path.dirname(torch.optim.__file__)\\n612:         ):\\n613:             # optimizer support is still incomplete see\\n614:             # test_state_dict in test/dynamo/test_optimizers.py\\n615:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n616: \\n617:         # Check if the frame is generated by an exec builtin call\\n618:         # TODO - Running exec generated frame seems propagates f_globals to the\\n619:         # next frames.\\n620:         if code.co_name == \\\"<module>\\\" and code.co_filename == \\\"<string>\\\":\\n621:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n622: \\n623:         if (\\n624:             code.co_name == \\\"<lambda>\\\"\\n625:             and code.co_filename == \\\"<string>\\\"\\n626:             and not bool(frame.f_builtins)\\n627:         ):\\n628:             # namedtuple subclass constructor. type: ignore[assignment]\\n655:         if (\\n656:             prev_frame\\n657:             and prev_frame.f_code is decorators._nonrecursive_disable_wrapper_code\\n658:         ):\\n659:             return ConvertFrameReturn(apply_to_code=False)\\n660: \\n661:         global initial_global_state\\n662:         initial_global_state = GlobalStateGuard()\\n663: \\n664:         compile_id = get_compile_id(frame_state)\\n665:         frame_id = compile_id.frame_id\\n666: \\n667:         signpost_event(\\n668:             \\\"dynamo\\\",\\n669:             \\\"_convert_frame_assert._compile\\\",\\n670:             {\\n671:                 \\\"co_name\\\": code.co_name,\\n672:                 \\\"frame_id\\\": frame_id,\\n673:                 \\\"compile_id\\\": str(compile_id),\\n674:                 \\\"co_filename\\\": code.co_filename,\\n675:                 \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": code.co_firstlineno,\\n676:                 \\\"cache_size\\\": cache_size.num_cache_entries_with_same_id_matched_objs,\\n677:                 \\\"accumulated_cache_size\\\": cache_size.num_cache_entries,\\n678:             },\\n679:         )\\n680: \\n681:         # Record traced frames, skipping Dynamo generated ones.\\n682:         if not code.co_name.startswith(TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX):\\n683:             info = f\\\"{code.co_name} {code.co_filename}:{code.co_firstlineno}\\\"\\n684:             dynamo_tls.traced_frame_infos.append(info)\\n685: \\n686:         with compile_context(CompileContext(compile_id)):\\n687:             result = _compile(\\n688:                 frame.f_code,\\n689:                 frame.f_globals,\\n690:                 frame.f_locals,\\n691:                 frame.f_builtins,\\n692:                 frame.closure,\\n693:                 self._torchdynamo_orig_backend,\\n694:                 self._one_graph,\\n695:                 self._export,\\n696:                 self._export_constraints,\\n697:                 hooks,\\n698:                 cache_entry,\\n699:                 cache_size,\\n700:                 frame,\\n701:                 frame_state=frame_state,\\n702:                 compile_id=compile_id,\\n703:                 skip=skip + 1,\\n704:                 package=self._package,\\n705:                 convert_frame_box=self._box,\\n706:             )\\n707: \\n708:         if config.caching_precompile and self._package is not None:\\n709:             from .package import DynamoCache\\n710: \\n711:             # Record that the dynamo package has changed\\n712:             DynamoCache.record_package(self._package)\\n713:         return result\\n714: \\n715: \\n716: def convert_frame_assert(\\n717:     compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\\n718:     one_graph: bool = True,\\n719:     export: bool = False,\\n720:     export_constraints: Optional[typing.Never] = None,\\n721:     package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\\n722: ) -> ConvertFrameAssert:\\n723:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Fully convert a frame into an FX graph, raising an exception if we fail.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n724:     return ConvertFrameAssert(\\n725:         compiler_fn, one_graph, export, export_constraints, package\\n726:     )\\n727: \\n728: \\n729: from collections import OrderedDict\\n730: \\n731: from torch.utils.hooks import RemovableHandle\\n732: \\n733: \\n734: if typing.TYPE_CHECKING:\\n735:     from .output_graph import OutputGraph\\n736: \\n737: # we have to use `OrderedDict` to make `RemovableHandle` work.\\n738: _bytecode_hooks: dict[int, BytecodeHook] = OrderedDict()\\n739: \\n740: \\n741: def register_bytecode_hook(hook: BytecodeHook) -> RemovableHandle:\\n742:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Register hooks for bytecode generated by Dynamo. The hook can do some\\n743:     logging, as well as return a new code object to be used. Please refer\\n744:     to `BytecodeHook` for the hook signature.\\n745:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n746:     handle = RemovableHandle(_bytecode_hooks)\\n747:     _bytecode_hooks[handle.id] = hook\\n748:     return handle\\n749: \\n750: \\n751: def _compile(\\n752:     code: CodeType,\\n753:     globals: dict[str, object],\\n754:     locals: dict[str, object],\\n755:     builtins: dict[str, object],\\n756:     closure: tuple[CellType],\\n757:     compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\\n758:     one_graph: bool,\\n759:     export: bool,\\n760:     export_constraints: Optional[typing.Never],\\n761:     hooks: Hooks,\\n762:     cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\\n763:     cache_size: CacheSizeRelevantForFrame,\\n764:     frame: Optional[DynamoFrameType] = None,\\n765:     frame_state: Optional[dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]]] = None,\\n766:     *,\\n767:     compile_id: CompileId,\\n768:     skip: int = 0,\\n769:     package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\\n770:     # Can be used to record things for the caller, both\\n771:     # in the case of normal and exception code paths\\n772:     convert_frame_box: Optional[ConvertFrameBox] = None,\\n773: ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n774:     from torch._inductor.async_compile import async_compile_pool_manager\\n775:     from torch.fx.experimental.validator import (\\n776:         bisect,\\n777:         BisectValidationException,\\n778:         translation_validation_enabled,\\n779:         ValidationException,\\n780:     )\\n781: \\n782:     # Only nonlocal defs here please!\\n783:     # Time spent compiling this frame before restarting or failing analysis\\n784:     dynamo_time_before_restart: float = 0.0\\n785:     output: Optional[OutputGraph] = None\\n786:     tracer: Optional[InstructionTranslator] = None\\n787: \\n788:     tf_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode] = (\\n789:         torch.overrides._get_current_function_mode_stack()\\n790:     )\\n791: \\n792:     @preserve_global_state\\n793:     def transform(\\n794:         instructions: list[Instruction], code_options: dict[str, object]\\n795:     ) -> None:\\n796:         nonlocal output\\n797:         nonlocal tracer\\n798:         speculation_log.restart()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n799:         exn_vt_stack = ExceptionStack()\\n800:         tracer = InstructionTranslator(\\n801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL500-800\"}",

    "content": "500:             ps.sort_stats(pstats.SortKey.TIME).print_stats(20)\n501:             ps.sort_stats(pstats.SortKey.CUMULATIVE).print_stats(20)\n502: \n503:         if manifold_link := maybe_upload_prof_stats_to_manifold(\n504:             str(profile_path)\n505:         ):  # fb-only\n506:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\n507:                 \"link\",\n508:                 lambda: {\"name\": \"cprofile_manifold_url\", \"url\": manifold_link},\n509:             )\n510:         return retval\n511: \n512:     return profile_wrapper\n513: \n514: \n515: @dataclass\n516: class ConvertFrameBox:\n517:     error_on_graph_break: Optional[bool] = None\n518: \n519: \n520: def _is_error_on_graph_break(tx: Optional[InstructionTranslator]) -> bool:\n521:     if tx is None:\n522:         return _get_error_on_graph_break()\n523:     return tx.error_on_graph_break\n524: \n525: \n526: def get_compile_id(\n527:     frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\n528: ) -> CompileId:\n529:     global FRAME_COUNTER\n530:     if \"_id\" not in frame_state:\n531:         frame_state[\"_id\"] = FRAME_COUNTER\n532:         FRAME_COUNTER += 1\n533:     frame_id = frame_state[\"_id\"]\n534:     assert isinstance(frame_id, int)\n535: \n536:     frame_compile_id = FRAME_COMPILE_COUNTER[frame_id]\n537:     FRAME_COMPILE_COUNTER[frame_id] += 1\n538: \n539:     compiled_autograd_id = None\n540:     if prior := CompileContext.current_compile_id():\n541:         compiled_autograd_id = prior.compiled_autograd_id\n542:     return CompileId(\n543:         compiled_autograd_id=compiled_autograd_id,\n544:         frame_id=frame_id,\n545:         frame_compile_id=frame_compile_id,\n546:     )\n547: \n548: \n549: class ConvertFrameAssert:\n550:     def __init__(\n551:         self,\n552:         compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n553:         one_graph: bool = True,\n554:         export: bool = False,\n555:         export_constraints: Optional[typing.Never] = None,\n556:         package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n557:     ) -> None:\n558:         # assert export_constraints is None\n559:         reset_graph_break_dup_checker()\n560:         self._torchdynamo_orig_backend = compiler_fn\n561:         self._one_graph = one_graph\n562:         self._export = export\n563:         self._export_constraints = export_constraints\n564:         self._package = package\n565:         self._box = ConvertFrameBox()\n566: \n567:     @property\n568:     def _clone_with_backend(self) -> Callable[[CompilerFn], ConvertFrameAssert]:\n569:         return lambda backend: convert_frame_assert(\n570:             backend,\n571:             self._one_graph,\n572:             self._export,\n573:             self._export_constraints,\n574:         )\n575: \n576:     def __call__(\n577:         self,\n578:         frame: DynamoFrameType,\n579:         cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\n580:         hooks: Hooks,\n581:         frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\n582:         *,\n583:         skip: int = 0,\n584:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n585:         increment_frame()\n586:         code = frame.f_code\n587: \n588:         cache_size = compute_cache_size(frame, cache_entry)\n589:         input_codes.add(code)\n590:         if code in output_codes:\n591:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\n592:         if (\n593:             os.environ.get(\"TORCHDYNAMO_DEBUG_FUNCTION\")\n594:             and os.environ.get(\"TORCHDYNAMO_DEBUG_FUNCTION\") != code.co_name\n595:         ):\n596:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\n597:         if code.co_name == \"<genexpr>\" and code.co_filename.endswith(\n598:             (\n599:                 \"transformers/file_utils.py\",\n600:                 \"transformers/utils/generic.py\",\n601:                 \"diffusers/utils/outputs.py\",\n602:             )\n603:         ):\n604:             # not needed, but cleans up torchbench error stats\n605:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\n606:         if code.co_name == \"__setattr__\":\n607:             # setattr could be tricky to handle generally,\n608:             # but also not likely useful to compile- skip the whole frame\n609:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\n610:         if code.co_name == \"__init__\" and code.co_filename.startswith(\n611:             os.path.dirname(torch.optim.__file__)\n612:         ):\n613:             # optimizer support is still incomplete see\n614:             # test_state_dict in test/dynamo/test_optimizers.py\n615:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\n616: \n617:         # Check if the frame is generated by an exec builtin call\n618:         # TODO - Running exec generated frame seems propagates f_globals to the\n619:         # next frames.\n620:         if code.co_name == \"<module>\" and code.co_filename == \"<string>\":\n621:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\n622: \n623:         if (\n624:             code.co_name == \"<lambda>\"\n625:             and code.co_filename == \"<string>\"\n626:             and not bool(frame.f_builtins)\n627:         ):\n628:             # namedtuple subclass constructor. Empty builtins cause issue with\n629:             # len keyword in LIST_LEN guard.\n630:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\n631: \n632:         if is_generator(code):\n633:             unimplemented_v2(\n634:                 gb_type=\"Attempt to trace generator\",\n635:                 context=\"\",\n636:                 explanation=\"Generators cannot be compiled directly with `torch.compile`.\",\n637:                 hints=[\n638:                     \"Call a generator from inside of a non-generator Python function and \"\n639:                     \"compile that function instead.\",\n640:                     *graph_break_hints.FUNDAMENTAL,\n641:                 ],\n642:             )\n643: \n644:         if not has_tensor_in_frame(frame):\n645:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\n646: \n647:         # skip tracing non-recursive disabled functions\n648:         # detect if the previous frame (non-convert_frame) is a non-recursive disable wrapper\n649:         prev_frame = sys._getframe()\n650:         while (\n651:             prev_frame\n652:             and \"torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py\" in prev_frame.f_code.co_filename\n653:         ):\n654:             prev_frame = prev_frame.f_back  # type: ignore[assignment]\n655:         if (\n656:             prev_frame\n657:             and prev_frame.f_code is decorators._nonrecursive_disable_wrapper_code\n658:         ):\n659:             return ConvertFrameReturn(apply_to_code=False)\n660: \n661:         global initial_global_state\n662:         initial_global_state = GlobalStateGuard()\n663: \n664:         compile_id = get_compile_id(frame_state)\n665:         frame_id = compile_id.frame_id\n666: \n667:         signpost_event(\n668:             \"dynamo\",\n669:             \"_convert_frame_assert._compile\",\n670:             {\n671:                 \"co_name\": code.co_name,\n672:                 \"frame_id\": frame_id,\n673:                 \"compile_id\": str(compile_id),\n674:                 \"co_filename\": code.co_filename,\n675:                 \"co_firstlineno\": code.co_firstlineno,\n676:                 \"cache_size\": cache_size.num_cache_entries_with_same_id_matched_objs,\n677:                 \"accumulated_cache_size\": cache_size.num_cache_entries,\n678:             },\n679:         )\n680: \n681:         # Record traced frames, skipping Dynamo generated ones.\n682:         if not code.co_name.startswith(TORCH_DYNAMO_RESUME_IN_PREFIX):\n683:             info = f\"{code.co_name} {code.co_filename}:{code.co_firstlineno}\"\n684:             dynamo_tls.traced_frame_infos.append(info)\n685: \n686:         with compile_context(CompileContext(compile_id)):\n687:             result = _compile(\n688:                 frame.f_code,\n689:                 frame.f_globals,\n690:                 frame.f_locals,\n691:                 frame.f_builtins,\n692:                 frame.closure,\n693:                 self._torchdynamo_orig_backend,\n694:                 self._one_graph,\n695:                 self._export,\n696:                 self._export_constraints,\n697:                 hooks,\n698:                 cache_entry,\n699:                 cache_size,\n700:                 frame,\n701:                 frame_state=frame_state,\n702:                 compile_id=compile_id,\n703:                 skip=skip + 1,\n704:                 package=self._package,\n705:                 convert_frame_box=self._box,\n706:             )\n707: \n708:         if config.caching_precompile and self._package is not None:\n709:             from .package import DynamoCache\n710: \n711:             # Record that the dynamo package has changed\n712:             DynamoCache.record_package(self._package)\n713:         return result\n714: \n715: \n716: def convert_frame_assert(\n717:     compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n718:     one_graph: bool = True,\n719:     export: bool = False,\n720:     export_constraints: Optional[typing.Never] = None,\n721:     package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n722: ) -> ConvertFrameAssert:\n723:     \"\"\"Fully convert a frame into an FX graph, raising an exception if we fail.\"\"\"\n724:     return ConvertFrameAssert(\n725:         compiler_fn, one_graph, export, export_constraints, package\n726:     )\n727: \n728: \n729: from collections import OrderedDict\n730: \n731: from torch.utils.hooks import RemovableHandle\n732: \n733: \n734: if typing.TYPE_CHECKING:\n735:     from .output_graph import OutputGraph\n736: \n737: # we have to use `OrderedDict` to make `RemovableHandle` work.\n738: _bytecode_hooks: dict[int, BytecodeHook] = OrderedDict()\n739: \n740: \n741: def register_bytecode_hook(hook: BytecodeHook) -> RemovableHandle:\n742:     \"\"\"Register hooks for bytecode generated by Dynamo. The hook can do some\n743:     logging, as well as return a new code object to be used. Please refer\n744:     to `BytecodeHook` for the hook signature.\n745:     \"\"\"\n746:     handle = RemovableHandle(_bytecode_hooks)\n747:     _bytecode_hooks[handle.id] = hook\n748:     return handle\n749: \n750: \n751: def _compile(\n752:     code: CodeType,\n753:     globals: dict[str, object],\n754:     locals: dict[str, object],\n755:     builtins: dict[str, object],\n756:     closure: tuple[CellType],\n757:     compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n758:     one_graph: bool,\n759:     export: bool,\n760:     export_constraints: Optional[typing.Never],\n761:     hooks: Hooks,\n762:     cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\n763:     cache_size: CacheSizeRelevantForFrame,\n764:     frame: Optional[DynamoFrameType] = None,\n765:     frame_state: Optional[dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]]] = None,\n766:     *,\n767:     compile_id: CompileId,\n768:     skip: int = 0,\n769:     package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n770:     #   "content": "{\"content\":\"800:         tracer = InstructionTranslator(\\n801:             instructions,\\n802:             code,\\n803:             locals,\\n804:             globals,\\n805:             builtins,\\n806:             closure,\\n807:             tf_mode_stack,\\n808:             code_options,\\n809:             compiler_fn,\\n810:             one_graph,\\n811:             export,\\n812:             export_constraints,\\n813:             frame_state=frame_state,\\n814:             speculation_log=speculation_log,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n815:             exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\\n816:             distributed_state=distributed_state,  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n817:             package=package,\\n818:         )\\n819: \\n820:         try:\\n821:             tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\\n822:             with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\\n823:                 tracer.run()\\n824:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\\n825:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n826:             raise\\n827:         except (\\n828:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\\n829:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\\n830:             exc.SkipFrame,\\n831:         ):\\n832:             raise\\n833:         except Exception:\\n834:             if translation_validation_enabled():\\n835:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\\n836:             raise\\n837:         finally:\\n838:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n839: \\n840:         output = tracer.output\\n841:         assert output is not None\\n842:         assert output.output_instructions\\n843:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\\n844:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\\n845:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\\n846:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\\n847:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\\n848: \\n849:     @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\\\"compile_inner\\\")\\n850:     def compile_inner(\\n851:         code: CodeType,\\n852:         one_graph: bool,\\n853:         hooks: Hooks,\\n854:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\\n855:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n856:         with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:\\n857:             stack.enter_context(\\n858:                 torch._dynamo.callback_handler.install_callbacks(\\n859:                     CallbackTrigger.DYNAMO, str(CompileContext.current_compile_id())\\n860:                 )\\n861:             )\\n862:             stack.enter_context(CompileTimeInstructionCounter.record())\\n863:             return _compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\\n864: \\n865:         return (\\n866:             ConvertFrameReturn()\\n867:         )  # dead, but see https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/7577\\n868: \\n869:     @maybe_cprofile\\n870:     def _compile_inner(\\n871:         code: CodeType,\\n872:         one_graph: bool,\\n873:         hooks: Hooks,\\n874:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\\n875:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\\n876:         nonlocal dynamo_time_before_restart\\n877:         last_attempt_start_time = start_time = time.time()\\n878: \\n879:         def log_bytecode(\\n880:             prefix: str, name: str, filename: str, line_no: int, code: CodeType\\n881:         ) -> None:\\n882:             if bytecode_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\\n883:                 bytecode_log.debug(\\n884:                     format_bytecode(prefix, name, filename, line_no, code)\\n885:                 )\\n886: \\n887:         log_bytecode(\\n888:             \\\"ORIGINAL BYTECODE\\\",\\n889:             code.co_name,\\n890:             code.co_filename,\\n891:             code.co_firstlineno,\\n892:             code,\\n893:         )\\n894: \\n895:         out_code = None\\n896:         for attempt in itertools.count():\\n897:             CompileContext.get().attempt = attempt\\n898:             try:\\n899:                 with dynamo_timed(\\n900:                     f\\\"compile_attempt_{attempt}\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True\\n901:                 ):\\n902:                     out_code = transform_code_object(code, transform)\\n903:                 break\\n904:             except exc.RestartAnalysis as e:\\n905:                 if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\\n906:                     TensorifyState.clear()\\n907:                 log.info(\\n908:                     \\\"Restarting analysis due to %s\\\",\\n909:                     LazyString(format_traceback_short, e.__traceback__),\\n910:                 )\\n911:                 # If restart reason is None just log the type of the exception\\n912:                 restart_reasons.add(e.restart_reason or str(type(e)))\\n913:                 # We now have a new \\\"last attempt\\\", reset the clock\\n914:                 last_attempt_start_time = time.time()\\n915:                 if attempt > 100:\\n916:                     unimplemented_v2(\\n917:                         gb_type=\\\"Excessive RestartAnalysis() calls\\\",\\n918:                         context=\\\"\\\",\\n919:                         explanation=\\\"Dynamo attempted to trace the same frame 100+ times. \\\"\\n920:                         \\\"Giving up on compiling as the compile time tradeoff is likely not \\\"\\n921:                         \\\"worth the performance gain.\\\",\\n922:                         hints=[],\\n923:                     )\\n924:             except exc.SkipFrame as e:\\n925:                 if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\\n926:                     TensorifyState.clear()\\n927:                 log.debug(\\n928:                     \\\"Skipping frame %s %s \\\\\\n929:                     %s %s\\\",\\n930:                     e,\\n931:                     code.co_name,\\n932:                     code.co_filename,\\n933:                     code.co_firstlineno,\\n934:                 )\\n935:                 if one_graph or _is_error_on_graph_break(tracer):\\n936:                     log.debug(\\n937:                         \\\"No graph captured with one_graph=True or error_on_graph_break=True\\\"\\n938:                     )\\n939:                 return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n940: \\n941:         assert distributed_state is None or distributed_state.all_states is not None, (  # type: ignore[has-type]\\n942:             \\\"compiler collective wasn't run before compilation completed\\\"\\n943:         )\\n944: \\n945:         assert out_code is not None\\n946:         log_bytecode(\\n947:             \\\"MODIFIED BYTECODE\\\",\\n948:             code.co_name,\\n949:             code.co_filename,\\n950:             code.co_firstlineno,\\n951:             out_code,\\n952:         )\\n953: \\n954:         for idx, hook in enumerate(_bytecode_hooks.values()):\\n955:             with dynamo_timed(f\\\"bytecode_hooks_{idx}\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\\n956:                 hook_output = hook(code, out_code)\\n957:                 if hook_output is not None:\\n958:                     out_code = hook_output\\n959: \\n960:         orig_code_map[out_code] = code\\n961:         output_codes.add(out_code)\\n962:         dynamo_time_before_restart = last_attempt_start_time - start_time\\n963:         assert output is not None\\n964: \\n965:         # Tests for new code objects.\\n966:         # The rationale for these tests can be found in torch/csrc/dynamo/eval_frame.c\\n967:         # Only test once the code object is created.\\n968:         # They are not tested during runtime.\\n969: \\n970:         def count_args(code: CodeType) -> int:\\n971:             import inspect\\n972: \\n973:             return (\\n974:                 code.co_argcount\\n975:                 + code.co_kwonlyargcount\\n976:                 + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARARGS)\\n977:                 + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARKEYWORDS)\\n978:             )\\n979: \\n980:         assert out_code is not None\\n981: \\n982:         total_argcount_old = count_args(code)\\n983:         total_argcount_new = count_args(out_code)\\n984:         msg = \\\"arg mismatch: \\\"\\n985:         msg += f\\\"old code object has args {code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]}, \\\"\\n986:         msg += f\\\"new code object has args {out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]}\\\"\\n987:         assert (\\n988:             code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]\\n989:             == out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]\\n990:         ), msg\\n991: \\n992:         msg = \\\"free var mismatch: \\\"\\n993:         msg += f\\\"old code object has free var {code.co_freevars}, \\\"\\n994:         msg += f\\\"new code object has free var {out_code.co_freevars}\\\"\\n995:         assert code.co_freevars == out_code.co_freevars, msg\\n996: \\n997:         msg = \\\"cell var mismatch: \\\"\\n998:         msg += f\\\"old code object has cell var {code.co_cellvars}, \\\"\\n999:         msg += f\\\"new code object has cell var {out_code.co_cellvars}\\\"\\n1000:         assert code.co_cellvars == out_code.co_cellvars, msg\\n1001: \\n1002:         # Skipping Dynamo on a frame without any extracted graph.\\n1003:         # This does not affect eager functionality. But this is necessary\\n1004:         # for export for cases where Dynamo-reconstructed bytecode can create\\n1005:         # new function frames, confusing export in thinking that there\\n1006:         # are extra graphs now.\\n1007: \\n1008:         if output.export and output.is_empty_graph():\\n1009:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\\n1010: \\n1011:         assert output.guards is not None\\n1012:         CleanupManager.instance[out_code] = output.cleanups\\n1013:         nonlocal cache_entry\\n1014:         with dynamo_timed(\\\"build_guards\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\\n1015:             check_fn = CheckFunctionManager(\\n1016:                 code,\\n1017:                 output,\\n1018:                 cache_entry,\\n1019:                 hooks.guard_fail_fn if hooks else None,\\n1020:                 hooks.guard_filter_fn if hooks else None,\\n1021:                 guards_serialization_mode=\\\"save\\\" if package else None,\\n1022:             )\\n1023: \\n1024:         if package is not None:\\n1025:             assert check_fn.guards_state is not None\\n1026:             package.add_guarded_code(check_fn.guards_state, out_code)\\n1027:             package.add_inlined_source(output.tracing_context.traced_code)\\n1028: \\n1029:         compile_id_str = str(compile_id) if compile_id is not None else \\\"Unknown\\\"\\n1030:         annotation_str = \\\"Torch-Compiled Region: \\\" + compile_id_str\\n1031:         guarded_code = GuardedCode(\\n1032:             out_code,\\n1033:             check_fn.guard_manager,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1034:             compile_id,\\n1035:             annotation_str,\\n1036:         )\\n1037: \\n1038:         if not output.is_empty_graph() and hooks.guard_export_fn is not None:\\n1039:             # We should not run the guard_export_fn when Dynamo does not\\n1040:             # generate any graph. This can happen in export when TorchDynamo\\n1041:             # generated bytecode has some reconstruction logic for mutated\\n1042:             # variables which can trigger TorchDynamo on the children frames but\\n1043:             # they are benign and do not generate any new graphs.\\n1044:             hooks.guard_export_fn(output.guards)\\n1045: \\n1046:         return wrap_guarded_code(guarded_code)\\n1047: \\n1048:     metrics_context = get_metrics_context()\\n1049:     code_context = (\\n1050:         package.code_context(code) if package is not None else contextlib.nullcontext()\\n1051:     )\\n1052:     with (\\n1053:         _use_lazy_graph_module(config.use_lazy_graph_module),\\n1054:         compile_context(CompileContext(compile_id)),\\n1055:         async_compile_pool_manager(),\\n1056:         chromium_event_timed(\\n1057:             \\\"dynamo\\\", reset_event_log_on_exit=True, log_pt2_compile_event=True\\n1058:         ),\\n1059:         _WaitCounter(\\\"pytorch.wait_counter.entire_forward_compile\\\").guard(),\\n1060:         metrics_context,\\n1061:         dynamo_timed(\\n1062:             \\\"_compile.compile_inner\\\",\\n1063:             phase_name=\\\"entire_frame_compile\\\",\\n1064:             dynamo_compile_column_us=\\\"dynamo_cumulative_compile_time_us\\\",\\n1065:         ),\\n1066:         code_context,\\n1067:     ):\\n1068:         restart_reasons: set[str] = set()\\n1069:         # This is shared across restarts\\n1070:         speculation_log = SpeculationLog()\\n1071:         if compile_pg := get_compile_pg():\\n1072:             distributed_state = DistributedState(compile_pg, LocalState())\\n1073:         else:\\n1074:             distributed_state = None\\n1075: \\n1076:         # Check recompilations\\n1077:         recompile_reason: Optional[str] = None\\n1078:         if is_recompilation(cache_size) and frame:\\n1079:             reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(cache_entry, frame)\\n1080:             recompile_reason = (\\n1081:                 \\\"Unable to find recompilation reasons\\\" if not reasons else reasons[0]\\n1082:             )\\n1083:         # Recheck for recompilation, for when inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is set to False\\n1084:         inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = False\\n1085:         if not config.inline_inbuilt_nn_modules and frame:\\n1086:             inbuilt_nn_reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(\\n1087:                 cache_entry, frame, skip_logging=True\\n1088:             )\\n1089:             inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason = (\\n1090:                 None if not inbuilt_nn_reasons else inbuilt_nn_reasons[0]\\n1091:             )\\n1092: \\n1093:             if (\\n1094:                 inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason is not None\\n1095:                 and \\\"[inline-inbuilt-nn-modules-candidate]\\\"\\n1096:                 in inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason\\n1097:             ):\\n1098:                 inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = True\\n1099: \\n1100:         # Set if the recompile is a candidate for inline_inbuilt_nn_modules\\n1101: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL800-1100\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "800:         tracer = InstructionTranslator(\n801:             instructions,\n802:             code,\n803:             locals,\n804:             globals,\n805:             builtins,\n806:             closure,\n807:             tf_mode_stack,\n808:             code_options,\n809:             compiler_fn,\n810:             one_graph,\n811:             export,\n812:             export_constraints,\n813:             frame_state=frame_state,\n814:             speculation_log=speculation_log,  # type: ignore[has-type]\n815:             exn_vt_stack=exn_vt_stack,\n816:             distributed_state=distributed_state,  # type: ignore[has-type]\n817:             package=package,\n818:         )\n819: \n820:         try:\n821:             tracer.output.mark_bytecode_tracing_start()\n822:             with tracing(tracer.output.tracing_context), tracer.set_current_tx():\n823:                 tracer.run()\n824:         except exc.UnspecializeRestartAnalysis:\n825:             speculation_log.clear()  # type: ignore[has-type]\n826:             raise\n827:         except (\n828:             exc.SpeculationRestartAnalysis,\n829:             exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis,\n830:             exc.SkipFrame,\n831:         ):\n832:             raise\n833:         except Exception:\n834:             if translation_validation_enabled():\n835:                 bisect(tracer.output.shape_env)\n836:             raise\n837:         finally:\n838:             tracer.output.call_cleanup_hooks()\n839: \n840:         output = tracer.output\n841:         assert output is not None\n842:         assert output.output_instructions\n843:         instructions[:] = output.output_instructions\n844:         code_options.update(output.code_options)\n845:         propagate_inst_exn_table_entries(instructions)\n846:         check_inst_exn_tab_entries_valid(instructions)\n847:         instructions[:] = remove_pointless_jumps(remove_dead_code(instructions))\n848: \n849:     @compile_time_strobelight_meta(phase_name=\"compile_inner\")\n850:     def compile_inner(\n851:         code: CodeType,\n852:         one_graph: bool,\n853:         hooks: Hooks,\n854:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\n855:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n856:         with contextlib.ExitStack() as stack:\n857:             stack.enter_context(\n858:                 torch._dynamo.callback_handler.install_callbacks(\n859:                     CallbackTrigger.DYNAMO, str(CompileContext.current_compile_id())\n860:                 )\n861:             )\n862:             stack.enter_context(CompileTimeInstructionCounter.record())\n863:             return _compile_inner(code, one_graph, hooks, transform)\n864: \n865:         return (\n866:             ConvertFrameReturn()\n867:         )  # dead, but see https://github.com/python/mypy/issues/7577\n868: \n869:     @maybe_cprofile\n870:     def _compile_inner(\n871:         code: CodeType,\n872:         one_graph: bool,\n873:         hooks: Hooks,\n874:         transform: Callable[[list[Instruction], dict[str, Any]], Any],\n875:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n876:         nonlocal dynamo_time_before_restart\n877:         last_attempt_start_time = start_time = time.time()\n878: \n879:         def log_bytecode(\n880:             prefix: str, name: str, filename: str, line_no: int, code: CodeType\n881:         ) -> None:\n882:             if bytecode_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\n883:                 bytecode_log.debug(\n884:                     format_bytecode(prefix, name, filename, line_no, code)\n885:                 )\n886: \n887:         log_bytecode(\n888:             \"ORIGINAL BYTECODE\",\n889:             code.co_name,\n890:             code.co_filename,\n891:             code.co_firstlineno,\n892:             code,\n893:         )\n894: \n895:         out_code = None\n896:         for attempt in itertools.count():\n897:             CompileContext.get().attempt = attempt\n898:             try:\n899:                 with dynamo_timed(\n900:                     f\"compile_attempt_{attempt}\", log_pt2_compile_event=True\n901:                 ):\n902:                     out_code = transform_code_object(code, transform)\n903:                 break\n904:             except exc.RestartAnalysis as e:\n905:                 if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\n906:                     TensorifyState.clear()\n907:                 log.info(\n908:                     \"Restarting analysis due to %s\",\n909:                     LazyString(format_traceback_short, e.__traceback__),\n910:                 )\n911:                 # If restart reason is None just log the type of the exception\n912:                 restart_reasons.add(e.restart_reason or str(type(e)))\n913:                 # We now have a new \"last attempt\", reset the clock\n914:                 last_attempt_start_time = time.time()\n915:                 if attempt > 100:\n916:                     unimplemented_v2(\n917:                         gb_type=\"Excessive RestartAnalysis() calls\",\n918:                         context=\"\",\n919:                         explanation=\"Dynamo attempted to trace the same frame 100+ times. \"\n920:                         \"Giving up on compiling as the compile time tradeoff is likely not \"\n921:                         \"worth the performance gain.\",\n922:                         hints=[],\n923:                     )\n924:             except exc.SkipFrame as e:\n925:                 if not isinstance(e, exc.TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis):\n926:                     TensorifyState.clear()\n927:                 log.debug(\n928:                     \"Skipping frame %s %s \\\n929:                     %s %s\",\n930:                     e,\n931:                     code.co_name,\n932:                     code.co_filename,\n933:                     code.co_firstlineno,\n934:                 )\n935:                 if one_graph or _is_error_on_graph_break(tracer):\n936:                     log.debug(\n937:                         \"No graph captured with one_graph=True or error_on_graph_break=True\"\n938:                     )\n939:                 return ConvertFrameReturn()\n940: \n941:         assert distributed_state is None or distributed_state.all_states is not None, (  # type: ignore[has-type]\n942:             \"compiler collective wasn't run before compilation completed\"\n943:         )\n944: \n945:         assert out_code is not None\n946:         log_bytecode(\n947:             \"MODIFIED BYTECODE\",\n948:             code.co_name,\n949:             code.co_filename,\n950:             code.co_firstlineno,\n951:             out_code,\n952:         )\n953: \n954:         for idx, hook in enumerate(_bytecode_hooks.values()):\n955:             with dynamo_timed(f\"bytecode_hooks_{idx}\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\n956:                 hook_output = hook(code, out_code)\n957:                 if hook_output is not None:\n958:                     out_code = hook_output\n959: \n960:         orig_code_map[out_code] = code\n961:         output_codes.add(out_code)\n962:         dynamo_time_before_restart = last_attempt_start_time - start_time\n963:         assert output is not None\n964: \n965:         # Tests for new code objects.\n966:         # The rationale for these tests can be found in torch/csrc/dynamo/eval_frame.c\n967:         # Only test once the code object is created.\n968:         # They are not tested during runtime.\n969: \n970:         def count_args(code: CodeType) -> int:\n971:             import inspect\n972: \n973:             return (\n974:                 code.co_argcount\n975:                 + code.co_kwonlyargcount\n976:                 + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARARGS)\n977:                 + bool(code.co_flags & inspect.CO_VARKEYWORDS)\n978:             )\n979: \n980:         assert out_code is not None\n981: \n982:         total_argcount_old = count_args(code)\n983:         total_argcount_new = count_args(out_code)\n984:         msg = \"arg mismatch: \"\n985:         msg += f\"old code object has args {code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]}, \"\n986:         msg += f\"new code object has args {out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]}\"\n987:         assert (\n988:             code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_old]\n989:             == out_code.co_varnames[:total_argcount_new]\n990:         ), msg\n991: \n992:         msg = \"free var mismatch: \"\n993:         msg += f\"old code object has free var {code.co_freevars}, \"\n994:         msg += f\"new code object has free var {out_code.co_freevars}\"\n995:         assert code.co_freevars == out_code.co_freevars, msg\n996: \n997:         msg = \"cell var mismatch: \"\n998:         msg += f\"old code object has cell var {code.co_cellvars}, \"\n999:         msg += f\"new code object has cell var {out_code.co_cellvars}\"\n1000:         assert code.co_cellvars == out_code.co_cellvars, msg\n1001: \n1002:         # Skipping Dynamo on a frame without any extracted graph.\n1003:         # This does not affect eager functionality. But this is necessary\n1004:         # for export for cases where Dynamo-reconstructed bytecode can create\n1005:         # new function frames, confusing export in thinking that there\n1006:         # are extra graphs now.\n1007: \n1008:         if output.export and output.is_empty_graph():\n1009:             return ConvertFrameReturn()\n1010: \n1011:         assert output.guards is not None\n1012:         CleanupManager.instance[out_code] = output.cleanups\n1013:         nonlocal cache_entry\n1014:         with dynamo_timed(\"build_guards\", log_pt2_compile_event=True):\n1015:             check_fn = CheckFunctionManager(\n1016:                 code,\n1017:                 output,\n1018:                 cache_entry,\n1019:                 hooks.guard_fail_fn if hooks else None,\n1020:                 hooks.guard_filter_fn if hooks else None,\n1021:                 guards_serialization_mode=\"save\" if package else None,\n1022:             )\n1023: \n1024:         if package is not None:\n1025:             assert check_fn.guards_state is not None\n1026:             package.add_guarded_code(check_fn.guards_state, out_code)\n1027:             package.add_inlined_source(output.tracing_context.traced_code)\n1028: \n1029:         compile_id_str = str(compile_id) if compile_id is not None else \"Unknown\"\n1030:         annotation_str = \"Torch-Compiled Region: \" + compile_id_str\n1031:         guarded_code = GuardedCode(\n1032:             out_code,\n1033:             check_fn.guard_manager,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n1034:             compile_id,\n1035:             annotation_str,\n1036:         )\n1037: \n1038:         if not output.is_empty_graph() and hooks.guard_export_fn is not None:\n1039:             # We should not run the guard_export_fn when Dynamo does not\n1040:             # generate any graph. This can happen in export when TorchDynamo\n1041:             # generated bytecode has some reconstruction logic for mutated\n1042:             # variables which can trigger TorchDynamo on the children frames but\n1043:             # they are benign and do not generate any new graphs.\n1044:             hooks.guard_export_fn(output.guards)\n1045: \n1046:         return wrap_guarded_code(guarded_code)\n1047: \n1048:     metrics_context = get_metrics_context()\n1049:     code_context = (\n1050:         package.code_context(code) if package is not None else contextlib.nullcontext()\n1051:     )\n1052:     with (\n1053:         _use_lazy_graph_module(config.use_lazy_graph_module),\n1054:         compile_context(CompileContext(compile_id)),\n1055:         async_compile_pool_manager(),\n1056:         chromium_event_timed(\n1057:             \"dynamo\", reset_event_log_on_exit=True, log_pt2_compile_event=True\n1058:         ),\n1059:         _WaitCounter(\"pytorch.wait_counter.entire_forward_compile\").guard(),\n1060:         metrics_context,\n1061:         dynamo_timed(\n1062:             \"_compile.compile_inner\",\n1063:             phase_name=\"entire_frame_compile\",\n1064:             dynamo_compile_column_us=\"dynamo_cumulative_compile_time_us\",\n1065:         ),\n1066:         code_context,\n1067:     ):\n1068:         restart_reasons: set[str] = set()\n1069:         # This is shared across restarts\n1070:         speculation_log = SpeculationLog()\n1071:         if compile_pg := get_compile_pg():\n1072:             distributed_state = DistributedState(compile_pg, LocalState())\n1073:         else:\n1074:             distributed_state = None\n1075: \n1076:         # Check recompilations\n1077:         recompile_reason: Optional[str] = None\n1078:         if is_recompilation(cache_size) and frame:\n1079:             reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(cache_entry, frame)\n1080:             recompile_reason = (\n1081:                 \"Unable to find recompilation reasons\" if not reasons else reasons[0]\n1082:             )\n1083:         # Recheck for recompilation, for when inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is set to False\n1084:         inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = False\n1085:         if not config.inline_inbuilt_nn_modules and frame:\n1086:             inbuilt_nn_reasons = get_and_maybe_log_recompilation_reasons(\n1087:                 cache_entry, frame, skip_logging=True\n1088:             )\n1089:             inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason = (\n1090:                 None if not inbuilt_nn_reasons else inbuilt_nn_reasons[0]\n1091:             )\n1092: \n1093:             if (\n1094:                 inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason is not None\n1095:                 and \"[inline-inbuilt-nn-modules-candidate]\"\n1096:                 in inbuilt_nn_recompile_reason\n1097:             ):\n1098:                 inline_inbuilt_nn_modules_candidate = True\n1099: \n1100:         # Set if the recompile is a candidate for inline_inbuilt_nn_modules\n1101: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL800-1100"
  }

NB: We only put_code_state in success case.  Success case here\\n1204:             # does include graph breaks; specifically, if a graph break still\\n1205:             # resulted in a partially compiled graph, we WILL return here.  An\\n1206:             # Unsupported exception will only bubble to the top level if we\\n1207:             # are unable to compile the frame at all.  In this case, there's\\n1208:             # no point in uploading the code state, because we will always\\n1209:             # fail exactly the same way even without the update. symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break at the time of the graph break -\\n1345:             # symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break may have been (correctly) changed during cleanup.\\n1346: = self._inner_convert(\\n1387:                 frame, cache_entry, hooks, frame_state, skip=skip + 1\\n1388:             )\\n1389:             counters[\\\"frames\\\"][\\\"ok\\\"] += 1\\n1390: do not recursively skip frames\n1156:                 unimplemented_v2(\n1157:                     gb_type=\"Dynamo cache limit exceeded\",\n1158:                     context=f\"Limit type: {limit_type}\",\n1159:                     explanation=\"Dynamo attempted to recompile the code object too many times, \"\n1160:                     f\"exceeding the {limit_type} cache size limit.\"\n1161:                     \"Giving up on compiling as the compile time tradeoff is likely not \"\n1162:                     \"worth the performance gain.\",\n1163:                     hints=[],\n1164:                 )\n1165: \n1166:         log.debug(\n1167:             \"torchdynamo start compiling %s %s:%s, stack (elided %s frames):\\n%s\",\n1168:             code.co_name,\n1169:             code.co_filename,\n1170:             code.co_firstlineno,\n1171:             skip + 2,\n1172:             # NB: We only put_code_state in success case.  Success case here\n1204:             # does include graph breaks; specifically, if a graph break still\n1205:             # resulted in a partially compiled graph, we WILL return here.  An\n1206:             # Unsupported exception will only bubble to the top level if we\n1207:             # are unable to compile the frame at all. So we grab the fail\n1219:             # info here and add it to the metrics context below.\n1220:             fail_type = type(e).__qualname__\n1221:             fail_reason = str(e)\n1222:             exception_handler(e, code, frame, export=export)\n1223:             # NB: this is the post-mutation exception\n1224:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\n1225:                 \"artifact\",\n1226:                 metadata_fn=lambda: {\n1227:                     \"name\": \"dynamo_error\",\n1228:                     \"encoding\": \"string\",\n1229:                 },\n1230:                 payload_fn=lambda: traceback.format_exc(),\n1231:             )\n1232:             fail_user_frame_filename, fail_user_frame_lineno = exc.get_exc_message(\n1233:                 e, compile_id\n1234:             )\n1235:             if tracer and tracer.is_tracing_resume_prologue:\n1236:                 # Do not allow any errors to be suppressed if tracer is currently tracing\n1237:                 # through resume function.\n1238:                 raise ResumePrologueTracingError(\n1239:                     \"Error while tracing through a Dynamo-generated resume function prologue. \"\n1240:                     \"Errors are not allowed when tracing resume function prologues.\\n\"\n1241:                     f\"{type(e).__qualname__}: {str(e)}\"\n1242:                 ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n1243:             elif isinstance(\n1244:                 e,\n1245:                 (\n1246:                     Unsupported,\n1247:                     TorchRuntimeError,\n1248:                     BackendCompilerFailed,\n1249:                     AssertionError,\n1250:                     ConstraintViolationError,\n1251:                     GuardOnDataDependentSymNode,\n1252:                     ValidationException,\n1253:                     UncapturedHigherOrderOpError,\n1254:                     BisectValidationException,\n1255:                     ShortenTraceback,\n1256:                     PackageError,\n1257:                 ),\n1258:             ):\n1259:                 raise\n1260:             else:\n1261:                 # Rewrap for clarity\n1262:                 raise InternalTorchDynamoError(\n1263:                     f\"{type(e).__qualname__}: {str(e)}\"\n1264:                 ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n1265:         finally:\n1266:             # === WARNING WARNING WARNING ===\n1267:             # If you commit a bug here, it will suppress writing to\n1268:             # dynamo_compile table, and we will not have telemetry.\n1269:             # Be extra careful when making changes here!\n1270: \n1271:             if torch._dynamo.config.run_gc_after_compile:\n1272:                 with dynamo_timed(\"gc\", dynamo_compile_column_us=\"gc_time_us\"):\n1273:                     log.info(\"run_gc_after_compile: running gc\")\n1274:                     gc.collect(1)\n1275: \n1276:             if tracer:\n1277:                 tracer.output.local_scope = {}\n1278:                 tracer.f_locals = {}\n1279: \n1280:             from .utils import curr_frame\n1281: \n1282:             frame_key = str(curr_frame)\n1283:             if fail_reason is None and output is not None:\n1284:                 guard_count = len(output.guards)\n1285:                 shape_env_guard_count = len(output.shape_env.guards)\n1286:                 graph_op_count = output.count_calls()\n1287:                 graph_node_count = len(output.graph.nodes)\n1288:                 graph_node_shapes = output.get_graph_sizes_structured()\n1289:                 graph_input_count = len(output.placeholders)\n1290:                 non_compliant_ops = {op.__qualname__ for op in output.non_compliant_ops}\n1291:                 compliant_custom_ops = {\n1292:                     op.__qualname__ for op in output.compliant_custom_ops\n1293:                 }\n1294:                 torch._dynamo.utils.ReinplaceCounters.log()\n1295:             else:\n1296:                 guard_count = None\n1297:                 shape_env_guard_count = None\n1298:                 graph_op_count = None\n1299:                 graph_node_count = None\n1300:                 graph_node_shapes = {}\n1301:                 graph_input_count = None\n1302:                 non_compliant_ops = set({})\n1303:                 compliant_custom_ops = set({})\n1304:                 restart_reasons = set()\n1305:                 # If compilation failed, the entire time is wasted\n1306:                 dynamo_time_before_restart = (time.time_ns() - start_time_ns) / 1e9\n1307: \n1308:             metrics = {\n1309:                 \"frame_key\": frame_key,\n1310:                 \"co_name\": code.co_name,\n1311:                 \"co_filename\": code.co_filename,\n1312:                 \"co_firstlineno\": code.co_firstlineno,\n1313:                 \"cache_size\": cache_size.num_cache_entries_with_same_id_matched_objs,\n1314:                 \"accumulated_cache_size\": cache_size.num_cache_entries,\n1315:                 \"guard_count\": guard_count,\n1316:                 \"shape_env_guard_count\": shape_env_guard_count,\n1317:                 \"graph_op_count\": graph_op_count,\n1318:                 \"graph_node_count\": graph_node_count,\n1319:                 \"graph_input_count\": graph_input_count,\n1320:                 \"fail_type\": fail_type,\n1321:                 \"fail_reason\": fail_reason,\n1322:                 \"fail_user_frame_filename\": fail_user_frame_filename,\n1323:                 \"fail_user_frame_lineno\": fail_user_frame_lineno,\n1324:                 \"non_compliant_ops\": non_compliant_ops,\n1325:                 \"compliant_custom_ops\": compliant_custom_ops,\n1326:                 \"restart_reasons\": restart_reasons,\n1327:                 \"dynamo_time_before_restart_s\": dynamo_time_before_restart,\n1328:                 \"has_guarded_code\": guarded_code is not None,\n1329:                 \"specialize_float\": config.specialize_float,\n1330:                 \"is_forward\": True,\n1331:                 \"dynamo_compile_time_before_restart_us\": to_int_us(\n1332:                     dynamo_time_before_restart\n1333:                 ),\n1334:                 \"stack_trace\": stack_trace,\n1335:                 \"graph_node_shapes\": str(graph_node_shapes),\n1336:             }\n1337:             # TODO: replace with CompileEventLogger.compilation_metrics\n1338:             # There are some columns here not in PT2 Compile Events\n1339:             # so we need to slightly change it\n1340:             metrics_context.update_outer(metrics)\n1341:             # === END WARNING WARNING WARNING ===\n1342: \n1343:             # If tracer is available, then tracer.error_on_graph_break reflects value of\n1344:             # global symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break at the time of the graph break -\n1345:             # symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break may have been (correctly) changed during cleanup.\n1346:             # If tracer is unavailable, then fallback to symbolic_convert.error_on_graph_break.\n1347:             if convert_frame_box:\n1348:                 convert_frame_box.error_on_graph_break = (\n1349:                     tracer.error_on_graph_break\n1350:                     if tracer\n1351:                     else _get_error_on_graph_break()\n1352:                 )\n1353: \n1354: \n1355: class ConvertFrame:\n1356:     def __init__(\n1357:         self,\n1358:         compiler_fn: CompilerFn,\n1359:         hooks: Hooks,\n1360:         package: Optional[CompilePackage] = None,\n1361:     ) -> None:\n1362:         self._torchdynamo_orig_backend = compiler_fn\n1363:         self._inner_convert = convert_frame_assert(\n1364:             compiler_fn, one_graph=False, package=package\n1365:         )\n1366:         self._hooks = hooks\n1367: \n1368:     @property\n1369:     def _clone_with_backend(self) -> Callable[[WrapBackendDebug], ConvertFrame]:\n1370:         return lambda backend: convert_frame(\n1371:             backend,\n1372:             self._hooks,\n1373:         )\n1374: \n1375:     def __call__(\n1376:         self,\n1377:         frame: DynamoFrameType,\n1378:         cache_entry: Optional[CacheEntry],\n1379:         hooks: Hooks,\n1380:         frame_state: dict[str, Union[int, FrameStateSizeEntry]],\n1381:         skip: int = 0,\n1382:     ) -> ConvertFrameReturn:\n1383:         input_codes.add(frame.f_code)\n1384:         counters[\"frames\"][\"total\"] += 1\n1385:         try:\n1386:             result = self._inner_convert(\n1387:                 frame, cache_entry, hooks, frame_state, skip=skip + 1\n1388:             )\n1389:             counters[\"frames\"][\"ok\"] += 1\n1390:             return result\n1391:         except Exception as e:\n1392:             # Do not allow errors to be suppressed if we're tracing a resume function prologue\n1393:             if isinstance(e, ResumePrologueTracingError):\n1394:                 raise\n1395: \n1396:             error_on_graph_break = (\n1397:                 self._inner_convert._box.error_on_graph_break is not None\n1398:             )\n1399:             assert error_on_graph_break is not None\n1400:             if self._inner_convert._box.error_on_graph_break:\n1401: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py%3FL1100-1400"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1400:             if self._inner_convert._box.error_on_graph_break:\\n1401:                 # NOTE we _might_ have to wrap the current in a custom exception\\n1402:                 # in order to correctly bubble up to the top-level compile wrapper in\\n1403:                 # eval_frame.py. But re-raising seems to work for now because exceptions from tracing\\n1404:                 # a nested call that results in a top-level frame compile will be handled by the caller\\n1405:                 # as an observed exception - we don't expect that exception to be suppressed.\\n1406:                 raise\\n1407: \\n1408:             # These two exception types are \\\"soft\\\" failure, in the sense that\\n1409:             # we know this is due to something we didn't implement all the\\n1410:             # way, scare the user less about it.  That being said, if you\\n1411:             # are trying to understand why a graph break happened, it's still\\n1412:             # important to have this information, so offer it.\\n1413:             #\\n1414:             # NB: NotImplementedError used to be on this list, but actually\\n1415:             # it is impossible for it to reach here, as it is converted into\\n1416:             # InternalTorchDynamoError.  This behavior seemed reasonable\\n1417:             # to me (ezyang, Aug 2023) so I kept it, but maybe at some point\\n1418:             # someone wanted these to also get suppressed.  If so, you'll\\n1419:             # need to make these exceptions not get wrapped\\n1420: \\n1421:             # We intentionally don't want to suppress error here.\\n1422:             if isinstance(e, UncapturedHigherOrderOpError):\\n1423:                 raise\\n1424: \\n1425:             soft_fail = isinstance(e, Unsupported)\\n1426: \\n1427:             # This is a soft failure. In the sense, the code path reaches here\\n1428:             # when we do not support graph breaks on bytecodes like LOAD_ATTR,\\n1429:             # BUILD_SET etc. In such case, we can fallback to eager without\\n1430:             # scaring users.\\n1431:             if soft_fail and graph_break_log.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG):\\n1432:                 # Log this message in the graph break. NB: It's very important to do the\\n1459:             # suppression logging HERE, where the actual suppression\\n1460:             # happens. Previously it was somewhere else and so it was\\n1461:             # possible to accidentally not log at all.\\n1462:             record_filename = getattr(e, \\\"record_filename\\\", None)\\n1463: exc_info=True)\\n1468:             else:\\n1469:                 log.warning(error_msg, exc_info=True)\\n1470: \\n1471:             if isinstance(e, SkipCodeRecursiveException):\\n1472: -> ConvertFrame:\\n1492:     \\\"\\\"\\\"Try to convert a frame into an FX graph, if error leave frame unmodified\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1493:     return ConvertFrame(compiler_fn, hooks, package=package)\\n1494: \\n1495: \\n1496: # TODO mlazos: add support for same args, or record them\\n1497: def replay(filename: str) -> None:\\n1498:     from .backends.debugging import eager\\n1499: first_real_inst_idx(frame.f_code)\\n1570:         if (\\n1571:             # TODO: the first condition is not covered by any test\\n1572:             has_started_execution\\n1573:             or is_skipfile\\n1574:             or config.disable\\n1575:             or (\\n1576:                 is_in_torch_dispatch_mode(include_infra_modes=False)\\n1577:                 and not CatchErrorsWrapper:\n1551:     def __init__(self, callback: ConvertFrameProtocol, hooks: Hooks) -> None:\n1552:         functools.wraps(callback)(self)\n1553:         self._torchdynamo_orig_backend = test\n1572:             has_started_execution\n1573:             or is_skipfile\n1574:             or config.disable\n1575:             or (\n1576:                 is_in_torch_dispatch_mode(include_infra_modes=False)\n1577:                 and not getattr(self._torchdynamo_orig_backend, \"_export\", False)\n1578:             )\n1579:         ):\n1580:
