if verify_correctness=True\n281:         try:\n282:             correct = self.gm.forward(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n283:             result = self.candidate(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n284: \n285:             # TODO: replace `same` function with the one in testing\n286:             if same(correct, result):\n287:                 return self.candidate\n288: \n289:             raise RuntimeError(f\"incorrect results of backend {self}\")\n290: \n291:         except Exception:\n292:             log.exception(\"error in verify_correctness\")\n293:             raise\n294:         finally:\n295:             self.restore()\n296: \n297: \n298: Scope = dict[str, object]\n299: \n300: \n301: @dataclass\n302: class OutputGraphGuardsState:\n303:     \"\"\"\n304:     A base class containing fields that are considered \"persistent\" when we\n305:     want to save all the important state for reconstrucing guards in a different\n306:     process. Normally we don't need to add states here, but we may have to when\n307:     the information is needed to serialize the guards, so the fields here are\n308:     supposed to be serializable as a requirement.\n309:     \"\"\"\n310: \n311:     local_scope: Scope\n312:     global_scope: Scope\n313:     # This records the initial torch function mode stack for guarding\n314:     torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode]\n315:     guard_on_key_order: set[Source]\n316:     # Map from graph input's `Source` to sizes / strides metadata\n317:     input_source_to_sizes_strides: dict[Source, dict[str, Any]]\n318:     dual_level: int\n319:     functorch_layers: list[torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.FuncTorchInterpreter]\n320:     current_device: Optional[torch.device]\n321:     global_state_guard: torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard\n322:     _guards: torch._guards.GuardsSet\n323:     _aotautograd_guards: list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]\n324: \n325:     export: bool = False\n326:     export_constraints: bool = False\n327:     name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: Optional[str] = None\n328: \n329:     @property\n330:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n331:         raise AssertionError(f\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\")\n332: \n333:     @property\n334:     def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n335:         return self._guards\n336: \n337:     @property\n338:     def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n339:         return self._aotautograd_guards\n340: \n341: \n342: @dataclass\n343: class StackLocalsMetadata:\n344:     \"\"\"\n345:     Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\n346:     \"\"\"\n347: \n348:     stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n349:     locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n350:     stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n351:     stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n352:     locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n353: \n354: \n355: def get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:\n356:     # f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n357:     # implementation detail -\n358:     # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n359: \n360:     # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n361:     # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n362:     # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n363: \n364:     # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n365:     # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n366:     # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n367:     # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n368: \n369:     f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n370:     if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n371:         f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n372:     return f_builtins\n373: \n374: \n375: class OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n376:     \"\"\"\n377:     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\n379: \n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\n382:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\n383:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\n384:     \"\"\"\n385: \n386:     side_effects: SideEffects\n387: \n388:     def __init__(\n389:         self,\n390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\n391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\n392:         root_tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n393:         export: bool,\n394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\n395:         frame_state: Any,\n396:         local_scope: Scope,\n397:         global_scope: Scope,\n398:         f_code: CodeType,\n399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\n400:         package: Optional[\"CompilePackage\"],\n401: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL100-400"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"400:         package: Optional[\\\"CompilePackage\\\"],\\n401:     ) -> None:\\n402:         super().__init__(\\n403:             local_scope,\\n404:             global_scope,\\n405:             torch_function_mode_stack,\\n406:             guard_on_key_order=set(),\\n407:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\\n408:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\\n409:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\\n410:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\\n411:             # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\\n412:             global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\\n413:             or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\\n414:             # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\\n415:             _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\\n416:             _aotautograd_guards=[],\\n417:         )\\n418:         self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\\n419:         # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\\n420:         # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\\n421:         self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\\n422:         self.export = export\\n423:         self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n424:         self.frame_state = frame_state\\n425:         self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\\n426:         # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\\n427:         self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\\n428:         # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\\n429:         self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\\n430: \\n431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\\n432:         # sure...\\n433:         self.co_fields = {\\n434:             \\\"co_name\\\": f_code.co_name,\\n435:             \\\"co_filename\\\": f_code.co_filename,\\n436:             \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\\n437:         }\\n438: \\n439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\\n440: \\n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n447: \\n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n451:             #\\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\\n455:             # the program execution.\\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\\n462:         )\\n463: \\n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\\n467: \\n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\\n473:                 export=self.export,\\n474:             )\\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\\n479:         )\\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\\n481: \\n482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\\n483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\\n484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\\n485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\\n486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\\n487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\\n488:             collections.defaultdict(list)\\n489:         )\\n490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\\n502: \\n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\\n505: \\n506:         # Not checkpointed\\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\\n509: \\n510:         self.package = package\\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\\n512:         # accessed it?\\n513:         #\\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\\n515:         #   - During export, and\\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\\n517:         #\\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\\n520:         # recording!\\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\\n522: \\n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\\n525:         self.should_exit = False\\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\\n527: \\n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\\n530: \\n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\\n535:         # back for backend errors.\\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\\n537: \\n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n541: \\n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n545: \\n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\\n551:         self.save_global_state()\\n552: \\n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\\n556: \\n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\\n561:         self.random_calls: list[\\n562:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\\n563:         ] = []\\n564:         self.random_values_var: Any = None\\n565: \\n566:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\\n567:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\\n568: \\n569:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\\n570:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\\n571:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\\n572:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\\n573: \\n574:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\\n575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\\n576:         )\\n577: \\n578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\\n579: \\n580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\\n581:         # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\\n582:         # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\\n583:         # context manager inside a compiled region\\n584:         self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\\n585:             self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\\n586:         )\\n587: \\n588:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\\n589:         self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\\n590:             dynamo_timed(\\n591:                 \\\"bytecode_tracing\\\",\\n592:                 log_pt2_compile_event=True,\\n593:             )\\n594:         )\\n595: \\n596:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\\n597:         self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\\n598: \\n599:     def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\\n600:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\\n601:         return self.install_global(\\\"__builtins_dict__\\\", f_builtins)\\n602: \\n603:     def add_backward_state_hook(\\n604:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \\\"hook\\\"\\n605:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\\n606:         name = f\\\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\\\"\\n607:         assert name not in self.backward_state\\n608:         self.backward_state[name] = hook\\n609:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\\n610: \\n611:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\\n612:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\\n613:             if self.export:\\n614:                 unimplemented_v2(\\n615:                     gb_type=\\\"backward_state does not support export\\\",\\n616:                     context=\\\"\\\",\\n617:                     explanation=\\\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\\\",\\n618:                     hints=[],\\n619:                 )\\n620:             example_value = BackwardState()\\n621:             self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\\n622:                 \\\"dynamo_backward_state\\\",\\n623:                 type(example_value),\\n624:                 example_value,\\n625:                 source=BackwardStateSource(),\\n626:             )\\n627:             self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\\n628:             self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\\n629:         return self.backward_state_proxy\\n630: \\n631:     # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\\n632:     def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\\n633:         # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\\n634:         # that show up in ShapeEnv\\n635:         self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\\n636: \\n637:         self.guards.add(\\n638:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\\n639:         )\\n640: \\n641:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\\n642: \\n643:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\\n644: \\n645:         self.guards.add(\\n646:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\\n647:         )\\n648: \\n649:         ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\\n650:         if ci is not None:\\n651:             self.guards.add(\\n652:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\\n653:             )\\n654:         if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\\n655:             self.guards.add(\\n656:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\\n657:                     GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\\n658:                 )\\n659:             )\\n660: \\n661:     def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\\n662:         if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\\n663:             return None\\n664: \\n665:         get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\\n666:         are_inline_hooks = (\\n667:             torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable\\n668:         )\\n669:         hooks = get_hooks()\\n670:         if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):\\n671:             return None\\n672: \\n673:         # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\\n674:         # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\\n675:         # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\\n676: \\n677:         pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\\n678:         pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\\n679:             \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\\\",\\n680:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\\n681:         )\\n682:         unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\\n683:             \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\\\",\\n684:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\\n685:         )\\n686:         assert pack_subgraph_name == \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0\\\"\\n687:         assert unpack_subgraph_name == \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0\\\"\\n688:         return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\\n689: \\n690:     def dump_guards_state(self) -> OutputGraphGuardsState:\\n691:         # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\\n692:         return OutputGraphGuardsState(\\n693:             local_scope=self.local_scope,\\n694:             global_scope=self.global_scope,\\n695:             torch_function_mode_stack=self.torch_function_mode_stack,\\n696:             guard_on_key_order=self.guard_on_key_order,\\n697: It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n447: \n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n451:             #\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n455:             # the program execution.\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\n462:         )\n463: \n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\n467: \n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n473:                 export=self.export,\n474:             )\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n479:         )\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\n481: \n482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\n483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\n484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\n485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\n486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\n487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\n488:             collections.defaultdict(list)\n489:         )\n490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\n502: \n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\n505: \n506:         # Not checkpointed\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\n509: \n510:         self.package = package\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\n512:         # accessed it?\n513:         #\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\n515:         #   - During export, and\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\n517:         #\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\n520:         # recording!\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\n522: \n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\n525:         self.should_exit = False\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\n527: \n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\n530: \n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\n535:         # back for backend errors.\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\n537: \n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n541: \n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n545: \n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n551:         self.save_global_state()\n552: \n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\n556: \n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\n561:         self.random_calls: list[\n562:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\n563:         ] = []\n564:         self.random_values_var: Any = None\n565: \n566:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\n567:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\n568: \n569:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\n570:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\n571:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\n572:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\n573: \n574:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\n575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\n576:         )\n577: \n578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n579: \n580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\n581:         # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\n582:         # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\n583:         # context manager inside a compiled region\n584:         self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\n585:             self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\n586:         )\n587: \n588:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\n589:         self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\n590:             dynamo_timed(\n591:                 \"bytecode_tracing\",\n592:                 log_pt2_compile_event=True,\n593:             )\n594:         )\n595: \n596:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\n597:         self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\n598: \n599:     def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\n600:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\n601:         return self.install_global(\"__builtins_dict__\", f_builtins)\n602: \n603:     def add_backward_state_hook(\n604:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \"hook\"\n605:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\n606:         name = f\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\"\n607:         assert name not in self.backward_state\n608:         self.backward_state[name] = hook\n609:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\n610: \n611:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n612:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\n613:             if self.export:\n614:                 unimplemented_v2(\n615:                     gb_type=\"backward_state does not support export\",\n616:                     context=\"\",\n617:                     explanation=\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\",\n618:                     hints=[],\n619:                 )\n620:             example_value = BackwardState()\n621:             self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\n622:                 \"dynamo_backward_state\",\n623:                 type(example_value),\n624:                 example_value,\n625:                 source=BackwardStateSource(),\n626:             )\n627:             self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\"grapharg\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\n628:             self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\n629:         return self.backward_state_proxy\n630: \n631:     # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\n632:     def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\n633:         # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\n634:         # that show up in ShapeEnv\n635:         self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\n636: \n637:         self.guards.add(\n638:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\n639:         )\n640: \n641:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\n642: \n643:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\n644: \n645:         self.guards.add(\n646:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\n647:         )\n648: \n649:         ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\n650:         if ci is not None:\n651:             self.guards.add(\n652:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\n653:             )\n654:         if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n655:             self.guards.add(\n656:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\n657:                     GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\n658:                 )\n659:             )\n660: \n661:     def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\n662:         if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n663:             return None\n664: \n665:         get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\n666:         are_inline_hooks = (\n667:             torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable\n668:         )\n669:         hooks = get_hooks()\n670:         if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):\n671:             return None\n672: \n673:         # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\n674:         # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\n675:         # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\n676: \n677:         pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\n678:         pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n679:             \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\",\n680:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\n681:         )\n682:         unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n683:             \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\",\n684:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\n685:         )\n686:         assert pack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0\"\n687:         assert unpack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0\"\n688:         return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\n689: \n690:     def dump_guards_state(self) -> OutputGraphGuardsState:\n691:         # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\n692:         return OutputGraphGuardsState(\n693:             local_scope=self.local_scope,\n694:             global_scope=self.global_scope,\n695:             torch_function_mode_stack=self.torch_function_mode_stack,\n696:             guard_on_key_order=self.guard_on_key_order,\n697:             input_source_to_sizes_strides=self.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\n698:             dual_level=self.dual_level,\n699:             functorch_layers=self.functorch_layers,\n700:             current_device=self.current_device,\n701: ",

None:\\n849:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n850:         Saves to out if it is provided. Else saves to the tracing context's global_state.\\n851:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n852:         global_state = cast(\\n853:             dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]],\\n854:             (\\n855:                 out\\n856:                 if out is not None\\n857:                 else self.tracing_context.global_context.global_state\\n858:             ),\\n859:         )\\n860: \\n861:         global_state[\\\"grad_enabled\\\"] = (torch.set_grad_enabled, torch.is_grad_enabled())\\n862: \\n863:         global_state[\\\"autocast_enabled\\\"] = (\\n864:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \\\"cuda\\\"),\\n865:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\\\"cuda\\\"),\\n866:         )\\n867:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cpu_enabled\\\"] = (\\n868:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \\\"cpu\\\"),\\n869:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\\\"cpu\\\"),\\n870:         )\\n871:         global_state[\\\"autocast_gpu_dtype\\\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\\n872:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \\\"cuda\\\"),\\n873:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\\\"cuda\\\"),\\n874:         )\\n875:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cpu_dtype\\\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\\n876:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \\\"cpu\\\"),\\n877:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\\\"cpu\\\"),\\n878:         )\\n879:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cache_enabled\\\"] = (\\n880:             torch.set_autocast_cache_enabled,\\n881:             torch.is_autocast_cache_enabled(),\\n882:         )\\n883: \\n884:     def push_tx(self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\") -> None:\\n885:         self._current_tx.append(tx)\\n886: \\n887:     def pop_tx(self) -> \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\":\\n888:         return self._current_tx.pop()\\n889: \\n890:     @property\\n891:     def current_tx(self) -> \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\":\\n892:         return self.root_tx if not self._current_tx else self._current_tx[-1]\\n893: \\n894:     def count_calls(self) -> int:\\n895:         return count_calls(self.graph)\\n896: \\n897:     def is_empty_graph(self) -> bool:\\n898:         return len(list(self.graph.nodes)) == 0\\n899: \\n900:     def get_submodule(self, keys: str) -> Union[torch.nn.Module, Any]:\\n901:         assert keys\\n902:         obj: Union[torch.nn.Module, dict[str, torch.nn.Module]] = self.nn_modules\\n903:         for k in keys.split(\\\".\\\"):\\n904:             if isinstance(obj, dict):\\n905:                 obj = obj[k]\\n906:             else:\\n907:                 obj = getattr(obj, k)\\n908:         return obj\\n909: \\n910:     def new_var(self, name: str = \\\"tmp\\\") -> str:\\n911:         existing = set(self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"])\\n912:         # In common case, this will be O(1)\\n913:         while True:\\n914:             var = f\\\"{name}_{next(self.unique_var_id)}\\\"\\n915:             if var not in existing:\\n916:                 self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] += (var,)\\n917:                 return var\\n918: \\n919:     def update_co_names(self, name: str) -> None:\\n920:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Ensure self.code_options.co_names contains name\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n921:         if name not in self.code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"]:\\n922:             self.code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"] += (name,)\\n923: \\n924:     @staticmethod\\n925:     def module_key_name(*names: Any) -> str:\\n926:         # create a new unique name\\n927:         name = \\\"_\\\".join(map(str, names))\\n928:         # Strip the guard lookup L/G access\\n929:         name = re.sub(r\\\"^[GL]\\\\['?(.*?)'?\\\\]$\\\", r\\\"\\\\1\\\", name)\\n930:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz[123].qkv with abc.xyz_123.qkv\\n931:         name = re.sub(r\\\"\\\\[(\\\\d+)\\\\]\\\", r\\\"_\\\\g<1>\\\", name)\\n932:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz_123.qkv with abc_xyz_123_qkv\\n933:         name = re.sub(r\\\"[^a-zA-Z0-9]\\\", \\\"_\\\", name)\\n934: \\n935:         if not name or not name[0].isalpha():\\n936:             name = \\\"sub\\\" + name\\n937: \\n938:         return name\\n939: \\n940:     def register_static_attr_and_return_proxy(\\n941:         self, attr_prefix: str, attr_value: Any\\n942:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n943:         attr_name = get_unique_name_wrt(attr_prefix, self.nn_modules)\\n944:         # TODO `nn_modules` has been historically overloaded to store a lot more\\n945:         # than just nn module objects, fix that.\\n946:         self.nn_modules[attr_name] = attr_value\\n947:         proxy = self.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", attr_name, (), {})\\n948:         set_example_value(proxy.node, attr_value)\\n949:         return proxy\\n950: \\n951:     def register_attr_or_module(\\n952:         self,\\n953:         target: Union[torch.nn.Module, torch.Tensor, Any],\\n954:         *names: Any,\\n955:         **options: Any,\\n956:     ) -> VariableTracker:\\n957:         if is_dynamic_nn_module(target, self.export):\\n958:             # Instead of returning UnspecializedNNModuleVariable, call\\n959:             # VariableTracker.build so that it is tracked for mutation.\\n960:             return VariableTracker.build(self.current_tx, target, **options)\\n961: \\n962:         options = dict(options)\\n963:         assert \\\"source\\\" in options\\n964:         source = options[\\\"source\\\"]\\n965:         assert not isinstance(source, ParamBufferSource)\\n966: \\n967:         if isinstance(target, torch.Tensor):\\n968:             tracer = self.current_tracer\\n969:             if not self.is_root_tracer():\\n970:                 # For higher order ops, we don't want to insert the get_attr in\\n971:                 # innermost graph. Instead, we want to raise the params/buffers\\n972:                 # as inputs to the higher-order graph, and register them as\\n973:                 # get_attrs in the root tracer.\\n974: \\n975:                 # Note that Dynamo will still call lift_tracked_freevar_to_input\\n976:                 # when these inputs are encountered for the inner graph. The\\n977:                 # only difference is what happens at the root tracer for\\n978:                 # nn.Parameters vs free inputs. The free inputs are registered\\n979:                 # as placeholders in the root graph, whereas the nn.Parameters\\n980:                 # are registered as get_attr nodes in the root graph.\\n981:                 tracer = self.root_tracer\\n982: \\n983:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n984:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\\n985:                 self.param_name_to_source[module_key] = source\\n986: \\n987:                 # Check if the attr has already been registered. This can happen\\n988:                 # when two different sources point to the same tensor.\\n989:                 assert self.root_tx is not None\\n990:                 if target in self.root_tx.output.side_effects:\\n991:                     return self.root_tx.output.side_effects[target]\\n992: \\n993:                 if get_static_address_type(target) == \\\"guarded\\\" and not isinstance(\\n994:                     source, NumpyTensorSource\\n995:                 ):\\n996:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.ID_MATCH))\\n997:                 elif not is_constant_source(source):\\n998:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.TENSOR_MATCH))\\n999: \\n1000:                 vt = wrap_fx_proxy(\\n1001: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL700-1000\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "700:             current_device=self.current_device,\n701:             global_state_guard=self.global_state_guard,\n702:             name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals=self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals,\n703:             export=self.export,\n704:             export_constraints=self.export_constraints,\n705:             _guards=self.guards,\n706:             _aotautograd_guards=self.aotautograd_guards,\n707:         )\n708: \n709:     def synthetic_graph_input(\n710:         self, fn: Callable[..., Any], args: tuple[Any, ...]\n711:     ) -> VariableTracker:\n712:         \"\"\"\n713:         call fn(*args) before the graph runs and turn the result into a fake input.\n714:         \"\"\"\n715:         example_value = fn(*args)\n716:         varname = self.new_var()\n717:         cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\n718:         cg.add_push_null(\n719:             lambda: cg.load_import_from(\n720:                 fn.__module__,\n721:                 fn.__name__,\n722:             )\n723:         )\n724:         cg.foreach(map(variables.ConstantVariable.create, args))\n725:         cg.call_function(len(args), False)\n726:         cg.store(varname)\n727:         self.pregraph_bytecode.extend(cg.get_instructions())\n728:         source = SyntheticLocalSource(varname)\n729:         result = VariableTracker.build(self.root_tx, example_value, source)\n730:         # Realize the VT because we will delete the guards on it in the next line.\n731:         result = result.realize()\n732:         TracingContext.get().guards_context.dynamo_guards.remove_guards_with_source(\n733:             source\n734:         )\n735:         return result\n736: \n737:     def add_cleanup_hook(self, fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> None:\n738:         self.cleanup_hooks.append(fn)\n739: \n740:     def call_cleanup_hooks(self) -> None:\n741:         for hook in reversed(self.cleanup_hooks):\n742:             hook()\n743:         self.cleanup_hooks.clear()\n744: \n745:     @property\n746:     def root_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n747:         return self.tracers[0]\n748: \n749:     @property\n750:     def current_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n751:         return self.tracers[-1]\n752: \n753:     def is_root_tracer(self) -> bool:\n754:         # Helper to tell if we are inside the higher order operator tracing.\n755:         return len(self.tracers) == 1\n756: \n757:     @property\n758:     def graph(self) -> torch.fx.Graph:\n759:         return self.current_tracer.graph\n760: \n761:     # TODO(rzou): can delete after we refactor speculate_subgraph to use nested GraphTracer.\n762:     @graph.setter\n763:     def graph(self, value: torch.fx.Graph) -> None:\n764:         self.current_tracer.graph = value\n765: \n766:     @property\n767:     def input_name_to_proxy(self) -> dict[str, fx.Proxy]:\n768:         return self.current_tracer.input_name_to_proxy\n769: \n770:     @property\n771:     def real_value_cache(self) -> dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor]:\n772:         return self.current_tracer.real_value_cache\n773: \n774:     @property\n775:     def bound_symbols(self) -> dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, \"LazyProxy\"]]:\n776:         return self.current_tracer.bound_symbols\n777: \n778:     # If you are here, and you're looking for create_graph_input,\n779:     # to avoid ambiguity, please call one of the following:\n780:     # - self.current_tracer.create_graph_input\n781:     # - self.root_tracer.create_graph_input\n782:     # See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] for more context.\n783: \n784:     def create_proxy(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n785:         return self.current_tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\n786: \n787:     def create_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Node:\n788:         return self.current_tracer.create_node(*args, **kwargs)\n789: \n790:     def remove_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\n791:         return self.current_tracer.remove_node(*args, **kwargs)\n792: \n793:     @contextlib.contextmanager\n794:     def subtracer(\n795:         self, source_target: Optional[Target], prior_tracer: \"SubgraphTracer\"\n796:     ) -> Generator[fx.Tracer, None, None]:\n797:         new_scope_ctx = enter_new_scope()\n798:         try:\n799:             if prior_tracer:\n800:                 # Lineage MUST stay preserved\n801:                 assert prior_tracer.parent is self.current_tracer\n802:             new_scope_ctx.__enter__()\n803:             tracer = (\n804:                 prior_tracer\n805:                 if prior_tracer\n806:                 else SubgraphTracer(\n807:                     self,\n808:                     parent=self.current_tracer,\n809:                     source_target=source_target,\n810:                     is_export=self.current_tracer.is_export,\n811:                 )\n812:             )\n813:             self.tracers.append(tracer)\n814:             yield tracer\n815:         finally:\n816:             new_scope_ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n817:             self.tracers.pop()\n818: \n819:     @property\n820:     def output(self) -> \"OutputGraph\":\n821:         return self\n822: \n823:     @property\n824:     def fake_mode(self) -> torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode:\n825:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n826:         return self.tracing_context.fake_mode\n827: \n828:     @property\n829:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n830:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n831:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\n832:         return self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env\n833: \n834:     @property\n835:     def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n836:         return self.tracing_context.guards_context.dynamo_guards\n837: \n838:     @property\n839:     def nn_modules(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n840:         return self.tracing_context.module_context.nn_modules\n841: \n842:     @property\n843:     def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n844:         return self.tracing_context.guards_context.aotautograd_guards\n845: \n846:     def save_global_state(\n847:         self, out: Optional[dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]]] = None\n848:     ) -> None:\n849:         \"\"\"\n850:         Saves to out if it is provided. Else saves to the tracing context's global_state.\n851:         \"\"\"\n852:         global_state = cast(\n853:             dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]],\n854:             (\n855:                 out\n856:                 if out is not None\n857:                 else self.tracing_context.global_context.global_state\n858:             ),\n859:         )\n860: \n861:         global_state[\"grad_enabled\"] = (torch.set_grad_enabled, torch.is_grad_enabled())\n862: \n863:         global_state[\"autocast_enabled\"] = (\n864:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \"cuda\"),\n865:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\"cuda\"),\n866:         )\n867:         global_state[\"autocast_cpu_enabled\"] = (\n868:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \"cpu\"),\n869:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\"cpu\"),\n870:         )\n871:         global_state[\"autocast_gpu_dtype\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\n872:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \"cuda\"),\n873:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\"cuda\"),\n874:         )\n875:         global_state[\"autocast_cpu_dtype\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\n876:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \"cpu\"),\n877:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\"cpu\"),\n878:         )\n879:         global_state[\"autocast_cache_enabled\"] = (\n880:             torch.set_autocast_cache_enabled,\n881:             torch.is_autocast_cache_enabled(),\n882:         )\n883: \n884:     def push_tx(self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\") -> None:\n885:         self._current_tx.append(tx)\n886: \n887:     def pop_tx(self) -> \"InstructionTranslatorBase\":\n888:         return self._current_tx.pop()\n889: \n890:     @property\n891:     def current_tx(self) -> \"InstructionTranslatorBase\":\n892:         return self.root_tx if not self._current_tx else self._current_tx[-1]\n893: \n894:     def count_calls(self) -> int:\n895:         return count_calls(self.graph)\n896: \n897:     def is_empty_graph(self) -> bool:\n898:         return len(list(self.graph.nodes)) == 0\n899: \n900:     def get_submodule(self, keys: str) -> Union[torch.nn.Module, Any]:\n901:         assert keys\n902:         obj: Union[torch.nn.Module, dict[str, torch.nn.Module]] = self.nn_modules\n903:         for k in keys.split(\".\"):\n904:             if isinstance(obj, dict):\n905:                 obj = obj[k]\n906:             else:\n907:                 obj = getattr(obj, k)\n908:         return obj\n909: \n910:     def new_var(self, name: str = \"tmp\") -> str:\n911:         existing = set(self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"])\n912:         # In common case, this will be O(1)\n913:         while True:\n914:             var = f\"{name}_{next(self.unique_var_id)}\"\n915:             if var not in existing:\n916:                 self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"] += (var,)\n917:                 return var\n918: \n919:     def update_co_names(self, name: str) -> None:\n920:         \"\"\"Ensure self.code_options.co_names contains name\"\"\"\n921:         if name not in self.code_options[\"co_names\"]:\n922:             self.code_options[\"co_names\"] += (name,)\n923: \n924:     @staticmethod\n925:     def module_key_name(*names: Any) -> str:\n926:         # create a new unique name\n927:         name = \"_\".join(map(str, names))\n928:         # Strip the guard lookup L/G access\n929:         name = re.sub(r\"^[GL]\\['?(.*?)'?\\]$\", r\"\\1\", name)\n930:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz[123].qkv with abc.xyz_123.qkv\n931:         name = re.sub(r\"\\[(\\d+)\\]\", r\"_\\g<1>\", name)\n932:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz_123.qkv with abc_xyz_123_qkv\n933:         name = re.sub(r\"[^a-zA-Z0-9]\", \"_\", name)\n934: \n935:         if not name or not name[0].isalpha():\n936:             name = \"sub\" + name\n937: \n938:         return name\n939: \n940:     def register_static_attr_and_return_proxy(\n941:         self, attr_prefix: str, attr_value: Any\n942:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\n943:         attr_name = get_unique_name_wrt(attr_prefix, self.nn_modules)\n944:         # TODO `nn_modules` has been historically overloaded to store a lot more\n945:         # than just nn module objects, fix that.\n946:         self.nn_modules[attr_name] = attr_value\n947:         proxy = self.create_proxy(\"get_attr\", attr_name, (), {})\n948:         set_example_value(proxy.node, attr_value)\n949:         return proxy\n950: \n951:     def register_attr_or_module(\n952:         self,\n953:         target: Union[torch.nn.Module, torch.Tensor, Any],\n954:         *names: Any,\n955:         **options: Any,\n956:     ) -> VariableTracker:\n957:         if is_dynamic_nn_module(target, self.export):\n958:             # Instead of returning UnspecializedNNModuleVariable, call\n959:             # VariableTracker.build so that it is tracked for mutation.\n960:             return VariableTracker.build(self.current_tx, target, **options)\n961: \n962:         options = dict(options)\n963:         assert \"source\" in options\n964:         source = options[\"source\"]\n965:         assert not isinstance(source, ParamBufferSource)\n966: \n967:         if isinstance(target, torch.Tensor):\n968:             tracer = self.current_tracer\n969:             if not self.is_root_tracer():\n970:                 # For higher order ops, we don't want to insert the get_attr in\n971:                 # innermost graph. Instead, we want to raise the params/buffers\n972:                 # as inputs to the higher-order graph, and register them as\n973:                 # get_attrs in the root tracer.\n974: \n975:                 # Note that Dynamo will still call lift_tracked_freevar_to_input\n976:                 # when these inputs are encountered for the inner graph. The\n977:                 # only difference is what happens at the root tracer for\n978:                 # nn.Parameters vs free inputs. The free inputs are registered\n979:                 # as placeholders in the root graph, whereas the nn.Parameters\n980:                 # are registered as get_attr nodes in the root graph.\n981:                 tracer = self.root_tracer\n982: \n983:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n984:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\n985:                 self.param_name_to_source[module_key] = source\n986: \n987:                 # Check if the attr has already been registered. This can happen\n988:                 # when two different sources point to the same tensor.\n989:                 assert self.root_tx is not None\n990:                 if target in self.root_tx.output.side_effects:\n991:                     return self.root_tx.output.side_effects[target]\n992: \n993:                 if get_static_address_type(target) == \"guarded\" and not isinstance(\n994:                     source, NumpyTensorSource\n995:                 ):\n996:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.ID_MATCH))\n997:                 elif not is_constant_source(source):\n998:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.TENSOR_MATCH))\n999: \n1000:                 vt = wrap_fx_proxy(\n1001: ",

OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name())\\n1079:                     ] = leaf_name\\n1080: \\n1081:             # annoying, but there are cases when we do not have parameters\\n1082:             # see test_nn_moduledict_contains\\n1083:             if hasattr(target, \\\"_parameters\\\"):\\n1084:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_parameters():\\n1085: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\"\\n1094:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], dict[Source, Source]]:\\n1095:         # If list inputs are stolen, but still needed after the function call, create aliases to keep them alive\\n1096:         maybe_gm = self.local_scope.get(\\\"self\\\")\\n1097:         stolen_list_names = get_locals_to_steal(maybe_gm)\\n1098:         if not stolen_list_names:\\n1099: x.source.base.local_name\\n1129:             if stolen_name not in needs_alias:\\n1130:                 needs_alias[stolen_name] = []\\n1131:             needs_alias[stolen_name].append(x)\\n1132: self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"]\\n1146:             for x in needs_alias[list_name]:\\n1147:                 # Skip if already handled.\\n1148:                 if x.source in overridden_sources:\\n1149:                     continue\\n1150: \\n1151:                 # A small codegen optimization because we might have different\\n1152:                 # VariableTrackers that share the same source.\\n1153:                 list_idx = x.source.index  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n1154:                 if list_idx not in visited:\\n1155:                     alias_name = self.new_var(\\n1156:                         f\\\"{list_name}_ref\\\"\\n1157:                     )  # self.new_var already adds unique id suffix\\n1158: \\n1159:                     visited[list_idx] = alias_name\\n1160:                     # bytecode of `alias_name = list_name[list_idx]`\\n1161:                     alias_insts.extend(\\n1162:                         [\\n1163:                             create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=list_name),\\n1164:                             create_load_const(list_idx),\\n1165:                             create_instruction(\\\"BINARY_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n1166:                             create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=alias_name),\\n1167:                         ]\\n1168: we want to avoid\\n1176:         # updating `source` in place because that might break invariants in\\n1177:         # other parts of Dynamo like guards.\\n1178:         return alias_insts, overridden_sources\\n1179: \\n1180:     def _get_stack_values_to_restore(\\n1181:         self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\", stack_pops: int\\n1182:     ) -> tuple[list[VariableTracker], list[str], StackLocalsMetadata]:\\n1183:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1184:         Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\\n1185: \\n1186:         Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.\\n1187: \\n1188:         NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`\\n1189:         elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\\n1190:         `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\\n1191: \\n1192:         Returns:\\n1193:             - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\\n1194:             - restore_vars: names of locals corresponding to the locals part of `stack_values`\\n1195:             - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\\n1196:                 (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\\n1197:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1198:         tx.prune_dead_locals()\\n1199: \\n1200:         stack_values = []\\n1201:         meta = StackLocalsMetadata()\\n1202: \\n1203:         # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\\n1204:         # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\\n1205:         for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\\n1206:             variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(value)\\n1207:             # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\\n1208:             if len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops:\\n1209:                 stack_values.append(value)\\n1210:                 continue\\n1211:             if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\\n1212:                 meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\\n1213:             else:\\n1214:                 stack_values.append(value)\\n1215:             if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):\\n1216:                 target_values = (\\n1217:                     () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)\\n1218:                 )\\n1219:                 # NOTE: track index in stack after NULLs have been removed\\n1220:                 meta.stack_ctx_args.append((len(stack_values) - 1, target_values))\\n1221:                 meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)\\n1222: \\n1223:         # Add all the local vars to the \\\"stack\\\" so restore at the end\\n1224:         restore_vars: list[str] = []\\n1225:         val_to_names: dict[VariableTracker, list[str]] = {}\\n1226:         # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\\n1227:         # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\\n1228:         # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\\n1229:         # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\\n1230:         # is only needed for partial graphs.\\n1231:         # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\\n1232:         # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\\n1233:         # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\\n1234:         for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\\n1235:             # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\\n1236:             # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.\\n1237:             # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.\\n1238:             # This was very tricky to debug. For an example, dump the graph at call_user_compiler\\n1239:             # while running test_subgraphs.py\\n1240:             if isinstance(v.source, LocalSource) and v.source.local_name == k:\\n1241:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state\\n1242:             if isinstance(v, CellVariable) and v.local_name == k:\\n1243:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state\\n1244:             # Do not load variable if it is NULL.\\n1245:             if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\\n1246:                 # Continuation function will load the NULL for v.\\n1247:                 if type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v):\\n1248:                     meta.locals_null_keys.append(k)\\n1249:                     continue\\n1250:             else:\\n1251:                 # A variable should never be NULL in < 3.12\\n1252:                 assert not type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v)\\n1253:             if isinstance(v, ContextWrappingVariable):\\n1254:                 target_values = (\\n1255:                     () if v.target_values is None else tuple(v.target_values)\\n1256:                 )\\n1257:                 meta.locals_ctx_args.append((k, target_values))\\n1258:             if v not in val_to_names:\\n1259:                 val_to_names[v] = []\\n1260:             val_to_names[v].append(k)\\n1261:         for v in val_to_names.keys():\\n1262:             restore_vars.extend(val_to_names[v])\\n1263:             stack_values.extend([v] * len(val_to_names[v]))\\n1264: \\n1265:         return stack_values, restore_vars, meta\\n1266: \\n1267:     def compile_subgraph(\\n1268:         self,\\n1269:         tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n1270:         reason: GraphCompileReason,\\n1271:         partial_convert: bool = False,\\n1272:         stack_pops: int = 0,\\n1273:     ) -> list[StackLocalsMetadata]:\\n1274:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1275:         Compiles the current subgraph, with inputs w.r.t. self.root_tx, and codegens:\\n1276:             - Call the compiled subgraph\\n1277:             - Apply side effects\\n1278:             - Codegen stack and locals\\n1279:             - Store the locals\\n1280: \\n1281:         Python does not allow NULL to be an arg to a function, so we do not codegen NULLs on the stack,\\n1282:         unless the value is one of the top `stack_pops` values on the stack (these values are expected to be\\n1283:         popped immediately after this generated code. The prologue of the resume function is expected to restore\\n1284:         any dropped NULLs.\\n1285: \\n1286:         Returns stack indices and locals keys where we dropped NULLs, and where we found inactive context manager objects.\\n1287:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1288: \\n1289:         assert self.root_tx is not None\\n1290: \\n1291:         # FIXME temporary assert to make sure we're not accidentally compiling nested graph breaks\\n1292:         # before we're done the full implementation\\n1293:         assert self.root_tx is tx\\n1294: \\n1295:         # bytecode tracing has finished. Pop the context manager for dynamo_timed\\n1296:         self.mark_bytecode_tracing_stop()\\n1297: \\n1298:         self.partial_convert = partial_convert\\n1299:         self.compile_subgraph_reason = reason\\n1300:         self.should_exit = True\\n1301: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1000-1300\"}",

NNModuleVariable tracker can't be\n1028:                 # sourceless, so let's return a unspecializedNNModule variable\n1029:                 # tracker.\n1030:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n1031:                     return variables.UnspecializedNNModuleVariable(target, **options)\n1032: \n1033:         elif isinstance(target, (torch.SymInt, torch.SymFloat)):\n1034:             # HACKY CODE REGION BEGIN\n1035:             # WE ARE PIGGYBACKING ON EXISTING INFRA TO REGISTER ATTRS\n1036:             # This ultimately gets written to self.nn_modules, which is unfortunate\n1037:             # Attrs that are tenors and symints and such need to be migrated to have their\n1038:             # own storage\n1039:             # alas, this is like this for now\n1040: \n1041:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n1042:                 return SymNodeVariable.create(\n1043:                     self,\n1044:                     self.create_proxy(\"get_attr\", module_key, (), {}),\n1045:                     sym_num=target,\n1046:                     **options,\n1047:                 )\n1048: \n1049:             # HACKY CODE REGION END\n1050:         else:\n1051: \n1052:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n1053:                 self.output.update_co_names(module_key)\n1054:                 self.global_scope[module_key] = target\n1055:                 return VariableTracker.build(\n1056:                     self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n1057:                     target,\n1058:                     ConstantSource(source_name=module_key),\n1059:                 )\n1060: \n1061:         for k, v in self.nn_modules.items():\n1062:             if v is target:\n1063:                 # it already exists\n1064:                 return wrap_name(k)\n1065: \n1066:         name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(*names)\n1067:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, self.global_scope)\n1068:         self.nn_modules[name] = target\n1069:         if isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\n1070: \n1071:             def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:\n1072:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\n1073:                 new_source = ParamBufferSource(source, leaf_name)\n1074:                 new_name = f\"{name}.{leaf_name}\"\n1075:                 self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source\n1076:                 if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\n1077:                     self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[\n1078:                         OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name())\n1079:                     ] = leaf_name\n1080: \n1081:             # annoying, but there are cases when we do not have parameters\n1082:             # see test_nn_moduledict_contains\n1083:             if hasattr(target, \"_parameters\"):\n1084:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_parameters():\n1085:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\n1086:             if hasattr(target, \"_buffers\"):\n1087:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_buffers():\n1088:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\n1089: \n1090:         return wrap_name(name)\n1091: \n1092:     def handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(\n1093:         self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\"\n1094:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], dict[Source, Source]]:\n1095:         # If list inputs are stolen, but still needed after the function call, create aliases to keep them alive\n1096:         maybe_gm = self.local_scope.get(\"self\")\n1097:         stolen_list_names = get_locals_to_steal(maybe_gm)\n1098:         if not stolen_list_names:\n1099:             return [], {}\n1100: \n1101:         alias_insts = []\n1102:         needs_alias: dict[str, list[VariableTracker]] = {}\n1103: \n1104:         queue = [\n1105:             *tx.stack,\n1106:             *tx.symbolic_locals.values(),\n1107:             *self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations.keys(),\n1108:         ]\n1109: \n1110:         while queue:\n1111:             x = queue.pop()\n1112:             if isinstance(x, BaseListVariable):\n1113: isinstance(x.source, GetItemSource)\n1123:                 and isinstance(x.source.base, LocalSource)\n1124:                 and x.source.base.local_name in stolen_list_names\n1125:             ):\n1126:                 continue\n1127: \n1128:             stolen_name = x.source.base.local_name\n1129:             if stolen_name not in needs_alias:\n1130:                 needs_alias[stolen_name] = []\n1131:             needs_alias[stolen_name].append(x)\n1132: \n1133:         visited = {}\n1134:         overridden_sources: dict[Source, Source] = {}\n1135:         for arg in self.graphargs:\n1136:             if not (\n1137:                 isinstance(arg._example, list)\n1138:                 and isinstance(arg.source, LocalSource)\n1139:                 and arg.source.local_name in needs_alias\n1140:             ):\n1141:                 continue\n1142: \n1143:             # arg is a list that will be cleared by the compiled function\n1144:             list_name = arg.source.local_name\n1145:             assert list_name in self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"]\n1146:             for x in needs_alias[list_name]:\n1147:                 # Skip if already handled.\n1148:                 if x.source in overridden_sources:\n1149:                     continue\n1150: \n1151:                 # A small codegen optimization because we might have different\n1152:                 # VariableTrackers that share the same source.\n1153:                 list_idx = x.source.index  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n1154:                 if list_idx not in visited:\n1155:                     alias_name = self.new_var(\n1156:                         f\"{list_name}_ref\"\n1157:                     )  # self.new_var already adds unique id suffix\n1158: \n1159:                     visited[list_idx] = alias_name\n1160:                     # bytecode of `alias_name = list_name[list_idx]`\n1161:                     alias_insts.extend(\n1162: want to avoid\n1176:         # updating `source` in place because that might break invariants in\n1177:         # other parts of Dynamo like guards.\n1178:         return alias_insts, overridden_sources\n1179: \n1180:     def _get_stack_values_to_restore(\n1181:         self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\", stack_pops: int\n1182:     ) -> tuple[list[VariableTracker], list[str], StackLocalsMetadata]:\n1183:         \"\"\"\n1184:         Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\n1185: \n1186:         Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.\n1187: \n1188:         NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`\n1189:         elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\n1190:         `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\n1191: \n1192:         Returns:\n1193:             - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\n1194:             - restore_vars: names of locals corresponding to the locals part of `stack_values`\n1195:             - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\n1196:                 (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\n1197:         \"\"\"\n1198:         tx.prune_dead_locals()\n1199: \n1200: attributes\n1205:         for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\n1206:             variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(value)\n1207:             # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\n1208:             if len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops:\n1209:                 stack_values.append(value)\n1210:                 continue\n1211:             if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\n1212:                 meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\n1213:             else:\n1214:                 stack_values.append(value)\n1215:             if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):\n1216:                 target_values = (\n1217:                     () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)\n1218:                 )\n1219: meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)\n1222: \n1223:         # Add all the local vars to the \"stack\" so restore at the end\n1224:         restore_vars: list[str] = []\n1225:         val_to_names: dict[VariableTracker, list[str]] = {}\n1226:         # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\n1227:         # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\n1228:         # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\n1229:         # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\n1230:         # is only needed for partial graphs.\n1231:         # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\n1232:         # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\n1233:         # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\n1234:         for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\n1235:             # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\n1236:             # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.\n1237:             # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.\n1238:             # This was very tricky to debug. For an example, dump the graph at call_user_compiler\n1239:             # while running test_subgraphs.py\n1240:             if isinstance(v.source, LocalSource) and v.source.local_name == k:\n1241:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state\n1242:             if isinstance(v, CellVariable) and v.local_name == k:\n1243:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state\n1244:             # Do not load variable if it is NULL.\n1245:             if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\n1246:                 # Continuation function will load the NULL for v.\n1247:                 if type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v):\n1248:                     meta.locals_null_keys.append(k)\n1249:                     continue\n1250:             else:\n1251:                 # A variable should never be NULL in < 3.12\n1252:                 assert not type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v)\n1253:             if isinstance(v, ContextWrappingVariable):\n1254:                 target_values = (\n1255:                     () if v.target_values is None else tuple(v.target_values)\n1256:                 )\n1257:                 meta.locals_ctx_args.append((k, target_values))\n1258:             if v not in val_to_names:\n1259:                 val_to_names[v] = []\n1260:             val_to_names[v].append(k)\n1261:         for v in val_to_names.keys():\n1262:             restore_vars.extend(val_to_names[v])\n1263:             stack_values.extend([v] * len(val_to_names[v]))\n1264: \n1265:         return stack_values, restore_vars, meta\n1266: \n1267:     def compile_subgraph(\n1268:         self,\n1269:         tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n1270:         reason: GraphCompileReason,\n1271:         partial_convert: bool = False,\n1272:         stack_pops: int = 0,\n1273:     ) -> list[StackLocalsMetadata]:\n1274:         \"\"\"\n1275:         Compiles the current subgraph, with inputs w.r.t. self.root_tx, and codegens:\n1276:             - Call the compiled subgraph\n1277:             - Apply side effects\n1278:             - Codegen stack and locals\n1279:             - Store the locals\n1280: \n1281:         Python does not allow NULL to be an arg to a function, so we do not codegen NULLs on the stack,\n1282:         unless the value is one of the top `stack_pops` values on the stack (these values are expected to be\n1283:         popped immediately after this generated code. The prologue of the resume function is expected to restore\n1284:         any dropped NULLs.\n1285: \n1286:         Returns stack indices and locals keys where we dropped NULLs, and where we found inactive context manager objects.\n1287:         \"\"\"\n1288: \n1289:         assert self.root_tx is not None\n1290: \n1291:         # FIXME temporary assert to make sure we're not accidentally compiling nested graph breaks\n1292:         # before we're done the full implementation\n1293:         assert self.root_tx is tx\n1294: \n1295:         # bytecode tracing has finished. Pop the context manager for dynamo_timed\n1296:         self.mark_bytecode_tracing_stop()\n1297: \n1298:         self.partial_convert = partial_convert\n1299:         self.compile_subgraph_reason = reason\n1300:         self.should_exit = True\n1301: ",
