  "content": "{\"content\":\"1900:         assert self.compiler_fn is not None\\n1901:         tot = 0\\n1902:         placeholders = []\\n1903:         for node in gm.graph.nodes:\\n1904:             if node.op in (\\\"call_function\\\", \\\"call_method\\\", \\\"call_module\\\"):\\n1905:                 tot += 1\\n1906:             if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n1907:                 placeholders.append(node)\\n1908:         increment_op_count(tot)\\n1909:         for pl in placeholders:\\n1910:             if not hasattr(pl, \\\"_dynamo_source\\\"):\\n1911:                 arg = pl.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n1912:                 # TODO: Why isn't this stored in meta :think:\\n1913:                 # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\\n1914:                 pl._dynamo_source = arg.source\\n1915: \\n1916:         # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\\n1917:         gm._param_name_to_source = self.param_name_to_source  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1918:         gm._source_to_user_stacks = self.source_to_user_stacks  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1919: \\n1920:         name = (\\n1921:             self.compiler_fn.__name__\\n1922:             if hasattr(self.compiler_fn, \\\"__name__\\\")\\n1923:             else \\\"<unknown compiler_fn>\\\"\\n1924:         )\\n1925:         try:\\n1926:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\\\"calling compiler function {name}\\\")\\n1927:             compiler_fn = self.compiler_fn\\n1928:             if config.verify_correctness:\\n1929:                 compiler_fn = WrapperBackend(compiler_fn)\\n1930:             compiled_fn = compiler_fn(gm, example_inputs)\\n1931:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\\\"done compiler function {name}\\\")\\n1932:             assert callable(compiled_fn), \\\"compiler_fn did not return callable\\\"\\n1933:         except (TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis, ShortenTraceback):\\n1934:             raise\\n1935:         except exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback as e:\\n1936:             if self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph:\\n1937:                 raise BackendCompilerFailed(\\n1938:                     self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\\n1939:                 ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\\n1940:             unimplemented_v2_with_warning(\\n1941:                 e,\\n1942:                 self.root_tx.f_code,\\n1943:                 gb_type=\\\"Backend compiler exception\\\",\\n1944:                 context=f\\\"Backend: {name}\\\\nException:{str(e)}\\\\nTraceback:\\\\n{self.root_tx.format_frame_summary()}\\\",\\n1945:                 explanation=f\\\"Backend compiler `{name}` failed with {str(e)}. Adding a graph break.\\\",\\n1946:                 hints=[\\n1947:                     \\\"Report an issue to the backend compiler repo.\\\",\\n1948:                 ],\\n1949:             )\\n1950:         except SkipFrame as e:\\n1951:             # The backend compiler has requested that we skip the frame, instead of\\n1952:             # aborting execution.\\n1953:             raise e\\n1954:         except Exception as e:\\n1955:             raise BackendCompilerFailed(\\n1956:                 self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\\n1957:             ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\\n1958: \\n1959:         signpost_event(\\n1960:             \\\"dynamo\\\",\\n1961:             \\\"OutputGraph.call_user_compiler\\\",\\n1962:             {\\n1963:                 **self.co_fields,\\n1964:                 \\\"op_count\\\": tot,\\n1965:                 \\\"node_count\\\": len(gm.graph.nodes),\\n1966:                 \\\"input_count\\\": len(placeholders),\\n1967:             },\\n1968:         )\\n1969: \\n1970:         return compiled_fn\\n1971: \\n1972:     def dedup_pass(self) -> dict[str, torch.fx.GraphModule]:\\n1973:         if torch._dynamo.config.use_graph_deduplication:\\n1974:             return apply_graph_deduplication(self)\\n1975:         else:\\n1976:             return {}\\n1977: \\n1978:     def install_subgraph(self, name: str, sub_gm: torch.fx.GraphModule) -> str:\\n1979:         next_name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, requires_suffix=True)\\n1980:         sub_gm.__name__ = next_name  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1981:         sub_gm.torchdynamo_force_dynamic = False  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1982:         # This graph module is not present in the user space, so it can't be\\n1983:         # accessed by a source. Set source=None.\\n1984:         self.register_attr_or_module(sub_gm, next_name, source=None)\\n1985:         return next_name\\n1986: \\n1987:     def example_inputs(self) -> list[torch.Tensor]:\\n1988:         result = [arg.example for arg in self.graphargs]\\n1989:         return result\\n1990: \\n1991:     def remove_unused_get_attr_nodes(self) -> None:\\n1992:         for node in sorted(self.graph.find_nodes(op=\\\"get_attr\\\"), reverse=True):\\n1993:             if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\\n1994:                 self.remove_node(node)\\n1995: \\n1996:     def remove_unused_graphargs(self) -> None:\\n1997:         # NB: It's OK to drop GraphArg for symbols that ended up being\\n1998:         # specialized iff they are not used in runtime assertions.  You don't\\n1999:         # even have to make a guard for it, because ShapeEnv produce_guards\\n2000:         # operates on tracked_fakes, which never gets pruned.\\n2001:         # That being said, you'll get marginally better generated\\n2002:         # guard code if you promote the guard into a Dynamo guard (since that\\n2003:         # allows for the guard to be done using C++ guards.)  If we get\\n2004:         # ShapeEnv guards to go into C++ guards, this will stop being a thing\\n2005:         # though!\\n2006: \\n2007:         assert self.should_exit\\n2008: \\n2009:         # Miniature DCE pass, but only for obviously trivial operations\\n2010:         def is_static_true(b_node: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\\n2011:             if b_node is True:\\n2012:                 return True\\n2013:             if not isinstance(b_node, fx.Node):\\n2014:                 return False\\n2015:             b = b_node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\")\\n2016:             if b is None:\\n2017:                 return False\\n2018:             if b is True:\\n2019:                 return True\\n2020:             if (\\n2021:                 isinstance(b, torch.SymBool)\\n2022:                 and (r := b.node.maybe_as_bool()) is not None\\n2023:             ):\\n2024:                 return r\\n2025:             # TODO: We can also technically remove all cases when the input\\n2026:             # doesn't have unbacked inputs, since it's all in the ShapeEnv\\n2027:             return False\\n2028: \\n2029:         def is_symnode_arg(a: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\\n2030:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\\n2031: \\n2032:             if isinstance(a, (int, float, bool)):\\n2033:                 return True\\n2034:             if isinstance(a, fx.Node):\\n2035:                 return isinstance(a.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\"), SymTypes)\\n2036:             return False\\n2037: \\n2038:         # NB: We assume that you cannot do mutations on int/float/bool,\\n2039:         # because they are immutable types, and therefore is always safe to\\n2040:         # DCE.\\n2041:         def is_symnode_compute_node(node: fx.Node) -> bool:\\n2042:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\\n2043: \\n2044:             if node.op != \\\"call_function\\\":\\n2045:                 return False\\n2046:             # TODO: I don't think it's possible to have a bare int/float here?\\n2047:             if not isinstance(node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\"), SymTypes):\\n2048:                 return False\\n2049:             # TODO: This will bail here if you ever end up with a more complicated\\n2050:             # computation function, like sum(list_of_ints), even though it\\n2051:             # should be DCE'able\\n2052:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.args):\\n2053:                 return False\\n2054:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.kwargs.values()):\\n2055:                 return False\\n2056:             return True\\n2057: \\n2058:         from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_accessor_node\\n2059: \\n2060:         for node in reversed(list(self.graph.nodes)):\\n2061:             if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\\n2062:                 if (\\n2063:                     node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2064:                     or (node.op == \\\"call_function\\\" and node.target is operator.getitem)\\n2065:                     or (\\n2066:                         node.op == \\\"call_function\\\"\\n2067:                         and node.target is torch._check\\n2068:                         and is_static_true(node.args[0])\\n2069:                     )\\n2070:                     or is_symnode_compute_node(node)\\n2071:                     or is_accessor_node(node)\\n2072:                 ):\\n2073:                     self.remove_node(node)\\n2074: \\n2075:         def placeholder_binds_symbol(node: fx.Node) -> Optional[sympy.Symbol]:\\n2076:             arg = node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2077:             example = arg.example\\n2078:             if isinstance(example, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(\\n2079:                 example.node.expr, sympy.Symbol\\n2080:             ):\\n2081:                 return example.node.expr\\n2082:             return None\\n2083: \\n2084:         def remove_unused(node: fx.Node) -> None:\\n2085:             log.debug(\\\"REMOVE UNUSED GRAPHARG %s\\\", node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].source.name())\\n2086:             # I'm not really sure why you need to delete these from the\\n2087:             # node since the node is going to get removed\\n2088:             del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2089:             self.remove_node(node)\\n2090:             self.real_value_cache.pop(node, None)\\n2091: \\n2092:         used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol] = set()\\n2093: \\n2094:         def update_used_symbols(\\n2095:             used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol], fake: Union[torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor]\\n2096:         ) -> None:\\n2097:             used_symbols |= free_symbols(fake)\\n2098: \\n2099:         recheck_placeholders = []\\n2100:         for node in self.placeholders:\\n2101:             binds_symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node) is not None\\n2102:             # Don't delete symbol bindings yet\\n2103:             if binds_symbol:\\n2104:                 if not node.users:\\n2105:                     recheck_placeholders.append(node)\\n2106:             else:\\n2107:                 if not node.users and not isinstance(\\n2108:                     node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"], BackwardStateGraphArg\\n2109:                 ):\\n2110:                     remove_unused(node)\\n2111:                 else:\\n2112:                     # Register the free symbols as uses\\n2113:                     arg = node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2114:                     if isinstance(arg, BackwardStateGraphArg):\\n2115:                         continue\\n2116:                     if isinstance(node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example, torch.ScriptObject):\\n2117:                         real_script_obj = node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2118:                         fake_script_obj = node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example_strong_ref\\n2119:                         if not torch._library.fake_class_registry.tracing_with_real(\\n2120:                             real_script_obj\\n2121:                         ):\\n2122:                             flat_dict = dict(real_script_obj.__obj_flatten__())  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n2123:                             for attr in flat_dict.keys():\\n2124:                                 fake_attr_val = getattr(\\n2125:                                     fake_script_obj.wrapped_obj, attr\\n2126:                                 )\\n2127:                                 pytree.tree_map_only(\\n2128:                                     (torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor),\\n2129:                                     lambda t: update_used_symbols(used_symbols, t),\\n2130:                                     fake_attr_val,\\n2131:                                 )\\n2132:                         continue\\n2133:                     fake = (\\n2134:                         arg.fake_tensor if arg.fake_tensor is not None else arg.example\\n2135:                     )\\n2136:                     update_used_symbols(used_symbols, fake)\\n2137: \\n2138:         # After removing unused graphargs, prune unused binds_symbol\\n2139:         for node in recheck_placeholders:\\n2140:             symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node)\\n2141:             if symbol is not None:\\n2142:                 if symbol not in used_symbols:\\n2143:                     remove_unused(node)\\n2144:                 else:\\n2145:                     # Make sure we delete later occurrences of the same symbol\\n2146:                     used_symbols.remove(symbol)\\n2147: \\n2148:     def remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs(self) -> None:\\n2149:         # This is a pretty interesting function. Basically we have this problem\\n2150:         # where our compiler tends to choke when we have unused inputs. The way\\n2151:         # we support dynamic float arguments is by doing a joint fx pass and\\n2152:         # tensorifying away as many symfloats as we can. For the remaining symfloats\\n2153:         # we have no choice but to specialize... HOWEVER at that point in time\\n2154:         # we can no longer remove graph inputs. So our sledgehammer solution is to\\n2155:         # save the state of what inputs we should have specialized in dynamo and\\n2156:         # restart analysis. This function incorporates this \\\"view from the future\\\"\\n2157:         # state and specializes inputs that we know we won't be able to tensorify\\n2158:         # away in the joint pass. In principle we shouldn't choke on unused inputs\\n2159:         # and so this shouldn't be necessary. In practice CUDA graphs choke on\\n2160:         # unused inputs so we need this for now.\\n2161: \\n2162:         # Import here to prevent circular import\\n2163:         from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import TensorifyState\\n2164: \\n2165:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2166:             example_value = node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\")\\n2167:             if (\\n2168:                 isinstance(example_value, FakeTensor)\\n2169:                 and example_value.item_memo is not None\\n2170:                 and hasattr(example_value.item_memo.node._expr, \\\"name\\\")\\n2171:                 and all(u.target == \\\"item\\\" for u in node.users)\\n2172:                 and TensorifyState.should_specialize(\\n2173:                     # We use _expr instead of expr b/c we want the symbol not the replacement\\n2174:                     example_value.item_memo.node._expr.name\\n2175:                 )\\n2176:             ):\\n2177:                 for u in list(node.users):\\n2178:                     u.replace_all_uses_with(guard_scalar(example_value.item_memo))\\n2179:                     self.remove_node(u)\\n2180:                 self.remove_node(node)\\n2181: \\n2182:     def add_output_instructions(self, prefix: list[Instruction]) -> None:\\n2183:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2184:         We call this on the creation of a new compiled subgraph that is inserted\\n2185:         before user code.\\n2186:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2187:         self.output_instructions.extend(prefix)\\n2188:         self.should_exit = True\\n2189: \\n2190:     def install_global_unsafe(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:\\n2191:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2192:         WARNING: prefer the safer `install_global_by_id/install_global`.\\n2193:         torch.compile instances should be independent of each other;\\n2194:         one footgun is to have one instance depend on the existence of\\n2195:         a global installed by another instance. This can happen if we mangle\\n2196:         a global the same way across both instances.\\n2197:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2198:         assert name not in self.installed_globals\\n2199:         self.installed_globals.add(name)\\n2200:         self.cleanups.append(CleanupHook.create(self.global_scope, name, value))\\n2201: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1900-2200\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1900:         assert self.compiler_fn is not None\n1901:         tot = 0\n1902:         placeholders = []\n1903:         for node in gm.graph.nodes:\n1904:             if node.op in (\"call_function\", \"call_method\", \"call_module\"):\n1905:                 tot += 1\n1906:             if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n1907:                 placeholders.append(node)\n1908:         increment_op_count(tot)\n1909:         for pl in placeholders:\n1910:             if not hasattr(pl, \"_dynamo_source\"):\n1911:                 arg = pl.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n1912:                 # TODO: Why isn't this stored in meta :think:\n1913:                 # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\n1914:                 pl._dynamo_source = arg.source\n1915: \n1916:         # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\n1917:         gm._param_name_to_source = self.param_name_to_source  # type: ignore[assignment]\n1918:         gm._source_to_user_stacks = self.source_to_user_stacks  # type: ignore[assignment]\n1919: \n1920:         name = (\n1921:             self.compiler_fn.__name__\n1922:             if hasattr(self.compiler_fn, \"__name__\")\n1923:             else \"<unknown compiler_fn>\"\n1924:         )\n1925:         try:\n1926:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\"calling compiler function {name}\")\n1927:             compiler_fn = self.compiler_fn\n1928:             if config.verify_correctness:\n1929:                 compiler_fn = WrapperBackend(compiler_fn)\n1930:             compiled_fn = compiler_fn(gm, example_inputs)\n1931:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\"done compiler function {name}\")\n1932:             assert callable(compiled_fn), \"compiler_fn did not return callable\"\n1933:         except (TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis, ShortenTraceback):\n1934:             raise\n1935:         except exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback as e:\n1936:             if self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph:\n1937:                 raise BackendCompilerFailed(\n1938:                     self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\n1939:                 ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n1940:             unimplemented_v2_with_warning(\n1941:                 e,\n1942:                 self.root_tx.f_code,\n1943:                 gb_type=\"Backend compiler exception\",\n1944:                 context=f\"Backend: {name}\\nException:{str(e)}\\nTraceback:\\n{self.root_tx.format_frame_summary()}\",\n1945:                 explanation=f\"Backend compiler `{name}` failed with {str(e)}. Adding a graph break.\",\n1946:                 hints=[\n1947:                     \"Report an issue to the backend compiler repo.\",\n1948:                 ],\n1949:             )\n1950:         except SkipFrame as e:\n1951:             # The backend compiler has requested that we skip the frame, instead of\n1952:             # aborting execution.\n1953:             raise e\n1954:         except Exception as e:\n1955:             raise BackendCompilerFailed(\n1956:                 self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\n1957:             ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n1958: \n1959:         signpost_event(\n1960:             \"dynamo\",\n1961:             \"OutputGraph.call_user_compiler\",\n1962:             {\n1963:                 **self.co_fields,\n1964:                 \"op_count\": tot,\n1965:                 \"node_count\": len(gm.graph.nodes),\n1966:                 \"input_count\": len(placeholders),\n1967:             },\n1968:         )\n1969: \n1970:         return compiled_fn\n1971: \n1972:     def dedup_pass(self) -> dict[str, torch.fx.GraphModule]:\n1973:         if torch._dynamo.config.use_graph_deduplication:\n1974:             return apply_graph_deduplication(self)\n1975:         else:\n1976:             return {}\n1977: \n1978:     def install_subgraph(self, name: str, sub_gm: torch.fx.GraphModule) -> str:\n1979:         next_name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, requires_suffix=True)\n1980:         sub_gm.__name__ = next_name  # type: ignore[assignment]\n1981:         sub_gm.torchdynamo_force_dynamic = False  # type: ignore[assignment]\n1982:         # This graph module is not present in the user space, so it can't be\n1983:         # accessed by a source. Set source=None.\n1984:         self.register_attr_or_module(sub_gm, next_name, source=None)\n1985:         return next_name\n1986: \n1987:     def example_inputs(self) -> list[torch.Tensor]:\n1988:         result = [arg.example for arg in self.graphargs]\n1989:         return result\n1990: \n1991:     def remove_unused_get_attr_nodes(self) -> None:\n1992:         for node in sorted(self.graph.find_nodes(op=\"get_attr\"), reverse=True):\n1993:             if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\n1994:                 self.remove_node(node)\n1995: \n1996:     def remove_unused_graphargs(self) -> None:\n1997:         # NB: It's OK to drop GraphArg for symbols that ended up being\n1998:         # specialized iff they are not used in runtime assertions.  You don't\n1999:         # even have to make a guard for it, because ShapeEnv produce_guards\n2000:         # operates on tracked_fakes, which never gets pruned.\n2001:         # That being said, you'll get marginally better generated\n2002:         # guard code if you promote the guard into a Dynamo guard (since that\n2003:         # allows for the guard to be done using C++ guards.)  If we get\n2004:         # ShapeEnv guards to go into C++ guards, this will stop being a thing\n2005:         # though!\n2006: \n2007:         assert self.should_exit\n2008: \n2009:         # Miniature DCE pass, but only for obviously trivial operations\n2010:         def is_static_true(b_node: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\n2011:             if b_node is True:\n2012:                 return True\n2013:             if not isinstance(b_node, fx.Node):\n2014:                 return False\n2015:             b = b_node.meta.get(\"example_value\")\n2016:             if b is None:\n2017:                 return False\n2018:             if b is True:\n2019:                 return True\n2020:             if (\n2021:                 isinstance(b, torch.SymBool)\n2022:                 and (r := b.node.maybe_as_bool()) is not None\n2023:             ):\n2024:                 return r\n2025:             # TODO: We can also technically remove all cases when the input\n2026:             # doesn't have unbacked inputs, since it's all in the ShapeEnv\n2027:             return False\n2028: \n2029:         def is_symnode_arg(a: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\n2030:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\n2031: \n2032:             if isinstance(a, (int, float, bool)):\n2033:                 return True\n2034:             if isinstance(a, fx.Node):\n2035:                 return isinstance(a.meta.get(\"example_value\"), SymTypes)\n2036:             return False\n2037: \n2038:         # NB: We assume that you cannot do mutations on int/float/bool,\n2039:         # because they are immutable types, and therefore is always safe to\n2040:         # DCE.\n2041:         def is_symnode_compute_node(node: fx.Node) -> bool:\n2042:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\n2043: \n2044:             if node.op != \"call_function\":\n2045:                 return False\n2046:             # TODO: I don't think it's possible to have a bare int/float here?\n2047:             if not isinstance(node.meta.get(\"example_value\"), SymTypes):\n2048:                 return False\n2049:             # TODO: This will bail here if you ever end up with a more complicated\n2050:             # computation function, like sum(list_of_ints), even though it\n2051:             # should be DCE'able\n2052:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.args):\n2053:                 return False\n2054:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.kwargs.values()):\n2055:                 return False\n2056:             return True\n2057: \n2058:         from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_accessor_node\n2059: \n2060:         for node in reversed(list(self.graph.nodes)):\n2061:             if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\n2062:                 if (\n2063:                     node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2064:                     or (node.op == \"call_function\" and node.target is operator.getitem)\n2065:                     or (\n2066:                         node.op == \"call_function\"\n2067:                         and node.target is torch._check\n2068:                         and is_static_true(node.args[0])\n2069:                     )\n2070:                     or is_symnode_compute_node(node)\n2071:                     or is_accessor_node(node)\n2072:                 ):\n2073:                     self.remove_node(node)\n2074: \n2075:         def placeholder_binds_symbol(node: fx.Node) -> Optional[sympy.Symbol]:\n2076:             arg = node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2077:             example = arg.example\n2078:             if isinstance(example, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(\n2079:                 example.node.expr, sympy.Symbol\n2080:             ):\n2081:                 return example.node.expr\n2082:             return None\n2083: \n2084:         def remove_unused(node: fx.Node) -> None:\n2085:             log.debug(\"REMOVE UNUSED GRAPHARG %s\", node.meta[\"grapharg\"].source.name())\n2086:             # I'm not really sure why you need to delete these from the\n2087:             # node since the node is going to get removed\n2088:             del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2089:             self.remove_node(node)\n2090:             self.real_value_cache.pop(node, None)\n2091: \n2092:         used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol] = set()\n2093: \n2094:         def update_used_symbols(\n2095:             used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol], fake: Union[torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor]\n2096:         ) -> None:\n2097:             used_symbols |= free_symbols(fake)\n2098: \n2099:         recheck_placeholders = []\n2100:         for node in self.placeholders:\n2101:             binds_symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node) is not None\n2102:             # Don't delete symbol bindings yet\n2103:             if binds_symbol:\n2104:                 if not node.users:\n2105:                     recheck_placeholders.append(node)\n2106:             else:\n2107:                 if not node.users and not isinstance(\n2108:                     node.meta[\"grapharg\"], BackwardStateGraphArg\n2109:                 ):\n2110:                     remove_unused(node)\n2111:                 else:\n2112:                     # Register the free symbols as uses\n2113:                     arg = node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2114:                     if isinstance(arg, BackwardStateGraphArg):\n2115:                         continue\n2116:                     if isinstance(node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example, torch.ScriptObject):\n2117:                         real_script_obj = node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2118:                         fake_script_obj = node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example_strong_ref\n2119:                         if not torch._library.fake_class_registry.tracing_with_real(\n2120:                             real_script_obj\n2121:                         ):\n2122:                             flat_dict = dict(real_script_obj.__obj_flatten__())  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n2123:                             for attr in flat_dict.keys():\n2124:                                 fake_attr_val = getattr(\n2125:                                     fake_script_obj.wrapped_obj, attr\n2126:                                 )\n2127:                                 pytree.tree_map_only(\n2128:                                     (torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor),\n2129:                                     lambda t: update_used_symbols(used_symbols, t),\n2130:                                     fake_attr_val,\n2131:                                 )\n2132:                         continue\n2133:                     fake = (\n2134:                         arg.fake_tensor if arg.fake_tensor is not None else arg.example\n2135:                     )\n2136:                     update_used_symbols(used_symbols, fake)\n2137: \n2138:         # After removing unused graphargs, prune unused binds_symbol\n2139:         for node in recheck_placeholders:\n2140:             symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node)\n2141:             if symbol is not None:\n2142:                 if symbol not in used_symbols:\n2143:                     remove_unused(node)\n2144:                 else:\n2145:                     # Make sure we delete later occurrences of the same symbol\n2146:                     used_symbols.remove(symbol)\n2147: \n2148:     def remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs(self) -> None:\n2149:         # This is a pretty interesting function. Basically we have this problem\n2150:         # where our compiler tends to choke when we have unused inputs. The way\n2151:         # we support dynamic float arguments is by doing a joint fx pass and\n2152:         # tensorifying away as many symfloats as we can. For the remaining symfloats\n2153:         # we have no choice but to specialize... HOWEVER at that point in time\n2154:         # we can no longer remove graph inputs. So our sledgehammer solution is to\n2155:         # save the state of what inputs we should have specialized in dynamo and\n2156:         # restart analysis. This function incorporates this \"view from the future\"\n2157:         # state and specializes inputs that we know we won't be able to tensorify\n2158:         # away in the joint pass. In principle we shouldn't choke on unused inputs\n2159:         # and so this shouldn't be necessary. In practice CUDA graphs choke on\n2160:         # unused inputs so we need this for now.\n2161: \n2162:         # Import here to prevent circular import\n2163:         from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import TensorifyState\n2164: \n2165:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2166:             example_value = node.meta.get(\"example_value\")\n2167:             if (\n2168:                 isinstance(example_value, FakeTensor)\n2169:                 and example_value.item_memo is not None\n2170:                 and hasattr(example_value.item_memo.node._expr, \"name\")\n2171:                 and all(u.target == \"item\" for u in node.users)\n2172:                 and TensorifyState.should_specialize(\n2173:                     # We use _expr instead of expr b/c we want the symbol not the replacement\n2174:                     example_value.item_memo.node._expr.name\n2175:                 )\n2176:             ):\n2177:                 for u in list(node.users):\n2178:                     u.replace_all_uses_with(guard_scalar(example_value.item_memo))\n2179:                     self.remove_node(u)\n2180:                 self.remove_node(node)\n2181: \n2182:     def add_output_instructions(self, prefix: list[Instruction]) -> None:\n2183:         \"\"\"\n2184:         We call this on the creation of a new compiled subgraph that is inserted\n2185:         before user code.\n2186:         \"\"\"\n2187:         self.output_instructions.extend(prefix)\n2188:         self.should_exit = True\n2189: \n2190:     def install_global_unsafe(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:\n2191:         \"\"\"\n2192:         WARNING: prefer the safer `install_global_by_id/install_global`.\n2193:         torch.compile instances should be independent of each other;\n2194:         one footgun is to have one instance depend on the existence of\n2195:         a global installed by another instance. This can happen if we mangle\n2196:         a global the same way across both instances.\n2197:         \"\"\"\n2198:         assert name not in self.installed_globals\n2199:         self.installed_globals.add(name)\n2200:         self.cleanups.append(CleanupHook.create(self.global_scope, name, value))\n2201: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1900-2200"
  }
}

different torch.compile instance\\n2211:         name = f\\\"{prefix}_{id(value)}_c{self.compile_id}\\\"\\n2212:         if name in self.installed_globals:\\n2213:             return name\\n2214: even across torch.compile instances\\n2224:         name = unique_id(prefix)\\n2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\\n2226:         return name\\n2227: \\n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n2234: \\n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2236:     self.backward_state.clear()\\n2248: \\n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n2251:     ) -> None:\\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n2253: \\n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n2255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2256:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n2257:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2258:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n2260: \\n2261: \\n2262: err_epilogue = (\\n2263:     \\\"With the current config, we will graph break \\\"\\n2264:     \\\"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \\\"\\n2265:     \\\"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \\\"\\n2266:     \\\"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \\\"\\n2267:     \\\"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\\\"\\n2268: )\\n2269: \\n2270: \\n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\\n2273: ) -> None:\\n2274:     if kind != \\\"call_function\\\":\\n2275:         return\\n2276: \\n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\\\"prim\\\", \\\"prims\\\", \\\"aten\\\"}:\\n2279:             return\\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\\n2281: \\n2282:     def encountered_non_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload, msg: str) -> None:\\n2283: non-PT2-compliant op\\\",\\n2287:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n2288:                 explanation=msg + \\\" \\\" + err_epilogue,\\n2289:                 hints=[],\\n2290:             )\\n2291: \\n2292:     if isinstance(target, torch._ops.OpOverload):\\n2293:         if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in target.tags:\\n2294:             encountered_compliant_op(target)\\n2295: resolution is expensive.\\n2305:         # If there's only one overload, we know what it will resolve to.\\n2306:         if len(overloads) == 1:\\n2307:             op = getattr(target, overloads[0])\\n2308:             if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in op.tags:\\n2309:                 encountered_compliant_op(op)\\n2310:                 return\\n2311:                 f\\\"that is not PT2 compliant. \\\",\\n2316:             )\\n2317:             return\\n2318: \\n2319:         args, kwargs = torch._dynamo.utils.get_fake_values_from_nodes(\\n2320: attempting to resolve op packet\\\",\\n2329:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n2330:                 explanation=str(e),\\n2331:                 hints=[],\\n2332:             )\\n2333: \\n2334: overload ({overload}) that is \\\"\\n2342:                 f\\\"not PT2 compliant.\\\",\\n2343:             )\\n2344: \\n2345: \\n2346: _compile_id_counter = itertools.count()\\n2347: \\n2348: P = ParamSpec(\\\"P\\\")\\n2349: R = TypeVar(\\\"R\\\")\\n2350: \\n2351: \\n2352: class LazyProxy:\\n2353:     def __init__(\\n2354:         self,\\n2355:         tracer: \\\"SubgraphTracer\\\",\\n2356:         fn: Callable[P, R],\\n2357:         *args: P.args,\\n2358:         **kwargs: P.kwargs,\\n2359:     ) -> None:\\n2360:         self.tracer = tracer\\n2361:         self.fn = fn\\n2362:         self.args = args\\n2363:         self.kwargs = kwargs\\n2364: \\n2365:     def __call__(self) -> Any:\\n2366:         return self.fn(*self.args, **self.kwargs)\\n2367: \\n2368: \\n2369: class SubgraphTracer(fx.Tracer):\\n2370:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2371:     Holds an FX graph that is being traced. OutputGraph owns a SubgraphTracer\\n2372:     and the separation of responsibilities is that SubgraphTracer is\\n2373:     responsible for building the graph while OutputGraph is responsible for\\n2374:     compiling and executing the graph.\\n2375:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2376: \\n2377:     def __init__(\\n2378:         self,\\n2379:         output_graph: \\\"OutputGraph\\\",\\n2380:         parent: Optional[\\\"SubgraphTracer\\\"] = None,\\n2381: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2200-2380\"}",

installed global.\n2208:         \"\"\"\n2209:         # NB: need self.compile_id to distinguish this global\n2210:         # from another global created in a different torch.compile instance\n2211:         name = f\"{prefix}_{id(value)}_c{self.compile_id}\"\n2212:         if name in self.installed_globals:\n2213:             return name\n2214:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\n2215:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\n2222:         \"\"\"\n2223:         # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances\n2224:         name = unique_id(prefix)\n2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\n2226:         return name\n2227: \n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\n2234: \n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2236:             if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n2237:                 del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\n2248: \n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n2251:     ) -> None:\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n2253: \n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n2255:         \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n2256:         if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n2257:             return node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2258:         assert node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\n2260: \n2261: \n2262: err_epilogue = (\n2263:     \"With the current config, we will graph break \"\n2264:     \"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \"\n2265:     \"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \"\n2266:     \"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \"\n2267:     \"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\"\n2268: )\n2269: \n2270: \n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\n2273: ) -> None:\n2274:     if kind != \"call_function\":\n2275:         return\n2276: \n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\"prim\", \"prims\", \"aten\"}:\n2279:             return\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\n2281: \n2282:     def encountered_non_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload, msg: str) -> None:\n2283:         output_graph.non_compliant_ops.add(target)\n2284:         if config.only_allow_pt2_compliant_ops:\n2285:             unimplemented_v2(\n2286:                 gb_type=\"Encountered non-PT2-compliant op\",\n2287:                 context=\"\",\n2288:                 explanation=msg + \" \" + err_epilogue,\n2289:                 hints=[],\n2290:             )\n2291: \n2292:     if isinstance(target, torch._ops.OpOverload):\n2293:         if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in target.tags:\n2294:             encountered_compliant_op(target)\n2295:             return\n2296:         encountered_non_compliant_op(\n2297:             target,\n2298:             f\"Encountered the torch.ops.OpOverload {target} that is not PT2 compliant.\",\n2299:         )\n2300:         return\n2301: \n2302:     if isinstance(target, torch._ops.OpOverloadPacket):\n2303:         overloads = tuple(target.overloads())\n2304:         # Optimization: Overload resolution is expensive.\n2305:         # If there's only one overload, we know what it will resolve to.\n2306:         if len(overloads) == 1:\n2307:             op = getattr(target, overloads[0])\n2308:             if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in op.tags:\n2309:                 encountered_compliant_op(op)\n2310:                 return\n2311:             encountered_non_compliant_op(\n2312:                 op,\n2313:                 f\"Encountered the non-overloaded \"\n2314:                 f\"torch.ops.OpOverloadPacket {target} \"\n2315:                 f\"that is not PT2 compliant. \",\n2316:             )\n2317:             return\n2318: \n2319:         args, kwargs = torch._dynamo.utils.get_fake_values_from_nodes(\n2320:             output_graph.current_tx, (args, kwargs), False\n2321:         )\n2322:         try:\n2323:             overload = torch._C._jit_resolve_packet(\n2324:                 target._qualified_op_name, *args, **kwargs\n2325:             )\n2326:         except RuntimeError as e:\n2327:             unimplemented_v2(\n2328:                 gb_type=\"Error when attempting to resolve op packet\",\n2329:                 context=\"\",\n2330:                 explanation=str(e),\n2331:                 hints=[],\n2332:             )\n2333: \n2334:         op = getattr(target, overload)\n2335:         if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in op.tags:\n2336:             encountered_compliant_op(op)\n2337:         else:\n2338:             encountered_non_compliant_op(\n2339:                 op,\n2340:                 f\"Encountered the torch.ops.OpOverloadPacket {target} \"\n2341:                 f\"which resolves to the overload ({overload}) that is \"\n2342:                 f\"not PT2 compliant.\",\n2343:             )\n2344: \n2345: \n2346: _compile_id_counter = itertools.count()\n2347: \n2348: P = ParamSpec(\"P\")\n2349: R = TypeVar(\"R\")\n2350: \n2351: \n2352: class LazyProxy:\n2353:     def __init__(\n2354:         self,\n2355:         tracer: \"SubgraphTracer\",\n2356:         fn: Callable[P, R],\n2357:         *args: P.args,\n2358:         **kwargs: P.kwargs,\n2359:     ) -> None:\n2360:         self.tracer = tracer\n2361:         self.fn = fn\n2362:         self.args = args\n2363:         self.kwargs = kwargs\n2364: \n2365:     def __call__(self) -> Any:\n2366:         return self.fn(*self.args, **self.kwargs)\n2367: \n2368: \n2369: class SubgraphTracer(fx.Tracer):\n2370:     \"\"\"\n2371:     Holds an FX graph that is being traced. OutputGraph owns a SubgraphTracer\n2372:     and the separation of responsibilities is that SubgraphTracer is\n2373:     responsible for building the graph while OutputGraph is responsible for\n2374:     compiling and executing the graph.\n2375:     \"\"\"\n2376: \n2377:     def __init__(\n2378:         self,\n2379:         output_graph: \"OutputGraph\",\n2380:         parent: Optional[\"SubgraphTracer\"] = None,\n2381: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2200-2380"
  }

{

  "content": "{\"content\":\"1: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2: Core graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\\n3: the essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\\n4: \\n5: - OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\\n6:   a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\\n7:   OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\\n8:   and tracking of side effects.\\n9: \\n10: - SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\\n11:   It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\\n12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\\n13: \\n14: The module supports key Dynamo features including:\\n15: - Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\\n16: - Graph deduplication for optimization\\n17: - Symbolic shape handling and propagation\\n18: - Side effect tracking and management\\n19: - Guard insertion and management\\n20: \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n21: \\n22: import collections\\n23: import contextlib\\n24: import copy\\n25: import functools\\n26: import inspect\\n27: import itertools\\n28: import logging\\n29: import operator\\n30: import re\\n31: import sys\\n32: import traceback\\n33: import warnings\\n34: import weakref\\n35: from collections.abc import Generator, Sequence\\n36: from dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\\n37: from types import CodeType\\n38: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\\n39: from typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\\n40: \\n41: import sympy\\n42: \\n43: import torch._guards\\n44: import torch._logging\\n45: import torch.distributed as dist\\n46: import torch.nn\\n47: import torch.utils._pytree as pytree\\n48: from torch import fx, Tensor\\n49: from torch._C._dynamo import guards\\n50: from torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\\n51: from torch._guards import (\\n52:     CompileContext,\\n53:     CompileId,\\n54:     GlobalContextCheckpointState,\\n55:     Source,\\n56:     tracing,\\n57:     TracingContext,\\n58: )\\n59: from torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor\\n60: from torch._utils_internal import signpost_event\\n61: from torch.export.dynamic_shapes import _ConstraintTarget\\n62: from torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _make_graph_module  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n63: from torch.fx.experimental._backward_state import BackwardState\\n64: from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\\n65:     free_symbols,\\n66:     guard_scalar,\\n67:     is_symbolic,\\n68:     ShapeEnv,\\n69:     Specialization,\\n70: )\\n71: from torch.fx.node import Target\\n72: from torch.fx.passes.runtime_assert import insert_deferred_runtime_asserts\\n73: from torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\\n74: from torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\\n75: from torch.utils._python_dispatch import is_traceable_wrapper_subclass\\n76: \\n77: from . import config, exc, logging as torchdynamo_logging, variables\\n78: from .backends.registry import CompiledFn, CompilerFn\\n79: from import (\\n91:     BackendCompilerFailed,\\n92:     exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback,\\n93:     SkipFrame,\\n94:     unimplemented_v2,\\n95:     unimplemented_v2_with_warning,\\n96: )\\n97: from import is_dynamic_nn_module\\n101:   "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1: \"\"\"\n2: Core graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\n3: the essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\n4: \n5: - OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\n6:   a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\n7:   OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\n8:   and tracking of side effects.\n9: \n10: - SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\n11:   It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\n12:   lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\n13: \n14: The module supports key Dynamo features including:\n15: - Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\n16: - Graph deduplication for optimization\n17: - Symbolic shape handling and propagation\n18: - Side effect tracking and management\n19: - Guard insertion and management\n20: \"\"\"\n21: \n22: import collections\n23: import contextlib\n24: import copy\n25: import functools\n26: import inspect\n27: import itertools\n28: import logging\n29: import operator\n30: import re\n31: import sys\n32: import traceback\n33: import warnings\n34: import weakref\n35: from collections.abc import Generator, Sequence\n36: from dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\n37: from types import CodeType\n38: from typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\n39: from typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\n40: \n41: import sympy\n42: \n43: import torch._guards\n44: import torch._logging\n45: import torch.distributed as dist\n46: import torch.nn\n47: import torch.utils._pytree as pytree\n48: from torch import fx, Tensor\n49: from torch._C._dynamo import guards\n50: from torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\n51: from torch._guards import (\n52:     CompileContext,\n53:     CompileId,\n54:     GlobalContextCheckpointState,\n55:     Source,\n56:     tracing,\n57:     TracingContext,\n58: )\n59: from torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor\n60: from torch._utils_internal import signpost_event\n61: from torch.export.dynamic_shapes import _ConstraintTarget\n62: from torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _make_graph_module  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n63: from torch.fx.experimental._backward_state import BackwardState\n64: from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\n65:     free_symbols,\n66:     guard_scalar,\n67:     is_symbolic,\n68:     ShapeEnv,\n69:     Specialization,\n70: )\n71: from torch.fx.node import Target\n72: from torch.fx.passes.runtime_assert import insert_deferred_runtime_asserts\n73: from torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\n74: from torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\n75: from torch.utils._python_dispatch import is_traceable_wrapper_subclass\n76: \n77: from . import config, exc, logging as torchdynamo_logging, variables\n78: from .backends.registry import CompiledFn, CompilerFn\n79: from .bytecode_transformation import (\n80:     create_call_function,\n81:     create_instruction,\n82:     create_load_const,\n83:     Instruction,\n84:     unique_id,\n85: )\n86: from .code_context import code_context\n87: from .codegen import PyCodegen\n88: from .current_scope_id import enter_new_scope\n89: from .device_interface import get_interface_for_device\n90: from .exc import (\n91:     BackendCompilerFailed,\n92: from .graph_region_tracker import GraphRegionTracker\n99: from .guards import GuardBuilder, install_guard\n100: from .mutation_guard import is_dynamic_nn_module\n101: ",

`test_parametrization[cleanup_fake_tensor_refs]` \u2014 verifies FakeTensorMode cycle is broken\\n76: - `test_parametrization[cleanup_shape_env_refs]` \u2014 verifies ShapeEnv \u2192 TrackedFake references are cleared\\n77: - `test_parametrization[gc_not_needed_after_cleanup]` \u2014 verifies no full GC is needed after cleanup\\n78: - `test_parametrization[repeated_failure_no_leak]` \u2014       },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "73: The following tests validate the cleanup behavior:\n74: - `test_parametrization[cleanup_graph_on_failure]` \u2014 verifies graph nodes are cleaned up after tracer failure\n75: - `test_parametrization[cleanup_fake_tensor_refs]` \u2014 verifies FakeTensorMode cycle is broken\n76: - `test_parametrization[cleanup_shape_env_refs]` \u2014 verifies ShapeEnv \u2192 TrackedFake references are cleared\n77: - `test_parametrization[gc_not_needed_after_cleanup]` \u2014 verifies no full GC is needed after cleanup\n78: - `test_parametrization[repeated_failure_no_leak]` \u2014 verifies no memory leak across repeated failures\n79: \n80: ",
            "endLine": 80,
            "startLine": 73
          },
          {
            "content": "193: }</pre></details><details><summary>2. <code>Bash</code> @ -</summary><pre>{\n194:   &quot;command&quot;: &quot;grep -r \\&quot;cleanup_graph_on_failure\\\\|cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\\\\|cleanup_shape_env_refs\\&quot; /workspace/test --include=\\&quot;*.py\\&quot; | head -20&quot;\n195: }</pre></details><details><summary>3. <code>Bash</code> @ -</summary><pre>{\n196: ",

      },
      {
        "chunks": [
          {
            "content": "73: The following tests validate the cleanup behavior:\n74: - `test_parametrization[cleanup_graph_on_failure]` \u2014 verifies graph nodes are cleaned up after tracer failure\n75: - `test_parametrization[cleanup_fake_tensor_refs]` \u2014 verifies FakeTensorMode cycle is broken\n76: - `test_parametrization[cleanup_shape_env_refs]` \u2014 verifies ShapeEnv \u2192 TrackedFake references are cleared\n77: - `test_parametrization[gc_not_needed_after_cleanup]` \u2014 verifies no full GC is needed after cleanup\n78: - `test_parametrization[repeated_failure_no_leak]` \u2014 verifies no memory leak across repeated failures\n79: \n80: ",
            "endLine": 80,
            "startLine": 73
          },
          {
            "content": "193: }</pre></details><details><summary>2. <code>Bash</code> @ -</summary><pre>{\n194:   &quot;command&quot;: &quot;grep -r \\&quot;cleanup_graph_on_failure\\\\|cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\\\\|cleanup_shape_env_refs\\&quot; /workspace/test --include=\\&quot;*.py\\&quot; | head -20&quot;\n195: }</pre></details><details><summary>3. <code>Bash</code> @ -</summary><pre>{\n196: ",

  "query": "repo:^github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1$ file:test/dynamo instantiate_parametrized_tests parametrize"

Output
{

See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\\n2398:         self.parent = parent\\n2399:         self.source_target = source_target\\n2400:         # A dict mapping previously free variables (Proxy objects)\\n2401:         # to new Proxy objects that wrap inputs to this subgraph.\\n2402:         #\\n2403:         # This dict maps proxies in outer graphs to placeholders in current graph.\\n2404:         # It serves two purposes:\\n2405:         # - Proxies are associated with VariableTrackers. If we see\\n2406:         # the same VariableTracker twice (and it is a free variable),\\n2407:         # then we want to use the same Proxy in the current subgraph to\\n2408:         # record the tracing.\\n2409:         # - If we are tracing a HigherOrderOperator's body_fn, then we\\n2410:         # need to keep track of what free variables were lifted so we can\\n2411:         # rewrite the HigherOrderOperator call using the traced body_fn.\\n2412:         # Dicts maintain the order of args for the HigherOrderOperator call.\\n2413:         self.lifted_freevars: dict[fx.Proxy, fx.Proxy] = {}\\n2414: \\n2415:         # map basic symbols (unbacked and unbacked) to their bound proxies.\\n2416:         # There are only two cases where bound_symbols will be recorded:\\n2417:         # 1. when we create_graph_input for a backed SymInt that's basic symbol\\n2418:         # 2. when we track_unbacked_symbols for intermediate results that contain unbacked symints.\\n2419:         self.bound_symbols: dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, LazyProxy]] = {}\\n2420: \\n2421:         self.prev_inst = None\\n2422:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing into torch.utils.checkpoint\\n2423:         # as part of speculate_subgraph.\\n2424:         self.under_activation_checkpoint = False\\n2425:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\\n2426:         # during this tracer's tracing of torch.utils.checkpoint (via speculate_subgraph).\\n2427:         # Only safe if we know for sure that *NOT* replaying these side-effects during\\n2428:         # backward recomputation of the checkpoint region doesn't affect its correctness.\\n2429:         self.allow_side_effects_under_checkpoint = False\\n2430:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\\n2431:         # during this tracer's tracing. This is currently only used by experimental AC out-of-tree\\n2432:         # via torch._dynamo.utils._disable_side_effect_safety_checks_for_current_subtracer.\\n2433:         # Note: Externally visible side-effects are allowed if this flag OR the above flag is True.\\n2434:         self.unsafe_allow_externally_visible_side_effects = False\\n2435: \\n2436:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing (reconstructing) into a Python generator\\n2437:         self.is_reconstructing_generator = False\\n2438: \\n2439:         self.debug_level: int = parent.debug_level + 1 if parent is not None else 0\\n2440: \\n2441:         self._cur_code = None\\n2442:         self._orig_gm_meta: Optional[list[Any]] = None\\n2443:         self._orig_gm_lineno_map: Optional[dict[int, Optional[int]]] = None\\n2444:         self._orig_gm_firstlineno: Optional[int] = None\\n2445:         # Each SubgraphTracer is associated with a source target, which indicates\\n2446:         # which operator this subgraph is attached to. We compute a source_fn_stack\\n2447:         # based on the source target. For the root tracer, it's set to [].\\n2448:         # This is useful for debugging and transforming the exported graph.\\n2449:         if self.parent is None:\\n2450:             self.source_fn_stack: list[Any] = []\\n2451:         else:\\n2452:             self.source_fn_stack = self.parent.source_fn_stack + [\\n2453:                 (self.graph._target_to_str(source_target), source_target)\\n2454:             ]\\n2455: \\n2456:         # This is used to create a unique name for the placeholder\\n2457:         self._used_names: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\\n2458:         # Stores the versions of the input tensors at the time they are inserted\\n2459:         # as placeholders in the graph. This is used to track input mutation.\\n2460:         self._input_versions_at_beginning: list[int] = []\\n2461:         if torch.is_inference_mode_enabled():\\n2462:             raise RuntimeError(\\n2463:                 \\\"Inference mode is supposed to be disabled during compilation. Please open an issue.\\\"\\n2464:             )\\n2465: \\n2466:     # preserve original meta if it is available\\n2467:     def _maybe_preserve_original_meta(\\n2468:         self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\", node: fx.Node\\n2469:     ) -> None:\\n2470:         if (\\n2471:             self._orig_gm_meta\\n2472:             and self._orig_gm_lineno_map\\n2473:             and self._orig_gm_firstlineno\\n2474:         ):\\n2475:             lineno = tx.current_instruction.starts_line\\n2476:             node_idx = None\\n2477:             if lineno is not None:\\n2478:                 node_idx = self._orig_gm_lineno_map.get(\\n2479:                     lineno - self._orig_gm_firstlineno, None\\n2480:                 )\\n2481:             if node_idx is not None:\\n2482:                 meta = self._orig_gm_meta[node_idx]\\n2483:                 for field in fx.proxy._COPY_META_FIELDS:\\n2484:                     if field in meta:\\n2485:                         node.meta[field] = meta[field]\\n2486:                 if \\\"stack_trace\\\" in meta:\\n2487:                     node.meta[\\\"stack_trace\\\"] = meta[\\\"stack_trace\\\"]\\n2488: \\n2489:     def create_proxy(\\n2490:         self,\\n2491:         kind: str,\\n2492:         target: Any,\\n2493:         args: Any,\\n2494:         kwargs: Any,\\n2495:         name: Optional[str] = None,\\n2496:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,\\n2497:         proxy_factory_fn: Optional[Callable[[fx.Node], fx.Proxy]] = None,\\n2498:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n2499:         # NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling]\\n2500:         # --------------------------------------------------------\\n2501:         # Read NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] first.\\n2502:         #\\n2503:         # Let's say we're in the middle of introspecting the body of a possibly\\n2504:         # nested HigherOrderOperator, and we see a free variable.\\n2505:         #\\n2506:         # There are two cases:\\n2507:         # 1. We see a free variable that is already tracked by Dynamo.\\n2508:         # 2. We see a free variable that has not been tracked by Dynamo\\n2509:         #\\n2510:         # In case 1, we call `maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input` (below)\\n2511:         # which will lift the freevar to be an input of this subgraph\\n2512:         # and also recursively lift it to be an input on the parent(s).\\n2513:         #\\n2514:         # In case 2, before the call to `create_proxy`, the InstructionTranslator\\n2515:         # will see the freevar when it gets loaded by Python bytecode.\\n2516:         # E.g. for Python 3.11 the bytecodes that may do this are LOAD_DEREF or\\n2517:         # LOAD_GLOBAL.\\n2518:         # There, the InstructionTranslator asks Dynamo to begin tracking the\\n2519:         # freevar by building a new Variable.\\n2520:         # Building a new Variable automatically lifts the freevar to be an\\n2521:         # input of the root SubgraphTracer.\\n2522:         #\\n2523:         # The implications for the code below are:\\n2524:         # - We will always be in Case 1 when we get to this code.\\n2525:         # - Any \\\"free variable\\\" we encounter here is guaranteed to already be\\n2526:         #   bound, that is, it is either a graph input of the root graph, or\\n2527:         #   some local variable of the root graph or a subgraph.\\n2528:         # - The additional work we need to do here is *only* that we need to\\n2529:         #   lift this free variable into inputs (recursively) of each nested\\n2530:         #   higher-order-op subgraph until we hit the subgraph where the free\\n2531:         #   variable is bound\\n2532:         if self.parent is not None:\\n2533:             flat_args, tree_spec = pytree.tree_flatten((args, kwargs))\\n2534:             new_flat_args = []\\n2535:             for arg in flat_args:\\n2536:                 maybe_new_arg = self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\\n2537:                 new_flat_args.append(maybe_new_arg)\\n2538: \\n2539:             args, kwargs = pytree.tree_unflatten(new_flat_args, tree_spec)\\n2540: \\n2541:         rv = super().create_proxy(\\n2542:             kind,\\n2543:             target,\\n2544:             args,\\n2545:             kwargs,\\n2546:             name,\\n2547:             type_expr,\\n2548:             proxy_factory_fn,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2549:         )\\n2550: \\n2551:         # append stack trace to fx node\\n2552:         tx = self.output_graph.current_tx\\n2553: \\n2554:         # log detailed location of line of code in 3.11\\n2555:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11) and kind in (\\n2556:             \\\"call_function\\\",\\n2557:             \\\"call_method\\\",\\n2558:             \\\"call_module\\\",\\n2559:         ):\\n2560:             cur_inst = tx.current_instruction\\n2561:             if (\\n2562:                 cur_inst is not self.prev_inst\\n2563:                 and cur_inst.positions is not None\\n2564:                 and cur_inst.positions.lineno is not None\\n2565:             ):\\n2566:                 tx_code = tx.f_code\\n2567:                 header = tx.get_line_of_code_header(lineno=cur_inst.positions.lineno)\\n2568: \\n2569:                 def get_trace_call_log_str() -> str:\\n2570:                     line = get_instruction_source_311(tx_code, cur_inst).rstrip()\\n2571:                     return f\\\"TRACE FX call {rv.node.name} from {header}\\\\n{line}\\\"\\n2572: \\n2573:                 trace_call_log.debug(\\\"%s\\\", LazyString(get_trace_call_log_str))\\n2574:                 self.prev_inst = cur_inst\\n2575: \\n2576:         # update reference to original meta if we're tracing a new code object\\n2577:         is_retracing = False\\n2578:         if tx.f_code is not self._cur_code:\\n2579:             orig_graphmodule_maybe = code_context.get_context(tx.f_code).get(\\n2580:                 \\\"orig_graphmodule\\\", lambda: None\\n2581:             )()\\n2582:             if isinstance(orig_graphmodule_maybe, torch.fx.GraphModule):\\n2583:                 is_retracing = True\\n2584:                 self._orig_gm_meta = [\\n2585:                     nd.meta for nd in orig_graphmodule_maybe.graph.nodes\\n2586:                 ]\\n2587:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = orig_graphmodule_maybe._lineno_map\\n2588:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = (\\n2589:                     orig_graphmodule_maybe.forward.__code__.co_firstlineno\\n2590:                 )\\n2591:             else:\\n2592:                 self._orig_gm_meta = None\\n2593:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = None\\n2594:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = None\\n2595:         nn_module_stack = tx.nn_module_stack\\n2596:         if nn_module_stack:\\n2597:             rv.node.meta[\\\"nn_module_stack\\\"] = nn_module_stack.copy()\\n2598: \\n2599:         if kind in {\\\"call_function\\\", \\\"call_method\\\"}:\\n2600:             rv.node.meta[\\\"source_fn_stack\\\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\\n2601: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2380-2600\"}",

This is currently only used by experimental AC out-of-tree\n2432:         # via torch._dynamo.utils._disable_side_effect_safety_checks_for_current_subtracer.\n2433:         # Note: Externally visible side-effects are allowed if this flag OR the above flag is True.\n2434:         self.unsafe_allow_externally_visible_side_effects = False\n2435: \n2436:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing (reconstructing) into a Python generator\n2437:         self.is_reconstructing_generator = False\n2438: \n2439:         self.debug_level: int = parent.debug_level + 1 if parent is not None else 0\n2440: \n2441:         self._cur_code = None\n2442:         self._orig_gm_meta: Optional[list[Any]] = None\n2443:         self._orig_gm_lineno_map: Optional[dict[int, Optional[int]]] = None\n2444:         self._orig_gm_firstlineno: Optional[int] = None\n2445:         # Each SubgraphTracer is associated with a source target, which indicates\n2446:         # which operator this subgraph is attached to. For the root tracer, it's set to [].\n2448:         # This is useful for debugging and transforming the exported graph.\n2449:         if self.parent is None:\n2450:             self.source_fn_stack: list[Any] = []\n2451:         else:\n2452:             self.source_fn_stack = self.parent.source_fn_stack + [\n2453:                 (self.graph._target_to_str(source_target), source_target)\n2454:             ]\n2455: \n2456:         # This is used to create a unique name for the placeholder\n2457:         self._used_names: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\n2458:         # Stores the versions of the input tensors at the time they are inserted\n2459:         # as placeholders in the graph. Please open an issue.\"\n2464:             )\n2465: \n2466:     # preserve original meta if it is available\n2467:     def _maybe_preserve_original_meta(\n2468:         self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\", node: fx.Node\n2469:     ) -> None:\n2470:         if (\n2471:             self._orig_gm_meta\n2472:             and self._orig_gm_lineno_map\n2473:             and self._orig_gm_firstlineno\n2474:         ):\n2475:             lineno = tx.current_instruction.starts_line\n2476:             node_idx = None\n2477:             if lineno is not None:\n2478:                 node_idx = self._orig_gm_lineno_map.get(\n2479:                     lineno - self._orig_gm_firstlineno, None\n2480:                 )\n2481:             if node_idx is not None:\n2482:                 meta = self._orig_gm_meta[node_idx]\n2483:                 for field in fx.proxy._COPY_META_FIELDS:\n2484:                     if field in meta:\n2485:                         node.meta[field] = meta[field]\n2486:                 if \"stack_trace\" in meta:\n2487:                     node.meta[\"stack_trace\"] = meta[\"stack_trace\"]\n2488: \n2489:     def create_proxy(\n2490:         self,\n2491:         kind: str,\n2492:         target: Any,\n2493:         args: Any,\n2494:         kwargs: Any,\n2495:         name: Optional[str] = None,\n2496:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,\n2497:         proxy_factory_fn: Optional[Callable[[fx.Node], fx.Proxy]] = None,\n2498:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\n2499:         # NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling]\n2500:         # --------------------------------------------------------\n2501:         # Read NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] first.\n2502:         #\n2503:         # Let's say we're in the middle of introspecting the body of a possibly\n2504:         # nested HigherOrderOperator, and we see a free variable.\n2505:         #\n2506:         # There are two cases:\n2507:         # 1. We see a free variable that is already tracked by Dynamo.\n2508:         # 2. We see a free variable that has not been tracked by Dynamo\n2509:         #\n2510:         # In case 1, we call `maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input` (below)\n2511:         # which will lift the freevar to be an input of this subgraph\n2512:         # and also recursively lift it to be an input on the parent(s).\n2513:         #\n2514:         # In case 2, before the call to `create_proxy`, the InstructionTranslator\n2515:         # will see the freevar when it gets loaded by Python bytecode.\n2516:         # E.g. for Python 3.11 the bytecodes that may do this are LOAD_DEREF or\n2517:         # LOAD_GLOBAL.\n2518:         # There, the InstructionTranslator asks Dynamo to begin tracking the\n2519:         # freevar by building a new Variable.\n2520:         # Building a new Variable automatically lifts the freevar to be an\n2521:         # input of the root SubgraphTracer.\n2522:         #\n2523:         # The implications for the code below are:\n2524:         # - We will always be in Case 1 when we get to this code.\n2525:         # - Any \"free variable\" we encounter here is guaranteed to already be\n2526:         #   bound, that is, it is either a graph input of the root graph, or\n2527:         #   some local variable of the root graph or a subgraph.\n2528:         # - The additional work we need to do here is *only* that we need to\n2529:         #   lift this free variable into inputs (recursively) of each nested\n2530:         #   higher-order-op subgraph until we hit the subgraph where the free\n2531:         #   variable is bound\n2532:         if self.parent is not None:\n2533:             flat_args, tree_spec = pytree.tree_flatten((args, kwargs))\n2534:             new_flat_args = []\n2535:             for arg in flat_args:\n2536:                 maybe_new_arg = self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\n2537:                 new_flat_args.append(maybe_new_arg)\n2538: \n2539:             args, kwargs = pytree.tree_unflatten(new_flat_args, tree_spec)\n2540: \n2541:         rv = super().create_proxy(\n2542:             kind,\n2543:             target,\n2544:             args,\n2545:             kwargs,\n2546:             name,\n2547:             type_expr,\n2548:             proxy_factory_fn,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2549:         )\n2550: \n2551:         # append stack trace to fx node\n2552:         tx = self.output_graph.current_tx\n2553: \n2554:         # log detailed location of line of code in 3.11\n2555:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11) and kind in (\n2556:             \"call_function\",\n2557:             \"call_method\",\n2558:             \"call_module\",\n2559:         ):\n2560:             cur_inst = tx.current_instruction\n2561:             if (\n2562:                 cur_inst is not self.prev_inst\n2563:                 and cur_inst.positions is not None\n2564:                 and cur_inst.positions.lineno is not None\n2565:             ):\n2566:                 tx_code = tx.f_code\n2567:                 header = tx.get_line_of_code_header(lineno=cur_inst.positions.lineno)\n2568: \n2569:                 def get_trace_call_log_str() -> str:\n2570:                     line = get_instruction_source_311(tx_code, cur_inst).rstrip()\n2571:                     return f\"TRACE FX call {rv.node.name} from {header}\\n{line}\"\n2572: \n2573:                 trace_call_log.debug(\"%s\", LazyString(get_trace_call_log_str))\n2574:                 self.prev_inst = cur_inst\n2575: \n2576:         # update reference to original meta if we're tracing a new code object\n2577:         is_retracing = False\n2578:         if tx.f_code is not self._cur_code:\n2579:             orig_graphmodule_maybe = code_context.get_context(tx.f_code).get(\n2580:                 \"orig_graphmodule\", lambda: None\n2581:             )()\n2582:             if isinstance(orig_graphmodule_maybe, torch.fx.GraphModule):\n2583:                 is_retracing = True\n2584:                 self._orig_gm_meta = [\n2585:                     nd.meta for nd in orig_graphmodule_maybe.graph.nodes\n2586:                 ]\n2587:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = orig_graphmodule_maybe._lineno_map\n2588:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = (\n2589:                     orig_graphmodule_maybe.forward.__code__.co_firstlineno\n2590:                 )\n2591:             else:\n2592:                 self._orig_gm_meta = None\n2593:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = None\n2594:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = None\n2595:         nn_module_stack = tx.nn_module_stack\n2596:         if nn_module_stack:\n2597:             rv.node.meta[\"nn_module_stack\"] = nn_module_stack.copy()\n2598: \n2599:         if kind in {\"call_function\", \"call_method\"}:\n2600:             rv.node.meta[\"source_fn_stack\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\n2601: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2380-2600"
  }
}
