Normally we don't need to add states here, but we may have to when\\n307:     the information is needed to serialize the guards, so the fields here are\\n308:     supposed to be serializable as a requirement.\\n309:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n310: \\n311:     local_scope: Scope\\n312:     global_scope: Scope\\n313:     # This records the initial torch function mode stack for guarding\\n314:     torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode]\\n315:     guard_on_key_order: set[Source]\\n316:     # Map from graph input's `Source` to sizes / strides metadata\\n317:     input_source_to_sizes_strides: dict[Source, dict[str, Any]]\\n318:     dual_level: int\\n319:     functorch_layers: list[torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.FuncTorchInterpreter]\\n320:     current_device: Optional[torch.device]\\n321:     global_state_guard: torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard\\n322:     _guards: torch._guards.GuardsSet\\n323:     _aotautograd_guards: list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]\\n324: \\n325:     export: bool = False\\n326:     export_constraints: bool = False\\n327:     name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: Optional[str] = None\\n328: \\n329:     @property\\n330:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\\n331:         raise AssertionError(f\\\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\\\")\\n332: \\n333:     @property\\n334:     def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\\n335:         return self._guards\\n336: \\n337:     @property\\n338:     def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\\n339:         return self._aotautograd_guards\\n340: \\n341: \\n342: @dataclass\\n343: class StackLocalsMetadata:\\n344:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n345:     Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\\n346:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n347: \\n348:     stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n349:     locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n350:     stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n351:     stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n352:     locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\\n353: \\n354: \\n355: def get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n356:     # f_globals[\\\"__builtins__\\\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\\n357:     # implementation detail -\\n358:     # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\\n359: \\n360:     # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\\n361:     # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\\n362:     # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\\n363: \\n364:     # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\\n365:     # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\\n366:     # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\\n367:     # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\\n368: \\n369:     f_builtins = global_scope[\\\"__builtins__\\\"]\\n370:     if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\\n371:         f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\\n372:     return f_builtins\\n373: \\n374: \\n375: class OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\\n376:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n377:     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\\n379: \\n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\\n381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\\n382:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\\n383:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\\n384:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n385: \\n386:     side_effects: SideEffects\\n387: \\n388:     def __init__(\\n389:         self,\\n390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\\n391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\\n392:         root_tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n393:         export: bool,\\n394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\\n395:         frame_state: Any,\\n396:         local_scope: Scope,\\n397:         global_scope: Scope,\\n398:         f_code: CodeType,\\n399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\\n400:         package: Optional[\\\"CompilePackage\\\"],\\n401: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL100-400\"}",

TODO: replace `same` function with the one in testing\n286:             if same(correct, result):\n287:                 return self.candidate\n288: \n289:             raise RuntimeError(f\"incorrect results of backend {self}\")\n290: \n291:         except Exception:\n292:             log.exception(\"error in verify_correctness\")\n293:             raise\n294:         finally:\n295:             self.restore()\n296: \n297: \n298: Scope = dict[str, object]\n299: \n300: \n301: @dataclass\n302: class OutputGraphGuardsState:\n303:     \"\"\"\n304:     A base class containing fields that are considered \"persistent\" when we\n305:     want to save all the important state for reconstrucing guards in a different\n306:     process. Normally we don't need to add states here, but we may have to when\n307:     the information is needed to serialize the guards, so the fields here are\n308:     supposed to be serializable as a requirement.\n309:     \"\"\"\n310: \n311:     local_scope: Scope\n312:     global_scope: Scope\n313:     # This records the initial torch function mode stack for guarding\n314:     torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode]\n315:     guard_on_key_order: set[Source]\n316:     # Map from graph input's `Source` to sizes / strides metadata\n317:     input_source_to_sizes_strides: dict[Source, dict[str, Any]]\n318:     dual_level: int\n319:     functorch_layers: list[torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.FuncTorchInterpreter]\n320:     current_device: Optional[torch.device]\n321:     global_state_guard: torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard\n322:     _guards: torch._guards.GuardsSet\n323:     _aotautograd_guards: list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]\n324: \n325:     export: bool = False\n326:     export_constraints: bool = False\n327:     name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: Optional[str] = None\n328: \n329:     @property\n330:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n331:         raise AssertionError(f\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\")\n332: \n333:     @property\n334:     def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n335:         return self._guards\n336: \n337:     @property\n338:     def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n339:         return self._aotautograd_guards\n340: \n341: \n342: @dataclass\n343: class StackLocalsMetadata:\n344:     \"\"\"\n345:     Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\n346:     \"\"\"\n347: \n348:     stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n349:     locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n350:     stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n351:     stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n352:     locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n353: \n354: \n355: def get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:\n356:     # f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n357:     # implementation detail -\n358:     # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n359: \n360:     # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n361:     # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n362:     # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n363: \n364:     # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n365:     # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n366:     # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n367:     # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n368: \n369:     f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n370:     if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n371:         f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n372:     return f_builtins\n373: \n374: \n375: class OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n376:     \"\"\"\n377:     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\n379: \n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\n382:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\n383:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\n384:     \"\"\"\n385: \n386:     side_effects: SideEffects\n387: \n388:     def __init__(\n389:         self,\n390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\n391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\n392:         root_tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n393:         export: bool,\n394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\n395:         frame_state: Any,\n396:         local_scope: Scope,\n397:         global_scope: Scope,\n398:         f_code: CodeType,\n399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\n400:         package: Optional[\"CompilePackage\"],\n401: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL100-400"
  }
}

This ensures that we keep the stack trace of\\n2658:                 # the user code.\\n2659:                 if not tx.is_co_filename_from_nn_modules():\\n2660:                     frame_summaries.append(tx.frame_summary())\\n2661:                 tx = getattr(tx, \\\"parent\\\", None)\\n2662:             # Reverse the frame_summaries, such that the innermost frame is at the last\\n2663:             frame_summaries.reverse()\\n2664: \\n2665:             # official from_list stub doesn't have new-style type\\n2666:             msgs = traceback.StackSummary.from_list(frame_summaries).format()\\n2667:             rv.node.stack_trace = \\\"\\\".join(msgs)\\n2668: \\n2669:         if (\\n2670:             torch._dynamo.config.use_graph_deduplication\\n2671:             or torch._dynamo.config.track_nodes_for_deduplication\\n2672:         ):\\n2673:             self.output_graph.region_tracker.track_node(\\n2674:                 self.output_graph.current_tx, rv.node\\n2675:             )\\n2676:         return rv\\n2677: \\n2678:     def create_node(\\n2679:         self,\\n2680:         op: str,\\n2681:         target: Target,\\n2682:         args: Any = None,\\n2683:         kwargs: Any = None,\\n2684:         name: Optional[str] = None,\\n2685:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,\\n2686:     ) -> fx.Node:\\n2687:         check_pt2_compliant_op(self.output_graph, op, target, args, kwargs)\\n2688:         if self.parent is not None:\\n2689:             flat_args = pytree.arg_tree_leaves(*args, **kwargs)\\n2690:             for arg in flat_args:\\n2691:                 if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Node):\\n2692:                     continue\\n2693:                 assert arg.graph == self.graph, (\\n2694:                     \\\"create_node using arg not from this SubgraphTracer\\\"\\n2695:                 )\\n2696: \\n2697:         node = super().create_node(op, target, args, kwargs, name, type_expr)\\n2698:         node.meta[\\\"creation_timestamp\\\"] = self.output_graph.timestamp\\n2699:         self._used_names.add(node.name)\\n2700:         return node\\n2701: \\n2702:     # Note: we did not override erase_node since\\n2703:     # we call self.graph.erase_node elsewhere\\n2704:     def remove_node(self, node: fx.Node) -> None:\\n2705:         if len(node.users) > 0:\\n2706:             user_graph_nodes: list[torch.fx.Node] = []\\n2707:             for user in node.users.keys():\\n2708:                 # For the case where user.graph == self.graph, that is a real bug and will raise\\n2709:                 # properly.\\n2710:                 if user.graph != self.graph:\\n2711:                     # This is a nested graph, which needs to be deleted.\\n2712:                     # If we do not do this, we will raise on attempting to remove this.\\n2713:                     # As we only get here during restoration cleanup, this is sound.\\n2714:                     user_graph_nodes.extend(reversed(list(user.graph.nodes)))\\n2715:             for other_graph_node in user_graph_nodes:\\n2716:                 other_graph_node.graph.erase_node(other_graph_node)\\n2717:         self.graph.erase_node(node)\\n2718:         self.input_name_to_proxy.pop(node.name, None)\\n2719: \\n2720:     # when before=True, we will insert this input before the most recent\\n2721:     # inserted proxy.  This is a hack to get around an ordering problem,\\n2722:     # where we first insert a tensor argument, and then insert bindings\\n2723:     # for SymInts that may occur in the tensor argument.\\n2724:     # Remove this if https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/99007 gets\\n2725:     # fixed.\\n2726:     def create_graph_input(\\n2727:         self,\\n2728:         name: str,\\n2729:         type_expr: Any,\\n2730:         example_value: Any,\\n2731:         before: bool = False,\\n2732:         source: Optional[Source] = None,\\n2733:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n2734:         if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n2735:             self._input_versions_at_beginning.append(example_value._version)\\n2736:         log.debug(\\n2737:             \\\"create_graph_input %s %s %s at debug_level %s before=%s\\\",\\n2738:             name,\\n2739:             source.name() if source is not None else \\\"(none)\\\",\\n2740:             example_value,\\n2741:             self.debug_level,\\n2742:             before,\\n2743:         )\\n2744:         if source is None:\\n2745:             assert self.parent is not None, (\\n2746:                 f\\\"you are required to provide a source for inputs {name} example_val {example_value} on the root tracer\\\"\\n2747:             )\\n2748: \\n2749:         # Note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\\n2750:         # In eager, we are generally OK with adding graph inputs whenever we\\n2751:         # want, because we take care of writing the bytecode that knows how\\n2752:         # to source all the inputs.\\n2753:         #\\n2754:         # In export, this is bad, because you want a self-contained export\\n2755:         # object which only depends on the inputs you explicitly passed to it.\\n2756:         # So we are a bit more strict about what sources can become inputs\\n2757:         # in export\\n2758:         if self.is_export and self.parent is None:\\n2759:             assert source is not None\\n2760:             if not is_from_local_source(source, only_allow_input=True):\\n2761:                 self.output_graph.source_to_user_stacks.setdefault(source, []).append(\\n2762:                     TracingContext.extract_stack()\\n2763:                 )\\n2764: \\n2765:         # _used_names contains the names of all the nodes in the graph,\\n2766:         # including intermediates. This ensures that we do not have a name\\n2767:         # collision.\\n2768:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self._used_names)\\n2769:         if self.input_name_to_proxy:\\n2770:             prev_name = next(reversed(self.input_name_to_proxy))\\n2771:             node = self.input_name_to_proxy[prev_name].node\\n2772:             if before:\\n2773:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(node)\\n2774:             else:\\n2775:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_after(node)\\n2776:         else:\\n2777:             ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(None)\\n2778:         with ctx:\\n2779:             proxy = self.create_proxy(\\\"placeholder\\\", name, (), {}, type_expr=type_expr)\\n2780:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\\n2781:             if self.input_name_to_proxy and before:\\n2782:                 k, v = self.input_name_to_proxy.popitem()\\n2783:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\\n2784:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[k] = v\\n2785:             else:\\n2786:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\\n2787: \\n2788:             # For placeholder nodes, `name` is passed as a str to the target,\\n2789:             # and then torch.fx decides the node.name. So, record the `target`\\n2790:             # name as well in the _used_names to prevent any collision.\\n2791:             self._used_names.add(name)\\n2792: \\n2793:             # NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]\\n2794:             # Whenever we call create_graph_input, we try to also lift the basic symbols in example values\\n2795:             # as graph input.\\n2796:             # This applies to both top-level graph and subgraphs in higher order ops.\\n2797:             # It has several cases:\\n2798:             #  1. When create_graph_input for a tensor that has symbolic shapes,\\n2799:             #     we look for basic symbols in its size and stride, we check if the symbol is bound\\n2800:             #     in current graph (i.e. bound_symbols), it it's not bound, we'll create a placeholder\\n2801: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2600-2800\"}",

This ensures that we keep the stack trace of\n2658:                 # the user code.\n2659:                 if not tx.is_co_filename_from_nn_modules():\n2660:                     frame_summaries.append(tx.frame_summary())\n2661:                 tx = getattr(tx, \"parent\", None)\n2662:             # Reverse the frame_summaries, such that the innermost frame is at the last\n2663:             frame_summaries.reverse()\n2664: \n2665:             # official from_list stub doesn't have new-style type\n2666:             msgs = traceback.StackSummary.from_list(frame_summaries).format()\n2667:             rv.node.stack_trace = \"\".join(msgs)\n2668: \n2669:         if (\n2670:             torch._dynamo.config.use_graph_deduplication\n2671:             or torch._dynamo.config.track_nodes_for_deduplication\n2672:         ):\n2673:             self.output_graph.region_tracker.track_node(\n2674:                 self.output_graph.current_tx, rv.node\n2675:             )\n2676:         return rv\n2677: \n2678:     def create_node(\n2679:         self,\n2680:         op: str,\n2681:         target: Target,\n2682:         args: Any = None,\n2683:         kwargs: Any = None,\n2684:         name: Optional[str] = None,\n2685:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,\n2686:     ) -> fx.Node:\n2687:         check_pt2_compliant_op(self.output_graph, op, target, args, kwargs)\n2688:         if self.parent is not None:\n2689:             flat_args = pytree.arg_tree_leaves(*args, **kwargs)\n2690:             for arg in flat_args:\n2691:                 if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Node):\n2692:                     continue\n2693:                 assert arg.graph == self.graph, (\n2694:                     \"create_node using arg not from this SubgraphTracer\"\n2695:                 )\n2696: \n2697:         node = super().create_node(op, target, args, kwargs, name, type_expr)\n2698:         node.meta[\"creation_timestamp\"] = self.output_graph.timestamp\n2699:         self._used_names.add(node.name)\n2700:         return node\n2701: \n2702:     # Note: we did not override erase_node since\n2703:     # we call self.graph.erase_node elsewhere\n2704:     def remove_node(self, node: fx.Node) -> None:\n2705:         if len(node.users) > 0:\n2706:             user_graph_nodes: list[torch.fx.Node] = []\n2707:             for user in node.users.keys():\n2708:                 # For the case where user.graph == self.graph, that is a real bug and will raise\n2709:                 # properly.\n2710:                 if user.graph != self.graph:\n2711:                     # This is a nested graph, which needs to be deleted.\n2712:                     # If we do not do this, we will raise on attempting to remove this.\n2713:                     # As we only get here during restoration cleanup, this is sound.\n2714:                     user_graph_nodes.extend(reversed(list(user.graph.nodes)))\n2715:             for other_graph_node in user_graph_nodes:\n2716:                 other_graph_node.graph.erase_node(other_graph_node)\n2717:         self.graph.erase_node(node)\n2718:         self.input_name_to_proxy.pop(node.name, None)\n2719: \n2720:     # when before=True, we will insert this input before the most recent\n2721:     # inserted proxy.  This is a hack to get around an ordering problem,\n2722:     # where we first insert a tensor argument, and then insert bindings\n2723:     # for SymInts that may occur in the tensor argument.\n2724:     # Remove this if https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/99007 gets\n2725:     # fixed.\n2726:     def create_graph_input(\n2727:         self,\n2728:         name: str,\n2729:         type_expr: Any,\n2730:         example_value: Any,\n2731:         before: bool = False,\n2732:         source: Optional[Source] = None,\n2733:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\n2734:         if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n2735:             self._input_versions_at_beginning.append(example_value._version)\n2736:         log.debug(\n2737:             \"create_graph_input %s %s %s at debug_level %s before=%s\",\n2738:             name,\n2739:             source.name() if source is not None else \"(none)\",\n2740:             example_value,\n2741:             self.debug_level,\n2742:             before,\n2743:         )\n2744:         if source is None:\n2745:             assert self.parent is not None, (\n2746:                 f\"you are required to provide a source for inputs {name} example_val {example_value} on the root tracer\"\n2747:             )\n2748: \n2749:         # Note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\n2750:         # In eager, we are generally OK with adding graph inputs whenever we\n2751:         # want, because we take care of writing the bytecode that knows how\n2752:         # to source all the inputs.\n2753:         #\n2754:         # In export, this is bad, because you want a self-contained export\n2755:         # object which only depends on the inputs you explicitly passed to it.\n2756:         # So we are a bit more strict about what sources can become inputs\n2757:         # in export\n2758:         if self.is_export and self.parent is None:\n2759:             assert source is not None\n2760:             if not is_from_local_source(source, only_allow_input=True):\n2761:                 self.output_graph.source_to_user_stacks.setdefault(source, []).append(\n2762:                     TracingContext.extract_stack()\n2763:                 )\n2764: \n2765:         # _used_names contains the names of all the nodes in the graph,\n2766:         # including intermediates. This ensures that we do not have a name\n2767:         # collision.\n2768:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self._used_names)\n2769:         if self.input_name_to_proxy:\n2770:             prev_name = next(reversed(self.input_name_to_proxy))\n2771:             node = self.input_name_to_proxy[prev_name].node\n2772:             if before:\n2773:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(node)\n2774:             else:\n2775:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_after(node)\n2776:         else:\n2777:             ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(None)\n2778:         with ctx:\n2779:             proxy = self.create_proxy(\"placeholder\", name, (), {}, type_expr=type_expr)\n2780:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\n2781:             if self.input_name_to_proxy and before:\n2782:                 k, v = self.input_name_to_proxy.popitem()\n2783:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\n2784:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[k] = v\n2785:             else:\n2786:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\n2787: \n2788:             # For placeholder nodes, `name` is passed as a str to the target,\n2789:             # and then torch.fx decides the node.name. So, record the `target`\n2790:             # name as well in the _used_names to prevent any collision.\n2791:             self._used_names.add(name)\n2792: \n2793:             # NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]\n2794:             # Whenever we call create_graph_input, we try to also lift the basic symbols in example values\n2795:             # as graph input.\n2796:             # This applies to both top-level graph and subgraphs in higher order ops.\n2797:             # It has several cases:\n2798:             #  1. When create_graph_input for a tensor that has symbolic shapes,\n2799:             #     we look for basic symbols in its size and stride, we check if the symbol is bound\n2800:             #     in current graph (i.e. bound_symbols), it it's not bound, we'll create a placeholder\n2801: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2600-2800"
  }
}

  "content": "{\"content\":\"400:         package: Optional[\\\"CompilePackage\\\"],\\n401:     ) -> None:\\n402:         super().__init__(\\n403:             local_scope,\\n404:             global_scope,\\n405:             torch_function_mode_stack,\\n406:             guard_on_key_order=set(),\\n407:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\\n408:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\\n409:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\\n410:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\\n411:             # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\\n412:             global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\\n413:             or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\\n414:             # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\\n415:             _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\\n416:             _aotautograd_guards=[],\\n417:         )\\n418:         self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\\n419:         # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\\n420:         # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\\n421:         self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\\n422:         self.export = export\\n423:         self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n424:         self.frame_state = frame_state\\n425:         self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\\n426:         # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\\n427:         self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\\n428:         # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\\n429:         self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\\n430: \\n431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\\n432:         # sure...\\n433:         self.co_fields = {\\n434:             \\\"co_name\\\": f_code.co_name,\\n435:             \\\"co_filename\\\": f_code.co_filename,\\n436:             \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\\n437:         }\\n438: \\n439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\\n440: \\n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n447: \\n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n451:             #\\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\\n455:             # the program execution.\\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\\n462:         )\\n463: \\n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\\n467: \\n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\\n473:                 export=self.export,\\n474:             )\\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\\n479:         )\\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\\n481: \\n482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\\n483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\\n484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\\n485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\\n486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\\n487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\\n488:             collections.defaultdict(list)\\n489:         )\\n490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\\n502: \\n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\\n505: \\n506:         # Not checkpointed\\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\\n509: \\n510:         self.package = package\\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\\n512:         # accessed it?\\n513:         #\\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\\n515:         #   - During export, and\\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\\n517:         #\\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\\n520:         # recording!\\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\\n522: \\n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\\n525:         self.should_exit = False\\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\\n527: \\n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\\n530: \\n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\\n535:         # back for backend errors.\\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\\n537: \\n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n541: \\n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n545: \\n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\\n551:         self.save_global_state()\\n552: \\n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\\n556: \\n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\\n561:         self.random_calls: list[\\n562:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\\n563:         ] = []\\n564:         self.random_values_var: Any = None\\n565: \\n566:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\\n567:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\\n568: \\n569:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\\n570:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\\n571:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\\n572:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\\n573: \\n574:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\\n575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\\n576:         )\\n577: \\n578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\\n579: \\n580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\\n581:         # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\\n582:         # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\\n583:         # context manager inside a compiled region\\n584:         self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\\n585:             self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\\n586:         )\\n587: \\n588:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\\n589:         self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\\n590:             dynamo_timed(\\n591:                 \\\"bytecode_tracing\\\",\\n592:                 log_pt2_compile_event=True,\\n593:             )\\n594:         )\\n595: \\n596:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\\n597:         self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\\n598: \\n599:     def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\\n600:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\\n601:         return self.install_global(\\\"__builtins_dict__\\\", f_builtins)\\n602: \\n603:     def add_backward_state_hook(\\n604:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \\\"hook\\\"\\n605:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\\n606:         name = f\\\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\\\"\\n607:         assert name not in self.backward_state\\n608:         self.backward_state[name] = hook\\n609:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\\n610: \\n611:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\\n612:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\\n613:             if self.export:\\n614:                 unimplemented_v2(\\n615:                     gb_type=\\\"backward_state does not support export\\\",\\n616:                     context=\\\"\\\",\\n617:                     explanation=\\\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\\\",\\n618:                     hints=[],\\n619:                 )\\n620:             example_value = BackwardState()\\n621:             self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\\n622:                 \\\"dynamo_backward_state\\\",\\n623:                 type(example_value),\\n624:                 example_value,\\n625:                 source=BackwardStateSource(),\\n626:             )\\n627:             self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\\n628:             self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\\n629:         return self.backward_state_proxy\\n630: \\n631:     # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\\n632:     def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\\n633:         # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\\n634:         # that show up in ShapeEnv\\n635:         self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\\n636: \\n637:         self.guards.add(\\n638:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\\n639:         )\\n640: \\n641:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\\n642: \\n643:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\\n644: \\n645:         self.guards.add(\\n646:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\\n647:         )\\n648: \\n649:         ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\\n650:         if ci is not None:\\n651:             self.guards.add(\\n652:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\\n653:             )\\n654:         if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\\n655:             self.guards.add(\\n656:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\\n657:                     GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\\n658:                 )\\n659:             )\\n660: \\n661:     def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\\n662:         if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\\n663:             return None\\n664: \\n665:         get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\\n666: are_inline_hooks(hooks):\\n671:             return None\\n672: \\n673:         # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\\n674:         # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\\n675:         # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\\n676: \\n677:         pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\\n678:         pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\\n679:             \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\\\",\\n680:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\\n681:         )\\n682:         unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\\n683:             \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\\\",\\n684:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\\n685:         )\\n686:         assert pack_subgraph_name == \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0\\\"\\n687:         assert unpack_subgraph_name == \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0\\\"\\n688:         return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\\n689: \\n690:     def dump_guards_state(self) -> OutputGraphGuardsState:\\n691:         # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\\n692:         return OutputGraphGuardsState(\\n693:             local_scope=self.local_scope,\\n694:             global_scope=self.global_scope,\\n695: Used by shape guard\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n447: \n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n451:             #\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n455:             # the program execution.\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\n462:         )\n463: \n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\n467: \n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n473:                 export=self.export,\n474:             )\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n479:         )\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\n481: \n482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\n483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\n484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\n485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\n486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\n487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\n488:             collections.defaultdict(list)\n489:         )\n490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\n502: \n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\n505: \n506:         # Not checkpointed\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\n509: \n510:         self.package = package\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\n512:         # accessed it?\n513:         #\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\n515:         #   - During export, and\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\n517:         #\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\n520:         # recording!\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\n522: \n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\n525:         self.should_exit = False\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\n527: \n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\n530: \n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\n535:         # back for backend errors.\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\n537: \n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n541: \n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n545: \n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n551:         self.save_global_state()\n552: \n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\n556: \n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\n561:         self.random_calls: list[\n562:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\n563:         ] = []\n564:         self.random_values_var: Any = None\n565: \n566:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\n567:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\n568: \n569:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\n570:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\n571:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\n572:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\n573: \n574:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\n575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\n576:         )\n577: \n578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n579: \n580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\n581:         # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\n582:         # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\n583:         # context manager inside a compiled region\n584:         self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\n585:             self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\n586:         )\n587: \n588:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\n589:         self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\n590:             dynamo_timed(\n591:                 \"bytecode_tracing\",\n592:                 log_pt2_compile_event=True,\n593:             )\n594:         )\n595: \n596:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\n597:         self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\n598: \n599:     def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\n600:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\n601:         return self.install_global(\"__builtins_dict__\", f_builtins)\n602: \n603:     def add_backward_state_hook(\n604:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \"hook\"\n605:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\n606:         name = f\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\"\n607:         assert name not in self.backward_state\n608:         self.backward_state[name] = hook\n609:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\n610: \n611:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n612:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\n613:             if self.export:\n614:                 unimplemented_v2(\n615:                     gb_type=\"backward_state does not support export\",\n616:                     context=\"\",\n617:                     explanation=\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\",\n618:                     hints=[],\n619:                 )\n620:             example_value = BackwardState()\n621:             self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\n622:                 \"dynamo_backward_state\",\n623:                 type(example_value),\n624:                 example_value,\n625:                 source=BackwardStateSource(),\n626:             )\n627:             self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\"grapharg\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\n628:             self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\n629:         return self.backward_state_proxy\n630: \n631:     # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\n632:     def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\n633:         # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\n634:         # that show up in ShapeEnv\n635:         self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\n636: \n637:         self.guards.add(\n638:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\n639:         )\n640: \n641:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\n642: \n643:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\n644: \n645:         self.guards.add(\n646:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\n647:         )\n648: \n649:         ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\n650:         if ci is not None:\n651:             self.guards.add(\n652:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\n653:             )\n654:         if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n655:             self.guards.add(\n656:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\n657:                     GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\n658:                 )\n659:             )\n660: \n661:     def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\n662:         if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n663:             return None\n664: \n665:         get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\n666:         are_inline_hooks = (\n667:             torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable\n668:         )\n669:         hooks = get_hooks()\n670:         if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):\n671:             return None\n672: \n673:         # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\n674:         # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\n675:         # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\n676: \n677:         pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\n678:         pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n679:             \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\",\n680:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\n681:         )\n682:         unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n683:             \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\",\n684:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\n685:         )\n686:         assert pack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0\"\n687:         assert unpack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0\"\n688:         return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\n689: \n690:     def dump_guards_state(self) -> OutputGraphGuardsState:\n691:         # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\n692:         return OutputGraphGuardsState(\n693:             local_scope=self.local_scope,\n694:             global_scope=self.global_scope,\n695:             torch_function_mode_stack=self.torch_function_mode_stack,\n696:             guard_on_key_order=self.guard_on_key_order,\n697:             input_source_to_sizes_strides=self.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\n698:             dual_level=self.dual_level,\n699:             functorch_layers=self.functorch_layers,\n700:             current_device=self.current_device,\n701: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL400-700"
  }

https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/113030\\n4424:         def func1(x, y):\\n4425:             x.data = y\\n4426:             x.add_(1)\\n4427:             return x\\n4428: \\n4429:         def func2(x, y):\\n4430:             x.data = y\\n4431:             y.data = torch.zeros([0])\\n4432:             return x\\n4433: \\n4434:         def func3(x, y):\\n4435:             z = x\\n4436:             x.data = y\\n4437:             y.data = torch.zeros([0])\\n4438:             return torch.tensor(x is z)\\n4439: \\n4440:         funcs = {\\\"func1\\\": func1, \\\"func2\\\": func2, \\\"func3\\\": func3}\\n4441:         func = funcs[func_name]\\n4442: \\n4443:         if backend != \\\"eager\\\" and func is func1:\\n4444:             # add_ not working w/ aot_autograd?\\n4445:             return\\n4446: \\n4447:         torch._dynamo.reset()\\n4448:         cnt = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounterWithBackend(backend)\\n4449: \\n4450:         compiled_fn = torch.compile(func, backend=cnt, fullgraph=True)\\n4451:         requires_grad = func is not func1\\n4452:         for _ in range(0, 5):\\n4453:             # Inputs\\n4454:             eager_a = torch.ones([6], requires_grad=requires_grad)\\n4455:             compiled_a = torch.ones([6], requires_grad=requires_grad)\\n4456: \\n4457:             eager_b = torch.ones([6], requires_grad=requires_grad)\\n4458:             compiled_b = torch.ones([6], requires_grad=requires_grad)\\n4459: \\n4460:             # Eager\\n4461:             out_eager = func(eager_a, eager_b)\\n4462:             # Compiled\\n4463:             out_compiled = compiled_fn(compiled_a, compiled_b)\\n4464:             self.assertEqual(eager_a, compiled_a)\\n4465:             self.assertEqual(eager_b, compiled_b)\\n4466:             self.assertTrue(torch.equal(out_eager, out_compiled))\\n4467: \\n4468:             # func1 hits a leaf Variable that requires grad is being used in an in-place operation\\n4469:             if requires_grad:\\n4470:                 bwd_inp_eager = torch.randn([6])\\n4471: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/test/dynamo/test_repros.py%3FL4413-4470\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "4413: \n4414:     @parametrize(\n4415:         \"backend\",\n4416:         [\"eager\", \"aot_eager\", \"inductor\"],\n4417:     )\n4418:     @parametrize(\n4419:         \"func_name\",\n4420:         [\"func1\", \"func2\", \"func3\"],\n4421:     )\n4422:     def test_tensor_set_data(self, backend, func_name):\n4423:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/113030\n4424:         def func1(x, y):\n4425:             x.data = y\n4426:             x.add_(1)\n4427:             return x\n4428: \n4429:         def func2(x, y):\n4430:             x.data = y\n4431:             y.data = torch.zeros([0])\n4432:             return x\n4433: \n4434:         def func3(x, y):\n4435:             z = x\n4436:             x.data = y\n4437:             y.data = torch.zeros([0])\n4438:             return torch.tensor(x is z)\n4439: \n4440:         funcs = {\"func1\": func1, \"func2\": func2, \"func3\": func3}\n4441:         func = funcs[func_name]\n4442: \n4443:         if backend != \"eager\" and func is func1:\n4444:             # add_ not working w/ aot_autograd?\n4445:             return\n4446: \n4447:         torch._dynamo.reset()\n4448:         cnt = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounterWithBackend(backend)\n4449: \n4450:         compiled_fn = torch.compile(func, backend=cnt, fullgraph=True)\n4451:         requires_grad = func is not func1\n4452:         for _ in range(0, 5):\n4453:             # Inputs\n4454:             eager_a = torch.ones([6], requires_grad=requires_grad)\n4455:             compiled_a = torch.ones([6], requires_grad=requires_grad)\n4456: \n4457:             eager_b = torch.ones([6], requires_grad=requires_grad)\n4458:             compiled_b = torch.ones([6], requires_grad=requires_grad)\n4459: \n4460:             # Eager\n4461:             out_eager = func(eager_a, eager_b)\n4462:             # Compiled\n4463:             out_compiled = compiled_fn(compiled_a, compiled_b)\n4464:             self.assertEqual(eager_a, compiled_a)\n4465:             self.assertEqual(eager_b, compiled_b)\n4466:             self.assertTrue(torch.equal(out_eager, out_compiled))\n4467: \n4468:             # func1 hits a leaf Variable that requires grad is being used in an in-place operation\n4469:             if requires_grad:\n4470:                 bwd_inp_eager = torch.randn([6])\n4471: ",

{
  "endLine": 2950,

Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"2800:             #     in current graph (i.e. bound_symbols), it it's not bound, we'll create a placeholder\\n2801:             #     for it then recursively check its parent, creates ph if not bound.\\n2802:             #     Every tracer maintains a mapping (i.e. lifted_freevars)\\n2803:             #     that maps from parent proxy to proxy in current tracer for the symbol.\\n2804:             #  2. When create_graph_input for a tensor with unbacked symbolic shapes,\\n2805:             #     Backed symbols all come from inputs's symbolic shape. But unbacked symbols\\n2806:             #     can be created while tracing. So we use track_unbacked_symbols will intercept\\n2807:             #     at wrap_fx_proxy, and try to bind the unbacked symbols immediately after they're\\n2808:             #     created.\\n2809:             #  3. subgraph will also lifted basic symbols in compound exprs of tensor shape.\\n2810:             #     For example, if an input to subgraph takes size [s1+s2//8], we'll look for the\\n2811:             #     the free symbols in the sizes and lift as inputs similar to 1 in _lift_symbols_in_symint)\\n2812:             #  4. When create_graph_input for a SymInt, if the symint is a basic symbol, we'll track it\\n2813:             #     in bound_symbols so that we don't lift the same basic symbol twice. When the symint is a\\n2814:             #     compound expr, we'll just create the proxy for the compouned expr but not lift its basic symbols.\\n2815:             # Also see NOTE: [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\\n2816:             is_strict_export = self.is_export\\n2817:             is_non_strict_export = torch.compiler.is_compiling()\\n2818:             if not is_strict_export and not is_non_strict_export:\\n2819:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n2820:                     self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value, source)\\n2821:                 elif isinstance(example_value, (list, tuple)):\\n2822:                     for i, e in enumerate(example_value):\\n2823:                         if not isinstance(e, torch.Tensor):\\n2824:                             continue\\n2825: \\n2826:                         e_source = None\\n2827:                         if source:\\n2828:                             e_source = GetItemSource(\\n2829:                                 base=source, index=i, index_is_slice=False\\n2830:                             )\\n2831: \\n2832:                         self._lift_basic_symbols(e, e_source)\\n2833: \\n2834:             # Bound the symbol to ph if example_value is a SymInt with basic symbol.\\n2835:             if isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(\\n2836:                 example_value.node.expr, sympy.Symbol\\n2837:             ):\\n2838:                 self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr] = proxy\\n2839:             return proxy\\n2840: \\n2841:     # See NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling] for more details\\n2842:     def lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(\\n2843:         self, proxy: fx.Proxy\\n2844:     ) -> Union[LazyProxy, fx.Proxy]:\\n2845:         # You're doing something wrong if we are the root SubgraphTracer because\\n2846:         # Dynamo adds tensors to graph inputs before creating a proxy for them.\\n2847:         assert self.parent is not None, (\\n2848:             \\\"lift_tracked_freevar_to_input should not be called on root SubgraphTracer\\\"\\n2849:         )\\n2850: \\n2851:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\\\"example_value\\\"]\\n2852: \\n2853:         # To avoid lifting the same symbol twice, we check whether basic symbols has been tracked.\\n2854:         # For example, the basic symbols may have already been lifted for current subgraph when\\n2855:         # we automatically lift basic symbols in the sizes/strides of a tensor t.\\n2856:         # Suppose parent graph calls sz = t.size()[0], it creates\\n2857:         # a proxy in parent and the subgraph accesses sz via closure. sz's proxy is not tracked\\n2858:         # in current sub-tracer so we may lift the same symbol twice.\\n2859:         if (\\n2860:             isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt)\\n2861:             and example_value.node.expr in self.bound_symbols\\n2862:         ):\\n2863:             return self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr]\\n2864: \\n2865:         # Proxies are associated with VariableTracker.\\n2866:         # It is possible that we've already lifted the Proxy to be an input.\\n2867:         # If that is the case, just return the already lifted Proxy.\\n2868:         if proxy in self.lifted_freevars:\\n2869:             return self.lifted_freevars[proxy]\\n2870: \\n2871:         # We first lift proxy to parent's graph then lift to current grpah's input\\n2872:         # so that when we bind symints of the sizes in current graph, those symints\\n2873:         # would already be lifted as inputs to parent graph.\\n2874:         if proxy.tracer != self.parent:\\n2875:             self.parent.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(proxy)\\n2876: \\n2877:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\\\"example_value\\\"]\\n2878:         new_proxy = self.create_graph_input(\\n2879:             proxy.node.name, type(example_value), example_value\\n2880:         )\\n2881:         self.lifted_freevars[proxy] = new_proxy\\n2882:         return new_proxy\\n2883: \\n2884:     def maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(self, arg: Any) -> Any:\\n2885:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2886:         If arg is a free variable, then lift it to be an input.\\n2887:         Returns the new lifted arg (if arg was a freevar), else the\\n2888:         original arg.\\n2889:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2890:         if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Proxy):\\n2891:             # Note: arg can be a python built-in slice type e.g.\\n2892:             # x[:max_seq] is represented as get_item(t, (slice(None, max_seq, None)))\\n2893:             # we need to also look into the slice variable itself to lift the\\n2894:             # proxies there.\\n2895:             if isinstance(arg, slice):\\n2896:                 return slice(\\n2897:                     *(\\n2898:                         self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(sub_arg)\\n2899:                         for sub_arg in (arg.start, arg.stop, arg.step)\\n2900:                     )\\n2901:                 )\\n2902:             else:\\n2903:                 return arg\\n2904:         elif arg.tracer == self:\\n2905:             return arg\\n2906:         return self.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\\n2907: \\n2908:     # See NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input] for overall design\\n2909:     # You MUST call this API every time when creating a proxy in wrap_fx_proxy for a call\\n2910:     # that produced unbacked symints or tensors with unbacked symint shapes.\\n2911:     # This function is used to track the unbacked symints with its proxies created during\\n2912:     # dynamo tracing so that subgraph knows how to bind a symbol input with parent's proxy.\\n2913:     # LazyProxy are created for tensor shapes that're unbacked so that we don't create proxies\\n2914:     # for symbols that're not going to be used.\\n2915:     def track_unbacked_symbols(\\n2916:         self, example_value: Any, e_proxy: Union[LazyProxy, torch.fx.Proxy]\\n2917:     ) -> None:\\n2918:         # When binding the symbols in an exmaple_value, we bind the symbols\\n2919:         # to the proxy's associated Tracer instead of current tracer.\\n2920:         # This is because:\\n2921:         # 1. We may be calling wrap_tensors during speculate_subgraph because\\n2922:         # the variables are lazily realized. The proxy are top-level phs but\\n2923:         # current tracer is a subtracer.\\n2924:         # 2. For autograd.Function, we trace the backward graph with a new tracer\\n2925:         # whose parent is the forward tracer, but we're using all the proxies created\\n2926:         # in forward tracer to trace the backward.\\n2927:         # For example, forward calls save_for_backward for a input tensor t.\\n2928:         # Backward calls t.tolist(). In this case, all the proxies that backward tracer\\n2929:         # sees are from parent tracer (i.e. the forward tracer). (e.g. t[0].item())\\n2930:         # See test_validate_outputs_unbacked for repro on 2.\\n2931:         tracer = e_proxy.tracer\\n2932:         assert isinstance(tracer, SubgraphTracer)\\n2933: \\n2934:         def need_bind(s: Any) -> bool:\\n2935:             from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_symbolic\\n2936: \\n2937:             return (\\n2938:                 is_symbolic(s)\\n2939:                 and isinstance(s.node.expr, sympy.Symbol)\\n2940:                 and s.node.shape_env.is_unbacked_symint(s.node.expr)\\n2941:                 and s.node.expr not in self.bound_symbols\\n2942:             )\\n2943: \\n2944:         def _proxy_with_example_value(\\n2945:             example_value: Any, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any\\n2946:         ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n2947:             proxy = tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\\n2948:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\\n2949:             return proxy\\n2950: \\n2951: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2800-2950\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2800:             #     in current graph (i.e. bound_symbols), it it's not bound, we'll create a placeholder\n2801:             #     for it then recursively check its parent, creates ph if not bound.\n2802:             #     Every tracer maintains a mapping (i.e. lifted_freevars)\n2803:             #     that maps from parent proxy to proxy in current tracer for the symbol.\n2804:             #  2. When create_graph_input for a tensor with unbacked symbolic shapes,\n2805:             #     Backed symbols all come from inputs's symbolic shape. But unbacked symbols\n2806:             #     can be created while tracing. So we use track_unbacked_symbols will intercept\n2807:             #     at wrap_fx_proxy, and try to bind the unbacked symbols immediately after they're\n2808:             #     created.\n2809:             #  3. subgraph will also lifted basic symbols in compound exprs of tensor shape.\n2810:             #     For example, if an input to subgraph takes size [s1+s2//8], we'll look for the\n2811:             #     the free symbols in the sizes and lift as inputs similar to 1 in _lift_symbols_in_symint)\n2812:             #  4. When create_graph_input for a SymInt, if the symint is a basic symbol, we'll track it\n2813:             #     in bound_symbols so that we don't lift the same basic symbol twice. When the symint is a\n2814:             #     compound expr, we'll just create the proxy for the compouned expr but not lift its basic symbols.\n2815:             # Also see NOTE: [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\n2816:             is_strict_export = self.is_export\n2817:             is_non_strict_export = torch.compiler.is_compiling()\n2818:             if not is_strict_export and not is_non_strict_export:\n2819:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n2820:                     self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value, source)\n2821:                 elif isinstance(example_value, (list, tuple)):\n2822:                     for i, e in enumerate(example_value):\n2823:                         if not isinstance(e, torch.Tensor):\n2824:                             continue\n2825: \n2826:                         e_source = None\n2827:                         if source:\n2828:                             e_source = GetItemSource(\n2829:                                 base=source, index=i, index_is_slice=False\n2830:                             )\n2831: \n2832:                         self._lift_basic_symbols(e, e_source)\n2833: \n2834:             # Bound the symbol to ph if example_value is a SymInt with basic symbol.\n2835:             if isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(\n2836:                 example_value.node.expr, sympy.Symbol\n2837:             ):\n2838:                 self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr] = proxy\n2839:             return proxy\n2840: \n2841:     # See NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling] for more details\n2842:     def lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(\n2843:         self, proxy: fx.Proxy\n2844:     ) -> Union[LazyProxy, fx.Proxy]:\n2845:         # You're doing something wrong if we are the root SubgraphTracer because\n2846:         # Dynamo adds tensors to graph inputs before creating a proxy for them.\n2847:         assert self.parent is not None, (\n2848:             \"lift_tracked_freevar_to_input should not be called on root SubgraphTracer\"\n2849:         )\n2850: \n2851:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\"example_value\"]\n2852: \n2853:         # To avoid lifting the same symbol twice, we check whether basic symbols has been tracked.\n2854:         # For example, the basic symbols may have already been lifted for current subgraph when\n2855:         # we automatically lift basic symbols in the sizes/strides of a tensor t.\n2856:         # Suppose parent graph calls sz = t.size()[0], it creates\n2857:         # a proxy in parent and the subgraph accesses sz via closure. sz's proxy is not tracked\n2858:         # in current sub-tracer so we may lift the same symbol twice.\n2859:         if (\n2860:             isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt)\n2861:             and example_value.node.expr in self.bound_symbols\n2862:         ):\n2863:             return self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr]\n2864: \n2865:         # Proxies are associated with VariableTracker.\n2866:         # It is possible that we've already lifted the Proxy to be an input.\n2867:         # If that is the case, just return the already lifted Proxy.\n2868:         if proxy in self.lifted_freevars:\n2869:             return self.lifted_freevars[proxy]\n2870: \n2871:         # We first lift proxy to parent's graph then lift to current grpah's input\n2872:         # so that when we bind symints of the sizes in current graph, those symints\n2873:         # would already be lifted as inputs to parent graph.\n2874:         if proxy.tracer != self.parent:\n2875:             self.parent.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(proxy)\n2876: \n2877:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\"example_value\"]\n2878:         new_proxy = self.create_graph_input(\n2879:             proxy.node.name, type(example_value), example_value\n2880:         )\n2881:         self.lifted_freevars[proxy] = new_proxy\n2882:         return new_proxy\n2883: \n2884:     def maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(self, arg: Any) -> Any:\n2885:         \"\"\"\n2886:         If arg is a free variable, then lift it to be an input.\n2887:         Returns the new lifted arg (if arg was a freevar), else the\n2888:         original arg.\n2889:         \"\"\"\n2890:         if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Proxy):\n2891:             # Note: arg can be a python built-in slice type e.g.\n2892:             # x[:max_seq] is represented as get_item(t, (slice(None, max_seq, None)))\n2893:             # we need to also look into the slice variable itself to lift the\n2894:             # proxies there.\n2895:             if isinstance(arg, slice):\n2896:                 return slice(\n2897:                     *(\n2898:                         self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(sub_arg)\n2899:                         for sub_arg in (arg.start, arg.stop, arg.step)\n2900:                     )\n2901:                 )\n2902:             else:\n2903:                 return arg\n2904:         elif arg.tracer == self:\n2905:             return arg\n2906:         return self.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\n2907: \n2908:     # See NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input] for overall design\n2909:     # You MUST call this API every time when creating a proxy in wrap_fx_proxy for a call\n2910:     # that produced unbacked symints or tensors with unbacked symint shapes.\n2911:     # This function is used to track the unbacked symints with its proxies created during\n2912:     # dynamo tracing so that subgraph knows how to bind a symbol input with parent's proxy.\n2913:     # LazyProxy are created for tensor shapes that're unbacked so that we don't create proxies\n2914:     # for symbols that're not going to be used.\n2915:     def track_unbacked_symbols(\n2916:         self, example_value: Any, e_proxy: Union[LazyProxy, torch.fx.Proxy]\n2917:     ) -> None:\n2918:         # When binding the symbols in an exmaple_value, we bind the symbols\n2919:         # to the proxy's associated Tracer instead of current tracer.\n2920:         # This is because:\n2921:         # 1. We may be calling wrap_tensors during speculate_subgraph because\n2922:         # the variables are lazily realized. The proxy are top-level phs but\n2923:         # current tracer is a subtracer.\n2924:         # 2. For autograd.Function, we trace the backward graph with a new tracer\n2925:         # whose parent is the forward tracer, but we're using all the proxies created\n2926:         # in forward tracer to trace the backward.\n2927:         # For example, forward calls save_for_backward for a input tensor t.\n2928:         # Backward calls t.tolist(). In this case, all the proxies that backward tracer\n2929:         # sees are from parent tracer (i.e. the forward tracer). (e.g. t[0].item())\n2930:         # See test_validate_outputs_unbacked for repro on 2.\n2931:         tracer = e_proxy.tracer\n2932:         assert isinstance(tracer, SubgraphTracer)\n2933: \n2934:         def need_bind(s: Any) -> bool:\n2935:             from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_symbolic\n2936: \n2937:             return (\n2938:                 is_symbolic(s)\n2939:                 and isinstance(s.node.expr, sympy.Symbol)\n2940:                 and s.node.shape_env.is_unbacked_symint(s.node.expr)\n2941:                 and s.node.expr not in self.bound_symbols\n2942:             )\n2943: \n2944:         def _proxy_with_example_value(\n2945:             example_value: Any, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any\n2946:         ) -> fx.Proxy:\n2947:             proxy = tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\n2948:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\n2949:             return proxy\n2950: \n2951: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2800-2950"
  }
}
