161. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Owner(s): [\"module: dynamo\"]\n\"\"\"\nTests for cleanup of reference cycles after failed dynamo traces.\n\nWhen dynamo tracing fails, reference cycles in the graph nodes and\nFakeTensor/ShapeEnv can prevent immediate garbage collection.\nThe cleanup_failed_trace() method on OutputGraph breaks these cycles\nexplicitly so that GC does not need a full collection pass.\n\nThe following tests validate the cleanup behavior:\n- cleanup_graph_on_failure: graph node doubly-linked-list cycle is broken\n- cleanup_fake_tensor_refs: FakeTensorMode cycle is broken via tracked_fakes\n- cleanup_shape_env_refs: ShapeEnv->TrackedFake references are cleared\n- gc_not_needed_after_cleanup: broken cycles allow immediate GC w/o gc.collect\n- repeated_failure_no_leak: no accumulation of leaked objects across failures\n\"\"\"\n\nimport gc\nimport weakref\n\nimport torch\nimport torch._dynamo\nfrom torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph\nfrom torch._dynamo.test_case import TestCase\nfrom torch.testing._internal.common_utils import instantiate_parametrized_tests, parametrize\n\n\nclass _ForcedTracingError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Sentinel exception used to force a tracing failure for testing.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef _run_tracing_failure():\n    \"\"\"\n    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return captured state from\n    the OutputGraph at the time cleanup_failed_trace() is called.\n\n    This function:\n    1. Patches OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace to capture pre/post state.\n    2. Patches InstructionTranslator.run to raise a forced exception.\n    3. Calls torch.compile to trigger the failure path in convert_frame.py.\n\n    If convert_frame.py has been fixed to call cleanup_failed_trace() in its\n    except-Exception block, the patched method will be called and `captured`\n    will be populated.  If convert_frame.py has NOT been fixed, `captured`\n    will remain empty.\n\n    Returns the `captured` dict (possibly empty if the fix is absent).\n    \"\"\"\n    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslator\n\n    captured: dict = {}\n\n    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace\n\n    def patched_cleanup(self):\n        # Record a weakref to the output graph itself.\n        captured[\"og_ref\"] = weakref.ref(self)\n\n        # Snapshot graph nodes before cleanup.\n        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\n        captured[\"nodes\"] = nodes\n        captured[\"node_refs\"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]\n\n        # Snapshot tracked_fakes list (same object as ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes).\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes\"] = self.tracked_fakes\n\n        # Call the real implementation.\n        original_cleanup(self)\n\n        # Record post-cleanup state.\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes_len_after\"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)\n        captured[\"node_prev_after\"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"node_next_after\"] = [n._next for n in nodes]\n\n    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run\n\n    def failing_run(self):\n        raise _ForcedTracingError(\"forced tracing failure for test\")\n\n    # Apply patches.\n    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup\n    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run\n    try:\n        def fn(x):\n            return x.sin()\n\n        try:\n            torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.randn(4))\n        except _ForcedTracingError:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup\n        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run\n\n    return captured\n\n\n@instantiate_parametrized_tests\nclass TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):\n    @parametrize(\n        \"scenario\",\n        [\n            \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\",\n            \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\",\n            \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\",\n            \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\",\n            \"repeated_failure_no_leak\",\n        ],\n    )\n    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):\n        \"\"\"\n        Tests that cleanup_failed_trace() is called during a tracing failure\n        and correctly breaks reference cycles.\n\n        These tests fail when:\n        - The fix to convert_frame.py (calling cleanup_failed_trace in the\n          except-Exception block) has not been applied, OR\n        - The cleanup_failed_trace method does not perform the expected cleanup.\n        \"\"\"\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n        captured = _run_tracing_failure()\n\n        # Primary assertion: cleanup_failed_trace MUST have been called.\n        # Before the fix, convert_frame.py does not call cleanup_failed_trace()\n        # in its except-Exception block, so `captured` will be empty and this\n        # assertion will fail.\n        self.assertIn(\n            \"og_ref\",\n            captured,\n            \"cleanup_failed_trace() was not called during the tracing failure. \"\n            \"Ensure convert_frame.py calls tracer.output.cleanup_failed_trace() \"\n            \"in its except Exception block inside transform().\",\n        )\n\n        if scenario == \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace, graph nodes must have their doubly-linked\n            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None.  This breaks the cycle:\n            #   Graph._root <-> Node1 <-> Node2 <-> ... <-> Graph._root\n            # that would otherwise prevent immediate GC of the nodes.\n            prev_after = captured.get(\"node_prev_after\", [])\n            next_after = captured.get(\"node_next_after\", [])\n            for prev in prev_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    prev,\n                    \"Node._prev must be None after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n                )\n            for nxt in next_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    nxt,\n                    \"Node._next must be None after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace, tracked_fakes must be empty.\n            # This breaks the cycle:\n            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor\n            #                                                -> FakeTensorMode\n            self.assertEqual(\n                captured.get(\"tracked_fakes_len_after\", -1),\n                0,\n                \"tracked_fakes must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace \"\n                \"to break the FakeTensorMode reference cycle\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\":\n            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the SAME list object as\n            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes.  Clearing it in cleanup_failed_trace\n            # therefore also clears the ShapeEnv's reference to TrackedFakes.\n            tracked_fakes = captured.get(\"tracked_fakes\")\n            self.assertIsNotNone(\n                tracked_fakes,\n                \"tracked_fakes must have been captured\",\n            )\n            # After cleanup, the list (shared with ShapeEnv) must be empty.\n            self.assertEqual(\n                len(tracked_fakes),\n                0,\n                \"ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes (same list as OutputGraph.tracked_fakes) \"\n                \"must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks the reference cycles, objects\n            # should be collectable by reference counting alone \u2014 no full\n            # gc.collect() pass needed.\n            nodes = captured.get(\"nodes\", [])\n            node_refs = captured.get(\"node_refs\", [])\n            # Drop all remaining strong references to the nodes.\n            del nodes\n            captured.pop(\"nodes\", None)\n            captured.pop(\"node_refs\", None)\n            # With cycles broken, reference counting should collect them\n            # immediately.  We assert they are gone without calling gc.collect().\n            for ref in node_refs:\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    ref(),\n                    \"Graph nodes must be immediately collectable (no gc.collect \"\n                    \"needed) after cleanup_failed_trace breaks their cycles\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"repeated_failure_no_leak\":\n            # Multiple tracing failures must not accumulate leaked objects.\n            for _ in range(3):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n                _run_tracing_failure()\n            # Force a full collection and verify nothing is in gc.garbage.\n            gc.collect()\n            self.assertEqual(\n                len(gc.garbage),\n                0,\n                \"No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated \"\n                \"tracing failures\",\n            )\n\n\ninstantiate_parametrized_tests(TestGraphCleanup)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests\n\n    run_tests()\n",
  "file_path": "/workspace/test/dynamo/test_graph_cleanup.py"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Owner(s): [\"module: dynamo\"]\n\"\"\"\nTests for cleanup of reference cycles after failed dynamo traces.\n\nWhen dynamo tracing fails, reference cycles in the graph nodes and\nFakeTensor/ShapeEnv can prevent immediate garbage collection.\nThe cleanup_failed_trace() method on OutputGraph breaks these cycles\nexplicitly so that GC does not need a full collection pass.\n\nThe following tests validate the cleanup behavior:\n- cleanup_graph_on_failure: graph node doubly-linked-list cycle is broken\n- cleanup_fake_tensor_refs: FakeTensorMode cycle is broken via tracked_fakes\n- cleanup_shape_env_refs: ShapeEnv->TrackedFake references are cleared\n- gc_not_needed_after_cleanup: broken cycles allow immediate GC w/o gc.collect\n- repeated_failure_no_leak: no accumulation of leaked objects across failures\n\"\"\"\n\nimport gc\nimport weakref\n\nimport torch\nimport torch._dynamo\nfrom torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph\nfrom torch._dynamo.test_case import TestCase\nfrom torch.testing._internal.common_utils import instantiate_parametrized_tests, parametrize\n\n\nclass _ForcedTracingError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Sentinel exception used to force a tracing failure for testing.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef _run_tracing_failure():\n    \"\"\"\n    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return captured state from\n    the OutputGraph at the time cleanup_failed_trace() is called.\n\n    This function:\n    1. Patches OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace to capture pre/post state.\n    2. Patches InstructionTranslator.run to raise a forced exception.\n    3. Calls torch.compile to trigger the failure path in convert_frame.py.\n\n    If convert_frame.py has been fixed to call cleanup_failed_trace() in its\n    except-Exception block, the patched method will be called and `captured`\n    will be populated.  If convert_frame.py has NOT been fixed, `captured`\n    will remain empty.\n\n    Returns the `captured` dict (possibly empty if the fix is absent).\n    \"\"\"\n    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslator\n\n    captured: dict = {}\n\n    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace\n\n    def patched_cleanup(self):\n        # Record a weakref to the output graph itself.\n        captured[\"og_ref\"] = weakref.ref(self)\n\n        # Snapshot graph nodes before cleanup.\n        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\n        captured[\"nodes\"] = nodes\n        captured[\"node_refs\"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]\n\n        # Snapshot tracked_fakes list (same object as ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes).\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes\"] = self.tracked_fakes\n\n        # Call the real implementation.\n        original_cleanup(self)\n\n        # Record post-cleanup state.\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes_len_after\"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)\n        captured[\"node_prev_after\"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"node_next_after\"] = [n._next for n in nodes]\n\n    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run\n\n    def failing_run(self):\n        raise _ForcedTracingError(\"forced tracing failure for test\")\n\n    # Apply patches.\n    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup\n    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run\n    try:\n        def fn(x):\n            return x.sin()\n\n        try:\n            torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.randn(4))\n        except _ForcedTracingError:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup\n        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run\n\n    return captured\n\n\n@instantiate_parametrized_tests\nclass TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):\n    @parametrize(\n        \"scenario\",\n        [\n            \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\",\n            \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\",\n            \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\",\n            \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\",\n            \"repeated_failure_no_leak\",\n        ],\n    )\n    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):\n        \"\"\"\n        Tests that cleanup_failed_trace() is called during a tracing failure\n        and correctly breaks reference cycles.\n\n        These tests fail when:\n        - The fix to convert_frame.py (calling cleanup_failed_trace in the\n          except-Exception block) has not been applied, OR\n        - The cleanup_failed_trace method does not perform the expected cleanup.\n        \"\"\"\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n        captured = _run_tracing_failure()\n\n        # Primary assertion: cleanup_failed_trace MUST have been called.\n        # Before the fix, convert_frame.py does not call cleanup_failed_trace()\n        # in its except-Exception block, so `captured` will be empty and this\n        # assertion will fail.\n        self.assertIn(\n            \"og_ref\",\n            captured,\n            \"cleanup_failed_trace() was not called during the tracing failure. \"\n            \"Ensure convert_frame.py calls tracer.output.cleanup_failed_trace() \"\n            \"in its except Exception block inside transform().\",\n        )\n\n        if scenario == \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace, graph nodes must have their doubly-linked\n            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None.  This breaks the cycle:\n            #   Graph._root <-> Node1 <-> Node2 <-> ... <-> Graph._root\n            # that would otherwise prevent immediate GC of the nodes.\n            prev_after = captured.get(\"node_prev_after\", [])\n            next_after = captured.get(\"node_next_after\", [])\n            for prev in prev_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    prev,\n                    \"Node._prev must be None after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n                )\n            for nxt in next_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    nxt,\n                    \"Node._next must be None after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace, tracked_fakes must be empty.\n            # This breaks the cycle:\n            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor\n            #                                                -> FakeTensorMode\n            self.assertEqual(\n                captured.get(\"tracked_fakes_len_after\", -1),\n                0,\n                \"tracked_fakes must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace \"\n                \"to break the FakeTensorMode reference cycle\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\":\n            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the SAME list object as\n            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes.  Clearing it in cleanup_failed_trace\n            # therefore also clears the ShapeEnv's reference to TrackedFakes.\n            tracked_fakes = captured.get(\"tracked_fakes\")\n            self.assertIsNotNone(\n                tracked_fakes,\n                \"tracked_fakes must have been captured\",\n            )\n            # After cleanup, the list (shared with ShapeEnv) must be empty.\n            self.assertEqual(\n                len(tracked_fakes),\n                0,\n                \"ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes (same list as OutputGraph.tracked_fakes) \"\n                \"must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks the reference cycles, objects\n            # should be collectable by reference counting alone \u2014 no full\n            # gc.collect() pass needed.\n            nodes = captured.get(\"nodes\", [])\n            node_refs = captured.get(\"node_refs\", [])\n            # Drop all remaining strong references to the nodes.\n            del nodes\n            captured.pop(\"nodes\", None)\n            captured.pop(\"node_refs\", None)\n            # With cycles broken, reference counting should collect them\n            # immediately.  We assert they are gone without calling gc.collect().\n            for ref in node_refs:\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    ref(),\n                    \"Graph nodes must be immediately collectable (no gc.collect \"\n                    \"needed) after cleanup_failed_trace breaks their cycles\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"repeated_failure_no_leak\":\n            # Multiple tracing failures must not accumulate leaked objects.\n            for _ in range(3):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n                _run_tracing_failure()\n            # Force a full collection and verify nothing is in gc.garbage.\n            gc.collect()\n            self.assertEqual(\n                len(gc.garbage),\n                0,\n                \"No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated \"\n                \"tracing failures\",\n            )\n\n\ninstantiate_parametrized_tests(TestGraphCleanup)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests\n\n    run_tests()\n",
  "filePath": "/workspace/test/dynamo/test_graph_cleanup.py",
  "originalFile": "# Owner(s): [\"module: dynamo\"]\n\"\"\"\nTests for cleanup of reference cycles after failed dynamo traces.\n\nWhen dynamo tracing fails, reference cycles in the graph nodes and\nFakeTensor/ShapeEnv can prevent immediate garbage collection.\nThe cleanup_failed_trace() method on OutputGraph breaks these cycles\nexplicitly so that GC does not need a full collection pass.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport gc\nimport sys\nimport weakref\n\nimport torch\nimport torch._dynamo\nimport torch.fx as fx\nfrom torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph\nfrom torch._dynamo.test_case import TestCase\nfrom torch.testing._internal.common_utils import instantiate_parametrized_tests, parametrize\n\n\nclass _ForcedTracingError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Sentinel exception used to force a tracing failure for testing.\"\"\"\n\n\ndef _run_tracing_failure():\n    \"\"\"\n    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return a weakref to the OutputGraph\n    so we can verify that reference cycles are broken.\n\n    We patch InstructionTranslator.run to fail after the OutputGraph is\n    set up (so tracked_fakes may be populated), then capture the OutputGraph\n    via a patched cleanup_failed_trace.\n    \"\"\"\n    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslator\n\n    captured = {}\n\n    # These assignments will raise AttributeError before the fix is applied,\n    # which is intentional: the tests should fail before the fix.\n    original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace\n\n    def patched_cleanup(self):\n        # Record weakrefs to key objects before cleanup\n        captured[\"og_ref\"] = weakref.ref(self)\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes\"] = self.tracked_fakes\n        captured[\"graph\"] = self.graph\n        # Record node refs and their _prev/_next before cleanup\n        nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\n        captured[\"node_refs\"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"nodes\"] = nodes\n        # Call the actual cleanup\n        original_cleanup(self)\n        # Record state after cleanup\n        captured[\"tracked_fakes_len_after\"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)\n        captured[\"node_prev_after\"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]\n        captured[\"node_next_after\"] = [n._next for n in nodes]\n\n    original_run = InstructionTranslator.run\n\n    def failing_run(self):\n        raise _ForcedTracingError(\"forced failure for test\")\n\n    OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup\n    InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run\n    try:\n        try:\n            def fn(x):\n                return x.sin()\n\n            torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.randn(4))\n        except _ForcedTracingError:\n            pass\n    finally:\n        OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = original_cleanup\n        InstructionTranslator.run = original_run\n\n    return captured\n\n\n@instantiate_parametrized_tests\nclass TestGraphCleanup(TestCase):\n    @parametrize(\n        \"scenario\",\n        [\n            \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\",\n            \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\",\n            \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\",\n            \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\",\n            \"repeated_failure_no_leak\",\n        ],\n    )\n    def test_parametrization(self, scenario):\n        \"\"\"\n        Test that cleanup_failed_trace() correctly breaks reference cycles\n        that would otherwise prevent garbage collection after a failed trace.\n        \"\"\"\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n        # This will raise AttributeError if cleanup_failed_trace does not exist\n        # (i.e., before the fix is applied), causing the test to fail.\n        captured = _run_tracing_failure()\n\n        if scenario == \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\":\n            # After cleanup, all graph nodes should have their doubly-linked\n            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None, breaking the cycle:\n            #   Graph._root -> Node -> ... -> Graph._root\n            prev_after = captured.get(\"node_prev_after\", [])\n            next_after = captured.get(\"node_next_after\", [])\n            for prev in prev_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(prev)\n            for nxt in next_after:\n                self.assertIsNone(nxt)\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\":\n            # After cleanup, tracked_fakes must be empty, breaking the cycle:\n            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode\n            self.assertEqual(\n                captured.get(\"tracked_fakes_len_after\", -1),\n                0,\n                \"tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup_failed_trace\",\n            )\n\n        elif scenario == \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\":\n            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the same list object as\n            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes, so clearing it clears the ShapeEnv\n            # references too.\n            tracked_fakes = captured.get(\"tracked_fakes\")\n            if tracked_fakes is not None:\n                # The list should be empty after cleanup (it was cleared in-place)\n                self.assertEqual(\n                    len(tracked_fakes),\n                    0,\n                    \"ShapeEnv tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\":\n            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks reference cycles, objects\n            # should be collectable by reference counting alone (no gc.collect()\n            # needed). We verify this by checking that nodes with no other\n            # strong references are dead even without gc.collect().\n            nodes = captured.get(\"nodes\", [])\n            node_refs = captured.get(\"node_refs\", [])\n            # Drop strong references to the nodes\n            del nodes\n            captured.pop(\"nodes\", None)\n            captured.pop(\"node_refs\", None)\n            # Without gc.collect(), reference-counted cleanup should suffice\n            # because the cycles were broken by cleanup_failed_trace.\n            for ref in node_refs:\n                # If cleanup_failed_trace broke the cycle, ref counting\n                # means ref() should already be None.\n                # (We allow gc.collect here only as a fallback check)\n                self.assertIsNone(\n                    ref(),\n                    \"Graph nodes should be collectable without a full GC pass\",\n                )\n\n        elif scenario == \"repeated_failure_no_leak\":\n            # Multiple tracing failures should not accumulate leaked objects.\n            for _ in range(3):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n                _run_tracing_failure()\n            # Verify no uncollectable garbage accumulated\n            gc.collect()\n            self.assertEqual(\n                len(gc.garbage),\n                0,\n                \"No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated failures\",\n            )\n\n\ninstantiate_parametrized_tests(TestGraphCleanup)\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    from torch._dynamo.test_case import run_tests\n\n    run_tests()\n",
  "structuredPatch": [
    {
      "lines": [
        " FakeTensor/ShapeEnv can prevent immediate garbage collection.",
        " The cleanup_failed_trace() method on OutputGraph breaks these cycles",
        " explicitly so that GC does not need a full collection pass.",
        "+",
        "+The following tests validate the cleanup behavior:",
        "+- cleanup_graph_on_failure: graph node doubly-linked-list cycle is broken",
        "+- cleanup_fake_tensor_refs: FakeTensorMode cycle is broken via tracked_fakes",
        "+- cleanup_shape_env_refs: ShapeEnv->TrackedFake references are cleared",
        "+- gc_not_needed_after_cleanup: broken cycles allow immediate GC w/o gc.collect",
        "+- repeated_failure_no_leak: no accumulation of leaked objects across failures",
        " \"\"\"",
        " ",
        " import gc",
        "-import sys",
        " import weakref",
        " ",
        " import torch",
        " import torch._dynamo",
        "-import torch.fx as fx",
        " from torch._dynamo.output_graph import OutputGraph",
        " from torch._dynamo.test_case import TestCase",
        " from torch.testing._internal.common_utils import instantiate_parametrized_tests, parametrize"
      ],
      "newLines": 20,
      "newStart": 6,
      "oldLines": 15,
      "oldStart": 6
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        " ",
        " def _run_tracing_failure():",
        "     \"\"\"",
        "-    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return a weakref to the OutputGraph",
        "-    so we can verify that reference cycles are broken.",
        "+    Force a dynamo tracing failure and return captured state from",
        "+    the OutputGraph at the time cleanup_failed_trace() is called.",
        " ",
        "-    We patch InstructionTranslator.run to fail after the OutputGraph is",
        "-    set up (so tracked_fakes may be populated), then capture the OutputGraph",
        "-    via a patched cleanup_failed_trace.",
        "+    This function:",
        "+    1. Patches OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace to capture pre/post state.",
        "+    2. Patches InstructionTranslator.run to raise a forced exception.",
        "+    3. Calls torch.compile to trigger the failure path in convert_frame.py.",
        "+",
        "+    If convert_frame.py has been fixed to call cleanup_failed_trace() in its",
        "+    except-Exception block, the patched method will be called and `captured`",
        "+    will be populated.  If convert_frame.py has NOT been fixed, `captured`",
        "+    will remain empty.",
        "+",
        "+    Returns the `captured` dict (possibly empty if the fix is absent).",
        "     \"\"\"",
        "     from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslator",
        " ",
        "-    captured = {}",
        "+    captured: dict = {}",
        " ",
        "-    # These assignments will raise AttributeError before the fix is applied,",
        "-    # which is intentional: the tests should fail before the fix.",
        "     original_cleanup = OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace",
        " ",
        "     def patched_cleanup(self):",
        "-        # Record weakrefs to key objects before cleanup",
        "+        # Record a weakref to the output graph itself.",
        "         captured[\"og_ref\"] = weakref.ref(self)",
        "-        captured[\"tracked_fakes\"] = self.tracked_fakes",
        "-        captured[\"graph\"] = self.graph",
        "-        # Record node refs and their _prev/_next before cleanup",
        "+",
        "+        # Snapshot graph nodes before cleanup.",
        "         nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)",
        "-        captured[\"node_refs\"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]",
        "         captured[\"nodes\"] = nodes",
        "-        # Call the actual cleanup",
        "+        captured[\"node_refs\"] = [weakref.ref(n) for n in nodes]",
        "+",
        "+        # Snapshot tracked_fakes list (same object as ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes).",
        "+        captured[\"tracked_fakes\"] = self.tracked_fakes",
        "+",
        "+        # Call the real implementation.",
        "         original_cleanup(self)",
        "-        # Record state after cleanup",
        "+",
        "+        # Record post-cleanup state.",
        "         captured[\"tracked_fakes_len_after\"] = len(self.tracked_fakes)",
        "         captured[\"node_prev_after\"] = [n._prev for n in nodes]",
        "         captured[\"node_next_after\"] = [n._next for n in nodes]"
      ],
      "newLines": 43,
      "newStart": 31,
      "oldLines": 33,
      "oldStart": 26
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        "     original_run = InstructionTranslator.run",
        " ",
        "     def failing_run(self):",
        "-        raise _ForcedTracingError(\"forced failure for test\")",
        "+        raise _ForcedTracingError(\"forced tracing failure for test\")",
        " ",
        "+    # Apply patches.",
        "     OutputGraph.cleanup_failed_trace = patched_cleanup",
        "     InstructionTranslator.run = failing_run",
        "     try:",
        "-        try:",
        "-            def fn(x):",
        "-                return x.sin()",
        "+        def fn(x):",
        "+            return x.sin()",
        " ",
        "+        try:",
        "             torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")(torch.randn(4))",
        "         except _ForcedTracingError:",
        "             pass"
      ],
      "newLines": 16,
      "newStart": 75,
      "oldLines": 15,
      "oldStart": 60
    },
    {
      "lines": [
        "     )",
        "     def test_parametrization(self, scenario):",
        "         \"\"\"",
        "-        Test that cleanup_failed_trace() correctly breaks reference cycles",
        "-        that would otherwise prevent garbage collection after a failed trace.",
        "+        Tests that cleanup_failed_trace() is called during a tracing failure",
        "+        and correctly breaks reference cycles.",
        "+",
        "+        These tests fail when:",
        "+        - The fix to convert_frame.py (calling cleanup_failed_trace in the",
        "+          except-Exception block) has not been applied, OR",
        "+        - The cleanup_failed_trace method does not perform the expected cleanup.",
        "         \"\"\"",
        "         torch._dynamo.reset()",
        " ",
        "-        # This will raise AttributeError if cleanup_failed_trace does not exist",
        "-        # (i.e., before the fix is applied), causing the test to fail.",
        "         captured = _run_tracing_failure()",
        " ",
        "+        # Primary assertion: cleanup_failed_trace MUST have been called.",
        "+        # Before the fix, convert_frame.py does not call cleanup_failed_trace()",
        "+        # in its except-Exception block, so `captured` will be empty and this",
        "+        # assertion will fail.",
        "+        self.assertIn(",
        "+            \"og_ref\",",
        "+            captured,",
        "+            \"cleanup_failed_trace() was not called during the tracing failure. \"",
        "+            \"Ensure convert_frame.py calls tracer.output.cleanup_failed_trace() \"",
        "+            \"in its except Exception block inside transform().\",",
        "+        )",
        "+",
        "         if scenario == \"cleanup_graph_on_failure\":",
        "-            # After cleanup, all graph nodes should have their doubly-linked",
        "-            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None, breaking the cycle:",
        "-            #   Graph._root -> Node -> ... -> Graph._root",
        "+            # After cleanup_failed_trace, graph nodes must have their doubly-linked",
        "+            # list pointers (_prev, _next) set to None.  This breaks the cycle:",
        "+            #   Graph._root <-> Node1 <-> Node2 <-> ... <-> Graph._root",
        "+            # that would otherwise prevent immediate GC of the nodes.",
        "             prev_after = captured.get(\"node_prev_after\", [])",
        "             next_after = captured.get(\"node_next_after\", [])",
        "             for prev in prev_after:",
        "-                self.assertIsNone(prev)",
        "+                self.assertIsNone(",
        "+                    prev,",
        "+                    \"Node._prev must be None after cleanup_failed_trace\",",
        "+                )",
        "             for nxt in next_after:",
        "-                self.assertIsNone(nxt)",
        "+                self.assertIsNone(",
        "+                    nxt,",
        "+                    \"Node._next must be None after cleanup_failed_trace\",",
        "+                )",
        " ",
        "         elif scenario == \"cleanup_fake_tensor_refs\":",
        "-            # After cleanup, tracked_fakes must be empty, breaking the cycle:",
        "-            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor -> FakeTensorMode",
        "+            # After cleanup_failed_trace, tracked_fakes must be empty.",
        "+            # This breaks the cycle:",
        "+            #   FakeTensorMode -> ShapeEnv -> TrackedFake -> FakeTensor",
        "+            #                                                -> FakeTensorMode",
        "             self.assertEqual(",
        "                 captured.get(\"tracked_fakes_len_after\", -1),",
        "                 0,",
        "-                \"tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup_failed_trace\",",
        "+                \"tracked_fakes must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace \"",
        "+                \"to break the FakeTensorMode reference cycle\",",
        "             )",
        " ",
        "         elif scenario == \"cleanup_shape_env_refs\":",
        "-            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the same list object as",
        "-            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes, so clearing it clears the ShapeEnv",
        "-            # references too.",
        "+            # ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes is the SAME list object as",
        "+            # OutputGraph.tracked_fakes.  Clearing it in cleanup_failed_trace",
        "+            # therefore also clears the ShapeEnv's reference to TrackedFakes.",
        "             tracked_fakes = captured.get(\"tracked_fakes\")",
        "-            if tracked_fakes is not None:",
        "-                # The list should be empty after cleanup (it was cleared in-place)",
        "-                self.assertEqual(",
        "-                    len(tracked_fakes),",
        "-                    0,",
        "-                    \"ShapeEnv tracked_fakes should be empty after cleanup\",",
        "-                )",
        "+            self.assertIsNotNone(",
        "+                tracked_fakes,",
        "+                \"tracked_fakes must have been captured\",",
        "+            )",
        "+            # After cleanup, the list (shared with ShapeEnv) must be empty.",
        "+            self.assertEqual(",
        "+                len(tracked_fakes),",
        "+                0,",
        "+                \"ShapeEnv.tracked_fakes (same list as OutputGraph.tracked_fakes) \"",
        "+                \"must be empty after cleanup_failed_trace\",",
        "+            )",
        " ",
        "         elif scenario == \"gc_not_needed_after_cleanup\":",
        "-            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks reference cycles, objects",
        "-            # should be collectable by reference counting alone (no gc.collect()",
        "-            # needed). We verify this by checking that nodes with no other",
        "-            # strong references are dead even without gc.collect().",
        "+            # After cleanup_failed_trace breaks the reference cycles, objects",
        "+            # should be collectable by reference counting alone \u2014 no full",
        "+            # gc.collect() pass needed.",
        "             nodes = captured.get(\"nodes\", [])",
        "             node_refs = captured.get(\"node_refs\", [])",
        "-            # Drop strong references to the nodes",
        "+            # Drop all remaining strong references to the nodes.",
        "             del nodes",
        "             captured.pop(\"nodes\", None)",
        "             captured.pop(\"node_refs\", None)",
        "-            # Without gc.collect(), reference-counted cleanup should suffice",
        "-            # because the cycles were broken by cleanup_failed_trace.",
        "+            # With cycles broken, reference counting should collect them",
        "+            # immediately.  We assert they are gone without calling gc.collect().",
        "             for ref in node_refs:",
        "-                # If cleanup_failed_trace broke the cycle, ref counting",
        "-                # means ref() should already be None.",
        "-                # (We allow gc.collect here only as a fallback check)",
        "                 self.assertIsNone(",
        "                     ref(),",
        "-                    \"Graph nodes should be collectable without a full GC pass\",",
        "+                    \"Graph nodes must be immediately collectable (no gc.collect \"",
        "+                    \"needed) after cleanup_failed_trace breaks their cycles\",",
        "                 )",
        " ",
        "         elif scenario == \"repeated_failure_no_leak\":",
        "-            # Multiple tracing failures should not accumulate leaked objects.",
        "+            # Multiple tracing failures must not accumulate leaked objects.",
        "             for _ in range(3):",
        "                 torch._dynamo.reset()",
        "                 _run_tracing_failure()",
        "-            # Verify no uncollectable garbage accumulated",
        "+            # Force a full collection and verify nothing is in gc.garbage.",
        "             gc.collect()",
        "             self.assertEqual(",
        "                 len(gc.garbage),",
        "                 0,",
        "-                \"No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated failures\",",
        "+                \"No uncollectable garbage should remain after repeated \"",
        "+                \"tracing failures\",",
        "             )",
        " ",
        " "
      ],
      "newLines": 108,
      "newStart": 109,
      "oldLines": 81,
      "oldStart": 93
    }
  ],
  "type": "update"
}
162. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "tail -50 /workspace/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py",
  "description": "Read end of convert_frame.py"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "                    frame.f_code.co_name,\n                    skip_reason,\n                    frame.f_code.co_filename,\n                )\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n\n        if (\n            frame.f_code.co_filename == \"<string>\" and frame.f_code.co_name == \"__new__\"\n        ) or (\n            frame.f_code.co_filename.endswith(\"collections/__init__.py\")\n            and frame.f_code.co_name == \"_make\"\n        ):\n            # nametuple constructor/_make\n            return ConvertFrameReturn()\n        if torch._dynamo.utils.get_optimize_ddp_mode() == \"ddp_optimizer\":\n            ddp_module = DistributedDataParallel._get_active_ddp_module()\n            if ddp_module:\n                with compile_lock:\n                    from torch._dynamo.backends.distributed import DDPOptimizer\n\n                    ddp_optimizer = DDPOptimizer(\n                        bucket_bytes_cap=ddp_module.bucket_bytes_cap,\n                        backend_compile_fn=self._torchdynamo_orig_backend._torchdynamo_orig_backend,  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n                    )\n                    assert hasattr(\n                        self._torchdynamo_orig_backend, \"_clone_with_backend\"\n                    ), (\n                        \"DDPOptimizer only supports callback fns that know how to clone themselves.\"\n                    )\n                    hijacked_callback = (\n                        self._torchdynamo_orig_backend._clone_with_backend(\n                            ddp_optimizer.compile_fn,\n                        )\n                    )\n                    return hijacked_callback(\n                        frame, cache_entry, self.hooks, frame_state\n                    )\n\n        with compile_lock, _disable_current_modes():\n            # skip=1: skip this frame\n            result = self._torchdynamo_orig_backend(\n                frame, cache_entry, self.hooks, frame_state, skip=1\n            )\n            return result\n\n\ndef catch_errors_wrapper(\n    callback: ConvertFrameProtocol, hooks: Hooks\n) -> CatchErrorsWrapper:\n    return CatchErrorsWrapper(callback, hooks)"
}
163. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "sed -n '1,30p' /workspace/torch/_dynamo/convert_frame.py",
  "description": "Read beginning of convert_frame.py to understand structure"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "\"\"\"\nThis module implements TorchDynamo's core frame conversion functionality, transforming Python\nframes into FX graphs. It handles:\n\n- Frame analysis and bytecode transformation\n- Guard creation and management for dynamic behaviors\n- Cache management for recompilation\n- Error handling and fallback mechanisms\n\nKey classes:\n- ConvertFrame: Main entry point for frame conversion with error handling\n- ConvertFrameAssert: Implements core frame to graph conversion logic\n- Tracker: Tracks input/output code objects during conversion\n- CatchErrorsWrapper: Provides error handling and suppression logic\n\nThe conversion process preserves program semantics while enabling optimizations\nthrough torch.compile() and related systems.\n\nNOTE: _torchdynamo_orig_backend is used for convert frame wrappers to identify the inner wrapped function.\nBy going down the _torchdynamo_orig_backend chain, one can recover the original unwrapped backend,\nwhich is checked for during the Dynamo cache lookup.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport collections\nimport contextlib\nimport cProfile\nimport dis\nimport functools"
}

167. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "limit": 50,
  "offset": 340
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\n\n@dataclass\nclass StackLocalsMetadata:\n    \"\"\"\n    Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\n    \"\"\"\n\n    stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n\n\ndef get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    # f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n    # implementation detail -\n    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n\n    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n\n    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n\n    f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n    return f_builtins\n\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
    "numLines": 35,
    "startLine": 340,
    "totalLines": 374
  },
  "type": "text"
}

{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"2220: \\n2221:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\\n2222:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2223:         # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances\\n2224:         name = unique_id(prefix)\\n2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\\n2226:         return name\\n2227: \\n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n2234: \\n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2236:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n2237:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n2248: \\n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n2251:     ) -> None:\\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n2253: \\n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n2255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2256:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n2257:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2258:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n2260: \\n2261: \\n2262: err_epilogue = (\\n2263:     \\\"With the current config, we will graph break \\\"\\n2264:     \\\"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \\\"\\n2265:     \\\"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \\\"\\n2266:     \\\"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \\\"\\n2267:     \\\"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\\\"\\n2268: )\\n2269: \\n2270: \\n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\\n2273: ) -> None:\\n2274:     if kind != \\\"call_function\\\":\\n2275:         return\\n2276: \\n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\\\"prim\\\", \\\"prims\\\", \\\"aten\\\"}:\\n2279:             return\\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\\n2281: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2220-2280\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2220: \n2221:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\n2222:         \"\"\"\n2223:         # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances\n2224:         name = unique_id(prefix)\n2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\n2226:         return name\n2227: \n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\n2234: \n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2236:             if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n2237:                 del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\n2248: \n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n2251:     ) -> None:\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n2253: \n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n2255:         \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n2256:         if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n2257:             return node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2258:         assert node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\n2260: \n2261: \n2262: err_epilogue = (\n2263:     \"With the current config, we will graph break \"\n2264:     \"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \"\n2265:     \"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \"\n2266:     \"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \"\n2267:     \"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\"\n2268: )\n2269: \n2270: \n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\n2273: ) -> None:\n2274:     if kind != \"call_function\":\n2275:         return\n2276: \n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\"prim\", \"prims\", \"aten\"}:\n2279:             return\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\n2281: ",

175. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 3340,
  "path": "torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
  "startLine": 3240
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"3240: \\n3241: # NOTE: [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\\n3242: # Ignoring HigherOrderOperators for a moment,\\n3243: # OutputGraph represents the graph being built by Dynamo that may be compiled\\n3244: # and executed. It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.\\n3245: #\\n3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.\\n3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect\\n3248: # the function passed to it (call this the \\\"body function\\\"), capture it into a\\n3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the\\n3250: # GraphModule.\\n3251: #\\n3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having\\n3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.\\n3254: #\\n3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. For example:\\n3260: # - the OutputGraph can receive new GraphArgs (if we discover any new\\n3261: #   untracked Tensors)\\n3262: # - side effects from the body function get accumulated into\\n3263: #   OutputGraph.side_effects\\n3264: # - guards produced by the body function get accumulated into OutputGraph.guards\\n3265: #\\n3266: # The traced function has some special properties that make it easier for us\\n3267: # to transform later down the line:\\n3268: # - we lift all free variables to being inputs.\\n3269: #\\n3270: # If the introspection fails (due to the existence of graph breaks), then\\n3271: # we roll back the current OutputGraph state and graph break on the\\n3272: # HigherOrderOperator.\\n3273: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL3240-3340\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "3240: \n3241: # NOTE: [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\n3242: # Ignoring HigherOrderOperators for a moment,\n3243: # OutputGraph represents the graph being built by Dynamo that may be compiled\n3244: # and executed. It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.\n3245: #\n3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.\n3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect\n3248: # the function passed to it (call this the \"body function\"), capture it into a\n3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the\n3250: # GraphModule.\n3251: #\n3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having\n3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.\n3254: #\n3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. For example:\n3260: # - the OutputGraph can receive new GraphArgs (if we discover any new\n3261: #   untracked Tensors)\n3262: # - side effects from the body function get accumulated into\n3263: #   OutputGraph.side_effects\n3264: # - guards produced by the body function get accumulated into OutputGraph.guards\n3265: #\n3266: # The traced function has some special properties that make it easier for us\n3267: # to transform later down the line:\n3268: # - we lift all free variables to being inputs.\n3269: #\n3270: # If the introspection fails (due to the existence of graph breaks), then\n3271: # we roll back the current OutputGraph state and graph break on the\n3272: # HigherOrderOperator.\n3273: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL3240-3340"
  }
}

Mainly the\\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\\n379: \\n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\\n381: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL368-380\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
