177. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
Input
{
  "endLine": 875,
  "path": "torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
  "repo": "github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1",
  "startLine": 375
}
Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"375: class OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\\n376:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n377:     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\\n379: \\n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\\n381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\\n382:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\\n383:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\\n384:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n385: \\n386:     side_effects: SideEffects\\n387: \\n388:     def __init__(\\n389:         self,\\n390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\\n391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\\n392:         root_tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n393:         export: bool,\\n394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\\n395:         frame_state: Any,\\n396:         local_scope: Scope,\\n397:         global_scope: Scope,\\n398:         f_code: CodeType,\\n399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\\n400:         package: Optional[\\\"CompilePackage\\\"],\\n401:     ) -> None:\\n402:         super().__init__(\\n403:             local_scope,\\n404:             global_scope,\\n405:             torch_function_mode_stack,\\n406:             guard_on_key_order=set(),\\n407:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\\n408:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\\n409:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\\n410:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\\n411:             # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\\n412:             global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\\n413:             or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\\n414:             # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\\n415:             _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\\n416:             _aotautograd_guards=[],\\n417:         )\\n418:         self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\\n419:         # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\\n420:         # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\\n421:         self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\\n422:         self.export = export\\n423:         self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n424:         self.frame_state = frame_state\\n425:         self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\\n426:         # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\\n427:         self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\\n428:         # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\\n429:         self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\\n430: \\n431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\\n432:         # sure...\\n433:         self.co_fields = {\\n434:             \\\"co_name\\\": f_code.co_name,\\n435:             \\\"co_filename\\\": f_code.co_filename,\\n436:             \\\"co_firstlineno\\\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\\n437:         }\\n438: \\n439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\\n440: \\n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\\n447: \\n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\\n451:             #\\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\\n455:             # the program execution.\\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\\n462:         )\\n463: \\n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\\n467: \\n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\\n473:                 export=self.export,\\n474:             )\\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\\n479:         )\\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\\n481: \\n482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\\n483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\\n484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\\n485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\\n486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\\n487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\\n488:             collections.defaultdict(list)\\n489:         )\\n490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\\n502: \\n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\\n505: \\n506:         # Not checkpointed\\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\\n509: \\n510:         self.package = package\\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\\n512:         # accessed it?\\n513:         #\\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\\n515:         #   - During export, and\\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\\n517:         #\\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\\n520:         # recording!\\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\\n522: \\n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\\n525:         self.should_exit = False\\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\\n527: \\n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\\n530: \\n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\\n535:         # back for backend errors.\\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\\n537: \\n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n541: \\n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \\\"pt2_compliant_tag\\\".\\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\\n545: \\n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\\n551:         self.save_global_state()\\n552: \\n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\\n556: \\n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\\n561:         self.random_calls: list[\\n562:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\\n563:         ] = []\\n564:         self.random_values_var: Any = None\\n565: \\n566:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\\n567:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\\n568: \\n569:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\\n570:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\\n571:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\\n572:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\\n573: \\n574:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\\n575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\\n576:         )\\n577: \\n578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\\n579: \\n580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\\n581:         # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\\n582:         # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\\n583:         # context manager inside a compiled region\\n584:         self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\\n585:             self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\\n586:         )\\n587: \\n588:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\\n589:         self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\\n590:             dynamo_timed(\\n591:                 \\\"bytecode_tracing\\\",\\n592:                 log_pt2_compile_event=True,\\n593:             )\\n594:         )\\n595: \\n596:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\\n597:         self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\\n598: \\n599:     def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\\n600:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\\n601:         return self.install_global(\\\"__builtins_dict__\\\", f_builtins)\\n602: \\n603:     def add_backward_state_hook(\\n604:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \\\"hook\\\"\\n605:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\\n606:         name = f\\\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\\\"\\n607:         assert name not in self.backward_state\\n608:         self.backward_state[name] = hook\\n609:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\\n610: \\n611:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\\n612:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\\n613:             if self.export:\\n614:                 unimplemented_v2(\\n615:                     gb_type=\\\"backward_state does not support export\\\",\\n616:                     context=\\\"\\\",\\n617:                     explanation=\\\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\\\",\\n618:                     hints=[],\\n619:                 )\\n620:             example_value = BackwardState()\\n621:             self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\\n622:                 \\\"dynamo_backward_state\\\",\\n623:                 type(example_value),\\n624:                 example_value,\\n625:                 source=BackwardStateSource(),\\n626:             )\\n627:             self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\\n628:             self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\\n629:         return self.backward_state_proxy\\n630: \\n631:     # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\\n632:     def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\\n633:         # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\\n634:         # that show up in ShapeEnv\\n635:         self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\\n636: \\n637:         self.guards.add(\\n638:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\\n639:         )\\n640: \\n641:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\\n642: \\n643:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\\n644: \\n645:         self.guards.add(\\n646:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\\n647:         )\\n648: \\n649:         ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\\n650:         if ci is not None:\\n651:             self.guards.add(\\n652:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\\n653:             )\\n654:         if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\\n655:             self.guards.add(\\n656:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\\n657:                     GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\\n658:                 )\\n659:             )\\n660: \\n661:     def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\\n662:         if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\\n663:             return None\\n664: \\n665:         get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\\n666:         are_inline_hooks = (\\n667:             torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable\\n668:         )\\n669:         hooks = get_hooks()\\n670:         if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):\\n671:             return None\\n672: \\n673:         # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\\n674:         # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\\n675:         # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\\n676: \\n677:         pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\\n678:         pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\\n679:             \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\\\",\\n680:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\\n681:         )\\n682:         unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\\n683:             \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\\\",\\n684:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\\n685:         )\\n686:         assert pack_subgraph_name == \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0\\\"\\n687:         assert unpack_subgraph_name == \\\"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0\\\"\\n688:         return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\\n689: \\n690:     def dump_guards_state(self) -> OutputGraphGuardsState:\\n691:         # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\\n692:         return OutputGraphGuardsState(\\n693:             local_scope=self.local_scope,\\n694:             global_scope=self.global_scope,\\n695:             torch_function_mode_stack=self.torch_function_mode_stack,\\n696:             guard_on_key_order=self.guard_on_key_order,\\n697:             input_source_to_sizes_strides=self.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\\n698:             dual_level=self.dual_level,\\n699:             functorch_layers=self.functorch_layers,\\n700:             current_device=self.current_device,\\n701:             global_state_guard=self.global_state_guard,\\n702:             name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals=self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals,\\n703:             export=self.export,\\n704:             export_constraints=self.export_constraints,\\n705:             _guards=self.guards,\\n706:             _aotautograd_guards=self.aotautograd_guards,\\n707:         )\\n708: \\n709:     def synthetic_graph_input(\\n710:         self, fn: Callable[..., Any], args: tuple[Any, ...]\\n711:     ) -> VariableTracker:\\n712:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n713:         call fn(*args) before the graph runs and turn the result into a fake input.\\n714:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n715:         example_value = fn(*args)\\n716:         varname = self.new_var()\\n717:         cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\\n718:         cg.add_push_null(\\n719:             lambda: cg.load_import_from(\\n720:                 fn.__module__,\\n721:                 fn.__name__,\\n722:             )\\n723:         )\\n724:         cg.foreach(map(variables.ConstantVariable.create, args))\\n725:         cg.call_function(len(args), False)\\n726:         cg.store(varname)\\n727:         self.pregraph_bytecode.extend(cg.get_instructions())\\n728:         source = SyntheticLocalSource(varname)\\n729:         result = VariableTracker.build(self.root_tx, example_value, source)\\n730:         # Realize the VT because we will delete the guards on it in the next line.\\n731:         result = result.realize()\\n732:         TracingContext.get().guards_context.dynamo_guards.remove_guards_with_source(\\n733:             source\\n734:         )\\n735:         return result\\n736: \\n737:     def add_cleanup_hook(self, fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> None:\\n738:         self.cleanup_hooks.append(fn)\\n739: \\n740:     def call_cleanup_hooks(self) -> None:\\n741:         for hook in reversed(self.cleanup_hooks):\\n742:             hook()\\n743:         self.cleanup_hooks.clear()\\n744: \\n745:     @property\\n746:     def root_tracer(self) -> \\\"SubgraphTracer\\\":\\n747:         return self.tracers[0]\\n748: \\n749:     @property\\n750:     def current_tracer(self) -> \\\"SubgraphTracer\\\":\\n751:         return self.tracers[-1]\\n752: \\n753:     def is_root_tracer(self) -> bool:\\n754:         # Helper to tell if we are inside the higher order operator tracing.\\n755:         return len(self.tracers) == 1\\n756: \\n757:     @property\\n758:     def graph(self) -> torch.fx.Graph:\\n759:         return self.current_tracer.graph\\n760: \\n761:     # TODO(rzou): can delete after we refactor speculate_subgraph to use nested GraphTracer.\\n762:     @graph.setter\\n763:     def graph(self, value: torch.fx.Graph) -> None:\\n764:         self.current_tracer.graph = value\\n765: \\n766:     @property\\n767:     def input_name_to_proxy(self) -> dict[str, fx.Proxy]:\\n768:         return self.current_tracer.input_name_to_proxy\\n769: \\n770:     @property\\n771:     def real_value_cache(self) -> dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor]:\\n772:         return self.current_tracer.real_value_cache\\n773: \\n774:     @property\\n775:     def bound_symbols(self) -> dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, \\\"LazyProxy\\\"]]:\\n776:         return self.current_tracer.bound_symbols\\n777: \\n778:     # If you are here, and you're looking for create_graph_input,\\n779:     # to avoid ambiguity, please call one of the following:\\n780:     # - self.current_tracer.create_graph_input\\n781:     # - self.root_tracer.create_graph_input\\n782:     # See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] for more context.\\n783: \\n784:     def create_proxy(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\\n785:         return self.current_tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\\n786: \\n787:     def create_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Node:\\n788:         return self.current_tracer.create_node(*args, **kwargs)\\n789: \\n790:     def remove_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\\n791:         return self.current_tracer.remove_node(*args, **kwargs)\\n792: \\n793:     @contextlib.contextmanager\\n794:     def subtracer(\\n795:         self, source_target: Optional[Target], prior_tracer: \\\"SubgraphTracer\\\"\\n796:     ) -> Generator[fx.Tracer, None, None]:\\n797:         new_scope_ctx = enter_new_scope()\\n798:         try:\\n799:             if prior_tracer:\\n800:                 # Lineage MUST stay preserved\\n801:                 assert prior_tracer.parent is self.current_tracer\\n802:             new_scope_ctx.__enter__()\\n803:             tracer = (\\n804:                 prior_tracer\\n805:                 if prior_tracer\\n806:                 else SubgraphTracer(\\n807:                     self,\\n808:                     parent=self.current_tracer,\\n809:                     source_target=source_target,\\n810:                     is_export=self.current_tracer.is_export,\\n811:                 )\\n812:             )\\n813:             self.tracers.append(tracer)\\n814:             yield tracer\\n815:         finally:\\n816:             new_scope_ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\\n817:             self.tracers.pop()\\n818: \\n819:     @property\\n820:     def output(self) -> \\\"OutputGraph\\\":\\n821:         return self\\n822: \\n823:     @property\\n824:     def fake_mode(self) -> torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode:\\n825:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\\n826:         return self.tracing_context.fake_mode\\n827: \\n828:     @property\\n829:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\\n830:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\\n831:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\\n832:         return self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env\\n833: \\n834:     @property\\n835:     def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\\n836:         return self.tracing_context.guards_context.dynamo_guards\\n837: \\n838:     @property\\n839:     def nn_modules(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\\n840:         return self.tracing_context.module_context.nn_modules\\n841: \\n842:     @property\\n843:     def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\\n844:         return self.tracing_context.guards_context.aotautograd_guards\\n845: \\n846:     def save_global_state(\\n847:         self, out: Optional[dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]]] = None\\n848:     ) -> None:\\n849:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n850:         Saves to out if it is provided. Else saves to the tracing context's global_state.\\n851:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n852:         global_state = cast(\\n853:             dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]],\\n854:             (\\n855:                 out\\n856:                 if out is not None\\n857:                 else self.tracing_context.global_context.global_state\\n858:             ),\\n859:         )\\n860: \\n861:         global_state[\\\"grad_enabled\\\"] = (torch.set_grad_enabled, torch.is_grad_enabled())\\n862: \\n863:         global_state[\\\"autocast_enabled\\\"] = (\\n864:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \\\"cuda\\\"),\\n865:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\\\"cuda\\\"),\\n866:         )\\n867:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cpu_enabled\\\"] = (\\n868:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \\\"cpu\\\"),\\n869:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\\\"cpu\\\"),\\n870:         )\\n871:         global_state[\\\"autocast_gpu_dtype\\\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\\n872:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \\\"cuda\\\"),\\n873:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\\\"cuda\\\"),\\n874:         )\\n875:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cpu_dtype\\\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\\n876: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL375-875\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "375: class OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n376:     \"\"\"\n377:     Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\n378:     generated fx.Graph.\n379: \n380:     OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n381:     with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\n382:     we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\n383:     the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\n384:     \"\"\"\n385: \n386:     side_effects: SideEffects\n387: \n388:     def __init__(\n389:         self,\n390:         code_options: dict[str, Any],\n391:         compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\n392:         root_tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n393:         export: bool,\n394:         export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\n395:         frame_state: Any,\n396:         local_scope: Scope,\n397:         global_scope: Scope,\n398:         f_code: CodeType,\n399:         torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\n400:         package: Optional[\"CompilePackage\"],\n401:     ) -> None:\n402:         super().__init__(\n403:             local_scope,\n404:             global_scope,\n405:             torch_function_mode_stack,\n406:             guard_on_key_order=set(),\n407:             input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\n408:             dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\n409:             functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\n410:             current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\n411:             # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\n412:             global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\n413:             or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\n414:             # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\n415:             _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\n416:             _aotautograd_guards=[],\n417:         )\n418:         self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\n419:         # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\n420:         # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\n421:         self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\n422:         self.export = export\n423:         self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\n424:         self.frame_state = frame_state\n425:         self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\n426:         # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\n427:         self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\n428:         # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\n429:         self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\n430: \n431:         # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\n432:         # sure...\n433:         self.co_fields = {\n434:             \"co_name\": f_code.co_name,\n435:             \"co_filename\": f_code.co_filename,\n436:             \"co_firstlineno\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\n437:         }\n438: \n439:         self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\n440: \n441:         # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\n442:         # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n443:         # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n444:         # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n445:         # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n446:         self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n447: \n448:         shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n449:             # Reference Cycle!\n450:             # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n451:             #\n452:             # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n453:             # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\n454:             # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n455:             # the program execution.\n456:             tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n457:             allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n458:             allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n459:             prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n460:             allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\n461:             co_fields=self.co_fields,\n462:         )\n463: \n464:         # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n465:         # of the user marked dynamic dims\n466:         import torch._functorch.config as _config\n467: \n468:         with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n469:             fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n470:                 shape_env=shape_env,\n471:                 # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n472:                 allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n473:                 export=self.export,\n474:             )\n475:         self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n476:         self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n477:         self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n478:             CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n479:         )\n480:         self.init_ambient_guards()\n481: \n482:         # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\n483:         # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\n484:         # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\n485:         # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\n486:         # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\n487:         self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\n488:             collections.defaultdict(list)\n489:         )\n490:         # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\n491:         self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\n492:         self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\n493:         # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\n494:         # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\n495:         self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\n496:         self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\n497:         self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\n498:         self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\n499:         # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\n500:         # and restore_graphstate\n501:         self.timestamp = 0\n502: \n503:         # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\n504:         self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\n505: \n506:         # Not checkpointed\n507:         self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\n508:         self.root_tx = root_tx\n509: \n510:         self.package = package\n511:         # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\n512:         # accessed it?\n513:         #\n514:         # For efficiency, we only populate this:\n515:         #   - During export, and\n516:         #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\n517:         #\n518:         # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\n519:         # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\n520:         # recording!\n521:         self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\n522: \n523:         self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\n524:         self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\n525:         self.should_exit = False\n526:         self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\n527: \n528:         # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\n529:         self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\n530: \n531:         # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\n532:         # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\n533:         # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\n534:         # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\n535:         # back for backend errors.\n536:         self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\n537: \n538:         # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n539:         # This information is useful for logging.\n540:         self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n541: \n542:         # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n543:         # This information is useful for logging.\n544:         self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n545: \n546:         # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\n547:         # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\n548:         # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\n549:         # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\n550:         # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n551:         self.save_global_state()\n552: \n553:         # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\n554:         # i.e. buffers and parameters.\n555:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\n556: \n557:         # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\n558:         # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\n559:         # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\n560:         # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\n561:         self.random_calls: list[\n562:             tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\n563:         ] = []\n564:         self.random_values_var: Any = None\n565: \n566:         # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\n567:         self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\n568: \n569:         # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\n570:         self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\n571:         self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\n572:         self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\n573: \n574:         self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\n575:             self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\n576:         )\n577: \n578:         self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n579: \n580:         # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\n581:         # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\n582:         # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\n583:         # context manager inside a compiled region\n584:         self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\n585:             self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\n586:         )\n587: \n588:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\n589:         self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\n590:             dynamo_timed(\n591:                 \"bytecode_tracing\",\n592:                 log_pt2_compile_event=True,\n593:             )\n594:         )\n595: \n596:     def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\n597:         self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\n598: \n599:     def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\n600:         f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\n601:         return self.install_global(\"__builtins_dict__\", f_builtins)\n602: \n603:     def add_backward_state_hook(\n604:         self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \"hook\"\n605:     ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\n606:         name = f\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\"\n607:         assert name not in self.backward_state\n608:         self.backward_state[name] = hook\n609:         return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\n610: \n611:     def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n612:         if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\n613:             if self.export:\n614:                 unimplemented_v2(\n615:                     gb_type=\"backward_state does not support export\",\n616:                     context=\"\",\n617:                     explanation=\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\",\n618:                     hints=[],\n619:                 )\n620:             example_value = BackwardState()\n621:             self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\n622:                 \"dynamo_backward_state\",\n623:                 type(example_value),\n624:                 example_value,\n625:                 source=BackwardStateSource(),\n626:             )\n627:             self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\"grapharg\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\n628:             self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\n629:         return self.backward_state_proxy\n630: \n631:     # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\n632:     def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\n633:         # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\n634:         # that show up in ShapeEnv\n635:         self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\n636: \n637:         self.guards.add(\n638:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\n639:         )\n640: \n641:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\n642: \n643:         self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\n644: \n645:         self.guards.add(\n646:             GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\n647:         )\n648: \n649:         ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\n650:         if ci is not None:\n651:             self.guards.add(\n652:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\n653:             )\n654:         if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n655:             self.guards.add(\n656:                 GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\n657:                     GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\n658:                 )\n659:             )\n660: \n661:     def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\n662:         if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n663:             return None\n664: \n665:         get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\n666:         are_inline_hooks = (\n667:             torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable\n668:         )\n669:         hooks = get_hooks()\n670:         if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):\n671:             return None\n672: \n673:         # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\n674:         # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\n675:         # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\n676: \n677:         pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\n678:         pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n679:             \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\",\n680:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\n681:         )\n682:         unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n683:             \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\",\n684:             torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\n685:         )\n686:         assert pack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0\"\n687:         assert unpack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0\"\n688:         return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\n689: \n690:     def dump_guards_state(self) -> OutputGraphGuardsState:\n691:         # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\n692:         return OutputGraphGuardsState(\n693:             local_scope=self.local_scope,\n694:             global_scope=self.global_scope,\n695:             torch_function_mode_stack=self.torch_function_mode_stack,\n696:             guard_on_key_order=self.guard_on_key_order,\n697:             input_source_to_sizes_strides=self.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\n698:             dual_level=self.dual_level,\n699:             functorch_layers=self.functorch_layers,\n700:             current_device=self.current_device,\n701:             global_state_guard=self.global_state_guard,\n702:             name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals=self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals,\n703:             export=self.export,\n704:             export_constraints=self.export_constraints,\n705:             _guards=self.guards,\n706:             _aotautograd_guards=self.aotautograd_guards,\n707:         )\n708: \n709:     def synthetic_graph_input(\n710:         self, fn: Callable[..., Any], args: tuple[Any, ...]\n711:     ) -> VariableTracker:\n712:         \"\"\"\n713:         call fn(*args) before the graph runs and turn the result into a fake input.\n714:         \"\"\"\n715:         example_value = fn(*args)\n716:         varname = self.new_var()\n717:         cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\n718:         cg.add_push_null(\n719:             lambda: cg.load_import_from(\n720:                 fn.__module__,\n721:                 fn.__name__,\n722:             )\n723:         )\n724:         cg.foreach(map(variables.ConstantVariable.create, args))\n725:         cg.call_function(len(args), False)\n726:         cg.store(varname)\n727:         self.pregraph_bytecode.extend(cg.get_instructions())\n728:         source = SyntheticLocalSource(varname)\n729:         result = VariableTracker.build(self.root_tx, example_value, source)\n730:         # Realize the VT because we will delete the guards on it in the next line.\n731:         result = result.realize()\n732:         TracingContext.get().guards_context.dynamo_guards.remove_guards_with_source(\n733:             source\n734:         )\n735:         return result\n736: \n737:     def add_cleanup_hook(self, fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> None:\n738:         self.cleanup_hooks.append(fn)\n739: \n740:     def call_cleanup_hooks(self) -> None:\n741:         for hook in reversed(self.cleanup_hooks):\n742:             hook()\n743:         self.cleanup_hooks.clear()\n744: \n745:     @property\n746:     def root_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n747:         return self.tracers[0]\n748: \n749:     @property\n750:     def current_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n751:         return self.tracers[-1]\n752: \n753:     def is_root_tracer(self) -> bool:\n754:         # Helper to tell if we are inside the higher order operator tracing.\n755:         return len(self.tracers) == 1\n756: \n757:     @property\n758:     def graph(self) -> torch.fx.Graph:\n759:         return self.current_tracer.graph\n760: \n761:     # TODO(rzou): can delete after we refactor speculate_subgraph to use nested GraphTracer.\n762:     @graph.setter\n763:     def graph(self, value: torch.fx.Graph) -> None:\n764:         self.current_tracer.graph = value\n765: \n766:     @property\n767:     def input_name_to_proxy(self) -> dict[str, fx.Proxy]:\n768:         return self.current_tracer.input_name_to_proxy\n769: \n770:     @property\n771:     def real_value_cache(self) -> dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor]:\n772:         return self.current_tracer.real_value_cache\n773: \n774:     @property\n775:     def bound_symbols(self) -> dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, \"LazyProxy\"]]:\n776:         return self.current_tracer.bound_symbols\n777: \n778:     # If you are here, and you're looking for create_graph_input,\n779:     # to avoid ambiguity, please call one of the following:\n780:     # - self.current_tracer.create_graph_input\n781:     # - self.root_tracer.create_graph_input\n782:     # See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] for more context.\n783: \n784:     def create_proxy(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n785:         return self.current_tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\n786: \n787:     def create_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Node:\n788:         return self.current_tracer.create_node(*args, **kwargs)\n789: \n790:     def remove_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\n791:         return self.current_tracer.remove_node(*args, **kwargs)\n792: \n793:     @contextlib.contextmanager\n794:     def subtracer(\n795:         self, source_target: Optional[Target], prior_tracer: \"SubgraphTracer\"\n796:     ) -> Generator[fx.Tracer, None, None]:\n797:         new_scope_ctx = enter_new_scope()\n798:         try:\n799:             if prior_tracer:\n800:                 # Lineage MUST stay preserved\n801:                 assert prior_tracer.parent is self.current_tracer\n802:             new_scope_ctx.__enter__()\n803:             tracer = (\n804:                 prior_tracer\n805:                 if prior_tracer\n806:                 else SubgraphTracer(\n807:                     self,\n808:                     parent=self.current_tracer,\n809:                     source_target=source_target,\n810:                     is_export=self.current_tracer.is_export,\n811:                 )\n812:             )\n813:             self.tracers.append(tracer)\n814:             yield tracer\n815:         finally:\n816:             new_scope_ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n817:             self.tracers.pop()\n818: \n819:     @property\n820:     def output(self) -> \"OutputGraph\":\n821:         return self\n822: \n823:     @property\n824:     def fake_mode(self) -> torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode:\n825:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n826:         return self.tracing_context.fake_mode\n827: \n828:     @property\n829:     def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n830:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n831:         assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\n832:         return self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env\n833: \n834:     @property\n835:     def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n836:         return self.tracing_context.guards_context.dynamo_guards\n837: \n838:     @property\n839:     def nn_modules(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n840:         return self.tracing_context.module_context.nn_modules\n841: \n842:     @property\n843:     def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n844:         return self.tracing_context.guards_context.aotautograd_guards\n845: \n846:     def save_global_state(\n847:         self, out: Optional[dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]]] = None\n848:     ) -> None:\n849:         \"\"\"\n850:         Saves to out if it is provided. Else saves to the tracing context's global_state.\n851:         \"\"\"\n852:         global_state = cast(\n853:             dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]],\n854:             (\n855:                 out\n856:                 if out is not None\n857:                 else self.tracing_context.global_context.global_state\n858:             ),\n859:         )\n860: \n861:         global_state[\"grad_enabled\"] = (torch.set_grad_enabled, torch.is_grad_enabled())\n862: \n863:         global_state[\"autocast_enabled\"] = (\n864:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \"cuda\"),\n865:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\"cuda\"),\n866:         )\n867:         global_state[\"autocast_cpu_enabled\"] = (\n868:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \"cpu\"),\n869:             torch.is_autocast_enabled(\"cpu\"),\n870:         )\n871:         global_state[\"autocast_gpu_dtype\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\n872:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \"cuda\"),\n873:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\"cuda\"),\n874:         )\n875:         global_state[\"autocast_cpu_dtype\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\n876: ",
