{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"876:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \\\"cpu\\\"),\\n877:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\\\"cpu\\\"),\\n878:         )\\n879:         global_state[\\\"autocast_cache_enabled\\\"] = (\\n880:             torch.set_autocast_cache_enabled,\\n881:             torch.is_autocast_cache_enabled(),\\n882:         )\\n883: \\n884:     def push_tx(self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\") -> None:\\n885:         self._current_tx.append(tx)\\n886: \\n887:     def pop_tx(self) -> \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\":\\n888:         return self._current_tx.pop()\\n889: \\n890:     @property\\n891:     def current_tx(self) -> \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\":\\n892:         return self.root_tx if not self._current_tx else self._current_tx[-1]\\n893: \\n894:     def count_calls(self) -> int:\\n895:         return count_calls(self.graph)\\n896: \\n897:     def is_empty_graph(self) -> bool:\\n898:         return len(list(self.graph.nodes)) == 0\\n899: \\n900:     def get_submodule(self, keys: str) -> Union[torch.nn.Module, Any]:\\n901:         assert keys\\n902:         obj: Union[torch.nn.Module, dict[str, torch.nn.Module]] = self.nn_modules\\n903:         for k in keys.split(\\\".\\\"):\\n904:             if isinstance(obj, dict):\\n905:                 obj = obj[k]\\n906:             else:\\n907:                 obj = getattr(obj, k)\\n908:         return obj\\n909: \\n910:     def new_var(self, name: str = \\\"tmp\\\") -> str:\\n911:         existing = set(self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"])\\n912:         # In common case, this will be O(1)\\n913:         while True:\\n914:             var = f\\\"{name}_{next(self.unique_var_id)}\\\"\\n915:             if var not in existing:\\n916:                 self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"] += (var,)\\n917:                 return var\\n918: \\n919:     def update_co_names(self, name: str) -> None:\\n920:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Ensure self.code_options.co_names contains name\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n921:         if name not in self.code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"]:\\n922:             self.code_options[\\\"co_names\\\"] += (name,)\\n923: \\n924:     @staticmethod\\n925:     def module_key_name(*names: Any) -> str:\\n926:         # create a new unique name\\n927:         name = \\\"_\\\".join(map(str, names))\\n928:         # Strip the guard lookup L/G access\\n929:         name = re.sub(r\\\"^[GL]\\\\['?(.*?)'?\\\\]$\\\", r\\\"\\\\1\\\", name)\\n930:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz[123].qkv with abc.xyz_123.qkv\\n931:         name = re.sub(r\\\"\\\\[(\\\\d+)\\\\]\\\", r\\\"_\\\\g<1>\\\", name)\\n932:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz_123.qkv with abc_xyz_123_qkv\\n933:         name = re.sub(r\\\"[^a-zA-Z0-9]\\\", \\\"_\\\", name)\\n934: \\n935:         if not name or not name[0].isalpha():\\n936:             name = \\\"sub\\\" + name\\n937: \\n938:         return name\\n939: \\n940:     def register_static_attr_and_return_proxy(\\n941:         self, attr_prefix: str, attr_value: Any\\n942:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n943:         attr_name = get_unique_name_wrt(attr_prefix, self.nn_modules)\\n944:         # TODO `nn_modules` has been historically overloaded to store a lot more\\n945:         # than just nn module objects, fix that.\\n946:         self.nn_modules[attr_name] = attr_value\\n947:         proxy = self.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", attr_name, (), {})\\n948:         set_example_value(proxy.node, attr_value)\\n949:         return proxy\\n950: \\n951:     def register_attr_or_module(\\n952:         self,\\n953:         target: Union[torch.nn.Module, torch.Tensor, Any],\\n954:         *names: Any,\\n955:         **options: Any,\\n956:     ) -> VariableTracker:\\n957:         if is_dynamic_nn_module(target, self.export):\\n958:             # Instead of returning UnspecializedNNModuleVariable, call\\n959:             # VariableTracker.build so that it is tracked for mutation.\\n960:             return VariableTracker.build(self.current_tx, target, **options)\\n961: \\n962:         options = dict(options)\\n963:         assert \\\"source\\\" in options\\n964:         source = options[\\\"source\\\"]\\n965:         assert not isinstance(source, ParamBufferSource)\\n966: \\n967:         if isinstance(target, torch.Tensor):\\n968:             tracer = self.current_tracer\\n969:             if not self.is_root_tracer():\\n970:                 # For higher order ops, we don't want to insert the get_attr in\\n971:                 # innermost graph. Instead, we want to raise the params/buffers\\n972:                 # as inputs to the higher-order graph, and register them as\\n973:                 # get_attrs in the root tracer.\\n974: \\n975:                 # Note that Dynamo will still call lift_tracked_freevar_to_input\\n976:                 # when these inputs are encountered for the inner graph. The\\n977:                 # only difference is what happens at the root tracer for\\n978:                 # nn.Parameters vs free inputs. The free inputs are registered\\n979:                 # as placeholders in the root graph, whereas the nn.Parameters\\n980:                 # are registered as get_attr nodes in the root graph.\\n981:                 tracer = self.root_tracer\\n982: \\n983:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n984:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\\n985:                 self.param_name_to_source[module_key] = source\\n986: \\n987:                 # Check if the attr has already been registered. This can happen\\n988:                 # when two different sources point to the same tensor.\\n989:                 assert self.root_tx is not None\\n990:                 if target in self.root_tx.output.side_effects:\\n991:                     return self.root_tx.output.side_effects[target]\\n992: \\n993:                 if get_static_address_type(target) == \\\"guarded\\\" and not isinstance(\\n994:                     source, NumpyTensorSource\\n995:                 ):\\n996:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.ID_MATCH))\\n997:                 elif not is_constant_source(source):\\n998:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.TENSOR_MATCH))\\n999: \\n1000:                 vt = wrap_fx_proxy(\\n1001:                     self.root_tx,\\n1002:                     tracer.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", module_key, (), {}),\\n1003:                     example_value=target,\\n1004:                     **options,\\n1005:                 )\\n1006: \\n1007:                 # Track the object so to avoid duplicate registration in case of\\n1008:                 # different sources pointing to the same tensor object.\\n1009:                 vt = self.root_tx.output.side_effects.track_object_existing(target, vt)\\n1010: \\n1011:                 assert \\\"tensor_dict\\\" not in vt.as_proxy().node.meta\\n1012:                 vt.as_proxy().node.meta[\\\"tensor_dict\\\"] = _extract_tensor_dict(target)\\n1013: \\n1014:                 return vt\\n1015: \\n1016:         elif isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\\n1017:             assert isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module)\\n1018: \\n1019:             if source:\\n1020:                 install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.NN_MODULE))\\n1021: \\n1022:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1023:                     return NNModuleVariable(type(target), module_key, target, **options)\\n1024: \\n1025:             else:\\n1026:                 # This is Dynamo created graph module, e.g., graph module coming\\n1027:                 # from higher order ops. NNModuleVariable tracker can't be\\n1028:                 # sourceless, so let's return a unspecializedNNModule variable\\n1029:                 # tracker.\\n1030:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1031:                     return variables.UnspecializedNNModuleVariable(target, **options)\\n1032: \\n1033:         elif isinstance(target, (torch.SymInt, torch.SymFloat)):\\n1034:             # HACKY CODE REGION BEGIN\\n1035:             # WE ARE PIGGYBACKING ON EXISTING INFRA TO REGISTER ATTRS\\n1036:             # This ultimately gets written to self.nn_modules, which is unfortunate\\n1037:             # Attrs that are tenors and symints and such need to be migrated to have their\\n1038:             # own storage\\n1039:             # alas, this is like this for now\\n1040: \\n1041:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1042:                 return SymNodeVariable.create(\\n1043:                     self,\\n1044:                     self.create_proxy(\\\"get_attr\\\", module_key, (), {}),\\n1045:                     sym_num=target,\\n1046:                     **options,\\n1047:                 )\\n1048: \\n1049:             # HACKY CODE REGION END\\n1050:         else:\\n1051: \\n1052:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\\n1053:                 self.output.update_co_names(module_key)\\n1054:                 self.global_scope[module_key] = target\\n1055:                 return VariableTracker.build(\\n1056:                     self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1057:                     target,\\n1058:                     ConstantSource(source_name=module_key),\\n1059:                 )\\n1060: \\n1061:         for k, v in self.nn_modules.items():\\n1062:             if v is target:\\n1063:                 # it already exists\\n1064:                 return wrap_name(k)\\n1065: \\n1066:         name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(*names)\\n1067:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, self.global_scope)\\n1068:         self.nn_modules[name] = target\\n1069:         if isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\\n1070: \\n1071:             def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:\\n1072:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\\n1073:                 new_source = ParamBufferSource(source, leaf_name)\\n1074:                 new_name = f\\\"{name}.{leaf_name}\\\"\\n1075:                 self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source\\n1076:                 if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\\n1077:                     self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[\\n1078:                         OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name())\\n1079:                     ] = leaf_name\\n1080: \\n1081:             # annoying, but there are cases when we do not have parameters\\n1082:             # see test_nn_moduledict_contains\\n1083:             if hasattr(target, \\\"_parameters\\\"):\\n1084:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_parameters():\\n1085:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\\n1086:             if hasattr(target, \\\"_buffers\\\"):\\n1087:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_buffers():\\n1088:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\\n1089: \\n1090:         return wrap_name(name)\\n1091: \\n1092:     def handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(\\n1093:         self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\"\\n1094:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], dict[Source, Source]]:\\n1095:         # If list inputs are stolen, but still needed after the function call, create aliases to keep them alive\\n1096:         maybe_gm = self.local_scope.get(\\\"self\\\")\\n1097:         stolen_list_names = get_locals_to_steal(maybe_gm)\\n1098:         if not stolen_list_names:\\n1099:             return [], {}\\n1100: \\n1101:         alias_insts = []\\n1102:         needs_alias: dict[str, list[VariableTracker]] = {}\\n1103: \\n1104:         queue = [\\n1105:             *tx.stack,\\n1106:             *tx.symbolic_locals.values(),\\n1107:             *self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations.keys(),\\n1108:         ]\\n1109: \\n1110:         while queue:\\n1111:             x = queue.pop()\\n1112:             if isinstance(x, BaseListVariable):\\n1113:                 assert isinstance(x.items, list)\\n1114:                 queue += x.items\\n1115:                 continue\\n1116: \\n1117:             if not (\\n1118:                 (\\n1119:                     x not in self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations\\n1120:                     or isinstance(x.mutation_type, AttributeMutationExisting)\\n1121:                 )\\n1122:                 and isinstance(x.source, GetItemSource)\\n1123:                 and isinstance(x.source.base, LocalSource)\\n1124:                 and x.source.base.local_name in stolen_list_names\\n1125:             ):\\n1126:                 continue\\n1127: \\n1128:             stolen_name = x.source.base.local_name\\n1129:             if stolen_name not in needs_alias:\\n1130:                 needs_alias[stolen_name] = []\\n1131:             needs_alias[stolen_name].append(x)\\n1132: \\n1133:         visited = {}\\n1134:         overridden_sources: dict[Source, Source] = {}\\n1135:         for arg in self.graphargs:\\n1136:             if not (\\n1137:                 isinstance(arg._example, list)\\n1138:                 and isinstance(arg.source, LocalSource)\\n1139:                 and arg.source.local_name in needs_alias\\n1140:             ):\\n1141:                 continue\\n1142: \\n1143:             # arg is a list that will be cleared by the compiled function\\n1144:             list_name = arg.source.local_name\\n1145:             assert list_name in self.code_options[\\\"co_varnames\\\"]\\n1146:             for x in needs_alias[list_name]:\\n1147:                 # Skip if already handled.\\n1148:                 if x.source in overridden_sources:\\n1149:                     continue\\n1150: \\n1151:                 # A small codegen optimization because we might have different\\n1152:                 # VariableTrackers that share the same source.\\n1153:                 list_idx = x.source.index  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n1154:                 if list_idx not in visited:\\n1155:                     alias_name = self.new_var(\\n1156:                         f\\\"{list_name}_ref\\\"\\n1157:                     )  # self.new_var already adds unique id suffix\\n1158: \\n1159:                     visited[list_idx] = alias_name\\n1160:                     # bytecode of `alias_name = list_name[list_idx]`\\n1161:                     alias_insts.extend(\\n1162:                         [\\n1163:                             create_instruction(\\\"LOAD_FAST\\\", argval=list_name),\\n1164:                             create_load_const(list_idx),\\n1165:                             create_instruction(\\\"BINARY_SUBSCR\\\"),\\n1166:                             create_instruction(\\\"STORE_FAST\\\", argval=alias_name),\\n1167:                         ]\\n1168:                     )\\n1169: \\n1170:                 # operate on alias, handled by suffix codegen\\n1171:                 old_source = x.source\\n1172:                 overridden_sources[old_source] = LocalSource(visited[list_idx])\\n1173: \\n1174:         # NOTE: we need `overridden_sources` because (1) we want to codegen for\\n1175:         # these list items to use the new local source, but (2) we want to avoid\\n1176:         # updating `source` in place because that might break invariants in\\n1177:         # other parts of Dynamo like guards.\\n1178:         return alias_insts, overridden_sources\\n1179: \\n1180:     def _get_stack_values_to_restore(\\n1181:         self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\", stack_pops: int\\n1182:     ) -> tuple[list[VariableTracker], list[str], StackLocalsMetadata]:\\n1183:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1184:         Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\\n1185: \\n1186:         Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.\\n1187: \\n1188:         NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`\\n1189:         elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\\n1190:         `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\\n1191: \\n1192:         Returns:\\n1193:             - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\\n1194:             - restore_vars: names of locals corresponding to the locals part of `stack_values`\\n1195:             - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\\n1196:                 (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\\n1197:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1198:         tx.prune_dead_locals()\\n1199: \\n1200:         stack_values = []\\n1201:         meta = StackLocalsMetadata()\\n1202: \\n1203:         # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\\n1204:         # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\\n1205:         for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\\n1206:             variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(value)\\n1207:             # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\\n1208:             if len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops:\\n1209:                 stack_values.append(value)\\n1210:                 continue\\n1211:             if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\\n1212:                 meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\\n1213:             else:\\n1214:                 stack_values.append(value)\\n1215:             if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):\\n1216:                 target_values = (\\n1217:                     () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)\\n1218:                 )\\n1219:                 # NOTE: track index in stack after NULLs have been removed\\n1220:                 meta.stack_ctx_args.append((len(stack_values) - 1, target_values))\\n1221:                 meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)\\n1222: \\n1223:         # Add all the local vars to the \\\"stack\\\" so restore at the end\\n1224:         restore_vars: list[str] = []\\n1225:         val_to_names: dict[VariableTracker, list[str]] = {}\\n1226:         # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\\n1227:         # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\\n1228:         # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\\n1229:         # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\\n1230:         # is only needed for partial graphs.\\n1231:         # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\\n1232:         # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\\n1233:         # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\\n1234:         for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\\n1235:             # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\\n1236:             # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.\\n1237:             # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.\\n1238:             # This was very tricky to debug. For an example, dump the graph at call_user_compiler\\n1239:             # while running test_subgraphs.py\\n1240:             if isinstance(v.source, LocalSource) and v.source.local_name == k:\\n1241:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state\\n1242:             if isinstance(v, CellVariable) and v.local_name == k:\\n1243:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state\\n1244:             # Do not load variable if it is NULL.\\n1245:             if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\\n1246:                 # Continuation function will load the NULL for v.\\n1247:                 if type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v):\\n1248:                     meta.locals_null_keys.append(k)\\n1249:                     continue\\n1250:             else:\\n1251:                 # A variable should never be NULL in < 3.12\\n1252:                 assert not type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v)\\n1253:             if isinstance(v, ContextWrappingVariable):\\n1254:                 target_values = (\\n1255:                     () if v.target_values is None else tuple(v.target_values)\\n1256:                 )\\n1257:                 meta.locals_ctx_args.append((k, target_values))\\n1258:             if v not in val_to_names:\\n1259:                 val_to_names[v] = []\\n1260:             val_to_names[v].append(k)\\n1261:         for v in val_to_names.keys():\\n1262:             restore_vars.extend(val_to_names[v])\\n1263:             stack_values.extend([v] * len(val_to_names[v]))\\n1264: \\n1265:         return stack_values, restore_vars, meta\\n1266: \\n1267:     def compile_subgraph(\\n1268:         self,\\n1269:         tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n1270:         reason: GraphCompileReason,\\n1271:         partial_convert: bool = False,\\n1272:         stack_pops: int = 0,\\n1273:     ) -> list[StackLocalsMetadata]:\\n1274:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1275:         Compiles the current subgraph, with inputs w.r.t. self.root_tx, and codegens:\\n1276:             - Call the compiled subgraph\\n1277:             - Apply side effects\\n1278:             - Codegen stack and locals\\n1279:             - Store the locals\\n1280: \\n1281:         Python does not allow NULL to be an arg to a function, so we do not codegen NULLs on the stack,\\n1282:         unless the value is one of the top `stack_pops` values on the stack (these values are expected to be\\n1283:         popped immediately after this generated code. The prologue of the resume function is expected to restore\\n1284:         any dropped NULLs.\\n1285: \\n1286:         Returns stack indices and locals keys where we dropped NULLs, and where we found inactive context manager objects.\\n1287:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1288: \\n1289:         assert self.root_tx is not None\\n1290: \\n1291:         # FIXME temporary assert to make sure we're not accidentally compiling nested graph breaks\\n1292:         # before we're done the full implementation\\n1293:         assert self.root_tx is tx\\n1294: \\n1295:         # bytecode tracing has finished. Pop the context manager for dynamo_timed\\n1296:         self.mark_bytecode_tracing_stop()\\n1297: \\n1298:         self.partial_convert = partial_convert\\n1299:         self.compile_subgraph_reason = reason\\n1300:         self.should_exit = True\\n1301: \\n1302:         log.debug(\\\"COMPILING GRAPH due to %s\\\", reason)\\n1303: \\n1304:         # prefix instructions (Python 3.11+)\\n1305:         prefix_insts: list[Instruction] = []\\n1306:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\\n1307:             for inst in tx.prefix_insts:\\n1308:                 if inst.opname == \\\"MAKE_CELL\\\":\\n1309:                     prefix_insts.append(\\n1310:                         create_instruction(\\\"MAKE_CELL\\\", argval=inst.argval)\\n1311:                     )\\n1312:                 elif inst.opname == \\\"COPY_FREE_VARS\\\":\\n1313:                     prefix_insts.append(\\n1314:                         create_instruction(\\n1315:                             \\\"COPY_FREE_VARS\\\", arg=len(tx.code_options[\\\"co_freevars\\\"])\\n1316:                         )\\n1317:                     )\\n1318:                 else:\\n1319:                     prefix_insts.append(copy.copy(inst))\\n1320:         self.add_output_instructions(prefix_insts)\\n1321: \\n1322:         assert not (self.pregraph_bytecode and self.export), (\\n1323:             \\\"export does not support pregraph_bytecode\\\"\\n1324:         )\\n1325:         self.add_output_instructions(self.pregraph_bytecode)\\n1326: \\n1327:         alias_insts, overridden_sources = self.handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(\\n1328:             self.root_tx\\n1329:         )\\n1330:         self.add_output_instructions(alias_insts)\\n1331: \\n1332:         # Exit from all context manager variables to make sure global state is restored\\n1333:         for block in reversed(self.root_tx.block_stack):\\n1334:             block.exit(self.root_tx, is_graph_break=reason.graph_break)\\n1335: \\n1336:         self.cleanup_graph()\\n1337: \\n1338:         # stack values and restore vars for each frame are pushed in reverse order\\n1339:         # i.e. last element corresponds to root frame, first element corresponds to current frame\\n1340:         all_stack_values = []\\n1341:         all_restore_vars = []\\n1342:         all_stack_locals_metas = []\\n1343:         cur_tx: Optional[InstructionTranslatorBase] = tx\\n1344:         while True:\\n1345:             assert cur_tx is not None\\n1346:             # this should have been checked by the caller\\n1347:             assert all(block.can_restore() for block in cur_tx.block_stack)\\n1348:             stack_values, restore_vars, meta = self._get_stack_values_to_restore(\\n1349:                 cur_tx, stack_pops\\n1350:             )\\n1351:             all_stack_values.append(stack_values)\\n1352:             all_restore_vars.append(restore_vars)\\n1353:             all_stack_locals_metas.append(meta)\\n1354:             if cur_tx is self.root_tx:\\n1355:                 break\\n1356:             cur_tx = tx.parent\\n1357: \\n1358:         # Use nn.Module \\\"proxies\\\" in the constructed GraphModule so that\\n1359:         # the resulting GM does not hold additional strong references to the original modules.\\n1360:         # This prevents a strong ref cycle where Dynamo created code holds on to references\\n1361:         # to modules that also have Dynamo code cache invalidation checks.\\n1362:         # When cache invalidation runs, the generated GM will be invalidated, which also deletes\\n1363:         # the proxies.\\n1364:         nn_modules_proxies = {\\n1365:             name: nn_module_proxy(mod) for name, mod in self.nn_modules.items()\\n1366:         }\\n1367:         root = FakeRootModule(nn_modules_proxies)\\n1368: \\n1369:         from .decorators import disable\\n1370: \\n1371:         # to handle random calls\\n1372:         if len(self.random_calls) > 0:\\n1373:             random_calls_instructions = []\\n1374:             self.random_values_var = self.new_var(\\\"random_values\\\")\\n1375:             rand_fn = disable(\\n1376:                 _get_gen_rand_values_fn(self.random_calls),\\n1377: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL876-1376\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "876:             functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \"cpu\"),\n877:             torch.get_autocast_dtype(\"cpu\"),\n878:         )\n879:         global_state[\"autocast_cache_enabled\"] = (\n880:             torch.set_autocast_cache_enabled,\n881:             torch.is_autocast_cache_enabled(),\n882:         )\n883: \n884:     def push_tx(self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\") -> None:\n885:         self._current_tx.append(tx)\n886: \n887:     def pop_tx(self) -> \"InstructionTranslatorBase\":\n888:         return self._current_tx.pop()\n889: \n890:     @property\n891:     def current_tx(self) -> \"InstructionTranslatorBase\":\n892:         return self.root_tx if not self._current_tx else self._current_tx[-1]\n893: \n894:     def count_calls(self) -> int:\n895:         return count_calls(self.graph)\n896: \n897:     def is_empty_graph(self) -> bool:\n898:         return len(list(self.graph.nodes)) == 0\n899: \n900:     def get_submodule(self, keys: str) -> Union[torch.nn.Module, Any]:\n901:         assert keys\n902:         obj: Union[torch.nn.Module, dict[str, torch.nn.Module]] = self.nn_modules\n903:         for k in keys.split(\".\"):\n904:             if isinstance(obj, dict):\n905:                 obj = obj[k]\n906:             else:\n907:                 obj = getattr(obj, k)\n908:         return obj\n909: \n910:     def new_var(self, name: str = \"tmp\") -> str:\n911:         existing = set(self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"])\n912:         # In common case, this will be O(1)\n913:         while True:\n914:             var = f\"{name}_{next(self.unique_var_id)}\"\n915:             if var not in existing:\n916:                 self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"] += (var,)\n917:                 return var\n918: \n919:     def update_co_names(self, name: str) -> None:\n920:         \"\"\"Ensure self.code_options.co_names contains name\"\"\"\n921:         if name not in self.code_options[\"co_names\"]:\n922:             self.code_options[\"co_names\"] += (name,)\n923: \n924:     @staticmethod\n925:     def module_key_name(*names: Any) -> str:\n926:         # create a new unique name\n927:         name = \"_\".join(map(str, names))\n928:         # Strip the guard lookup L/G access\n929:         name = re.sub(r\"^[GL]\\['?(.*?)'?\\]$\", r\"\\1\", name)\n930:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz[123].qkv with abc.xyz_123.qkv\n931:         name = re.sub(r\"\\[(\\d+)\\]\", r\"_\\g<1>\", name)\n932:         # e.g. replace abc.xyz_123.qkv with abc_xyz_123_qkv\n933:         name = re.sub(r\"[^a-zA-Z0-9]\", \"_\", name)\n934: \n935:         if not name or not name[0].isalpha():\n936:             name = \"sub\" + name\n937: \n938:         return name\n939: \n940:     def register_static_attr_and_return_proxy(\n941:         self, attr_prefix: str, attr_value: Any\n942:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\n943:         attr_name = get_unique_name_wrt(attr_prefix, self.nn_modules)\n944:         # TODO `nn_modules` has been historically overloaded to store a lot more\n945:         # than just nn module objects, fix that.\n946:         self.nn_modules[attr_name] = attr_value\n947:         proxy = self.create_proxy(\"get_attr\", attr_name, (), {})\n948:         set_example_value(proxy.node, attr_value)\n949:         return proxy\n950: \n951:     def register_attr_or_module(\n952:         self,\n953:         target: Union[torch.nn.Module, torch.Tensor, Any],\n954:         *names: Any,\n955:         **options: Any,\n956:     ) -> VariableTracker:\n957:         if is_dynamic_nn_module(target, self.export):\n958:             # Instead of returning UnspecializedNNModuleVariable, call\n959:             # VariableTracker.build so that it is tracked for mutation.\n960:             return VariableTracker.build(self.current_tx, target, **options)\n961: \n962:         options = dict(options)\n963:         assert \"source\" in options\n964:         source = options[\"source\"]\n965:         assert not isinstance(source, ParamBufferSource)\n966: \n967:         if isinstance(target, torch.Tensor):\n968:             tracer = self.current_tracer\n969:             if not self.is_root_tracer():\n970:                 # For higher order ops, we don't want to insert the get_attr in\n971:                 # innermost graph. Instead, we want to raise the params/buffers\n972:                 # as inputs to the higher-order graph, and register them as\n973:                 # get_attrs in the root tracer.\n974: \n975:                 # Note that Dynamo will still call lift_tracked_freevar_to_input\n976:                 # when these inputs are encountered for the inner graph. The\n977:                 # only difference is what happens at the root tracer for\n978:                 # nn.Parameters vs free inputs. The free inputs are registered\n979:                 # as placeholders in the root graph, whereas the nn.Parameters\n980:                 # are registered as get_attr nodes in the root graph.\n981:                 tracer = self.root_tracer\n982: \n983:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n984:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\n985:                 self.param_name_to_source[module_key] = source\n986: \n987:                 # Check if the attr has already been registered. This can happen\n988:                 # when two different sources point to the same tensor.\n989:                 assert self.root_tx is not None\n990:                 if target in self.root_tx.output.side_effects:\n991:                     return self.root_tx.output.side_effects[target]\n992: \n993:                 if get_static_address_type(target) == \"guarded\" and not isinstance(\n994:                     source, NumpyTensorSource\n995:                 ):\n996:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.ID_MATCH))\n997:                 elif not is_constant_source(source):\n998:                     install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.TENSOR_MATCH))\n999: \n1000:                 vt = wrap_fx_proxy(\n1001:                     self.root_tx,\n1002:                     tracer.create_proxy(\"get_attr\", module_key, (), {}),\n1003:                     example_value=target,\n1004:                     **options,\n1005:                 )\n1006: \n1007:                 # Track the object so to avoid duplicate registration in case of\n1008:                 # different sources pointing to the same tensor object.\n1009:                 vt = self.root_tx.output.side_effects.track_object_existing(target, vt)\n1010: \n1011:                 assert \"tensor_dict\" not in vt.as_proxy().node.meta\n1012:                 vt.as_proxy().node.meta[\"tensor_dict\"] = _extract_tensor_dict(target)\n1013: \n1014:                 return vt\n1015: \n1016:         elif isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\n1017:             assert isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module)\n1018: \n1019:             if source:\n1020:                 install_guard(source.make_guard(GuardBuilder.NN_MODULE))\n1021: \n1022:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n1023:                     return NNModuleVariable(type(target), module_key, target, **options)\n1024: \n1025:             else:\n1026:                 # This is Dynamo created graph module, e.g., graph module coming\n1027:                 # from higher order ops. NNModuleVariable tracker can't be\n1028:                 # sourceless, so let's return a unspecializedNNModule variable\n1029:                 # tracker.\n1030:                 def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n1031:                     return variables.UnspecializedNNModuleVariable(target, **options)\n1032: \n1033:         elif isinstance(target, (torch.SymInt, torch.SymFloat)):\n1034:             # HACKY CODE REGION BEGIN\n1035:             # WE ARE PIGGYBACKING ON EXISTING INFRA TO REGISTER ATTRS\n1036:             # This ultimately gets written to self.nn_modules, which is unfortunate\n1037:             # Attrs that are tenors and symints and such need to be migrated to have their\n1038:             # own storage\n1039:             # alas, this is like this for now\n1040: \n1041:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n1042:                 return SymNodeVariable.create(\n1043:                     self,\n1044:                     self.create_proxy(\"get_attr\", module_key, (), {}),\n1045:                     sym_num=target,\n1046:                     **options,\n1047:                 )\n1048: \n1049:             # HACKY CODE REGION END\n1050:         else:\n1051: \n1052:             def wrap_name(module_key: str) -> VariableTracker:\n1053:                 self.output.update_co_names(module_key)\n1054:                 self.global_scope[module_key] = target\n1055:                 return VariableTracker.build(\n1056:                     self,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n1057:                     target,\n1058:                     ConstantSource(source_name=module_key),\n1059:                 )\n1060: \n1061:         for k, v in self.nn_modules.items():\n1062:             if v is target:\n1063:                 # it already exists\n1064:                 return wrap_name(k)\n1065: \n1066:         name = OutputGraph.module_key_name(*names)\n1067:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, self.global_scope)\n1068:         self.nn_modules[name] = target\n1069:         if isinstance(target, torch.nn.Module):\n1070: \n1071:             def register_leaf_name(leaf_name: str) -> None:\n1072:                 assert self.param_name_to_source is not None\n1073:                 new_source = ParamBufferSource(source, leaf_name)\n1074:                 new_name = f\"{name}.{leaf_name}\"\n1075:                 self.param_name_to_source[new_name] = new_source\n1076:                 if isinstance(source, LocalSource):\n1077:                     self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn[\n1078:                         OutputGraph.module_key_name(new_source.name())\n1079:                     ] = leaf_name\n1080: \n1081:             # annoying, but there are cases when we do not have parameters\n1082:             # see test_nn_moduledict_contains\n1083:             if hasattr(target, \"_parameters\"):\n1084:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_parameters():\n1085:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\n1086:             if hasattr(target, \"_buffers\"):\n1087:                 for leaf_name, _ in target.named_buffers():\n1088:                     register_leaf_name(leaf_name)\n1089: \n1090:         return wrap_name(name)\n1091: \n1092:     def handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(\n1093:         self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\"\n1094:     ) -> tuple[list[Instruction], dict[Source, Source]]:\n1095:         # If list inputs are stolen, but still needed after the function call, create aliases to keep them alive\n1096:         maybe_gm = self.local_scope.get(\"self\")\n1097:         stolen_list_names = get_locals_to_steal(maybe_gm)\n1098:         if not stolen_list_names:\n1099:             return [], {}\n1100: \n1101:         alias_insts = []\n1102:         needs_alias: dict[str, list[VariableTracker]] = {}\n1103: \n1104:         queue = [\n1105:             *tx.stack,\n1106:             *tx.symbolic_locals.values(),\n1107:             *self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations.keys(),\n1108:         ]\n1109: \n1110:         while queue:\n1111:             x = queue.pop()\n1112:             if isinstance(x, BaseListVariable):\n1113:                 assert isinstance(x.items, list)\n1114:                 queue += x.items\n1115:                 continue\n1116: \n1117:             if not (\n1118:                 (\n1119:                     x not in self.side_effects.store_attr_mutations\n1120:                     or isinstance(x.mutation_type, AttributeMutationExisting)\n1121:                 )\n1122:                 and isinstance(x.source, GetItemSource)\n1123:                 and isinstance(x.source.base, LocalSource)\n1124:                 and x.source.base.local_name in stolen_list_names\n1125:             ):\n1126:                 continue\n1127: \n1128:             stolen_name = x.source.base.local_name\n1129:             if stolen_name not in needs_alias:\n1130:                 needs_alias[stolen_name] = []\n1131:             needs_alias[stolen_name].append(x)\n1132: \n1133:         visited = {}\n1134:         overridden_sources: dict[Source, Source] = {}\n1135:         for arg in self.graphargs:\n1136:             if not (\n1137:                 isinstance(arg._example, list)\n1138:                 and isinstance(arg.source, LocalSource)\n1139:                 and arg.source.local_name in needs_alias\n1140:             ):\n1141:                 continue\n1142: \n1143:             # arg is a list that will be cleared by the compiled function\n1144:             list_name = arg.source.local_name\n1145:             assert list_name in self.code_options[\"co_varnames\"]\n1146:             for x in needs_alias[list_name]:\n1147:                 # Skip if already handled.\n1148:                 if x.source in overridden_sources:\n1149:                     continue\n1150: \n1151:                 # A small codegen optimization because we might have different\n1152:                 # VariableTrackers that share the same source.\n1153:                 list_idx = x.source.index  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n1154:                 if list_idx not in visited:\n1155:                     alias_name = self.new_var(\n1156:                         f\"{list_name}_ref\"\n1157:                     )  # self.new_var already adds unique id suffix\n1158: \n1159:                     visited[list_idx] = alias_name\n1160:                     # bytecode of `alias_name = list_name[list_idx]`\n1161:                     alias_insts.extend(\n1162:                         [\n1163:                             create_instruction(\"LOAD_FAST\", argval=list_name),\n1164:                             create_load_const(list_idx),\n1165:                             create_instruction(\"BINARY_SUBSCR\"),\n1166:                             create_instruction(\"STORE_FAST\", argval=alias_name),\n1167:                         ]\n1168:                     )\n1169: \n1170:                 # operate on alias, handled by suffix codegen\n1171:                 old_source = x.source\n1172:                 overridden_sources[old_source] = LocalSource(visited[list_idx])\n1173: \n1174:         # NOTE: we need `overridden_sources` because (1) we want to codegen for\n1175:         # these list items to use the new local source, but (2) we want to avoid\n1176:         # updating `source` in place because that might break invariants in\n1177:         # other parts of Dynamo like guards.\n1178:         return alias_insts, overridden_sources\n1179: \n1180:     def _get_stack_values_to_restore(\n1181:         self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\", stack_pops: int\n1182:     ) -> tuple[list[VariableTracker], list[str], StackLocalsMetadata]:\n1183:         \"\"\"\n1184:         Gets the stack + locals values belonging to tx that need to be restored.\n1185: \n1186:         Also prunes dead tx locals and realizes all VTs in the tx's stack.\n1187: \n1188:         NullVariables in stack/locals will NOT be restored, unless they are the top `stack_pops`\n1189:         elements of the stack - it is expected that the next instruction to run will pop the top\n1190:         `stack_pops` elements of the stack, so we should codegen NULLs.\n1191: \n1192:         Returns:\n1193:             - stack_values: stack and locals values that need to be restored\n1194:             - restore_vars: names of locals corresponding to the locals part of `stack_values`\n1195:             - meta: locations of NULLs and ContextWrappingVariables in the stack/locals\n1196:                 (ignores the top `stack_pops` values on the stack)\n1197:         \"\"\"\n1198:         tx.prune_dead_locals()\n1199: \n1200:         stack_values = []\n1201:         meta = StackLocalsMetadata()\n1202: \n1203:         # realize any unrealized tensor VTs in case they\n1204:         # need to be added to self.nn_modules as attributes\n1205:         for i, value in enumerate(tx.stack):\n1206:             variables.LazyVariableTracker.realize_all(value)\n1207:             # ignore top `stack_pops` values on the stack\n1208:             if len(tx.stack) - i <= stack_pops:\n1209:                 stack_values.append(value)\n1210:                 continue\n1211:             if isinstance(value, NullVariable):\n1212:                 meta.stack_null_idxes.append(i)\n1213:             else:\n1214:                 stack_values.append(value)\n1215:             if isinstance(value, ContextWrappingVariable):\n1216:                 target_values = (\n1217:                     () if value.target_values is None else tuple(value.target_values)\n1218:                 )\n1219:                 # NOTE: track index in stack after NULLs have been removed\n1220:                 meta.stack_ctx_args.append((len(stack_values) - 1, target_values))\n1221:                 meta.stack_ctx_idxes_orig.append(i)\n1222: \n1223:         # Add all the local vars to the \"stack\" so restore at the end\n1224:         restore_vars: list[str] = []\n1225:         val_to_names: dict[VariableTracker, list[str]] = {}\n1226:         # NB: Typically (i.e., for graph compile from RETURN_VALUE),\n1227:         # symbolic_locals will be empty at this point, as prune_dead_locals\n1228:         # will clear out all of symbolic_locals because RETURN_VALUE is the\n1229:         # last instruction and no more locals are used.  The fanciness here\n1230:         # is only needed for partial graphs.\n1231:         # NOTE: All cell and free variables are represented as CellVariable,\n1232:         # so checks for NULLs and context managers in the case of codegen'ing resume\n1233:         # functions will not be performed on them. This is expected behavior.\n1234:         for k, v in tx.symbolic_locals.items():\n1235:             # Note! this explicitly uses .local_name for matching\n1236:             # Failure to do so will cause spurious registrations in val_to_names.\n1237:             # This will in turn result in spurious variables showing up in the graph.\n1238:             # This was very tricky to debug. For an example, dump the graph at call_user_compiler\n1239:             # while running test_subgraphs.py\n1240:             if isinstance(v.source, LocalSource) and v.source.local_name == k:\n1241:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state\n1242:             if isinstance(v, CellVariable) and v.local_name == k:\n1243:                 continue  # no need to restore initial state\n1244:             # Do not load variable if it is NULL.\n1245:             if sys.version_info >= (3, 12):\n1246:                 # Continuation function will load the NULL for v.\n1247:                 if type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v):\n1248:                     meta.locals_null_keys.append(k)\n1249:                     continue\n1250:             else:\n1251:                 # A variable should never be NULL in < 3.12\n1252:                 assert not type.__instancecheck__(NullVariable, v)\n1253:             if isinstance(v, ContextWrappingVariable):\n1254:                 target_values = (\n1255:                     () if v.target_values is None else tuple(v.target_values)\n1256:                 )\n1257:                 meta.locals_ctx_args.append((k, target_values))\n1258:             if v not in val_to_names:\n1259:                 val_to_names[v] = []\n1260:             val_to_names[v].append(k)\n1261:         for v in val_to_names.keys():\n1262:             restore_vars.extend(val_to_names[v])\n1263:             stack_values.extend([v] * len(val_to_names[v]))\n1264: \n1265:         return stack_values, restore_vars, meta\n1266: \n1267:     def compile_subgraph(\n1268:         self,\n1269:         tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n1270:         reason: GraphCompileReason,\n1271:         partial_convert: bool = False,\n1272:         stack_pops: int = 0,\n1273:     ) -> list[StackLocalsMetadata]:\n1274:         \"\"\"\n1275:         Compiles the current subgraph, with inputs w.r.t. self.root_tx, and codegens:\n1276:             - Call the compiled subgraph\n1277:             - Apply side effects\n1278:             - Codegen stack and locals\n1279:             - Store the locals\n1280: \n1281:         Python does not allow NULL to be an arg to a function, so we do not codegen NULLs on the stack,\n1282:         unless the value is one of the top `stack_pops` values on the stack (these values are expected to be\n1283:         popped immediately after this generated code. The prologue of the resume function is expected to restore\n1284:         any dropped NULLs.\n1285: \n1286:         Returns stack indices and locals keys where we dropped NULLs, and where we found inactive context manager objects.\n1287:         \"\"\"\n1288: \n1289:         assert self.root_tx is not None\n1290: \n1291:         # FIXME temporary assert to make sure we're not accidentally compiling nested graph breaks\n1292:         # before we're done the full implementation\n1293:         assert self.root_tx is tx\n1294: \n1295:         # bytecode tracing has finished. Pop the context manager for dynamo_timed\n1296:         self.mark_bytecode_tracing_stop()\n1297: \n1298:         self.partial_convert = partial_convert\n1299:         self.compile_subgraph_reason = reason\n1300:         self.should_exit = True\n1301: \n1302:         log.debug(\"COMPILING GRAPH due to %s\", reason)\n1303: \n1304:         # prefix instructions (Python 3.11+)\n1305:         prefix_insts: list[Instruction] = []\n1306:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n1307:             for inst in tx.prefix_insts:\n1308:                 if inst.opname == \"MAKE_CELL\":\n1309:                     prefix_insts.append(\n1310:                         create_instruction(\"MAKE_CELL\", argval=inst.argval)\n1311:                     )\n1312:                 elif inst.opname == \"COPY_FREE_VARS\":\n1313:                     prefix_insts.append(\n1314:                         create_instruction(\n1315:                             \"COPY_FREE_VARS\", arg=len(tx.code_options[\"co_freevars\"])\n1316:                         )\n1317:                     )\n1318:                 else:\n1319:                     prefix_insts.append(copy.copy(inst))\n1320:         self.add_output_instructions(prefix_insts)\n1321: \n1322:         assert not (self.pregraph_bytecode and self.export), (\n1323:             \"export does not support pregraph_bytecode\"\n1324:         )\n1325:         self.add_output_instructions(self.pregraph_bytecode)\n1326: \n1327:         alias_insts, overridden_sources = self.handle_aliases_for_stolen_lists(\n1328:             self.root_tx\n1329:         )\n1330:         self.add_output_instructions(alias_insts)\n1331: \n1332:         # Exit from all context manager variables to make sure global state is restored\n1333:         for block in reversed(self.root_tx.block_stack):\n1334:             block.exit(self.root_tx, is_graph_break=reason.graph_break)\n1335: \n1336:         self.cleanup_graph()\n1337: \n1338:         # stack values and restore vars for each frame are pushed in reverse order\n1339:         # i.e. last element corresponds to root frame, first element corresponds to current frame\n1340:         all_stack_values = []\n1341:         all_restore_vars = []\n1342:         all_stack_locals_metas = []\n1343:         cur_tx: Optional[InstructionTranslatorBase] = tx\n1344:         while True:\n1345:             assert cur_tx is not None\n1346:             # this should have been checked by the caller\n1347:             assert all(block.can_restore() for block in cur_tx.block_stack)\n1348:             stack_values, restore_vars, meta = self._get_stack_values_to_restore(\n1349:                 cur_tx, stack_pops\n1350:             )\n1351:             all_stack_values.append(stack_values)\n1352:             all_restore_vars.append(restore_vars)\n1353:             all_stack_locals_metas.append(meta)\n1354:             if cur_tx is self.root_tx:\n1355:                 break\n1356:             cur_tx = tx.parent\n1357: \n1358:         # Use nn.Module \"proxies\" in the constructed GraphModule so that\n1359:         # the resulting GM does not hold additional strong references to the original modules.\n1360:         # This prevents a strong ref cycle where Dynamo created code holds on to references\n1361:         # to modules that also have Dynamo code cache invalidation checks.\n1362:         # When cache invalidation runs, the generated GM will be invalidated, which also deletes\n1363:         # the proxies.\n1364:         nn_modules_proxies = {\n1365:             name: nn_module_proxy(mod) for name, mod in self.nn_modules.items()\n1366:         }\n1367:         root = FakeRootModule(nn_modules_proxies)\n1368: \n1369:         from .decorators import disable\n1370: \n1371:         # to handle random calls\n1372:         if len(self.random_calls) > 0:\n1373:             random_calls_instructions = []\n1374:             self.random_values_var = self.new_var(\"random_values\")\n1375:             rand_fn = disable(\n1376:                 _get_gen_rand_values_fn(self.random_calls),\n1377: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL876-1376"
  }
}
