  "content": "{\"content\":\"1377:                 reason=\\\"do not trace into Dynamo rng recovery function\\\",\\n1378:             )\\n1379:             rand_fn_name = self.install_global(\\\"__gen_rand_values\\\", rand_fn)\\n1380:             codegen = PyCodegen(\\n1381:                 self.root_tx, root, overridden_sources=overridden_sources\\n1382:             )\\n1383:             random_calls_instructions.extend(\\n1384:                 codegen.load_function_name(rand_fn_name, True)\\n1385:             )\\n1386:             random_calls_instructions.extend(create_call_function(0, False))\\n1387:             random_calls_instructions.append(\\n1388:                 codegen.create_store(self.random_values_var),\\n1389:             )\\n1390:             self.add_output_instructions(random_calls_instructions)\\n1391: \\n1392:         # call compiled fx graph\\n1393:         graph_output_var = None\\n1394:         stored_graph_output_var = False\\n1395:         root_stack_values = all_stack_values[-1]\\n1396:         if (\\n1397:             self.root_tx is tx\\n1398:             and root_stack_values\\n1399:             and all(\\n1400:                 not isinstance(\\n1401:                     v,\\n1402:                     (\\n1403:                         UnspecializedPythonVariable,\\n1404:                         NumpyNdarrayVariable,\\n1405:                         TensorWithTFOverrideVariable,\\n1406:                     ),\\n1407:                 )\\n1408:                 and not (isinstance(v, SymNodeVariable) and v.python_type() is float)\\n1409:                 for v in root_stack_values\\n1410:             )\\n1411:             and all(isinstance(x, TensorVariable) for x in root_stack_values)\\n1412:             and len(set(root_stack_values)) == len(root_stack_values)\\n1413:             and self.side_effects.is_empty()\\n1414:             and not tx.debug_locals\\n1415:             and not self.backward_state\\n1416:             and not all_stack_locals_metas[-1].stack_null_idxes\\n1417:             and not all_stack_locals_metas[-1].locals_null_keys\\n1418:         ):\\n1419:             # optimization to generate better code in a common case\\n1420:             self.add_output_instructions(\\n1421:                 self.compile_and_call_fx_graph(\\n1422:                     tx, list(reversed(root_stack_values)), root\\n1423:                 )\\n1424:                 + [create_instruction(\\\"UNPACK_SEQUENCE\\\", arg=len(root_stack_values))]\\n1425:             )\\n1426:         else:\\n1427:             graph_output_var = self.new_var(\\\"graph_out\\\")\\n1428:             # load stack values in a flat manner for now - will likely change later.\\n1429:             stack_values_flat = [\\n1430:                 val for vals in reversed(all_stack_values) for val in vals\\n1431:             ]\\n1432:             pass1 = PyCodegen(\\n1433:                 self.root_tx,\\n1434:                 root,\\n1435:                 graph_output_var,\\n1436:                 overridden_sources=overridden_sources,\\n1437:             )\\n1438:             self.codegen_suffix(tx, stack_values_flat, pass1)\\n1439: \\n1440:             # Use `pass1.uses` to selectively cache multi-user variables into a\\n1441:             # temporary local source. This (a). speeds up loading VTs with long\\n1442:             # chained source, and (b). avoids redundantly saving single-user VT\\n1443:             # into a temporary local.\\n1444:             tempvars = {}  # type: ignore[var-annotated]\\n1445:             for val, count in pass1.uses.items():\\n1446:                 # If it's already a local source, no need to cache it\\n1447:                 if count > 1 and not istype(val, (SyntheticLocalSource, LocalSource)):\\n1448:                     tempvars[val] = None\\n1449:             pass2 = PyCodegen(\\n1450:                 self.root_tx,\\n1451:                 root,\\n1452:                 graph_output_var,\\n1453:                 tempvars=tempvars,\\n1454:                 overridden_sources=overridden_sources,\\n1455:             )\\n1456:             self.codegen_suffix(tx, stack_values_flat, pass2)\\n1457: \\n1458:             output = []\\n1459:             if count_calls(self.graph) != 0 or len(pass2.graph_outputs) != 0:\\n1460:                 output.extend(\\n1461:                     self.compile_and_call_fx_graph(tx, pass2.graph_output_vars(), root)\\n1462:                 )\\n1463: \\n1464:                 if len(pass2.graph_outputs) != 0:\\n1465:                     output.append(pass2.create_store(graph_output_var))\\n1466:                     stored_graph_output_var = True\\n1467:                 else:\\n1468:                     output.append(create_instruction(\\\"POP_TOP\\\"))\\n1469:             else:\\n1470:                 # NB: Important to run compiler collective even when there is\\n1471:                 # a graph break\\n1472:                 self.run_compiler_collective()\\n1473:             self.add_output_instructions(output + pass2.get_instructions())\\n1474: \\n1475:         # restore all the live local vars of the root\\n1476:         local_restore_cg = PyCodegen(\\n1477:             self.root_tx, overridden_sources=overridden_sources\\n1478:         )\\n1479:         # TODO this local restoration should be removed when fully implementing nested graph breaks\\n1480:         self.add_output_instructions(\\n1481:             [\\n1482:                 local_restore_cg.create_store(var)\\n1483:                 for var in reversed(all_restore_vars[-1])\\n1484:             ]\\n1485:         )\\n1486: \\n1487:         if graph_output_var and stored_graph_output_var:\\n1488:             self.add_output_instructions(\\n1489:                 [local_restore_cg.create_delete(graph_output_var)]\\n1490:             )\\n1491: \\n1492:         if self.export:\\n1493:             from torch.export._trace import _ExportModuleSpecTrackerDict\\n1494: \\n1495:             potential_side_effects = []\\n1496:             for var in self.side_effects._get_modified_vars():\\n1497:                 if hasattr(var, \\\"mutation_type\\\"):\\n1498:                     mut_type = var.mutation_type\\n1499:                     # Make sure to skip codegen specific mutations\\n1500:                     if isinstance(\\n1501:                         mut_type, (AttributeMutationExisting, ValueMutationExisting)\\n1502:                     ):\\n1503:                         # export uses tracepoint pass to dump submodule inp/out spec\\n1504:                         # into global state, so we filter it here\\n1505:                         if not (\\n1506:                             isinstance(var, UserDefinedDictVariable)\\n1507:                             and isinstance(var.value, _ExportModuleSpecTrackerDict)\\n1508:                         ):\\n1509:                             potential_side_effects.append(var)\\n1510: \\n1511:             side_effect_refs = [\\n1512:                 _get_source_debug_name(var.source) for var in potential_side_effects\\n1513:             ]\\n1514: \\n1515:             if len(side_effect_refs):\\n1516:                 warnings.warn(\\n1517:                     f\\\"While exporting, we found certain side effects happened in the model.forward. \\\"\\n1518:                     f\\\"Here are the list of potential sources you can double check: {side_effect_refs}\\\"\\n1519:                 )\\n1520: \\n1521:         return all_stack_locals_metas\\n1522: \\n1523:     def codegen_suffix(\\n1524:         self,\\n1525:         tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n1526:         stack_values: list[VariableTracker],\\n1527:         cg: PyCodegen,\\n1528:     ) -> None:\\n1529:         # NOTE: `codegen_save_tempvars` must run first to update `source` fields\\n1530:         # for variables with `AttributeMutationNew`, as they don't implement\\n1531:         # `reconstruct` themselves.\\n1532:         self.side_effects.codegen_save_tempvars(cg)\\n1533:         if self.backward_state:\\n1534:             assert not self.export\\n1535:             for name, val in self.backward_state.items():\\n1536:                 cg(val)\\n1537:                 assert self.backward_state_var is not None\\n1538:                 cg.append_output(cg.create_load(self.backward_state_var))\\n1539:                 cg.store_attr(name)\\n1540:         self.side_effects.codegen_hooks(cg)\\n1541: \\n1542:         # Return variables used for logging at the end\\n1543:         for debug_var, args in tx.debug_locals:\\n1544:             cg.add_push_null(lambda: cg(debug_var))\\n1545:             for arg in args:\\n1546:                 cg(arg)\\n1547:             cg.extend_output(create_call_function(len(args), False))\\n1548:             cg.extend_output([create_instruction(\\\"POP_TOP\\\")])\\n1549: \\n1550:         cg.restore_stack(stack_values, value_from_source=not tx.export)\\n1551:         self.side_effects.codegen_update_mutated(cg)\\n1552: \\n1553:     def cleanup_graph(self) -> None:\\n1554:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1555:         Remove \\\"creation_timestamp\\\" from node meta\\n1556: \\n1557:         Remove this pattern from the graph:\\n1558:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(False)\\n1559:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(True)\\n1560:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1561:         assert self.should_exit\\n1562:         nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\\n1563:         for node in nodes:\\n1564:             node.meta.pop(\\\"creation_timestamp\\\", None)\\n1565: \\n1566:         grad_enabled = torch.is_grad_enabled()\\n1567:         for node1, node2 in zip(nodes, nodes[1:]):\\n1568:             if (\\n1569:                 node1.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\\n1570:                 and tuple(node1.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\\n1571:                 and not node1._erased\\n1572:             ):\\n1573:                 grad_enabled = node1.args[0]\\n1574:                 if (\\n1575:                     node2.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\\n1576:                     and tuple(node2.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\\n1577:                     and not node2._erased\\n1578:                 ):\\n1579:                     grad_enabled = node2.args[0]\\n1580:                     self.graph.erase_node(node1)\\n1581:                     self.graph.erase_node(node2)\\n1582: \\n1583:     def get_graph_sizes_structured(self) -> dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]]:\\n1584:         ret: dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]] = {}\\n1585:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n1586:             example_value = node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\", None)\\n1587:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):\\n1588:                 size = example_value.size()\\n1589:                 ret[node.name] = [s if isinstance(s, int) else repr(s) for s in size]\\n1590:         return ret\\n1591: \\n1592:     def get_graph_sizes(self, name: str) -> str:\\n1593:         graph_sizes_str = \\\"TRACED GRAPH TENSOR SIZES\\\\n\\\"\\n1594:         graph_sizes_str += f\\\"===== {name} =====\\\\n\\\"\\n1595:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n1596:             example_value = node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\", None)\\n1597:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):\\n1598:                 size = example_value.size()\\n1599:                 graph_sizes_str += f\\\"{node.name}: {tuple(size)}\\\\n\\\"\\n1600:                 concrete_size = []\\n1601:                 has_symint = False\\n1602:                 for sz in size:\\n1603:                     if isinstance(sz, int):\\n1604:                         concrete_size.append(sz)\\n1605:                     elif isinstance(sz, torch.SymInt):\\n1606:                         has_symint = True\\n1607:                         concrete_size.append(sz.node.hint)\\n1608:                     else:\\n1609:                         break\\n1610:                 else:\\n1611:                     if has_symint:\\n1612:                         graph_sizes_str += (\\n1613:                             f\\\"{node.name} (concrete): {tuple(concrete_size)}\\\\n\\\"\\n1614:                         )\\n1615:         return graph_sizes_str\\n1616: \\n1617:     @contextlib.contextmanager\\n1618:     def restore_global_state(self) -> Any:\\n1619:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1620:         Momentarily restores the global state to what it was prior to tracing the current output\\n1621:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1622:         prior_global_state = self.tracing_context.global_context.copy_graphstate()\\n1623:         current_global_state: dict[str, tuple[Any, bool]] = {}\\n1624:         self.save_global_state(out=current_global_state)\\n1625:         try:\\n1626:             # Set to state prior to tracing the graph\\n1627:             self.tracing_context.global_context.restore_graphstate(prior_global_state)\\n1628:             yield\\n1629:         finally:\\n1630:             # Reset to state at the current time (e.g. before calling the user compiler)\\n1631:             self.tracing_context.global_context.restore_graphstate(\\n1632:                 GlobalContextCheckpointState(current_global_state)\\n1633:             )\\n1634: \\n1635:     def run_compiler_collective(self) -> None:\\n1636:         tx = self.root_tx\\n1637:         assert tx is not None\\n1638:         if (ds := tx.distributed_state) is not None and ds.all_states is None:\\n1639:             compile_pg = ds.compile_pg\\n1640:             log.info(\\\"compiler_collective %s\\\", ds.local_state)\\n1641:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\\n1642:                 \\\"artifact\\\",\\n1643:                 metadata_fn=lambda: {\\n1644:                     \\\"name\\\": \\\"compiler_collective\\\",\\n1645:                     \\\"encoding\\\": \\\"string\\\",\\n1646:                 },\\n1647:                 payload_fn=lambda: ds.local_state.render(),\\n1648:             )\\n1649:             device_types = compile_pg._device_types\\n1650:             assert len(device_types) == 1, (\\n1651:                 \\\"Expect only one device type but got {}\\\".format(\\\"+\\\".join(device_types))\\n1652:             )\\n1653:             with (\\n1654:                 get_interface_for_device(device_types.pop()).device(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n1655:                     compile_pg.rank() % torch.accelerator.device_count()\\n1656:                 ),\\n1657:                 dynamo_timed(\\\"compiler_collective\\\", log_pt2_compile_event=True),\\n1658:             ):\\n1659:                 all_states: list[Any] = [None] * compile_pg.size()\\n1660:                 dist.all_gather_object(all_states, ds.local_state, group=compile_pg)\\n1661:                 ds.all_states = all_states\\n1662:             # Clear speculation log, because are tracing may diverge due to\\n1663:             # this information from the compiler collective\\n1664:             tx.speculation_log.clear()\\n1665:             raise exc.CompileCollectiveRestartAnalysis\\n1666: \\n1667:     def compile_and_call_fx_graph(\\n1668:         self,\\n1669:         tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\",\\n1670:         rv: list[VariableTracker],\\n1671:         root: FakeRootModule,\\n1672:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\\n1673:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1674:         Generate code from self.graph and return the Instruction()s to\\n1675:         call that generated code.\\n1676: \\n1677:         Code is generated w.r.t. self.root_tx.\\n1678:         tx is only used for preserving GraphModule metadata\\n1679:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n1680:         with torch._guards.TracingContext.clear_frame():\\n1681:             from .decorators import disable\\n1682: \\n1683:             assert self.should_exit\\n1684: \\n1685:             self.run_compiler_collective()\\n1686: \\n1687:             name = unique_id(\\\"__compiled_fn\\\", with_uuid=True)\\n1688: \\n1689:             assert isinstance(rv, list)\\n1690:             assert isinstance(root, FakeRootModule)\\n1691: \\n1692:             output_node = self.create_node(\\n1693:                 \\\"output\\\",\\n1694:                 \\\"output\\\",\\n1695:                 (self.current_tracer.create_arg(tuple(x.as_proxy() for x in rv)),),\\n1696:                 {},\\n1697:             )\\n1698:             sub_gms = self.dedup_pass()\\n1699:             root.add_nn_modules(sub_gms)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n1700: \\n1701:             self.current_tracer._maybe_preserve_original_meta(tx, output_node)\\n1702:             if not config.do_not_emit_runtime_asserts:\\n1703:                 # There is a rare scenario where codegen_suffix adds a new entry\\n1704:                 # to self.nn_modules while `root` knows only about the\\n1705:                 # nn_modules at the time of its creation. This causes failures\\n1706:                 # while creating the graph module because self.graph and root\\n1707:                 # are out of sync. This only happens for `get_attr` nodes, so\\n1708:                 # here we clean up the get_attr nodes that are unused.\\n1709:                 self.remove_unused_get_attr_nodes()\\n1710:                 insert_deferred_runtime_asserts(\\n1711:                     fx.GraphModule(root, self.graph),\\n1712:                     self.shape_env,\\n1713:                     name,\\n1714:                     export=self.export,\\n1715:                 )\\n1716:             # NB: deferred runtime asserts can keep graphargs live, so make sure\\n1717:             # those are inserted before pruning\\n1718:             self.remove_unused_graphargs()\\n1719:             ncalls = count_calls(self.graph)\\n1720:             counters[\\\"stats\\\"][\\\"calls_captured\\\"] += ncalls\\n1721: \\n1722:             self.remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs()\\n1723: \\n1724:             # free a bit of memory\\n1725:             self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n1726: \\n1727:             gm = _make_graph_module(root, self.graph)\\n1728: \\n1729:             # Saved tensors hooks are not used by the graph.\\n1730:             # GraphModule by default only copies used in the graph submodules.\\n1731:             # Copying them into the result graph manually.\\n1732:             if self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names:\\n1733:                 for subgraph_name in self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names:\\n1734:                     setattr(gm, subgraph_name, getattr(root, subgraph_name))\\n1735: \\n1736:             for register_finalizer in self.register_finalizer_fns:\\n1737:                 register_finalizer(gm)\\n1738: \\n1739:             gm._backend_id = name\\n1740:             gm.compile_subgraph_reason = self.compile_subgraph_reason\\n1741:             gm.meta[\\\"dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn\\\"] = (\\n1742:                 self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.copy()\\n1743:             )\\n1744:             gm.meta[\\\"dynamo_compile_id\\\"] = self.dynamo_compile_id\\n1745: \\n1746:             graph_code_log.debug(\\n1747:                 \\\"%s\\\",\\n1748:                 lazy_format_graph_code(\\n1749:                     name, gm, include_stride=True, include_device=True, colored=True\\n1750:                 ),\\n1751:             )\\n1752:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\\n1753:                 \\\"dynamo_output_graph\\\",\\n1754:                 lambda: {\\\"sizes\\\": self.get_graph_sizes_structured()},\\n1755:                 payload_fn=lambda: gm.print_readable(\\n1756:                     print_output=False, include_stride=True, include_device=True\\n1757:                 ),\\n1758:             )\\n1759:             self.call_cleanup_hooks()\\n1760:             old_fake_mode = self.tracing_context.fake_mode\\n1761:             assert old_fake_mode is not None\\n1762:             if not self.export:\\n1763:                 import torch._functorch.config as _config\\n1764: \\n1765:                 with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\\n1766:                     # TODO(voz): The way export uses gm, and fake tensors, is not supported with us resetting\\n1767:                     backend_fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\\n1768:                         shape_env=old_fake_mode.shape_env,\\n1769:                     )\\n1770:                 # TODO(voz): Ostensibily, this should be scoped and\\n1771:                 # restore back to old_fake_mode, but doing so currently violates\\n1772:                 # a lot of fake_tensor ownership assumptions and runs afoul of detect_fake_mode\\n1773:                 self.tracing_context.fake_mode = backend_fake_mode\\n1774: \\n1775:             with self.restore_global_state():\\n1776:                 compiled_fn = self.call_user_compiler(gm, self.example_inputs())\\n1777: \\n1778:             from torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _LazyGraphModule\\n1779: \\n1780:             if isinstance(compiled_fn, _LazyGraphModule) or (\\n1781:                 isinstance(getattr(compiled_fn, \\\"__self__\\\", None), _LazyGraphModule)\\n1782:                 and compiled_fn.__name__ == \\\"_lazy_forward\\\"  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n1783:             ):\\n1784:                 # Since dynamo will run the forward method for the GraphModule shortly\\n1785:                 # anyways, it does not hurt to do the real recompilation here if\\n1786:                 # this is a _LazyGraphModule. This makes it easier for dynamo to\\n1787:                 # optimize a _LazyGraphModule.\\n1788: \\n1789:                 lazy_gm = (\\n1790:                     compiled_fn\\n1791:                     if isinstance(compiled_fn, _LazyGraphModule)\\n1792:                     else compiled_fn.__self__  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n1793:                 )\\n1794: \\n1795:                 _LazyGraphModule.force_recompile(lazy_gm)\\n1796: \\n1797:                 if not isinstance(compiled_fn, _LazyGraphModule):\\n1798:                     # replace compiled_fn with the real forward method\\n1799:                     compiled_fn = lazy_gm.forward\\n1800: \\n1801:             if self.package is not None:\\n1802:                 self.package.add_backend_id(name, compiled_fn)\\n1803: \\n1804:             compiled_fn = disable(\\n1805:                 compiled_fn, reason=\\\"do not trace Dynamo-compiled graph\\\"\\n1806:             )\\n1807: \\n1808:             counters[\\\"stats\\\"][\\\"unique_graphs\\\"] += 1\\n1809:             assert old_fake_mode.shape_env is not None\\n1810:             if specializations := old_fake_mode.shape_env.specializations:\\n1811:                 specialization_guards = []\\n1812:                 specialization_cache: dict[Specialization, Callable[[Any], Any]] = {}\\n1813:                 sources = [a.source for a in self.graphargs]\\n1814:                 for specialization in specializations:\\n1815:                     source_index = sources.index(specialization.source)\\n1816:                     check_fn_source = inspect.getsource(specialization.check_fn).strip()\\n1817:                     # Required because the LABDA_GUARD API requires a root guard manager\\n1818:                     unused_root_guard_manager = RootGuardManager()\\n1819:                     check_fn = guards.LAMBDA_GUARD(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n1820:                         unused_root_guard_manager,\\n1821:                         specialization.check_fn,\\n1822:                         [check_fn_source],\\n1823:                     )\\n1824: \\n1825:                     log.debug(\\n1826:                         \\\"Compiling backend specialized graph with specialization=%s\\\",\\n1827:                         check_fn_source,\\n1828:                     )\\n1829: \\n1830:                     specialization_guards.append(\\n1831:                         (\\n1832:                             functools.partial(\\n1833:                                 lambda idx, args, check_fn=check_fn: check_fn(\\n1834:                                     args[idx]\\n1835:                                 ),\\n1836:                                 source_index,\\n1837:                             ),\\n1838:                             specialization,\\n1839:                         )\\n1840:                     )\\n1841: \\n1842:                 @torch._dynamo.disable(reason=\\\"do not trace Dynamo-compiled graph\\\")  # type: ignore[misc]\\n1843:                 def specialized_dispatch(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:\\n1844:                     for check_fn, specialization in specialization_guards:\\n1845:                         if check_fn(args):\\n1846:                             if specialization in specialization_cache:\\n1847:                                 return specialization_cache[specialization](\\n1848:                                     *args, **kwargs\\n1849:                                 )\\n1850: \\n1851:                             with self.shape_env.patch_source_specialization(\\n1852:                                 specialization.source, specialization.check_fn\\n1853:                             ):\\n1854:                                 # Modify gm so AOTAutogradCache key changes per specialization\\n1855:                                 gm.meta[\\\"specialization\\\"] = specialization\\n1856:                                 example_inputs: list[Tensor] = list(args)\\n1857:                                 with tracing(self.tracing_context):\\n1858:                                     specialization_cache[specialization] = (\\n1859:                                         self.call_user_compiler(gm, example_inputs)\\n1860:                                     )\\n1861: \\n1862:                             return specialization_cache[specialization](*args, **kwargs)\\n1863:                     return compiled_fn(*args, **kwargs)\\n1864: \\n1865:                 # This is safe because we pre-process name to be unique\\n1866:                 self.install_global_unsafe(name, specialized_dispatch)\\n1867:             else:\\n1868:                 # This is safe because we pre-process name to be unique\\n1869:                 self.install_global_unsafe(name, compiled_fn)\\n1870: \\n1871:             assert self.root_tx is not None\\n1872:             cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\\n1873:             cg.make_call_generated_code(name)\\n1874:             return cg.get_instructions()\\n1875: \\n1876:     @property\\n1877:     def placeholders(self) -> list[fx.Node]:\\n1878: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1377-1877\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1377:                 reason=\"do not trace into Dynamo rng recovery function\",\n1378:             )\n1379:             rand_fn_name = self.install_global(\"__gen_rand_values\", rand_fn)\n1380:             codegen = PyCodegen(\n1381:                 self.root_tx, root, overridden_sources=overridden_sources\n1382:             )\n1383:             random_calls_instructions.extend(\n1384:                 codegen.load_function_name(rand_fn_name, True)\n1385:             )\n1386:             random_calls_instructions.extend(create_call_function(0, False))\n1387:             random_calls_instructions.append(\n1388:                 codegen.create_store(self.random_values_var),\n1389:             )\n1390:             self.add_output_instructions(random_calls_instructions)\n1391: \n1392:         # call compiled fx graph\n1393:         graph_output_var = None\n1394:         stored_graph_output_var = False\n1395:         root_stack_values = all_stack_values[-1]\n1396:         if (\n1397:             self.root_tx is tx\n1398:             and root_stack_values\n1399:             and all(\n1400:                 not isinstance(\n1401:                     v,\n1402:                     (\n1403:                         UnspecializedPythonVariable,\n1404:                         NumpyNdarrayVariable,\n1405:                         TensorWithTFOverrideVariable,\n1406:                     ),\n1407:                 )\n1408:                 and not (isinstance(v, SymNodeVariable) and v.python_type() is float)\n1409:                 for v in root_stack_values\n1410:             )\n1411:             and all(isinstance(x, TensorVariable) for x in root_stack_values)\n1412:             and len(set(root_stack_values)) == len(root_stack_values)\n1413:             and self.side_effects.is_empty()\n1414:             and not tx.debug_locals\n1415:             and not self.backward_state\n1416:             and not all_stack_locals_metas[-1].stack_null_idxes\n1417:             and not all_stack_locals_metas[-1].locals_null_keys\n1418:         ):\n1419:             # optimization to generate better code in a common case\n1420:             self.add_output_instructions(\n1421:                 self.compile_and_call_fx_graph(\n1422:                     tx, list(reversed(root_stack_values)), root\n1423:                 )\n1424:                 + [create_instruction(\"UNPACK_SEQUENCE\", arg=len(root_stack_values))]\n1425:             )\n1426:         else:\n1427:             graph_output_var = self.new_var(\"graph_out\")\n1428:             # load stack values in a flat manner for now - will likely change later.\n1429:             stack_values_flat = [\n1430:                 val for vals in reversed(all_stack_values) for val in vals\n1431:             ]\n1432:             pass1 = PyCodegen(\n1433:                 self.root_tx,\n1434:                 root,\n1435:                 graph_output_var,\n1436:                 overridden_sources=overridden_sources,\n1437:             )\n1438:             self.codegen_suffix(tx, stack_values_flat, pass1)\n1439: \n1440:             # Use `pass1.uses` to selectively cache multi-user variables into a\n1441:             # temporary local source. This (a). speeds up loading VTs with long\n1442:             # chained source, and (b). avoids redundantly saving single-user VT\n1443:             # into a temporary local.\n1444:             tempvars = {}  # type: ignore[var-annotated]\n1445:             for val, count in pass1.uses.items():\n1446:                 # If it's already a local source, no need to cache it\n1447:                 if count > 1 and not istype(val, (SyntheticLocalSource, LocalSource)):\n1448:                     tempvars[val] = None\n1449:             pass2 = PyCodegen(\n1450:                 self.root_tx,\n1451:                 root,\n1452:                 graph_output_var,\n1453:                 tempvars=tempvars,\n1454:                 overridden_sources=overridden_sources,\n1455:             )\n1456:             self.codegen_suffix(tx, stack_values_flat, pass2)\n1457: \n1458:             output = []\n1459:             if count_calls(self.graph) != 0 or len(pass2.graph_outputs) != 0:\n1460:                 output.extend(\n1461:                     self.compile_and_call_fx_graph(tx, pass2.graph_output_vars(), root)\n1462:                 )\n1463: \n1464:                 if len(pass2.graph_outputs) != 0:\n1465:                     output.append(pass2.create_store(graph_output_var))\n1466:                     stored_graph_output_var = True\n1467:                 else:\n1468:                     output.append(create_instruction(\"POP_TOP\"))\n1469:             else:\n1470:                 # NB: Important to run compiler collective even when there is\n1471:                 # a graph break\n1472:                 self.run_compiler_collective()\n1473:             self.add_output_instructions(output + pass2.get_instructions())\n1474: \n1475:         # restore all the live local vars of the root\n1476:         local_restore_cg = PyCodegen(\n1477:             self.root_tx, overridden_sources=overridden_sources\n1478:         )\n1479:         # TODO this local restoration should be removed when fully implementing nested graph breaks\n1480:         self.add_output_instructions(\n1481:             [\n1482:                 local_restore_cg.create_store(var)\n1483:                 for var in reversed(all_restore_vars[-1])\n1484:             ]\n1485:         )\n1486: \n1487:         if graph_output_var and stored_graph_output_var:\n1488:             self.add_output_instructions(\n1489:                 [local_restore_cg.create_delete(graph_output_var)]\n1490:             )\n1491: \n1492:         if self.export:\n1493:             from torch.export._trace import _ExportModuleSpecTrackerDict\n1494: \n1495:             potential_side_effects = []\n1496:             for var in self.side_effects._get_modified_vars():\n1497:                 if hasattr(var, \"mutation_type\"):\n1498:                     mut_type = var.mutation_type\n1499:                     # Make sure to skip codegen specific mutations\n1500:                     if isinstance(\n1501:                         mut_type, (AttributeMutationExisting, ValueMutationExisting)\n1502:                     ):\n1503:                         # export uses tracepoint pass to dump submodule inp/out spec\n1504:                         # into global state, so we filter it here\n1505:                         if not (\n1506:                             isinstance(var, UserDefinedDictVariable)\n1507:                             and isinstance(var.value, _ExportModuleSpecTrackerDict)\n1508:                         ):\n1509:                             potential_side_effects.append(var)\n1510: \n1511:             side_effect_refs = [\n1512:                 _get_source_debug_name(var.source) for var in potential_side_effects\n1513:             ]\n1514: \n1515:             if len(side_effect_refs):\n1516:                 warnings.warn(\n1517:                     f\"While exporting, we found certain side effects happened in the model.forward. \"\n1518:                     f\"Here are the list of potential sources you can double check: {side_effect_refs}\"\n1519:                 )\n1520: \n1521:         return all_stack_locals_metas\n1522: \n1523:     def codegen_suffix(\n1524:         self,\n1525:         tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n1526:         stack_values: list[VariableTracker],\n1527:         cg: PyCodegen,\n1528:     ) -> None:\n1529:         # NOTE: `codegen_save_tempvars` must run first to update `source` fields\n1530:         # for variables with `AttributeMutationNew`, as they don't implement\n1531:         # `reconstruct` themselves.\n1532:         self.side_effects.codegen_save_tempvars(cg)\n1533:         if self.backward_state:\n1534:             assert not self.export\n1535:             for name, val in self.backward_state.items():\n1536:                 cg(val)\n1537:                 assert self.backward_state_var is not None\n1538:                 cg.append_output(cg.create_load(self.backward_state_var))\n1539:                 cg.store_attr(name)\n1540:         self.side_effects.codegen_hooks(cg)\n1541: \n1542:         # Return variables used for logging at the end\n1543:         for debug_var, args in tx.debug_locals:\n1544:             cg.add_push_null(lambda: cg(debug_var))\n1545:             for arg in args:\n1546:                 cg(arg)\n1547:             cg.extend_output(create_call_function(len(args), False))\n1548:             cg.extend_output([create_instruction(\"POP_TOP\")])\n1549: \n1550:         cg.restore_stack(stack_values, value_from_source=not tx.export)\n1551:         self.side_effects.codegen_update_mutated(cg)\n1552: \n1553:     def cleanup_graph(self) -> None:\n1554:         \"\"\"\n1555:         Remove \"creation_timestamp\" from node meta\n1556: \n1557:         Remove this pattern from the graph:\n1558:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(False)\n1559:             torch._C._set_grad_enabled(True)\n1560:         \"\"\"\n1561:         assert self.should_exit\n1562:         nodes = list(self.graph.nodes)\n1563:         for node in nodes:\n1564:             node.meta.pop(\"creation_timestamp\", None)\n1565: \n1566:         grad_enabled = torch.is_grad_enabled()\n1567:         for node1, node2 in zip(nodes, nodes[1:]):\n1568:             if (\n1569:                 node1.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\n1570:                 and tuple(node1.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\n1571:                 and not node1._erased\n1572:             ):\n1573:                 grad_enabled = node1.args[0]\n1574:                 if (\n1575:                     node2.target is torch._C._set_grad_enabled\n1576:                     and tuple(node2.args) == (not grad_enabled,)\n1577:                     and not node2._erased\n1578:                 ):\n1579:                     grad_enabled = node2.args[0]\n1580:                     self.graph.erase_node(node1)\n1581:                     self.graph.erase_node(node2)\n1582: \n1583:     def get_graph_sizes_structured(self) -> dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]]:\n1584:         ret: dict[str, list[Union[int, str]]] = {}\n1585:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n1586:             example_value = node.meta.get(\"example_value\", None)\n1587:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):\n1588:                 size = example_value.size()\n1589:                 ret[node.name] = [s if isinstance(s, int) else repr(s) for s in size]\n1590:         return ret\n1591: \n1592:     def get_graph_sizes(self, name: str) -> str:\n1593:         graph_sizes_str = \"TRACED GRAPH TENSOR SIZES\\n\"\n1594:         graph_sizes_str += f\"===== {name} =====\\n\"\n1595:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n1596:             example_value = node.meta.get(\"example_value\", None)\n1597:             if isinstance(example_value, torch._subclasses.FakeTensor):\n1598:                 size = example_value.size()\n1599:                 graph_sizes_str += f\"{node.name}: {tuple(size)}\\n\"\n1600:                 concrete_size = []\n1601:                 has_symint = False\n1602:                 for sz in size:\n1603:                     if isinstance(sz, int):\n1604:                         concrete_size.append(sz)\n1605:                     elif isinstance(sz, torch.SymInt):\n1606:                         has_symint = True\n1607:                         concrete_size.append(sz.node.hint)\n1608:                     else:\n1609:                         break\n1610:                 else:\n1611:                     if has_symint:\n1612:                         graph_sizes_str += (\n1613:                             f\"{node.name} (concrete): {tuple(concrete_size)}\\n\"\n1614:                         )\n1615:         return graph_sizes_str\n1616: \n1617:     @contextlib.contextmanager\n1618:     def restore_global_state(self) -> Any:\n1619:         \"\"\"\n1620:         Momentarily restores the global state to what it was prior to tracing the current output\n1621:         \"\"\"\n1622:         prior_global_state = self.tracing_context.global_context.copy_graphstate()\n1623:         current_global_state: dict[str, tuple[Any, bool]] = {}\n1624:         self.save_global_state(out=current_global_state)\n1625:         try:\n1626:             # Set to state prior to tracing the graph\n1627:             self.tracing_context.global_context.restore_graphstate(prior_global_state)\n1628:             yield\n1629:         finally:\n1630:             # Reset to state at the current time (e.g. before calling the user compiler)\n1631:             self.tracing_context.global_context.restore_graphstate(\n1632:                 GlobalContextCheckpointState(current_global_state)\n1633:             )\n1634: \n1635:     def run_compiler_collective(self) -> None:\n1636:         tx = self.root_tx\n1637:         assert tx is not None\n1638:         if (ds := tx.distributed_state) is not None and ds.all_states is None:\n1639:             compile_pg = ds.compile_pg\n1640:             log.info(\"compiler_collective %s\", ds.local_state)\n1641:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\n1642:                 \"artifact\",\n1643:                 metadata_fn=lambda: {\n1644:                     \"name\": \"compiler_collective\",\n1645:                     \"encoding\": \"string\",\n1646:                 },\n1647:                 payload_fn=lambda: ds.local_state.render(),\n1648:             )\n1649:             device_types = compile_pg._device_types\n1650:             assert len(device_types) == 1, (\n1651:                 \"Expect only one device type but got {}\".format(\"+\".join(device_types))\n1652:             )\n1653:             with (\n1654:                 get_interface_for_device(device_types.pop()).device(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n1655:                     compile_pg.rank() % torch.accelerator.device_count()\n1656:                 ),\n1657:                 dynamo_timed(\"compiler_collective\", log_pt2_compile_event=True),\n1658:             ):\n1659:                 all_states: list[Any] = [None] * compile_pg.size()\n1660:                 dist.all_gather_object(all_states, ds.local_state, group=compile_pg)\n1661:                 ds.all_states = all_states\n1662:             # Clear speculation log, because are tracing may diverge due to\n1663:             # this information from the compiler collective\n1664:             tx.speculation_log.clear()\n1665:             raise exc.CompileCollectiveRestartAnalysis\n1666: \n1667:     def compile_and_call_fx_graph(\n1668:         self,\n1669:         tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n1670:         rv: list[VariableTracker],\n1671:         root: FakeRootModule,\n1672:     ) -> list[Instruction]:\n1673:         \"\"\"\n1674:         Generate code from self.graph and return the Instruction()s to\n1675:         call that generated code.\n1676: \n1677:         Code is generated w.r.t. self.root_tx.\n1678:         tx is only used for preserving GraphModule metadata\n1679:         \"\"\"\n1680:         with torch._guards.TracingContext.clear_frame():\n1681:             from .decorators import disable\n1682: \n1683:             assert self.should_exit\n1684: \n1685:             self.run_compiler_collective()\n1686: \n1687:             name = unique_id(\"__compiled_fn\", with_uuid=True)\n1688: \n1689:             assert isinstance(rv, list)\n1690:             assert isinstance(root, FakeRootModule)\n1691: \n1692:             output_node = self.create_node(\n1693:                 \"output\",\n1694:                 \"output\",\n1695:                 (self.current_tracer.create_arg(tuple(x.as_proxy() for x in rv)),),\n1696:                 {},\n1697:             )\n1698:             sub_gms = self.dedup_pass()\n1699:             root.add_nn_modules(sub_gms)  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n1700: \n1701:             self.current_tracer._maybe_preserve_original_meta(tx, output_node)\n1702:             if not config.do_not_emit_runtime_asserts:\n1703:                 # There is a rare scenario where codegen_suffix adds a new entry\n1704:                 # to self.nn_modules while `root` knows only about the\n1705:                 # nn_modules at the time of its creation. This causes failures\n1706:                 # while creating the graph module because self.graph and root\n1707:                 # are out of sync. This only happens for `get_attr` nodes, so\n1708:                 # here we clean up the get_attr nodes that are unused.\n1709:                 self.remove_unused_get_attr_nodes()\n1710:                 insert_deferred_runtime_asserts(\n1711:                     fx.GraphModule(root, self.graph),\n1712:                     self.shape_env,\n1713:                     name,\n1714:                     export=self.export,\n1715:                 )\n1716:             # NB: deferred runtime asserts can keep graphargs live, so make sure\n1717:             # those are inserted before pruning\n1718:             self.remove_unused_graphargs()\n1719:             ncalls = count_calls(self.graph)\n1720:             counters[\"stats\"][\"calls_captured\"] += ncalls\n1721: \n1722:             self.remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs()\n1723: \n1724:             # free a bit of memory\n1725:             self.real_value_cache.clear()\n1726: \n1727:             gm = _make_graph_module(root, self.graph)\n1728: \n1729:             # Saved tensors hooks are not used by the graph.\n1730:             # GraphModule by default only copies used in the graph submodules.\n1731:             # Copying them into the result graph manually.\n1732:             if self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names:\n1733:                 for subgraph_name in self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names:\n1734:                     setattr(gm, subgraph_name, getattr(root, subgraph_name))\n1735: \n1736:             for register_finalizer in self.register_finalizer_fns:\n1737:                 register_finalizer(gm)\n1738: \n1739:             gm._backend_id = name\n1740:             gm.compile_subgraph_reason = self.compile_subgraph_reason\n1741:             gm.meta[\"dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn\"] = (\n1742:                 self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.copy()\n1743:             )\n1744:             gm.meta[\"dynamo_compile_id\"] = self.dynamo_compile_id\n1745: \n1746:             graph_code_log.debug(\n1747:                 \"%s\",\n1748:                 lazy_format_graph_code(\n1749:                     name, gm, include_stride=True, include_device=True, colored=True\n1750:                 ),\n1751:             )\n1752:             torch._logging.trace_structured(\n1753:                 \"dynamo_output_graph\",\n1754:                 lambda: {\"sizes\": self.get_graph_sizes_structured()},\n1755:                 payload_fn=lambda: gm.print_readable(\n1756:                     print_output=False, include_stride=True, include_device=True\n1757:                 ),\n1758:             )\n1759:             self.call_cleanup_hooks()\n1760:             old_fake_mode = self.tracing_context.fake_mode\n1761:             assert old_fake_mode is not None\n1762:             if not self.export:\n1763:                 import torch._functorch.config as _config\n1764: \n1765:                 with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n1766:                     # TODO(voz): The way export uses gm, and fake tensors, is not supported with us resetting\n1767:                     backend_fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n1768:                         shape_env=old_fake_mode.shape_env,\n1769:                     )\n1770:                 # TODO(voz): Ostensibily, this should be scoped and\n1771:                 # restore back to old_fake_mode, but doing so currently violates\n1772:                 # a lot of fake_tensor ownership assumptions and runs afoul of detect_fake_mode\n1773:                 self.tracing_context.fake_mode = backend_fake_mode\n1774: \n1775:             with self.restore_global_state():\n1776:                 compiled_fn = self.call_user_compiler(gm, self.example_inputs())\n1777: \n1778:             from torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _LazyGraphModule\n1779: \n1780:             if isinstance(compiled_fn, _LazyGraphModule) or (\n1781:                 isinstance(getattr(compiled_fn, \"__self__\", None), _LazyGraphModule)\n1782:                 and compiled_fn.__name__ == \"_lazy_forward\"  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n1783:             ):\n1784:                 # Since dynamo will run the forward method for the GraphModule shortly\n1785:                 # anyways, it does not hurt to do the real recompilation here if\n1786:                 # this is a _LazyGraphModule. This makes it easier for dynamo to\n1787:                 # optimize a _LazyGraphModule.\n1788: \n1789:                 lazy_gm = (\n1790:                     compiled_fn\n1791:                     if isinstance(compiled_fn, _LazyGraphModule)\n1792:                     else compiled_fn.__self__  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n1793:                 )\n1794: \n1795:                 _LazyGraphModule.force_recompile(lazy_gm)\n1796: \n1797:                 if not isinstance(compiled_fn, _LazyGraphModule):\n1798:                     # replace compiled_fn with the real forward method\n1799:                     compiled_fn = lazy_gm.forward\n1800: \n1801:             if self.package is not None:\n1802:                 self.package.add_backend_id(name, compiled_fn)\n1803: \n1804:             compiled_fn = disable(\n1805:                 compiled_fn, reason=\"do not trace Dynamo-compiled graph\"\n1806:             )\n1807: \n1808:             counters[\"stats\"][\"unique_graphs\"] += 1\n1809:             assert old_fake_mode.shape_env is not None\n1810:             if specializations := old_fake_mode.shape_env.specializations:\n1811:                 specialization_guards = []\n1812:                 specialization_cache: dict[Specialization, Callable[[Any], Any]] = {}\n1813:                 sources = [a.source for a in self.graphargs]\n1814:                 for specialization in specializations:\n1815:                     source_index = sources.index(specialization.source)\n1816:                     check_fn_source = inspect.getsource(specialization.check_fn).strip()\n1817:                     # Required because the LABDA_GUARD API requires a root guard manager\n1818:                     unused_root_guard_manager = RootGuardManager()\n1819:                     check_fn = guards.LAMBDA_GUARD(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n1820:                         unused_root_guard_manager,\n1821:                         specialization.check_fn,\n1822:                         [check_fn_source],\n1823:                     )\n1824: \n1825:                     log.debug(\n1826:                         \"Compiling backend specialized graph with specialization=%s\",\n1827:                         check_fn_source,\n1828:                     )\n1829: \n1830:                     specialization_guards.append(\n1831:                         (\n1832:                             functools.partial(\n1833:                                 lambda idx, args, check_fn=check_fn: check_fn(\n1834:                                     args[idx]\n1835:                                 ),\n1836:                                 source_index,\n1837:                             ),\n1838:                             specialization,\n1839:                         )\n1840:                     )\n1841: \n1842:                 @torch._dynamo.disable(reason=\"do not trace Dynamo-compiled graph\")  # type: ignore[misc]\n1843:                 def specialized_dispatch(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:\n1844:                     for check_fn, specialization in specialization_guards:\n1845:                         if check_fn(args):\n1846:                             if specialization in specialization_cache:\n1847:                                 return specialization_cache[specialization](\n1848:                                     *args, **kwargs\n1849:                                 )\n1850: \n1851:                             with self.shape_env.patch_source_specialization(\n1852:                                 specialization.source, specialization.check_fn\n1853:                             ):\n1854:                                 # Modify gm so AOTAutogradCache key changes per specialization\n1855:                                 gm.meta[\"specialization\"] = specialization\n1856:                                 example_inputs: list[Tensor] = list(args)\n1857:                                 with tracing(self.tracing_context):\n1858:                                     specialization_cache[specialization] = (\n1859:                                         self.call_user_compiler(gm, example_inputs)\n1860:                                     )\n1861: \n1862:                             return specialization_cache[specialization](*args, **kwargs)\n1863:                     return compiled_fn(*args, **kwargs)\n1864: \n1865:                 # This is safe because we pre-process name to be unique\n1866:                 self.install_global_unsafe(name, specialized_dispatch)\n1867:             else:\n1868:                 # This is safe because we pre-process name to be unique\n1869:                 self.install_global_unsafe(name, compiled_fn)\n1870: \n1871:             assert self.root_tx is not None\n1872:             cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\n1873:             cg.make_call_generated_code(name)\n1874:             return cg.get_instructions()\n1875: \n1876:     @property\n1877:     def placeholders(self) -> list[fx.Node]:\n1878: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1377-1877"
