{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"1878:         return self.graph.find_nodes(op=\\\"placeholder\\\")\\n1879: \\n1880:     @property\\n1881:     def graphargs(self) -> list[GraphArg]:\\n1882:         return [node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"] for node in self.placeholders]\\n1883: \\n1884:     def call_user_compiler(\\n1885:         self, gm: fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[Tensor]\\n1886:     ) -> CompiledFn:\\n1887:         with dynamo_timed(\\n1888:             \\\"OutputGraph.call_user_compiler\\\",\\n1889:             phase_name=\\\"backend_compile\\\",\\n1890:             log_pt2_compile_event=True,\\n1891:             log_waitcounter=True,\\n1892:             waitcounter_name_override=\\\"compile_aot_autograd\\\",\\n1893:             dynamo_compile_column_us=\\\"aot_autograd_cumulative_compile_time_us\\\",\\n1894:         ):\\n1895:             return self._call_user_compiler(gm, example_inputs)\\n1896: \\n1897:     def _call_user_compiler(\\n1898:         self, gm: fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[Tensor]\\n1899:     ) -> CompiledFn:\\n1900:         assert self.compiler_fn is not None\\n1901:         tot = 0\\n1902:         placeholders = []\\n1903:         for node in gm.graph.nodes:\\n1904:             if node.op in (\\\"call_function\\\", \\\"call_method\\\", \\\"call_module\\\"):\\n1905:                 tot += 1\\n1906:             if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n1907:                 placeholders.append(node)\\n1908:         increment_op_count(tot)\\n1909:         for pl in placeholders:\\n1910:             if not hasattr(pl, \\\"_dynamo_source\\\"):\\n1911:                 arg = pl.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n1912:                 # TODO: Why isn't this stored in meta :think:\\n1913:                 # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\\n1914:                 pl._dynamo_source = arg.source\\n1915: \\n1916:         # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\\n1917:         gm._param_name_to_source = self.param_name_to_source  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1918:         gm._source_to_user_stacks = self.source_to_user_stacks  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1919: \\n1920:         name = (\\n1921:             self.compiler_fn.__name__\\n1922:             if hasattr(self.compiler_fn, \\\"__name__\\\")\\n1923:             else \\\"<unknown compiler_fn>\\\"\\n1924:         )\\n1925:         try:\\n1926:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\\\"calling compiler function {name}\\\")\\n1927:             compiler_fn = self.compiler_fn\\n1928:             if config.verify_correctness:\\n1929:                 compiler_fn = WrapperBackend(compiler_fn)\\n1930:             compiled_fn = compiler_fn(gm, example_inputs)\\n1931:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\\\"done compiler function {name}\\\")\\n1932:             assert callable(compiled_fn), \\\"compiler_fn did not return callable\\\"\\n1933:         except (TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis, ShortenTraceback):\\n1934:             raise\\n1935:         except exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback as e:\\n1936:             if self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph:\\n1937:                 raise BackendCompilerFailed(\\n1938:                     self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\\n1939:                 ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\\n1940:             unimplemented_v2_with_warning(\\n1941:                 e,\\n1942:                 self.root_tx.f_code,\\n1943:                 gb_type=\\\"Backend compiler exception\\\",\\n1944:                 context=f\\\"Backend: {name}\\\\nException:{str(e)}\\\\nTraceback:\\\\n{self.root_tx.format_frame_summary()}\\\",\\n1945:                 explanation=f\\\"Backend compiler `{name}` failed with {str(e)}. Adding a graph break.\\\",\\n1946:                 hints=[\\n1947:                     \\\"Report an issue to the backend compiler repo.\\\",\\n1948:                 ],\\n1949:             )\\n1950:         except SkipFrame as e:\\n1951:             # The backend compiler has requested that we skip the frame, instead of\\n1952:             # aborting execution.\\n1953:             raise e\\n1954:         except Exception as e:\\n1955:             raise BackendCompilerFailed(\\n1956:                 self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\\n1957:             ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\\n1958: \\n1959:         signpost_event(\\n1960:             \\\"dynamo\\\",\\n1961:             \\\"OutputGraph.call_user_compiler\\\",\\n1962:             {\\n1963:                 **self.co_fields,\\n1964:                 \\\"op_count\\\": tot,\\n1965:                 \\\"node_count\\\": len(gm.graph.nodes),\\n1966:                 \\\"input_count\\\": len(placeholders),\\n1967:             },\\n1968:         )\\n1969: \\n1970:         return compiled_fn\\n1971: \\n1972:     def dedup_pass(self) -> dict[str, torch.fx.GraphModule]:\\n1973:         if torch._dynamo.config.use_graph_deduplication:\\n1974:             return apply_graph_deduplication(self)\\n1975:         else:\\n1976:             return {}\\n1977: \\n1978:     def install_subgraph(self, name: str, sub_gm: torch.fx.GraphModule) -> str:\\n1979:         next_name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, requires_suffix=True)\\n1980:         sub_gm.__name__ = next_name  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1981:         sub_gm.torchdynamo_force_dynamic = False  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n1982:         # This graph module is not present in the user space, so it can't be\\n1983:         # accessed by a source. Set source=None.\\n1984:         self.register_attr_or_module(sub_gm, next_name, source=None)\\n1985:         return next_name\\n1986: \\n1987:     def example_inputs(self) -> list[torch.Tensor]:\\n1988:         result = [arg.example for arg in self.graphargs]\\n1989:         return result\\n1990: \\n1991:     def remove_unused_get_attr_nodes(self) -> None:\\n1992:         for node in sorted(self.graph.find_nodes(op=\\\"get_attr\\\"), reverse=True):\\n1993:             if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\\n1994:                 self.remove_node(node)\\n1995: \\n1996:     def remove_unused_graphargs(self) -> None:\\n1997:         # NB: It's OK to drop GraphArg for symbols that ended up being\\n1998:         # specialized iff they are not used in runtime assertions.  You don't\\n1999:         # even have to make a guard for it, because ShapeEnv produce_guards\\n2000:         # operates on tracked_fakes, which never gets pruned.\\n2001:         # That being said, you'll get marginally better generated\\n2002:         # guard code if you promote the guard into a Dynamo guard (since that\\n2003:         # allows for the guard to be done using C++ guards.)  If we get\\n2004:         # ShapeEnv guards to go into C++ guards, this will stop being a thing\\n2005:         # though!\\n2006: \\n2007:         assert self.should_exit\\n2008: \\n2009:         # Miniature DCE pass, but only for obviously trivial operations\\n2010:         def is_static_true(b_node: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\\n2011:             if b_node is True:\\n2012:                 return True\\n2013:             if not isinstance(b_node, fx.Node):\\n2014:                 return False\\n2015:             b = b_node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\")\\n2016:             if b is None:\\n2017:                 return False\\n2018:             if b is True:\\n2019:                 return True\\n2020:             if (\\n2021:                 isinstance(b, torch.SymBool)\\n2022:                 and (r := b.node.maybe_as_bool()) is not None\\n2023:             ):\\n2024:                 return r\\n2025:             # TODO: We can also technically remove all cases when the input\\n2026:             # doesn't have unbacked inputs, since it's all in the ShapeEnv\\n2027:             return False\\n2028: \\n2029:         def is_symnode_arg(a: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\\n2030:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\\n2031: \\n2032:             if isinstance(a, (int, float, bool)):\\n2033:                 return True\\n2034:             if isinstance(a, fx.Node):\\n2035:                 return isinstance(a.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\"), SymTypes)\\n2036:             return False\\n2037: \\n2038:         # NB: We assume that you cannot do mutations on int/float/bool,\\n2039:         # because they are immutable types, and therefore is always safe to\\n2040:         # DCE.\\n2041:         def is_symnode_compute_node(node: fx.Node) -> bool:\\n2042:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\\n2043: \\n2044:             if node.op != \\\"call_function\\\":\\n2045:                 return False\\n2046:             # TODO: I don't think it's possible to have a bare int/float here?\\n2047:             if not isinstance(node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\"), SymTypes):\\n2048:                 return False\\n2049:             # TODO: This will bail here if you ever end up with a more complicated\\n2050:             # computation function, like sum(list_of_ints), even though it\\n2051:             # should be DCE'able\\n2052:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.args):\\n2053:                 return False\\n2054:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.kwargs.values()):\\n2055:                 return False\\n2056:             return True\\n2057: \\n2058:         from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_accessor_node\\n2059: \\n2060:         for node in reversed(list(self.graph.nodes)):\\n2061:             if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\\n2062:                 if (\\n2063:                     node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2064:                     or (node.op == \\\"call_function\\\" and node.target is operator.getitem)\\n2065:                     or (\\n2066:                         node.op == \\\"call_function\\\"\\n2067:                         and node.target is torch._check\\n2068:                         and is_static_true(node.args[0])\\n2069:                     )\\n2070:                     or is_symnode_compute_node(node)\\n2071:                     or is_accessor_node(node)\\n2072:                 ):\\n2073:                     self.remove_node(node)\\n2074: \\n2075:         def placeholder_binds_symbol(node: fx.Node) -> Optional[sympy.Symbol]:\\n2076:             arg = node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2077:             example = arg.example\\n2078:             if isinstance(example, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(\\n2079:                 example.node.expr, sympy.Symbol\\n2080:             ):\\n2081:                 return example.node.expr\\n2082:             return None\\n2083: \\n2084:         def remove_unused(node: fx.Node) -> None:\\n2085:             log.debug(\\\"REMOVE UNUSED GRAPHARG %s\\\", node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].source.name())\\n2086:             # I'm not really sure why you need to delete these from the\\n2087:             # node since the node is going to get removed\\n2088:             del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2089:             self.remove_node(node)\\n2090:             self.real_value_cache.pop(node, None)\\n2091: \\n2092:         used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol] = set()\\n2093: \\n2094:         def update_used_symbols(\\n2095:             used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol], fake: Union[torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor]\\n2096:         ) -> None:\\n2097:             used_symbols |= free_symbols(fake)\\n2098: \\n2099:         recheck_placeholders = []\\n2100:         for node in self.placeholders:\\n2101:             binds_symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node) is not None\\n2102:             # Don't delete symbol bindings yet\\n2103:             if binds_symbol:\\n2104:                 if not node.users:\\n2105:                     recheck_placeholders.append(node)\\n2106:             else:\\n2107:                 if not node.users and not isinstance(\\n2108:                     node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"], BackwardStateGraphArg\\n2109:                 ):\\n2110:                     remove_unused(node)\\n2111:                 else:\\n2112:                     # Register the free symbols as uses\\n2113:                     arg = node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2114:                     if isinstance(arg, BackwardStateGraphArg):\\n2115:                         continue\\n2116:                     if isinstance(node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example, torch.ScriptObject):\\n2117:                         real_script_obj = node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2118:                         fake_script_obj = node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example_strong_ref\\n2119:                         if not torch._library.fake_class_registry.tracing_with_real(\\n2120:                             real_script_obj\\n2121:                         ):\\n2122:                             flat_dict = dict(real_script_obj.__obj_flatten__())  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\\n2123:                             for attr in flat_dict.keys():\\n2124:                                 fake_attr_val = getattr(\\n2125:                                     fake_script_obj.wrapped_obj, attr\\n2126:                                 )\\n2127:                                 pytree.tree_map_only(\\n2128:                                     (torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor),\\n2129:                                     lambda t: update_used_symbols(used_symbols, t),\\n2130:                                     fake_attr_val,\\n2131:                                 )\\n2132:                         continue\\n2133:                     fake = (\\n2134:                         arg.fake_tensor if arg.fake_tensor is not None else arg.example\\n2135:                     )\\n2136:                     update_used_symbols(used_symbols, fake)\\n2137: \\n2138:         # After removing unused graphargs, prune unused binds_symbol\\n2139:         for node in recheck_placeholders:\\n2140:             symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node)\\n2141:             if symbol is not None:\\n2142:                 if symbol not in used_symbols:\\n2143:                     remove_unused(node)\\n2144:                 else:\\n2145:                     # Make sure we delete later occurrences of the same symbol\\n2146:                     used_symbols.remove(symbol)\\n2147: \\n2148:     def remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs(self) -> None:\\n2149:         # This is a pretty interesting function. Basically we have this problem\\n2150:         # where our compiler tends to choke when we have unused inputs. The way\\n2151:         # we support dynamic float arguments is by doing a joint fx pass and\\n2152:         # tensorifying away as many symfloats as we can. For the remaining symfloats\\n2153:         # we have no choice but to specialize... HOWEVER at that point in time\\n2154:         # we can no longer remove graph inputs. So our sledgehammer solution is to\\n2155:         # save the state of what inputs we should have specialized in dynamo and\\n2156:         # restart analysis. This function incorporates this \\\"view from the future\\\"\\n2157:         # state and specializes inputs that we know we won't be able to tensorify\\n2158:         # away in the joint pass. In principle we shouldn't choke on unused inputs\\n2159:         # and so this shouldn't be necessary. In practice CUDA graphs choke on\\n2160:         # unused inputs so we need this for now.\\n2161: \\n2162:         # Import here to prevent circular import\\n2163:         from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import TensorifyState\\n2164: \\n2165:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2166:             example_value = node.meta.get(\\\"example_value\\\")\\n2167:             if (\\n2168:                 isinstance(example_value, FakeTensor)\\n2169:                 and example_value.item_memo is not None\\n2170:                 and hasattr(example_value.item_memo.node._expr, \\\"name\\\")\\n2171:                 and all(u.target == \\\"item\\\" for u in node.users)\\n2172:                 and TensorifyState.should_specialize(\\n2173:                     # We use _expr instead of expr b/c we want the symbol not the replacement\\n2174:                     example_value.item_memo.node._expr.name\\n2175:                 )\\n2176:             ):\\n2177:                 for u in list(node.users):\\n2178:                     u.replace_all_uses_with(guard_scalar(example_value.item_memo))\\n2179:                     self.remove_node(u)\\n2180:                 self.remove_node(node)\\n2181: \\n2182:     def add_output_instructions(self, prefix: list[Instruction]) -> None:\\n2183:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2184:         We call this on the creation of a new compiled subgraph that is inserted\\n2185:         before user code.\\n2186:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2187:         self.output_instructions.extend(prefix)\\n2188:         self.should_exit = True\\n2189: \\n2190:     def install_global_unsafe(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:\\n2191:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2192:         WARNING: prefer the safer `install_global_by_id/install_global`.\\n2193:         torch.compile instances should be independent of each other;\\n2194:         one footgun is to have one instance depend on the existence of\\n2195:         a global installed by another instance. This can happen if we mangle\\n2196:         a global the same way across both instances.\\n2197:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2198:         assert name not in self.installed_globals\\n2199:         self.installed_globals.add(name)\\n2200:         self.cleanups.append(CleanupHook.create(self.global_scope, name, value))\\n2201: \\n2202:     def install_global_by_id(self, prefix: str, value: Any) -> str:\\n2203:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2204:         Installs a global if it hasn't been installed already.\\n2205:         This is determined by (prefix, id(value)) pair.\\n2206: \\n2207:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\\n2208:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2209:         # NB: need self.compile_id to distinguish this global\\n2210:         # from another global created in a different torch.compile instance\\n2211:         name = f\\\"{prefix}_{id(value)}_c{self.compile_id}\\\"\\n2212:         if name in self.installed_globals:\\n2213:             return name\\n2214:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\\n2215:         return name\\n2216: \\n2217:     def install_global(self, prefix: str, value: Any) -> str:\\n2218:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2219:         Installs a global, generating a unique name for it.\\n2220: \\n2221:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\\n2222:         \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2223:         # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances\\n2224:         name = unique_id(prefix)\\n2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\\n2226:         return name\\n2227: \\n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\\n2234: \\n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n2236:             if \\\"grapharg\\\" in node.meta:\\n2237:                 del node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"]\\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\\n2248: \\n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\\n2251:     ) -> None:\\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\\n2253: \\n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\\n2255:         \\\"\\\"\\\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2256:         if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n2257:             return node.meta[\\\"grapharg\\\"].example\\n2258:         assert node.op == \\\"get_attr\\\"\\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\\n2260: \\n2261: \\n2262: err_epilogue = (\\n2263:     \\\"With the current config, we will graph break \\\"\\n2264:     \\\"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \\\"\\n2265:     \\\"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \\\"\\n2266:     \\\"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \\\"\\n2267:     \\\"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\\\"\\n2268: )\\n2269: \\n2270: \\n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\\n2273: ) -> None:\\n2274:     if kind != \\\"call_function\\\":\\n2275:         return\\n2276: \\n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\\\"prim\\\", \\\"prims\\\", \\\"aten\\\"}:\\n2279:             return\\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\\n2281: \\n2282:     def encountered_non_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload, msg: str) -> None:\\n2283:         output_graph.non_compliant_ops.add(target)\\n2284:         if config.only_allow_pt2_compliant_ops:\\n2285:             unimplemented_v2(\\n2286:                 gb_type=\\\"Encountered non-PT2-compliant op\\\",\\n2287:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n2288:                 explanation=msg + \\\" \\\" + err_epilogue,\\n2289:                 hints=[],\\n2290:             )\\n2291: \\n2292:     if isinstance(target, torch._ops.OpOverload):\\n2293:         if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in target.tags:\\n2294:             encountered_compliant_op(target)\\n2295:             return\\n2296:         encountered_non_compliant_op(\\n2297:             target,\\n2298:             f\\\"Encountered the torch.ops.OpOverload {target} that is not PT2 compliant.\\\",\\n2299:         )\\n2300:         return\\n2301: \\n2302:     if isinstance(target, torch._ops.OpOverloadPacket):\\n2303:         overloads = tuple(target.overloads())\\n2304:         # Optimization: Overload resolution is expensive.\\n2305:         # If there's only one overload, we know what it will resolve to.\\n2306:         if len(overloads) == 1:\\n2307:             op = getattr(target, overloads[0])\\n2308:             if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in op.tags:\\n2309:                 encountered_compliant_op(op)\\n2310:                 return\\n2311:             encountered_non_compliant_op(\\n2312:                 op,\\n2313:                 f\\\"Encountered the non-overloaded \\\"\\n2314:                 f\\\"torch.ops.OpOverloadPacket {target} \\\"\\n2315:                 f\\\"that is not PT2 compliant. \\\",\\n2316:             )\\n2317:             return\\n2318: \\n2319:         args, kwargs = torch._dynamo.utils.get_fake_values_from_nodes(\\n2320:             output_graph.current_tx, (args, kwargs), False\\n2321:         )\\n2322:         try:\\n2323:             overload = torch._C._jit_resolve_packet(\\n2324:                 target._qualified_op_name, *args, **kwargs\\n2325:             )\\n2326:         except RuntimeError as e:\\n2327:             unimplemented_v2(\\n2328:                 gb_type=\\\"Error when attempting to resolve op packet\\\",\\n2329:                 context=\\\"\\\",\\n2330:                 explanation=str(e),\\n2331:                 hints=[],\\n2332:             )\\n2333: \\n2334:         op = getattr(target, overload)\\n2335:         if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in op.tags:\\n2336:             encountered_compliant_op(op)\\n2337:         else:\\n2338:             encountered_non_compliant_op(\\n2339:                 op,\\n2340:                 f\\\"Encountered the torch.ops.OpOverloadPacket {target} \\\"\\n2341:                 f\\\"which resolves to the overload ({overload}) that is \\\"\\n2342:                 f\\\"not PT2 compliant.\\\",\\n2343:             )\\n2344: \\n2345: \\n2346: _compile_id_counter = itertools.count()\\n2347: \\n2348: P = ParamSpec(\\\"P\\\")\\n2349: R = TypeVar(\\\"R\\\")\\n2350: \\n2351: \\n2352: class LazyProxy:\\n2353:     def __init__(\\n2354:         self,\\n2355:         tracer: \\\"SubgraphTracer\\\",\\n2356:         fn: Callable[P, R],\\n2357:         *args: P.args,\\n2358:         **kwargs: P.kwargs,\\n2359:     ) -> None:\\n2360:         self.tracer = tracer\\n2361:         self.fn = fn\\n2362:         self.args = args\\n2363:         self.kwargs = kwargs\\n2364: \\n2365:     def __call__(self) -> Any:\\n2366:         return self.fn(*self.args, **self.kwargs)\\n2367: \\n2368: \\n2369: class SubgraphTracer(fx.Tracer):\\n2370:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2371:     Holds an FX graph that is being traced. OutputGraph owns a SubgraphTracer\\n2372:     and the separation of responsibilities is that SubgraphTracer is\\n2373:     responsible for building the graph while OutputGraph is responsible for\\n2374:     compiling and executing the graph.\\n2375:     \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n2376: \\n2377:     def __init__(\\n2378:         self,\\n2379: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1878-2378\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "1878:         return self.graph.find_nodes(op=\"placeholder\")\n1879: \n1880:     @property\n1881:     def graphargs(self) -> list[GraphArg]:\n1882:         return [node.meta[\"grapharg\"] for node in self.placeholders]\n1883: \n1884:     def call_user_compiler(\n1885:         self, gm: fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[Tensor]\n1886:     ) -> CompiledFn:\n1887:         with dynamo_timed(\n1888:             \"OutputGraph.call_user_compiler\",\n1889:             phase_name=\"backend_compile\",\n1890:             log_pt2_compile_event=True,\n1891:             log_waitcounter=True,\n1892:             waitcounter_name_override=\"compile_aot_autograd\",\n1893:             dynamo_compile_column_us=\"aot_autograd_cumulative_compile_time_us\",\n1894:         ):\n1895:             return self._call_user_compiler(gm, example_inputs)\n1896: \n1897:     def _call_user_compiler(\n1898:         self, gm: fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[Tensor]\n1899:     ) -> CompiledFn:\n1900:         assert self.compiler_fn is not None\n1901:         tot = 0\n1902:         placeholders = []\n1903:         for node in gm.graph.nodes:\n1904:             if node.op in (\"call_function\", \"call_method\", \"call_module\"):\n1905:                 tot += 1\n1906:             if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n1907:                 placeholders.append(node)\n1908:         increment_op_count(tot)\n1909:         for pl in placeholders:\n1910:             if not hasattr(pl, \"_dynamo_source\"):\n1911:                 arg = pl.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n1912:                 # TODO: Why isn't this stored in meta :think:\n1913:                 # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\n1914:                 pl._dynamo_source = arg.source\n1915: \n1916:         # NOTE: can't move these into meta: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/141640\n1917:         gm._param_name_to_source = self.param_name_to_source  # type: ignore[assignment]\n1918:         gm._source_to_user_stacks = self.source_to_user_stacks  # type: ignore[assignment]\n1919: \n1920:         name = (\n1921:             self.compiler_fn.__name__\n1922:             if hasattr(self.compiler_fn, \"__name__\")\n1923:             else \"<unknown compiler_fn>\"\n1924:         )\n1925:         try:\n1926:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\"calling compiler function {name}\")\n1927:             compiler_fn = self.compiler_fn\n1928:             if config.verify_correctness:\n1929:                 compiler_fn = WrapperBackend(compiler_fn)\n1930:             compiled_fn = compiler_fn(gm, example_inputs)\n1931:             _step_logger()(logging.INFO, f\"done compiler function {name}\")\n1932:             assert callable(compiled_fn), \"compiler_fn did not return callable\"\n1933:         except (TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis, ShortenTraceback):\n1934:             raise\n1935:         except exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback as e:\n1936:             if self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph:\n1937:                 raise BackendCompilerFailed(\n1938:                     self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\n1939:                 ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n1940:             unimplemented_v2_with_warning(\n1941:                 e,\n1942:                 self.root_tx.f_code,\n1943:                 gb_type=\"Backend compiler exception\",\n1944:                 context=f\"Backend: {name}\\nException:{str(e)}\\nTraceback:\\n{self.root_tx.format_frame_summary()}\",\n1945:                 explanation=f\"Backend compiler `{name}` failed with {str(e)}. Adding a graph break.\",\n1946:                 hints=[\n1947:                     \"Report an issue to the backend compiler repo.\",\n1948:                 ],\n1949:             )\n1950:         except SkipFrame as e:\n1951:             # The backend compiler has requested that we skip the frame, instead of\n1952:             # aborting execution.\n1953:             raise e\n1954:         except Exception as e:\n1955:             raise BackendCompilerFailed(\n1956:                 self.compiler_fn, e, inspect.currentframe()\n1957:             ).with_traceback(e.__traceback__) from None\n1958: \n1959:         signpost_event(\n1960:             \"dynamo\",\n1961:             \"OutputGraph.call_user_compiler\",\n1962:             {\n1963:                 **self.co_fields,\n1964:                 \"op_count\": tot,\n1965:                 \"node_count\": len(gm.graph.nodes),\n1966:                 \"input_count\": len(placeholders),\n1967:             },\n1968:         )\n1969: \n1970:         return compiled_fn\n1971: \n1972:     def dedup_pass(self) -> dict[str, torch.fx.GraphModule]:\n1973:         if torch._dynamo.config.use_graph_deduplication:\n1974:             return apply_graph_deduplication(self)\n1975:         else:\n1976:             return {}\n1977: \n1978:     def install_subgraph(self, name: str, sub_gm: torch.fx.GraphModule) -> str:\n1979:         next_name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self.nn_modules, requires_suffix=True)\n1980:         sub_gm.__name__ = next_name  # type: ignore[assignment]\n1981:         sub_gm.torchdynamo_force_dynamic = False  # type: ignore[assignment]\n1982:         # This graph module is not present in the user space, so it can't be\n1983:         # accessed by a source. Set source=None.\n1984:         self.register_attr_or_module(sub_gm, next_name, source=None)\n1985:         return next_name\n1986: \n1987:     def example_inputs(self) -> list[torch.Tensor]:\n1988:         result = [arg.example for arg in self.graphargs]\n1989:         return result\n1990: \n1991:     def remove_unused_get_attr_nodes(self) -> None:\n1992:         for node in sorted(self.graph.find_nodes(op=\"get_attr\"), reverse=True):\n1993:             if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\n1994:                 self.remove_node(node)\n1995: \n1996:     def remove_unused_graphargs(self) -> None:\n1997:         # NB: It's OK to drop GraphArg for symbols that ended up being\n1998:         # specialized iff they are not used in runtime assertions.  You don't\n1999:         # even have to make a guard for it, because ShapeEnv produce_guards\n2000:         # operates on tracked_fakes, which never gets pruned.\n2001:         # That being said, you'll get marginally better generated\n2002:         # guard code if you promote the guard into a Dynamo guard (since that\n2003:         # allows for the guard to be done using C++ guards.)  If we get\n2004:         # ShapeEnv guards to go into C++ guards, this will stop being a thing\n2005:         # though!\n2006: \n2007:         assert self.should_exit\n2008: \n2009:         # Miniature DCE pass, but only for obviously trivial operations\n2010:         def is_static_true(b_node: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\n2011:             if b_node is True:\n2012:                 return True\n2013:             if not isinstance(b_node, fx.Node):\n2014:                 return False\n2015:             b = b_node.meta.get(\"example_value\")\n2016:             if b is None:\n2017:                 return False\n2018:             if b is True:\n2019:                 return True\n2020:             if (\n2021:                 isinstance(b, torch.SymBool)\n2022:                 and (r := b.node.maybe_as_bool()) is not None\n2023:             ):\n2024:                 return r\n2025:             # TODO: We can also technically remove all cases when the input\n2026:             # doesn't have unbacked inputs, since it's all in the ShapeEnv\n2027:             return False\n2028: \n2029:         def is_symnode_arg(a: fx.node.Argument) -> bool:\n2030:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\n2031: \n2032:             if isinstance(a, (int, float, bool)):\n2033:                 return True\n2034:             if isinstance(a, fx.Node):\n2035:                 return isinstance(a.meta.get(\"example_value\"), SymTypes)\n2036:             return False\n2037: \n2038:         # NB: We assume that you cannot do mutations on int/float/bool,\n2039:         # because they are immutable types, and therefore is always safe to\n2040:         # DCE.\n2041:         def is_symnode_compute_node(node: fx.Node) -> bool:\n2042:             from torch.fx.experimental.sym_node import SymTypes\n2043: \n2044:             if node.op != \"call_function\":\n2045:                 return False\n2046:             # TODO: I don't think it's possible to have a bare int/float here?\n2047:             if not isinstance(node.meta.get(\"example_value\"), SymTypes):\n2048:                 return False\n2049:             # TODO: This will bail here if you ever end up with a more complicated\n2050:             # computation function, like sum(list_of_ints), even though it\n2051:             # should be DCE'able\n2052:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.args):\n2053:                 return False\n2054:             if not all(is_symnode_arg(a) for a in node.kwargs.values()):\n2055:                 return False\n2056:             return True\n2057: \n2058:         from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_accessor_node\n2059: \n2060:         for node in reversed(list(self.graph.nodes)):\n2061:             if len(list(node.users)) == 0:\n2062:                 if (\n2063:                     node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2064:                     or (node.op == \"call_function\" and node.target is operator.getitem)\n2065:                     or (\n2066:                         node.op == \"call_function\"\n2067:                         and node.target is torch._check\n2068:                         and is_static_true(node.args[0])\n2069:                     )\n2070:                     or is_symnode_compute_node(node)\n2071:                     or is_accessor_node(node)\n2072:                 ):\n2073:                     self.remove_node(node)\n2074: \n2075:         def placeholder_binds_symbol(node: fx.Node) -> Optional[sympy.Symbol]:\n2076:             arg = node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2077:             example = arg.example\n2078:             if isinstance(example, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(\n2079:                 example.node.expr, sympy.Symbol\n2080:             ):\n2081:                 return example.node.expr\n2082:             return None\n2083: \n2084:         def remove_unused(node: fx.Node) -> None:\n2085:             log.debug(\"REMOVE UNUSED GRAPHARG %s\", node.meta[\"grapharg\"].source.name())\n2086:             # I'm not really sure why you need to delete these from the\n2087:             # node since the node is going to get removed\n2088:             del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2089:             self.remove_node(node)\n2090:             self.real_value_cache.pop(node, None)\n2091: \n2092:         used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol] = set()\n2093: \n2094:         def update_used_symbols(\n2095:             used_symbols: set[sympy.Symbol], fake: Union[torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor]\n2096:         ) -> None:\n2097:             used_symbols |= free_symbols(fake)\n2098: \n2099:         recheck_placeholders = []\n2100:         for node in self.placeholders:\n2101:             binds_symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node) is not None\n2102:             # Don't delete symbol bindings yet\n2103:             if binds_symbol:\n2104:                 if not node.users:\n2105:                     recheck_placeholders.append(node)\n2106:             else:\n2107:                 if not node.users and not isinstance(\n2108:                     node.meta[\"grapharg\"], BackwardStateGraphArg\n2109:                 ):\n2110:                     remove_unused(node)\n2111:                 else:\n2112:                     # Register the free symbols as uses\n2113:                     arg = node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2114:                     if isinstance(arg, BackwardStateGraphArg):\n2115:                         continue\n2116:                     if isinstance(node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example, torch.ScriptObject):\n2117:                         real_script_obj = node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2118:                         fake_script_obj = node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example_strong_ref\n2119:                         if not torch._library.fake_class_registry.tracing_with_real(\n2120:                             real_script_obj\n2121:                         ):\n2122:                             flat_dict = dict(real_script_obj.__obj_flatten__())  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n2123:                             for attr in flat_dict.keys():\n2124:                                 fake_attr_val = getattr(\n2125:                                     fake_script_obj.wrapped_obj, attr\n2126:                                 )\n2127:                                 pytree.tree_map_only(\n2128:                                     (torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor),\n2129:                                     lambda t: update_used_symbols(used_symbols, t),\n2130:                                     fake_attr_val,\n2131:                                 )\n2132:                         continue\n2133:                     fake = (\n2134:                         arg.fake_tensor if arg.fake_tensor is not None else arg.example\n2135:                     )\n2136:                     update_used_symbols(used_symbols, fake)\n2137: \n2138:         # After removing unused graphargs, prune unused binds_symbol\n2139:         for node in recheck_placeholders:\n2140:             symbol = placeholder_binds_symbol(node)\n2141:             if symbol is not None:\n2142:                 if symbol not in used_symbols:\n2143:                     remove_unused(node)\n2144:                 else:\n2145:                     # Make sure we delete later occurrences of the same symbol\n2146:                     used_symbols.remove(symbol)\n2147: \n2148:     def remove_tensorify_specialized_graphargs(self) -> None:\n2149:         # This is a pretty interesting function. Basically we have this problem\n2150:         # where our compiler tends to choke when we have unused inputs. The way\n2151:         # we support dynamic float arguments is by doing a joint fx pass and\n2152:         # tensorifying away as many symfloats as we can. For the remaining symfloats\n2153:         # we have no choice but to specialize... HOWEVER at that point in time\n2154:         # we can no longer remove graph inputs. So our sledgehammer solution is to\n2155:         # save the state of what inputs we should have specialized in dynamo and\n2156:         # restart analysis. This function incorporates this \"view from the future\"\n2157:         # state and specializes inputs that we know we won't be able to tensorify\n2158:         # away in the joint pass. In principle we shouldn't choke on unused inputs\n2159:         # and so this shouldn't be necessary. In practice CUDA graphs choke on\n2160:         # unused inputs so we need this for now.\n2161: \n2162:         # Import here to prevent circular import\n2163:         from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import TensorifyState\n2164: \n2165:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2166:             example_value = node.meta.get(\"example_value\")\n2167:             if (\n2168:                 isinstance(example_value, FakeTensor)\n2169:                 and example_value.item_memo is not None\n2170:                 and hasattr(example_value.item_memo.node._expr, \"name\")\n2171:                 and all(u.target == \"item\" for u in node.users)\n2172:                 and TensorifyState.should_specialize(\n2173:                     # We use _expr instead of expr b/c we want the symbol not the replacement\n2174:                     example_value.item_memo.node._expr.name\n2175:                 )\n2176:             ):\n2177:                 for u in list(node.users):\n2178:                     u.replace_all_uses_with(guard_scalar(example_value.item_memo))\n2179:                     self.remove_node(u)\n2180:                 self.remove_node(node)\n2181: \n2182:     def add_output_instructions(self, prefix: list[Instruction]) -> None:\n2183:         \"\"\"\n2184:         We call this on the creation of a new compiled subgraph that is inserted\n2185:         before user code.\n2186:         \"\"\"\n2187:         self.output_instructions.extend(prefix)\n2188:         self.should_exit = True\n2189: \n2190:     def install_global_unsafe(self, name: str, value: Any) -> None:\n2191:         \"\"\"\n2192:         WARNING: prefer the safer `install_global_by_id/install_global`.\n2193:         torch.compile instances should be independent of each other;\n2194:         one footgun is to have one instance depend on the existence of\n2195:         a global installed by another instance. This can happen if we mangle\n2196:         a global the same way across both instances.\n2197:         \"\"\"\n2198:         assert name not in self.installed_globals\n2199:         self.installed_globals.add(name)\n2200:         self.cleanups.append(CleanupHook.create(self.global_scope, name, value))\n2201: \n2202:     def install_global_by_id(self, prefix: str, value: Any) -> str:\n2203:         \"\"\"\n2204:         Installs a global if it hasn't been installed already.\n2205:         This is determined by (prefix, id(value)) pair.\n2206: \n2207:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\n2208:         \"\"\"\n2209:         # NB: need self.compile_id to distinguish this global\n2210:         # from another global created in a different torch.compile instance\n2211:         name = f\"{prefix}_{id(value)}_c{self.compile_id}\"\n2212:         if name in self.installed_globals:\n2213:             return name\n2214:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\n2215:         return name\n2216: \n2217:     def install_global(self, prefix: str, value: Any) -> str:\n2218:         \"\"\"\n2219:         Installs a global, generating a unique name for it.\n2220: \n2221:         Returns the name of the newly installed global.\n2222:         \"\"\"\n2223:         # NB: unique_id is unique, even across torch.compile instances\n2224:         name = unique_id(prefix)\n2225:         self.install_global_unsafe(name, value)\n2226:         return name\n2227: \n2228:     def cleanup(self) -> None:\n2229:         # There is a reference cycle between tracer and OutputGraph, causing\n2230:         # some of the tensor objects to be held alive for longer than necessary.\n2231:         self.root_tx = None  # type: ignore[assignment]\n2232:         self.nn_modules.clear()\n2233:         self.param_name_to_source = None\n2234: \n2235:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n2236:             if \"grapharg\" in node.meta:\n2237:                 del node.meta[\"grapharg\"]\n2238:         self.real_value_cache.clear()\n2239:         self.input_name_to_proxy.clear()\n2240:         self.side_effects.clear()\n2241:         self.variable_tracker_cache.clear()\n2242:         self.register_finalizer_fns.clear()\n2243:         self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn.clear()\n2244:         self.tracing_context.clear()\n2245:         self.input_source_to_var.clear()\n2246:         self.unspec_variable_map.clear()\n2247:         self.backward_state.clear()\n2248: \n2249:     def add_graph_finalizer(\n2250:         self, register_finalizer: Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]\n2251:     ) -> None:\n2252:         self.register_finalizer_fns.append(register_finalizer)\n2253: \n2254:     def example_value_from_input_node(self, node: torch.fx.Node) -> Any:\n2255:         \"\"\"Extract the non-fake example tensor\"\"\"\n2256:         if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n2257:             return node.meta[\"grapharg\"].example\n2258:         assert node.op == \"get_attr\"\n2259:         return self.nn_modules[node.target]  # type: ignore[index]\n2260: \n2261: \n2262: err_epilogue = (\n2263:     \"With the current config, we will graph break \"\n2264:     \"(and fall back to eager-mode PyTorch) on all ops \"\n2265:     \"that have do not have the 'pt2_compliant_tag'. \"\n2266:     \"Please see the following doc for how to mark this op as PT2 compliant \"\n2267:     \"https://pytorch.org/tutorials/advanced/custom_ops_landing_page.html\"\n2268: )\n2269: \n2270: \n2271: def check_pt2_compliant_op(\n2272:     output_graph: OutputGraph, kind: str, target: Any, args: Any, kwargs: Any\n2273: ) -> None:\n2274:     if kind != \"call_function\":\n2275:         return\n2276: \n2277:     def encountered_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload) -> None:\n2278:         if target.namespace in {\"prim\", \"prims\", \"aten\"}:\n2279:             return\n2280:         output_graph.compliant_custom_ops.add(target)\n2281: \n2282:     def encountered_non_compliant_op(target: torch._ops.OpOverload, msg: str) -> None:\n2283:         output_graph.non_compliant_ops.add(target)\n2284:         if config.only_allow_pt2_compliant_ops:\n2285:             unimplemented_v2(\n2286:                 gb_type=\"Encountered non-PT2-compliant op\",\n2287:                 context=\"\",\n2288:                 explanation=msg + \" \" + err_epilogue,\n2289:                 hints=[],\n2290:             )\n2291: \n2292:     if isinstance(target, torch._ops.OpOverload):\n2293:         if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in target.tags:\n2294:             encountered_compliant_op(target)\n2295:             return\n2296:         encountered_non_compliant_op(\n2297:             target,\n2298:             f\"Encountered the torch.ops.OpOverload {target} that is not PT2 compliant.\",\n2299:         )\n2300:         return\n2301: \n2302:     if isinstance(target, torch._ops.OpOverloadPacket):\n2303:         overloads = tuple(target.overloads())\n2304:         # Optimization: Overload resolution is expensive.\n2305:         # If there's only one overload, we know what it will resolve to.\n2306:         if len(overloads) == 1:\n2307:             op = getattr(target, overloads[0])\n2308:             if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in op.tags:\n2309:                 encountered_compliant_op(op)\n2310:                 return\n2311:             encountered_non_compliant_op(\n2312:                 op,\n2313:                 f\"Encountered the non-overloaded \"\n2314:                 f\"torch.ops.OpOverloadPacket {target} \"\n2315:                 f\"that is not PT2 compliant. \",\n2316:             )\n2317:             return\n2318: \n2319:         args, kwargs = torch._dynamo.utils.get_fake_values_from_nodes(\n2320:             output_graph.current_tx, (args, kwargs), False\n2321:         )\n2322:         try:\n2323:             overload = torch._C._jit_resolve_packet(\n2324:                 target._qualified_op_name, *args, **kwargs\n2325:             )\n2326:         except RuntimeError as e:\n2327:             unimplemented_v2(\n2328:                 gb_type=\"Error when attempting to resolve op packet\",\n2329:                 context=\"\",\n2330:                 explanation=str(e),\n2331:                 hints=[],\n2332:             )\n2333: \n2334:         op = getattr(target, overload)\n2335:         if torch.Tag.pt2_compliant_tag in op.tags:\n2336:             encountered_compliant_op(op)\n2337:         else:\n2338:             encountered_non_compliant_op(\n2339:                 op,\n2340:                 f\"Encountered the torch.ops.OpOverloadPacket {target} \"\n2341:                 f\"which resolves to the overload ({overload}) that is \"\n2342:                 f\"not PT2 compliant.\",\n2343:             )\n2344: \n2345: \n2346: _compile_id_counter = itertools.count()\n2347: \n2348: P = ParamSpec(\"P\")\n2349: R = TypeVar(\"R\")\n2350: \n2351: \n2352: class LazyProxy:\n2353:     def __init__(\n2354:         self,\n2355:         tracer: \"SubgraphTracer\",\n2356:         fn: Callable[P, R],\n2357:         *args: P.args,\n2358:         **kwargs: P.kwargs,\n2359:     ) -> None:\n2360:         self.tracer = tracer\n2361:         self.fn = fn\n2362:         self.args = args\n2363:         self.kwargs = kwargs\n2364: \n2365:     def __call__(self) -> Any:\n2366:         return self.fn(*self.args, **self.kwargs)\n2367: \n2368: \n2369: class SubgraphTracer(fx.Tracer):\n2370:     \"\"\"\n2371:     Holds an FX graph that is being traced. OutputGraph owns a SubgraphTracer\n2372:     and the separation of responsibilities is that SubgraphTracer is\n2373:     responsible for building the graph while OutputGraph is responsible for\n2374:     compiling and executing the graph.\n2375:     \"\"\"\n2376: \n2377:     def __init__(\n2378:         self,\n2379: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL1878-2378"
  }
}
181. mcp__sourcegraph__sg_read_file @ -
