Output
{
  "content": "{\"content\":\"2379:         output_graph: \\\"OutputGraph\\\",\\n2380:         parent: Optional[\\\"SubgraphTracer\\\"] = None,\\n2381:         is_export: bool = False,\\n2382:         source_target: Optional[Target] = None,\\n2383:     ) -> None:\\n2384:         super().__init__()\\n2385:         self.output_graph = weakref.proxy(output_graph)\\n2386:         self.graph = torch.fx.Graph()\\n2387: \\n2388:         # See note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\\n2389:         self.is_export = is_export\\n2390:         # Map from graph input name to its placeholder proxy object, where the\\n2391:         # map's keys give all current placeholder node names and can be used to\\n2392:         # create unique node names\\n2393:         self.input_name_to_proxy: dict[str, fx.Proxy] = {}\\n2394:         # Node => computed real value (see utils.get_real_value)\\n2395:         self.real_value_cache: dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor] = {}\\n2396: \\n2397:         # SubgraphTracers can be nested. See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\\n2398:         self.parent = parent\\n2399:         self.source_target = source_target\\n2400:         # A dict mapping previously free variables (Proxy objects)\\n2401:         # to new Proxy objects that wrap inputs to this subgraph.\\n2402:         #\\n2403:         # This dict maps proxies in outer graphs to placeholders in current graph.\\n2404:         # It serves two purposes:\\n2405:         # - Proxies are associated with VariableTrackers. If we see\\n2406:         # the same VariableTracker twice (and it is a free variable),\\n2407:         # then we want to use the same Proxy in the current subgraph to\\n2408:         # record the tracing.\\n2409:         # - If we are tracing a HigherOrderOperator's body_fn, then we\\n2410:         # need to keep track of what free variables were lifted so we can\\n2411:         # rewrite the HigherOrderOperator call using the traced body_fn.\\n2412:         # Dicts maintain the order of args for the HigherOrderOperator call.\\n2413:         self.lifted_freevars: dict[fx.Proxy, fx.Proxy] = {}\\n2414: \\n2415:         # map basic symbols (unbacked and unbacked) to their bound proxies.\\n2416:         # There are only two cases where bound_symbols will be recorded:\\n2417:         # 1. when we create_graph_input for a backed SymInt that's basic symbol\\n2418:         # 2. when we track_unbacked_symbols for intermediate results that contain unbacked symints.\\n2419:         self.bound_symbols: dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, LazyProxy]] = {}\\n2420: \\n2421:         self.prev_inst = None\\n2422:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing into torch.utils.checkpoint\\n2423:         # as part of speculate_subgraph.\\n2424:         self.under_activation_checkpoint = False\\n2425:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\\n2426:         # during this tracer's tracing of torch.utils.checkpoint (via speculate_subgraph).\\n2427:         # Only safe if we know for sure that *NOT* replaying these side-effects during\\n2428:         # backward recomputation of the checkpoint region doesn't affect its correctness.\\n2429:         self.allow_side_effects_under_checkpoint = False\\n2430:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\\n2431:         # during this tracer's tracing. This is currently only used by experimental AC out-of-tree\\n2432:         # via torch._dynamo.utils._disable_side_effect_safety_checks_for_current_subtracer.\\n2433:         # Note: Externally visible side-effects are allowed if this flag OR the above flag is True.\\n2434:         self.unsafe_allow_externally_visible_side_effects = False\\n2435: \\n2436:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing (reconstructing) into a Python generator\\n2437:         self.is_reconstructing_generator = False\\n2438: \\n2439:         self.debug_level: int = parent.debug_level + 1 if parent is not None else 0\\n2440: \\n2441:         self._cur_code = None\\n2442:         self._orig_gm_meta: Optional[list[Any]] = None\\n2443:         self._orig_gm_lineno_map: Optional[dict[int, Optional[int]]] = None\\n2444:         self._orig_gm_firstlineno: Optional[int] = None\\n2445:         # Each SubgraphTracer is associated with a source target, which indicates\\n2446:         # which operator this subgraph is attached to. We compute a source_fn_stack\\n2447:         # based on the source target. For the root tracer, it's set to [].\\n2448:         # This is useful for debugging and transforming the exported graph.\\n2449:         if self.parent is None:\\n2450:             self.source_fn_stack: list[Any] = []\\n2451:         else:\\n2452:             self.source_fn_stack = self.parent.source_fn_stack + [\\n2453:                 (self.graph._target_to_str(source_target), source_target)\\n2454:             ]\\n2455: \\n2456:         # This is used to create a unique name for the placeholder\\n2457:         self._used_names: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\\n2458:         # Stores the versions of the input tensors at the time they are inserted\\n2459:         # as placeholders in the graph. This is used to track input mutation.\\n2460:         self._input_versions_at_beginning: list[int] = []\\n2461:         if torch.is_inference_mode_enabled():\\n2462:             raise RuntimeError(\\n2463:                 \\\"Inference mode is supposed to be disabled during compilation. Please open an issue.\\\"\\n2464:             )\\n2465: \\n2466:     # preserve original meta if it is available\\n2467:     def _maybe_preserve_original_meta(\\n2468:         self, tx: \\\"InstructionTranslatorBase\\\", node: fx.Node\\n2469:     ) -> None:\\n2470:         if (\\n2471:             self._orig_gm_meta\\n2472:             and self._orig_gm_lineno_map\\n2473:             and self._orig_gm_firstlineno\\n2474:         ):\\n2475:             lineno = tx.current_instruction.starts_line\\n2476:             node_idx = None\\n2477:             if lineno is not None:\\n2478:                 node_idx = self._orig_gm_lineno_map.get(\\n2479:                     lineno - self._orig_gm_firstlineno, None\\n2480:                 )\\n2481:             if node_idx is not None:\\n2482:                 meta = self._orig_gm_meta[node_idx]\\n2483:                 for field in fx.proxy._COPY_META_FIELDS:\\n2484:                     if field in meta:\\n2485:                         node.meta[field] = meta[field]\\n2486:                 if \\\"stack_trace\\\" in meta:\\n2487:                     node.meta[\\\"stack_trace\\\"] = meta[\\\"stack_trace\\\"]\\n2488: \\n2489:     def create_proxy(\\n2490:         self,\\n2491:         kind: str,\\n2492:         target: Any,\\n2493:         args: Any,\\n2494:         kwargs: Any,\\n2495:         name: Optional[str] = None,\\n2496:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,\\n2497:         proxy_factory_fn: Optional[Callable[[fx.Node], fx.Proxy]] = None,\\n2498:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n2499:         # NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling]\\n2500:         # --------------------------------------------------------\\n2501:         # Read NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] first.\\n2502:         #\\n2503:         # Let's say we're in the middle of introspecting the body of a possibly\\n2504:         # nested HigherOrderOperator, and we see a free variable.\\n2505:         #\\n2506:         # There are two cases:\\n2507:         # 1. We see a free variable that is already tracked by Dynamo.\\n2508:         # 2. We see a free variable that has not been tracked by Dynamo\\n2509:         #\\n2510:         # In case 1, we call `maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input` (below)\\n2511:         # which will lift the freevar to be an input of this subgraph\\n2512:         # and also recursively lift it to be an input on the parent(s).\\n2513:         #\\n2514:         # In case 2, before the call to `create_proxy`, the InstructionTranslator\\n2515:         # will see the freevar when it gets loaded by Python bytecode.\\n2516:         # E.g. for Python 3.11 the bytecodes that may do this are LOAD_DEREF or\\n2517:         # LOAD_GLOBAL.\\n2518:         # There, the InstructionTranslator asks Dynamo to begin tracking the\\n2519:         # freevar by building a new Variable.\\n2520:         # Building a new Variable automatically lifts the freevar to be an\\n2521:         # input of the root SubgraphTracer.\\n2522:         #\\n2523:         # The implications for the code below are:\\n2524:         # - We will always be in Case 1 when we get to this code.\\n2525:         # - Any \\\"free variable\\\" we encounter here is guaranteed to already be\\n2526:         #   bound, that is, it is either a graph input of the root graph, or\\n2527:         #   some local variable of the root graph or a subgraph.\\n2528:         # - The additional work we need to do here is *only* that we need to\\n2529:         #   lift this free variable into inputs (recursively) of each nested\\n2530:         #   higher-order-op subgraph until we hit the subgraph where the free\\n2531:         #   variable is bound\\n2532:         if self.parent is not None:\\n2533:             flat_args, tree_spec = pytree.tree_flatten((args, kwargs))\\n2534:             new_flat_args = []\\n2535:             for arg in flat_args:\\n2536:                 maybe_new_arg = self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\\n2537:                 new_flat_args.append(maybe_new_arg)\\n2538: \\n2539:             args, kwargs = pytree.tree_unflatten(new_flat_args, tree_spec)\\n2540: \\n2541:         rv = super().create_proxy(\\n2542:             kind,\\n2543:             target,\\n2544:             args,\\n2545:             kwargs,\\n2546:             name,\\n2547:             type_expr,\\n2548:             proxy_factory_fn,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\\n2549:         )\\n2550: \\n2551:         # append stack trace to fx node\\n2552:         tx = self.output_graph.current_tx\\n2553: \\n2554:         # log detailed location of line of code in 3.11\\n2555:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11) and kind in (\\n2556:             \\\"call_function\\\",\\n2557:             \\\"call_method\\\",\\n2558:             \\\"call_module\\\",\\n2559:         ):\\n2560:             cur_inst = tx.current_instruction\\n2561:             if (\\n2562:                 cur_inst is not self.prev_inst\\n2563:                 and cur_inst.positions is not None\\n2564:                 and cur_inst.positions.lineno is not None\\n2565:             ):\\n2566:                 tx_code = tx.f_code\\n2567:                 header = tx.get_line_of_code_header(lineno=cur_inst.positions.lineno)\\n2568: \\n2569:                 def get_trace_call_log_str() -> str:\\n2570:                     line = get_instruction_source_311(tx_code, cur_inst).rstrip()\\n2571:                     return f\\\"TRACE FX call {rv.node.name} from {header}\\\\n{line}\\\"\\n2572: \\n2573:                 trace_call_log.debug(\\\"%s\\\", LazyString(get_trace_call_log_str))\\n2574:                 self.prev_inst = cur_inst\\n2575: \\n2576:         # update reference to original meta if we're tracing a new code object\\n2577:         is_retracing = False\\n2578:         if tx.f_code is not self._cur_code:\\n2579:             orig_graphmodule_maybe = code_context.get_context(tx.f_code).get(\\n2580:                 \\\"orig_graphmodule\\\", lambda: None\\n2581:             )()\\n2582:             if isinstance(orig_graphmodule_maybe, torch.fx.GraphModule):\\n2583:                 is_retracing = True\\n2584:                 self._orig_gm_meta = [\\n2585:                     nd.meta for nd in orig_graphmodule_maybe.graph.nodes\\n2586:                 ]\\n2587:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = orig_graphmodule_maybe._lineno_map\\n2588:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = (\\n2589:                     orig_graphmodule_maybe.forward.__code__.co_firstlineno\\n2590:                 )\\n2591:             else:\\n2592:                 self._orig_gm_meta = None\\n2593:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = None\\n2594:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = None\\n2595:         nn_module_stack = tx.nn_module_stack\\n2596:         if nn_module_stack:\\n2597:             rv.node.meta[\\\"nn_module_stack\\\"] = nn_module_stack.copy()\\n2598: \\n2599:         if kind in {\\\"call_function\\\", \\\"call_method\\\"}:\\n2600:             rv.node.meta[\\\"source_fn_stack\\\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\\n2601:                 (rv.node.name, target)\\n2602:             ]\\n2603:         elif kind == \\\"call_module\\\":\\n2604:             if self.parent is not None:\\n2605:                 # TODO can remove once inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is always True\\n2606:                 unimplemented_v2(\\n2607:                     gb_type=\\\"Invoking an nn.Module inside a higher order operator\\\",\\n2608:                     context=f\\\"Higher order op name: {self.source_target}\\\",\\n2609:                     explanation=\\\"This is not supported.\\\",\\n2610:                     hints=[],\\n2611:                 )\\n2612:             # For modules we store the class\\n2613:             rv.node.meta[\\\"source_fn_stack\\\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\\n2614:                 (\\n2615:                     rv.node.name,\\n2616:                     next(\\n2617:                         ty\\n2618:                         for k, (_, ty) in rv.node.meta[\\\"nn_module_stack\\\"].items()\\n2619:                         if k.split(\\\"@\\\")[0] == target\\n2620:                     ),\\n2621:                 )\\n2622:             ]\\n2623: \\n2624:         self._maybe_preserve_original_meta(tx, rv.node)\\n2625: \\n2626:         if not is_retracing:\\n2627:             if \\\"nn_module_stack\\\" not in rv.node.meta:\\n2628:                 nn_module_stack = tx.nn_module_stack\\n2629:                 if nn_module_stack:\\n2630:                     rv.node.meta[\\\"nn_module_stack\\\"] = nn_module_stack.copy()\\n2631: \\n2632:             if \\\"source_fn_stack\\\" not in rv.node.meta:\\n2633:                 if kind in {\\\"call_function\\\", \\\"call_method\\\"}:\\n2634:                     rv.node.meta[\\\"source_fn_stack\\\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\\n2635:                         (rv.node.name, target)\\n2636:                     ]\\n2637:                 elif kind == \\\"call_module\\\":\\n2638:                     if self.parent is not None:\\n2639:                         # TODO can remove once inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is always True\\n2640:                         unimplemented_v2(\\n2641:                             gb_type=\\\"Invoking an nn.Module inside a HigherOrderOperator\\\",\\n2642:                             context=\\\"\\\",\\n2643:                             explanation=\\\"This is not supported.\\\",\\n2644:                             hints=[],\\n2645:                         )\\n2646:                     # For modules we store the class\\n2647:                     rv.node.meta[\\\"source_fn_stack\\\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\\n2648:                         (\\n2649:                             rv.node.name,\\n2650:                             rv.node.meta[\\\"nn_module_stack\\\"][target][1],\\n2651:                         )\\n2652:                     ]\\n2653: \\n2654:         if \\\"stack_trace\\\" not in rv.node.meta:\\n2655:             frame_summaries: list[traceback.FrameSummary] = []\\n2656:             while tx:\\n2657:                 # Avoid frame summaries from inside the torch/nn/modules. This ensures that we keep the stack trace of\\n2658:                 # the user code.\\n2659:                 if not tx.is_co_filename_from_nn_modules():\\n2660:                     frame_summaries.append(tx.frame_summary())\\n2661:                 tx = getattr(tx, \\\"parent\\\", None)\\n2662:             # Reverse the frame_summaries, such that the innermost frame is at the last\\n2663:             frame_summaries.reverse()\\n2664: \\n2665:             # official from_list stub doesn't have new-style type\\n2666:             msgs = traceback.StackSummary.from_list(frame_summaries).format()\\n2667:             rv.node.stack_trace = \\\"\\\".join(msgs)\\n2668: \\n2669:         if (\\n2670:             torch._dynamo.config.use_graph_deduplication\\n2671:             or torch._dynamo.config.track_nodes_for_deduplication\\n2672:         ):\\n2673:             self.output_graph.region_tracker.track_node(\\n2674:                 self.output_graph.current_tx, rv.node\\n2675:             )\\n2676:         return rv\\n2677: \\n2678:     def create_node(\\n2679:         self,\\n2680:         op: str,\\n2681:         target: Target,\\n2682:         args: Any = None,\\n2683:         kwargs: Any = None,\\n2684:         name: Optional[str] = None,\\n2685:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,\\n2686:     ) -> fx.Node:\\n2687:         check_pt2_compliant_op(self.output_graph, op, target, args, kwargs)\\n2688:         if self.parent is not None:\\n2689:             flat_args = pytree.arg_tree_leaves(*args, **kwargs)\\n2690:             for arg in flat_args:\\n2691:                 if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Node):\\n2692:                     continue\\n2693:                 assert arg.graph == self.graph, (\\n2694:                     \\\"create_node using arg not from this SubgraphTracer\\\"\\n2695:                 )\\n2696: \\n2697:         node = super().create_node(op, target, args, kwargs, name, type_expr)\\n2698:         node.meta[\\\"creation_timestamp\\\"] = self.output_graph.timestamp\\n2699:         self._used_names.add(node.name)\\n2700:         return node\\n2701: \\n2702:     # Note: we did not override erase_node since\\n2703:     # we call self.graph.erase_node elsewhere\\n2704:     def remove_node(self, node: fx.Node) -> None:\\n2705:         if len(node.users) > 0:\\n2706:             user_graph_nodes: list[torch.fx.Node] = []\\n2707:             for user in node.users.keys():\\n2708:                 # For the case where user.graph == self.graph, that is a real bug and will raise\\n2709:                 # properly.\\n2710:                 if user.graph != self.graph:\\n2711:                     # This is a nested graph, which needs to be deleted.\\n2712:                     # If we do not do this, we will raise on attempting to remove this.\\n2713:                     # As we only get here during restoration cleanup, this is sound.\\n2714:                     user_graph_nodes.extend(reversed(list(user.graph.nodes)))\\n2715:             for other_graph_node in user_graph_nodes:\\n2716:                 other_graph_node.graph.erase_node(other_graph_node)\\n2717:         self.graph.erase_node(node)\\n2718:         self.input_name_to_proxy.pop(node.name, None)\\n2719: \\n2720:     # when before=True, we will insert this input before the most recent\\n2721:     # inserted proxy.  This is a hack to get around an ordering problem,\\n2722:     # where we first insert a tensor argument, and then insert bindings\\n2723:     # for SymInts that may occur in the tensor argument.\\n2724:     # Remove this if https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/99007 gets\\n2725:     # fixed.\\n2726:     def create_graph_input(\\n2727:         self,\\n2728:         name: str,\\n2729:         type_expr: Any,\\n2730:         example_value: Any,\\n2731:         before: bool = False,\\n2732:         source: Optional[Source] = None,\\n2733:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n2734:         if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n2735:             self._input_versions_at_beginning.append(example_value._version)\\n2736:         log.debug(\\n2737:             \\\"create_graph_input %s %s %s at debug_level %s before=%s\\\",\\n2738:             name,\\n2739:             source.name() if source is not None else \\\"(none)\\\",\\n2740:             example_value,\\n2741:             self.debug_level,\\n2742:             before,\\n2743:         )\\n2744:         if source is None:\\n2745:             assert self.parent is not None, (\\n2746:                 f\\\"you are required to provide a source for inputs {name} example_val {example_value} on the root tracer\\\"\\n2747:             )\\n2748: \\n2749:         # Note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\\n2750:         # In eager, we are generally OK with adding graph inputs whenever we\\n2751:         # want, because we take care of writing the bytecode that knows how\\n2752:         # to source all the inputs.\\n2753:         #\\n2754:         # In export, this is bad, because you want a self-contained export\\n2755:         # object which only depends on the inputs you explicitly passed to it.\\n2756:         # So we are a bit more strict about what sources can become inputs\\n2757:         # in export\\n2758:         if self.is_export and self.parent is None:\\n2759:             assert source is not None\\n2760:             if not is_from_local_source(source, only_allow_input=True):\\n2761:                 self.output_graph.source_to_user_stacks.setdefault(source, []).append(\\n2762:                     TracingContext.extract_stack()\\n2763:                 )\\n2764: \\n2765:         # _used_names contains the names of all the nodes in the graph,\\n2766:         # including intermediates. This ensures that we do not have a name\\n2767:         # collision.\\n2768:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self._used_names)\\n2769:         if self.input_name_to_proxy:\\n2770:             prev_name = next(reversed(self.input_name_to_proxy))\\n2771:             node = self.input_name_to_proxy[prev_name].node\\n2772:             if before:\\n2773:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(node)\\n2774:             else:\\n2775:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_after(node)\\n2776:         else:\\n2777:             ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(None)\\n2778:         with ctx:\\n2779:             proxy = self.create_proxy(\\\"placeholder\\\", name, (), {}, type_expr=type_expr)\\n2780:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\\n2781:             if self.input_name_to_proxy and before:\\n2782:                 k, v = self.input_name_to_proxy.popitem()\\n2783:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\\n2784:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[k] = v\\n2785:             else:\\n2786:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\\n2787: \\n2788:             # For placeholder nodes, `name` is passed as a str to the target,\\n2789:             # and then torch.fx decides the node.name. So, record the `target`\\n2790:             # name as well in the _used_names to prevent any collision.\\n2791:             self._used_names.add(name)\\n2792: \\n2793:             # NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]\\n2794:             # Whenever we call create_graph_input, we try to also lift the basic symbols in example values\\n2795:             # as graph input.\\n2796:             # This applies to both top-level graph and subgraphs in higher order ops.\\n2797:             # It has several cases:\\n2798:             #  1. When create_graph_input for a tensor that has symbolic shapes,\\n2799:             #     we look for basic symbols in its size and stride, we check if the symbol is bound\\n2800:             #     in current graph (i.e. bound_symbols), it it's not bound, we'll create a placeholder\\n2801:             #     for it then recursively check its parent, creates ph if not bound.\\n2802:             #     Every tracer maintains a mapping (i.e. lifted_freevars)\\n2803:             #     that maps from parent proxy to proxy in current tracer for the symbol.\\n2804:             #  2. When create_graph_input for a tensor with unbacked symbolic shapes,\\n2805:             #     Backed symbols all come from inputs's symbolic shape. But unbacked symbols\\n2806:             #     can be created while tracing. So we use track_unbacked_symbols will intercept\\n2807:             #     at wrap_fx_proxy, and try to bind the unbacked symbols immediately after they're\\n2808:             #     created.\\n2809:             #  3. subgraph will also lifted basic symbols in compound exprs of tensor shape.\\n2810:             #     For example, if an input to subgraph takes size [s1+s2//8], we'll look for the\\n2811:             #     the free symbols in the sizes and lift as inputs similar to 1 in _lift_symbols_in_symint)\\n2812:             #  4. When create_graph_input for a SymInt, if the symint is a basic symbol, we'll track it\\n2813:             #     in bound_symbols so that we don't lift the same basic symbol twice. When the symint is a\\n2814:             #     compound expr, we'll just create the proxy for the compouned expr but not lift its basic symbols.\\n2815:             # Also see NOTE: [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\\n2816:             is_strict_export = self.is_export\\n2817:             is_non_strict_export = torch.compiler.is_compiling()\\n2818:             if not is_strict_export and not is_non_strict_export:\\n2819:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n2820:                     self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value, source)\\n2821:                 elif isinstance(example_value, (list, tuple)):\\n2822:                     for i, e in enumerate(example_value):\\n2823:                         if not isinstance(e, torch.Tensor):\\n2824:                             continue\\n2825: \\n2826:                         e_source = None\\n2827:                         if source:\\n2828:                             e_source = GetItemSource(\\n2829:                                 base=source, index=i, index_is_slice=False\\n2830:                             )\\n2831: \\n2832:                         self._lift_basic_symbols(e, e_source)\\n2833: \\n2834:             # Bound the symbol to ph if example_value is a SymInt with basic symbol.\\n2835:             if isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(\\n2836:                 example_value.node.expr, sympy.Symbol\\n2837:             ):\\n2838:                 self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr] = proxy\\n2839:             return proxy\\n2840: \\n2841:     # See NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling] for more details\\n2842:     def lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(\\n2843:         self, proxy: fx.Proxy\\n2844:     ) -> Union[LazyProxy, fx.Proxy]:\\n2845:         # You're doing something wrong if we are the root SubgraphTracer because\\n2846:         # Dynamo adds tensors to graph inputs before creating a proxy for them.\\n2847:         assert self.parent is not None, (\\n2848:             \\\"lift_tracked_freevar_to_input should not be called on root SubgraphTracer\\\"\\n2849:         )\\n2850: \\n2851:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\\\"example_value\\\"]\\n2852: \\n2853:         # To avoid lifting the same symbol twice, we check whether basic symbols has been tracked.\\n2854:         # For example, the basic symbols may have already been lifted for current subgraph when\\n2855:         # we automatically lift basic symbols in the sizes/strides of a tensor t.\\n2856:         # Suppose parent graph calls sz = t.size()[0], it creates\\n2857:         # a proxy in parent and the subgraph accesses sz via closure. sz's proxy is not tracked\\n2858:         # in current sub-tracer so we may lift the same symbol twice.\\n2859:         if (\\n2860:             isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt)\\n2861:             and example_value.node.expr in self.bound_symbols\\n2862:         ):\\n2863:             return self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr]\\n2864: \\n2865:         # Proxies are associated with VariableTracker.\\n2866:         # It is possible that we've already lifted the Proxy to be an input.\\n2867:         # If that is the case, just return the already lifted Proxy.\\n2868:         if proxy in self.lifted_freevars:\\n2869:             return self.lifted_freevars[proxy]\\n2870: \\n2871:         # We first lift proxy to parent's graph then lift to current grpah's input\\n2872:         # so that when we bind symints of the sizes in current graph, those symints\\n2873:         # would already be lifted as inputs to parent graph.\\n2874:         if proxy.tracer != self.parent:\\n2875:             self.parent.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(proxy)\\n2876: \\n2877:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\\\"example_value\\\"]\\n2878:         new_proxy = self.create_graph_input(\\n2879:             proxy.node.name, type(example_value), example_value\\n2880: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2379-2879\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2379:         output_graph: \"OutputGraph\",\n2380:         parent: Optional[\"SubgraphTracer\"] = None,\n2381:         is_export: bool = False,\n2382:         source_target: Optional[Target] = None,\n2383:     ) -> None:\n2384:         super().__init__()\n2385:         self.output_graph = weakref.proxy(output_graph)\n2386:         self.graph = torch.fx.Graph()\n2387: \n2388:         # See note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\n2389:         self.is_export = is_export\n2390:         # Map from graph input name to its placeholder proxy object, where the\n2391:         # map's keys give all current placeholder node names and can be used to\n2392:         # create unique node names\n2393:         self.input_name_to_proxy: dict[str, fx.Proxy] = {}\n2394:         # Node => computed real value (see utils.get_real_value)\n2395:         self.real_value_cache: dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor] = {}\n2396: \n2397:         # SubgraphTracers can be nested. See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\n2398:         self.parent = parent\n2399:         self.source_target = source_target\n2400:         # A dict mapping previously free variables (Proxy objects)\n2401:         # to new Proxy objects that wrap inputs to this subgraph.\n2402:         #\n2403:         # This dict maps proxies in outer graphs to placeholders in current graph.\n2404:         # It serves two purposes:\n2405:         # - Proxies are associated with VariableTrackers. If we see\n2406:         # the same VariableTracker twice (and it is a free variable),\n2407:         # then we want to use the same Proxy in the current subgraph to\n2408:         # record the tracing.\n2409:         # - If we are tracing a HigherOrderOperator's body_fn, then we\n2410:         # need to keep track of what free variables were lifted so we can\n2411:         # rewrite the HigherOrderOperator call using the traced body_fn.\n2412:         # Dicts maintain the order of args for the HigherOrderOperator call.\n2413:         self.lifted_freevars: dict[fx.Proxy, fx.Proxy] = {}\n2414: \n2415:         # map basic symbols (unbacked and unbacked) to their bound proxies.\n2416:         # There are only two cases where bound_symbols will be recorded:\n2417:         # 1. when we create_graph_input for a backed SymInt that's basic symbol\n2418:         # 2. when we track_unbacked_symbols for intermediate results that contain unbacked symints.\n2419:         self.bound_symbols: dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, LazyProxy]] = {}\n2420: \n2421:         self.prev_inst = None\n2422:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing into torch.utils.checkpoint\n2423:         # as part of speculate_subgraph.\n2424:         self.under_activation_checkpoint = False\n2425:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\n2426:         # during this tracer's tracing of torch.utils.checkpoint (via speculate_subgraph).\n2427:         # Only safe if we know for sure that *NOT* replaying these side-effects during\n2428:         # backward recomputation of the checkpoint region doesn't affect its correctness.\n2429:         self.allow_side_effects_under_checkpoint = False\n2430:         # True if we want to allow externally visible side-effects (doesn't throw error on their existence)\n2431:         # during this tracer's tracing. This is currently only used by experimental AC out-of-tree\n2432:         # via torch._dynamo.utils._disable_side_effect_safety_checks_for_current_subtracer.\n2433:         # Note: Externally visible side-effects are allowed if this flag OR the above flag is True.\n2434:         self.unsafe_allow_externally_visible_side_effects = False\n2435: \n2436:         # True if this tracer is currently tracing (reconstructing) into a Python generator\n2437:         self.is_reconstructing_generator = False\n2438: \n2439:         self.debug_level: int = parent.debug_level + 1 if parent is not None else 0\n2440: \n2441:         self._cur_code = None\n2442:         self._orig_gm_meta: Optional[list[Any]] = None\n2443:         self._orig_gm_lineno_map: Optional[dict[int, Optional[int]]] = None\n2444:         self._orig_gm_firstlineno: Optional[int] = None\n2445:         # Each SubgraphTracer is associated with a source target, which indicates\n2446:         # which operator this subgraph is attached to. We compute a source_fn_stack\n2447:         # based on the source target. For the root tracer, it's set to [].\n2448:         # This is useful for debugging and transforming the exported graph.\n2449:         if self.parent is None:\n2450:             self.source_fn_stack: list[Any] = []\n2451:         else:\n2452:             self.source_fn_stack = self.parent.source_fn_stack + [\n2453:                 (self.graph._target_to_str(source_target), source_target)\n2454:             ]\n2455: \n2456:         # This is used to create a unique name for the placeholder\n2457:         self._used_names: OrderedSet[str] = OrderedSet()\n2458:         # Stores the versions of the input tensors at the time they are inserted\n2459:         # as placeholders in the graph. This is used to track input mutation.\n2460:         self._input_versions_at_beginning: list[int] = []\n2461:         if torch.is_inference_mode_enabled():\n2462:             raise RuntimeError(\n2463:                 \"Inference mode is supposed to be disabled during compilation. Please open an issue.\"\n2464:             )\n2465: \n2466:     # preserve original meta if it is available\n2467:     def _maybe_preserve_original_meta(\n2468:         self, tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\", node: fx.Node\n2469:     ) -> None:\n2470:         if (\n2471:             self._orig_gm_meta\n2472:             and self._orig_gm_lineno_map\n2473:             and self._orig_gm_firstlineno\n2474:         ):\n2475:             lineno = tx.current_instruction.starts_line\n2476:             node_idx = None\n2477:             if lineno is not None:\n2478:                 node_idx = self._orig_gm_lineno_map.get(\n2479:                     lineno - self._orig_gm_firstlineno, None\n2480:                 )\n2481:             if node_idx is not None:\n2482:                 meta = self._orig_gm_meta[node_idx]\n2483:                 for field in fx.proxy._COPY_META_FIELDS:\n2484:                     if field in meta:\n2485:                         node.meta[field] = meta[field]\n2486:                 if \"stack_trace\" in meta:\n2487:                     node.meta[\"stack_trace\"] = meta[\"stack_trace\"]\n2488: \n2489:     def create_proxy(\n2490:         self,\n2491:         kind: str,\n2492:         target: Any,\n2493:         args: Any,\n2494:         kwargs: Any,\n2495:         name: Optional[str] = None,\n2496:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,\n2497:         proxy_factory_fn: Optional[Callable[[fx.Node], fx.Proxy]] = None,\n2498:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\n2499:         # NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling]\n2500:         # --------------------------------------------------------\n2501:         # Read NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] first.\n2502:         #\n2503:         # Let's say we're in the middle of introspecting the body of a possibly\n2504:         # nested HigherOrderOperator, and we see a free variable.\n2505:         #\n2506:         # There are two cases:\n2507:         # 1. We see a free variable that is already tracked by Dynamo.\n2508:         # 2. We see a free variable that has not been tracked by Dynamo\n2509:         #\n2510:         # In case 1, we call `maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input` (below)\n2511:         # which will lift the freevar to be an input of this subgraph\n2512:         # and also recursively lift it to be an input on the parent(s).\n2513:         #\n2514:         # In case 2, before the call to `create_proxy`, the InstructionTranslator\n2515:         # will see the freevar when it gets loaded by Python bytecode.\n2516:         # E.g. for Python 3.11 the bytecodes that may do this are LOAD_DEREF or\n2517:         # LOAD_GLOBAL.\n2518:         # There, the InstructionTranslator asks Dynamo to begin tracking the\n2519:         # freevar by building a new Variable.\n2520:         # Building a new Variable automatically lifts the freevar to be an\n2521:         # input of the root SubgraphTracer.\n2522:         #\n2523:         # The implications for the code below are:\n2524:         # - We will always be in Case 1 when we get to this code.\n2525:         # - Any \"free variable\" we encounter here is guaranteed to already be\n2526:         #   bound, that is, it is either a graph input of the root graph, or\n2527:         #   some local variable of the root graph or a subgraph.\n2528:         # - The additional work we need to do here is *only* that we need to\n2529:         #   lift this free variable into inputs (recursively) of each nested\n2530:         #   higher-order-op subgraph until we hit the subgraph where the free\n2531:         #   variable is bound\n2532:         if self.parent is not None:\n2533:             flat_args, tree_spec = pytree.tree_flatten((args, kwargs))\n2534:             new_flat_args = []\n2535:             for arg in flat_args:\n2536:                 maybe_new_arg = self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\n2537:                 new_flat_args.append(maybe_new_arg)\n2538: \n2539:             args, kwargs = pytree.tree_unflatten(new_flat_args, tree_spec)\n2540: \n2541:         rv = super().create_proxy(\n2542:             kind,\n2543:             target,\n2544:             args,\n2545:             kwargs,\n2546:             name,\n2547:             type_expr,\n2548:             proxy_factory_fn,  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n2549:         )\n2550: \n2551:         # append stack trace to fx node\n2552:         tx = self.output_graph.current_tx\n2553: \n2554:         # log detailed location of line of code in 3.11\n2555:         if sys.version_info >= (3, 11) and kind in (\n2556:             \"call_function\",\n2557:             \"call_method\",\n2558:             \"call_module\",\n2559:         ):\n2560:             cur_inst = tx.current_instruction\n2561:             if (\n2562:                 cur_inst is not self.prev_inst\n2563:                 and cur_inst.positions is not None\n2564:                 and cur_inst.positions.lineno is not None\n2565:             ):\n2566:                 tx_code = tx.f_code\n2567:                 header = tx.get_line_of_code_header(lineno=cur_inst.positions.lineno)\n2568: \n2569:                 def get_trace_call_log_str() -> str:\n2570:                     line = get_instruction_source_311(tx_code, cur_inst).rstrip()\n2571:                     return f\"TRACE FX call {rv.node.name} from {header}\\n{line}\"\n2572: \n2573:                 trace_call_log.debug(\"%s\", LazyString(get_trace_call_log_str))\n2574:                 self.prev_inst = cur_inst\n2575: \n2576:         # update reference to original meta if we're tracing a new code object\n2577:         is_retracing = False\n2578:         if tx.f_code is not self._cur_code:\n2579:             orig_graphmodule_maybe = code_context.get_context(tx.f_code).get(\n2580:                 \"orig_graphmodule\", lambda: None\n2581:             )()\n2582:             if isinstance(orig_graphmodule_maybe, torch.fx.GraphModule):\n2583:                 is_retracing = True\n2584:                 self._orig_gm_meta = [\n2585:                     nd.meta for nd in orig_graphmodule_maybe.graph.nodes\n2586:                 ]\n2587:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = orig_graphmodule_maybe._lineno_map\n2588:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = (\n2589:                     orig_graphmodule_maybe.forward.__code__.co_firstlineno\n2590:                 )\n2591:             else:\n2592:                 self._orig_gm_meta = None\n2593:                 self._orig_gm_lineno_map = None\n2594:                 self._orig_gm_firstlineno = None\n2595:         nn_module_stack = tx.nn_module_stack\n2596:         if nn_module_stack:\n2597:             rv.node.meta[\"nn_module_stack\"] = nn_module_stack.copy()\n2598: \n2599:         if kind in {\"call_function\", \"call_method\"}:\n2600:             rv.node.meta[\"source_fn_stack\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\n2601:                 (rv.node.name, target)\n2602:             ]\n2603:         elif kind == \"call_module\":\n2604:             if self.parent is not None:\n2605:                 # TODO can remove once inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is always True\n2606:                 unimplemented_v2(\n2607:                     gb_type=\"Invoking an nn.Module inside a higher order operator\",\n2608:                     context=f\"Higher order op name: {self.source_target}\",\n2609:                     explanation=\"This is not supported.\",\n2610:                     hints=[],\n2611:                 )\n2612:             # For modules we store the class\n2613:             rv.node.meta[\"source_fn_stack\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\n2614:                 (\n2615:                     rv.node.name,\n2616:                     next(\n2617:                         ty\n2618:                         for k, (_, ty) in rv.node.meta[\"nn_module_stack\"].items()\n2619:                         if k.split(\"@\")[0] == target\n2620:                     ),\n2621:                 )\n2622:             ]\n2623: \n2624:         self._maybe_preserve_original_meta(tx, rv.node)\n2625: \n2626:         if not is_retracing:\n2627:             if \"nn_module_stack\" not in rv.node.meta:\n2628:                 nn_module_stack = tx.nn_module_stack\n2629:                 if nn_module_stack:\n2630:                     rv.node.meta[\"nn_module_stack\"] = nn_module_stack.copy()\n2631: \n2632:             if \"source_fn_stack\" not in rv.node.meta:\n2633:                 if kind in {\"call_function\", \"call_method\"}:\n2634:                     rv.node.meta[\"source_fn_stack\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\n2635:                         (rv.node.name, target)\n2636:                     ]\n2637:                 elif kind == \"call_module\":\n2638:                     if self.parent is not None:\n2639:                         # TODO can remove once inline_inbuilt_nn_modules is always True\n2640:                         unimplemented_v2(\n2641:                             gb_type=\"Invoking an nn.Module inside a HigherOrderOperator\",\n2642:                             context=\"\",\n2643:                             explanation=\"This is not supported.\",\n2644:                             hints=[],\n2645:                         )\n2646:                     # For modules we store the class\n2647:                     rv.node.meta[\"source_fn_stack\"] = self.source_fn_stack + [\n2648:                         (\n2649:                             rv.node.name,\n2650:                             rv.node.meta[\"nn_module_stack\"][target][1],\n2651:                         )\n2652:                     ]\n2653: \n2654:         if \"stack_trace\" not in rv.node.meta:\n2655:             frame_summaries: list[traceback.FrameSummary] = []\n2656:             while tx:\n2657:                 # Avoid frame summaries from inside the torch/nn/modules. This ensures that we keep the stack trace of\n2658:                 # the user code.\n2659:                 if not tx.is_co_filename_from_nn_modules():\n2660:                     frame_summaries.append(tx.frame_summary())\n2661:                 tx = getattr(tx, \"parent\", None)\n2662:             # Reverse the frame_summaries, such that the innermost frame is at the last\n2663:             frame_summaries.reverse()\n2664: \n2665:             # official from_list stub doesn't have new-style type\n2666:             msgs = traceback.StackSummary.from_list(frame_summaries).format()\n2667:             rv.node.stack_trace = \"\".join(msgs)\n2668: \n2669:         if (\n2670:             torch._dynamo.config.use_graph_deduplication\n2671:             or torch._dynamo.config.track_nodes_for_deduplication\n2672:         ):\n2673:             self.output_graph.region_tracker.track_node(\n2674:                 self.output_graph.current_tx, rv.node\n2675:             )\n2676:         return rv\n2677: \n2678:     def create_node(\n2679:         self,\n2680:         op: str,\n2681:         target: Target,\n2682:         args: Any = None,\n2683:         kwargs: Any = None,\n2684:         name: Optional[str] = None,\n2685:         type_expr: Optional[Any] = None,\n2686:     ) -> fx.Node:\n2687:         check_pt2_compliant_op(self.output_graph, op, target, args, kwargs)\n2688:         if self.parent is not None:\n2689:             flat_args = pytree.arg_tree_leaves(*args, **kwargs)\n2690:             for arg in flat_args:\n2691:                 if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Node):\n2692:                     continue\n2693:                 assert arg.graph == self.graph, (\n2694:                     \"create_node using arg not from this SubgraphTracer\"\n2695:                 )\n2696: \n2697:         node = super().create_node(op, target, args, kwargs, name, type_expr)\n2698:         node.meta[\"creation_timestamp\"] = self.output_graph.timestamp\n2699:         self._used_names.add(node.name)\n2700:         return node\n2701: \n2702:     # Note: we did not override erase_node since\n2703:     # we call self.graph.erase_node elsewhere\n2704:     def remove_node(self, node: fx.Node) -> None:\n2705:         if len(node.users) > 0:\n2706:             user_graph_nodes: list[torch.fx.Node] = []\n2707:             for user in node.users.keys():\n2708:                 # For the case where user.graph == self.graph, that is a real bug and will raise\n2709:                 # properly.\n2710:                 if user.graph != self.graph:\n2711:                     # This is a nested graph, which needs to be deleted.\n2712:                     # If we do not do this, we will raise on attempting to remove this.\n2713:                     # As we only get here during restoration cleanup, this is sound.\n2714:                     user_graph_nodes.extend(reversed(list(user.graph.nodes)))\n2715:             for other_graph_node in user_graph_nodes:\n2716:                 other_graph_node.graph.erase_node(other_graph_node)\n2717:         self.graph.erase_node(node)\n2718:         self.input_name_to_proxy.pop(node.name, None)\n2719: \n2720:     # when before=True, we will insert this input before the most recent\n2721:     # inserted proxy.  This is a hack to get around an ordering problem,\n2722:     # where we first insert a tensor argument, and then insert bindings\n2723:     # for SymInts that may occur in the tensor argument.\n2724:     # Remove this if https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/99007 gets\n2725:     # fixed.\n2726:     def create_graph_input(\n2727:         self,\n2728:         name: str,\n2729:         type_expr: Any,\n2730:         example_value: Any,\n2731:         before: bool = False,\n2732:         source: Optional[Source] = None,\n2733:     ) -> fx.Proxy:\n2734:         if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n2735:             self._input_versions_at_beginning.append(example_value._version)\n2736:         log.debug(\n2737:             \"create_graph_input %s %s %s at debug_level %s before=%s\",\n2738:             name,\n2739:             source.name() if source is not None else \"(none)\",\n2740:             example_value,\n2741:             self.debug_level,\n2742:             before,\n2743:         )\n2744:         if source is None:\n2745:             assert self.parent is not None, (\n2746:                 f\"you are required to provide a source for inputs {name} example_val {example_value} on the root tracer\"\n2747:             )\n2748: \n2749:         # Note [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\n2750:         # In eager, we are generally OK with adding graph inputs whenever we\n2751:         # want, because we take care of writing the bytecode that knows how\n2752:         # to source all the inputs.\n2753:         #\n2754:         # In export, this is bad, because you want a self-contained export\n2755:         # object which only depends on the inputs you explicitly passed to it.\n2756:         # So we are a bit more strict about what sources can become inputs\n2757:         # in export\n2758:         if self.is_export and self.parent is None:\n2759:             assert source is not None\n2760:             if not is_from_local_source(source, only_allow_input=True):\n2761:                 self.output_graph.source_to_user_stacks.setdefault(source, []).append(\n2762:                     TracingContext.extract_stack()\n2763:                 )\n2764: \n2765:         # _used_names contains the names of all the nodes in the graph,\n2766:         # including intermediates. This ensures that we do not have a name\n2767:         # collision.\n2768:         name = get_unique_name_wrt(name, self._used_names)\n2769:         if self.input_name_to_proxy:\n2770:             prev_name = next(reversed(self.input_name_to_proxy))\n2771:             node = self.input_name_to_proxy[prev_name].node\n2772:             if before:\n2773:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(node)\n2774:             else:\n2775:                 ctx = self.graph.inserting_after(node)\n2776:         else:\n2777:             ctx = self.graph.inserting_before(None)\n2778:         with ctx:\n2779:             proxy = self.create_proxy(\"placeholder\", name, (), {}, type_expr=type_expr)\n2780:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\n2781:             if self.input_name_to_proxy and before:\n2782:                 k, v = self.input_name_to_proxy.popitem()\n2783:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\n2784:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[k] = v\n2785:             else:\n2786:                 self.input_name_to_proxy[name] = proxy\n2787: \n2788:             # For placeholder nodes, `name` is passed as a str to the target,\n2789:             # and then torch.fx decides the node.name. So, record the `target`\n2790:             # name as well in the _used_names to prevent any collision.\n2791:             self._used_names.add(name)\n2792: \n2793:             # NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]\n2794:             # Whenever we call create_graph_input, we try to also lift the basic symbols in example values\n2795:             # as graph input.\n2796:             # This applies to both top-level graph and subgraphs in higher order ops.\n2797:             # It has several cases:\n2798:             #  1. When create_graph_input for a tensor that has symbolic shapes,\n2799:             #     we look for basic symbols in its size and stride, we check if the symbol is bound\n2800:             #     in current graph (i.e. bound_symbols), it it's not bound, we'll create a placeholder\n2801:             #     for it then recursively check its parent, creates ph if not bound.\n2802:             #     Every tracer maintains a mapping (i.e. lifted_freevars)\n2803:             #     that maps from parent proxy to proxy in current tracer for the symbol.\n2804:             #  2. When create_graph_input for a tensor with unbacked symbolic shapes,\n2805:             #     Backed symbols all come from inputs's symbolic shape. But unbacked symbols\n2806:             #     can be created while tracing. So we use track_unbacked_symbols will intercept\n2807:             #     at wrap_fx_proxy, and try to bind the unbacked symbols immediately after they're\n2808:             #     created.\n2809:             #  3. subgraph will also lifted basic symbols in compound exprs of tensor shape.\n2810:             #     For example, if an input to subgraph takes size [s1+s2//8], we'll look for the\n2811:             #     the free symbols in the sizes and lift as inputs similar to 1 in _lift_symbols_in_symint)\n2812:             #  4. When create_graph_input for a SymInt, if the symint is a basic symbol, we'll track it\n2813:             #     in bound_symbols so that we don't lift the same basic symbol twice. When the symint is a\n2814:             #     compound expr, we'll just create the proxy for the compouned expr but not lift its basic symbols.\n2815:             # Also see NOTE: [Export inputs must be explicitly passed in]\n2816:             is_strict_export = self.is_export\n2817:             is_non_strict_export = torch.compiler.is_compiling()\n2818:             if not is_strict_export and not is_non_strict_export:\n2819:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n2820:                     self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value, source)\n2821:                 elif isinstance(example_value, (list, tuple)):\n2822:                     for i, e in enumerate(example_value):\n2823:                         if not isinstance(e, torch.Tensor):\n2824:                             continue\n2825: \n2826:                         e_source = None\n2827:                         if source:\n2828:                             e_source = GetItemSource(\n2829:                                 base=source, index=i, index_is_slice=False\n2830:                             )\n2831: \n2832:                         self._lift_basic_symbols(e, e_source)\n2833: \n2834:             # Bound the symbol to ph if example_value is a SymInt with basic symbol.\n2835:             if isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt) and isinstance(\n2836:                 example_value.node.expr, sympy.Symbol\n2837:             ):\n2838:                 self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr] = proxy\n2839:             return proxy\n2840: \n2841:     # See NOTE: [Nested SubgraphTracer and free_variable handling] for more details\n2842:     def lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(\n2843:         self, proxy: fx.Proxy\n2844:     ) -> Union[LazyProxy, fx.Proxy]:\n2845:         # You're doing something wrong if we are the root SubgraphTracer because\n2846:         # Dynamo adds tensors to graph inputs before creating a proxy for them.\n2847:         assert self.parent is not None, (\n2848:             \"lift_tracked_freevar_to_input should not be called on root SubgraphTracer\"\n2849:         )\n2850: \n2851:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\"example_value\"]\n2852: \n2853:         # To avoid lifting the same symbol twice, we check whether basic symbols has been tracked.\n2854:         # For example, the basic symbols may have already been lifted for current subgraph when\n2855:         # we automatically lift basic symbols in the sizes/strides of a tensor t.\n2856:         # Suppose parent graph calls sz = t.size()[0], it creates\n2857:         # a proxy in parent and the subgraph accesses sz via closure. sz's proxy is not tracked\n2858:         # in current sub-tracer so we may lift the same symbol twice.\n2859:         if (\n2860:             isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt)\n2861:             and example_value.node.expr in self.bound_symbols\n2862:         ):\n2863:             return self.bound_symbols[example_value.node.expr]\n2864: \n2865:         # Proxies are associated with VariableTracker.\n2866:         # It is possible that we've already lifted the Proxy to be an input.\n2867:         # If that is the case, just return the already lifted Proxy.\n2868:         if proxy in self.lifted_freevars:\n2869:             return self.lifted_freevars[proxy]\n2870: \n2871:         # We first lift proxy to parent's graph then lift to current grpah's input\n2872:         # so that when we bind symints of the sizes in current graph, those symints\n2873:         # would already be lifted as inputs to parent graph.\n2874:         if proxy.tracer != self.parent:\n2875:             self.parent.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(proxy)\n2876: \n2877:         example_value = proxy.node.meta[\"example_value\"]\n2878:         new_proxy = self.create_graph_input(\n2879:             proxy.node.name, type(example_value), example_value\n2880: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2379-2879"
  }
}

Note: arg can be a python built-in slice type e.g.\\n2892:             # x[:max_seq] is represented as get_item(t, (slice(None, max_seq, None)))\\n2893:             # we need to also look into the slice variable itself to lift the\\n2894:             # proxies there.\\n2895:             if isinstance(arg, slice):\\n2896:                 return slice(\\n2897:                     *(\\n2898:                         self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(sub_arg)\\n2899:                         for sub_arg in (arg.start, arg.stop, arg.step)\\n2900:                     )\\n2901:                 )\\n2902:             else:\\n2903:                 return arg\\n2904:         elif arg.tracer == self:\\n2905:             return arg\\n2906:         return self.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\\n2907: \\n2908:     # See NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input] for overall design\\n2909:     # You MUST call this API every time when creating a proxy in wrap_fx_proxy for a call\\n2910:     # that produced unbacked symints or tensors with unbacked symint shapes.\\n2911:     # This function is used to track the unbacked symints with its proxies created during\\n2912:     # dynamo tracing so that subgraph knows how to bind a symbol input with parent's proxy.\\n2913:     # LazyProxy are created for tensor shapes that're unbacked so that we don't create proxies\\n2914:     # for symbols that're not going to be used.\\n2915:     def track_unbacked_symbols(\\n2916:         self, example_value: Any, e_proxy: Union[LazyProxy, torch.fx.Proxy]\\n2917:     ) -> None:\\n2918:         # When binding the symbols in an exmaple_value, we bind the symbols\\n2919:         # to the proxy's associated Tracer instead of current tracer.\\n2920:         # This is because:\\n2921:         # 1. We may be calling wrap_tensors during speculate_subgraph because\\n2922:         # the variables are lazily realized. The proxy are top-level phs but\\n2923:         # current tracer is a subtracer.\\n2924:         # 2. For autograd.Function, we trace the backward graph with a new tracer\\n2925:         # whose parent is the forward tracer, but we're using all the proxies created\\n2926:         # in forward tracer to trace the backward.\\n2927:         # For example, forward calls save_for_backward for a input tensor t.\\n2928:         # Backward calls t.tolist(). In this case, all the proxies that backward tracer\\n2929:         # sees are from parent tracer (i.e. the forward tracer). (e.g. t[0].item())\\n2930:         # See test_validate_outputs_unbacked for repro on 2.\\n2931:         tracer = e_proxy.tracer\\n2932:         assert isinstance(tracer, SubgraphTracer)\\n2933: \\n2934:         def need_bind(s: Any) -> bool:\\n2935:             from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_symbolic\\n2936: \\n2937:             return (\\n2938:                 is_symbolic(s)\\n2939:                 and isinstance(s.node.expr, sympy.Symbol)\\n2940:                 and s.node.shape_env.is_unbacked_symint(s.node.expr)\\n2941:                 and s.node.expr not in self.bound_symbols\\n2942:             )\\n2943: \\n2944:         def _proxy_with_example_value(\\n2945:             example_value: Any, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any\\n2946:         ) -> fx.Proxy:\\n2947:             proxy = tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\\n2948:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\\n2949:             return proxy\\n2950: \\n2951:         if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n2952:             for i, s in enumerate(example_value.size()):\\n2953:                 if need_bind(s):\\n2954:                     log.debug(\\n2955:                         \\\"_track_unbacked_symbols %s for %s.size()[%s] at debug_level %s\\\",\\n2956:                         s,\\n2957:                         e_proxy,\\n2958:                         i,\\n2959:                         tracer.debug_level,\\n2960:                     )\\n2961:                     lazy_proxy = LazyProxy(\\n2962:                         tracer,\\n2963:                         _proxy_with_example_value,\\n2964:                         s,\\n2965:                         \\\"call_function\\\",\\n2966:                         torch.ops.aten.sym_size.int,\\n2967:                         (e_proxy, i),\\n2968:                         {},\\n2969:                         type_expr=type(s),\\n2970:                     )\\n2971:                  s,\\n2979:                             e_proxy,\\n2980:                             i,\\n2981:                             tracer.debug_level,\\n2982:                         )\\n2983:                         lazy_proxy = LazyProxy(\\n2984:                             tracer,\\n2985:                             _proxy_with_example_value,\\n2986:                             s,\\n2987:                             \\\"call_function\\\",\\n2988:                             torch.ops.aten.sym_stride.int,\\n2989:                             (e_proxy, i),\\n2990:                             {},\\n2991:                             type_expr=type(s),\\n2992:                         )\\n2993:                         self.track_unbacked_symbols(s, lazy_proxy)\\n2994: \\n2995: self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value._values(), e_proxy)\\n2998:             elif example_value.layout in {torch.sparse_csr, torch.sparse_bsr}:\\n2999:                 self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value.crow_indices(), e_proxy)\\n3000:                 self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value.col_indices(), e_proxy)\\n3001:             elif example_value.layout in {torch.sparse_csc, torch.sparse_bsc}:\\n3002:                 self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value.ccol_indices(), e_proxy)\\n3003: self.track_unbacked_symbols(inner_t, getattr(e_proxy, attr))\\n3009:         elif isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt):\\n3010:             # Only bind unbacked symbols. backed symbols are lifted as inputs.\\n3011:             if need_bind(example_value):\\n3012:                 expr = example_value.node.expr\\n3013:                 tracer.bound_symbols[expr] = e_proxy\\n3014: \\n3015:     # See Note [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]\\n3016:     def _lift_basic_symbols(\\n3017:         self, example_value: Union[torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor], src: Optional[Source]\\n3018:     ) -> None:\\n3019:         # The before arg is for inserting symints in the sizes/strides of a tensor\\n3020:         # before the tensor. This ordering ensures that when we look at the tensor's\\n3021:         # symbols, they're already lifted/tracked. E.g. this assumption is used\\n3022:         # in insert_deferred_runtime_asserts.\\n3023:         def _lift_symbols_in_symint(\\n3024:             s: Union[int, torch.SymInt],\\n3025:             source: Optional[Source],\\n3026:             before: bool = False,\\n3027:         ) -> None:\\n3028:             if not is_symbolic(s):\\n3029:                 return\\n3030: \\n3031:             assert isinstance(s, torch.SymInt)\\n3032:             self_to_be_bound = self.lookup_unbound_symbols(s)\\n3033:             if len(self_to_be_bound) == 0:\\n3034:                 return\\n3035: \\n3036:             # For subgraph\\n3037:             if self.parent is not None:\\n3038:                 # Recursively lift symbols in symint until top-level.\\n3039:                 self.parent._lift_basic_symbols(s, source)\\n3040:                 for s0 in self_to_be_bound:\\n3041:                     parent_proxy = self.parent.bound_symbols[s0]\\n3042:                     example_val = parent_proxy.node.meta[\\\"example_value\\\"]  # type: ignore[index]\\n3058:             # For root_tracer:\\n3059:             else:\\n3060:                 assert len(self_to_be_bound) == 1, (\\n3061:                     f\\\"For root tracer, we only expect to bind basic symbols (compound symbols \\\"\\n3062:                     f\\\"should be cached before) but got unbound symbols {self_to_be_bound} in {s}\\\"\\n3063:                 )\\n3064:                 assert source is not None, (\\n3065:                     f\\\"Source of '{s}' is None when lifting it to input of top-level. a source when create_graph_input for `{s}` at root tracer.\\\"\\n3068:                 )\\n3069:                 s0 = next(iter(self_to_be_bound))\\n3070:                 ph = create_graph_input]\\n3146:     def lookup_unbound_symbols(self, s: torch.SymInt) -> list[sympy.Symbol]:\\n3147:         free_symbols = s.node.expr.free_symbols\\n3148:         if len(free_symbols) == 0:\\n3149:             return []\\n3150: \\n3151:         to_be_bound = []\\n3152:         for s0 in free_symbols:\\n3153:             if s0 not in self.bound_symbols:\\n3154:
