proxy()\\n3160:                 self.bound_symbols[s0] = proxy\\n3161:             assert isinstance(proxy, torch.fx.Proxy) and proxy.tracer is self, (\\n3162:                 f\\\"The proxy of symbol {s0} doesn't belong to current tracer.\\\"\\n3163:             )\\n3164:         # Sort the symbols so that we can have a deterministic lifting order\\n3165:         return sorted(to_be_bound, key=lambda s: s.name)\\n3166: \\n3167:     def has_input_mutation(self) -> MutationInfo:\\n3168:         input_versions_at_beginning = self._input_versions_at_beginning\\n3169:         input_nodes = []\\n3170: \\n3171:         input_versions_at_end = []\\n3172:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n3173:             if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n3174:                 example_value = node.meta[\\\"example_value\\\"]\\n3175:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n3176:                     input_versions_at_end.append(example_value._version)\\n3177:                     input_nodes.append(node)\\n3178:             else:\\n3179:                 break\\n3180: \\n3181:         mutated_inputs = [\\n3182:             i\\n3183:             for i, (v1, v2) in enumerate(\\n3184:                 zip(input_versions_at_beginning, input_versions_at_end)\\n3185:             )\\n3186:             if v1 != v2\\n3187:         ]\\n3188: \\n3189:         if len(mutated_inputs):\\n3190:             mutated_nodes = [input_nodes[i] for i in mutated_inputs]\\n3191:             msg = f\\\"Input mutation detected at {mutated_nodes}\\\"\\n3192:             return MutationInfo(True, msg)\\n3193: \\n3194:         return MutationInfo(False, \\\"\\\")\\n3195: \\n3196:     def has_aliasing(self) -> AliasingInfo:\\n3197:         from torch._higher_order_ops.utils import _collect_fake_inputs\\n3198: \\n3199:         input_storages: dict[StorageWeakRef, torch.fx.Node] = dict()\\n3200: \\n3201:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\\n3202:             if node.op == \\\"placeholder\\\":\\n3203:                 example_value = _collect_fake_inputs([node])[0]\\n3204:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n3205:                     storage = StorageWeakRef(example_value._typed_storage())\\n3206:                     if storage in input_storages:\\n3207:                         # input-input aliasing\\n3208:                         msg = f\\\"Input-to-input aliasing detected at nodes {input_storages[storage]} and {node}\\\"\\n3209:                         return AliasingInfo(True, msg)\\n3210:                     input_storages[storage] = node\\n3211:             else:\\n3212:                 break\\n3213: \\n3214:         output_storages: dict[StorageWeakRef, torch.fx.Node] = dict()\\n3215:         out_nodes = self.graph.find_nodes(op=\\\"output\\\")[0]\\n3216:         for out_node in pytree.tree_leaves(out_nodes.args[0]):\\n3217:             if out_node:\\n3218:                 example_value = _collect_fake_inputs([out_node])[0]\\n3219:                 assert not isinstance(example_value, list)\\n3220:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\\n3221:                     storage = StorageWeakRef(example_value._typed_storage())\\n3222:                     if storage in output_storages:\\n3223:                         # output-output aliasing\\n3224:                         msg = f\\\"Output-to-output aliasing detected at nodes {output_storages[storage]} and {out_node}\\\"\\n3225:                         return AliasingInfo(True, msg)\\n3226:                     output_storages[storage] = out_node\\n3227: \\n3228:         intersected_storages = input_storages.keys() & output_storages.keys()\\n3229:         if len(intersected_storages) > 0:\\n3230:             # input-output aliasing\\n3231:             aliased = [\\n3232:                 (input_storages[s], output_storages[s]) for s in intersected_storages\\n3233:             ]\\n3234:             aliased = \\\", \\\".join([f\\\"{i} and {o}\\\" for i, o in aliased])\\n3235:             msg = f\\\"Input-to-output aliasing detected at nodes {aliased}\\\"\\n3236:             return AliasingInfo(True, msg)\\n3237: \\n3238:         return AliasingInfo(False, \\\"\\\")\\n3239: \\n3240: \\n3241: # NOTE: [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\\n3242: # Ignoring HigherOrderOperators for a moment,\\n3243: # OutputGraph represents the graph being built by Dynamo that may be compiled\\n3244: # and executed. It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.\\n3245: #\\n3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.\\n3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect\\n3248: # the function passed to it (call this the \\\"body function\\\"), capture it into a\\n3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the\\n3250: # GraphModule.\\n3251: #\\n3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having\\n3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.\\n3254: #\\n3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. For example:\\n3260: # - the OutputGraph can receive new GraphArgs (if we discover any new\\n3261: #   untracked Tensors)\\n3262: # - side effects from the body function get accumulated into\\n3263: #   OutputGraph.side_effects\\n3264: # - guards produced by the body function get accumulated into OutputGraph.guards\\n3265: #\\n3266: # The traced function has some special properties that make it easier for us\\n3267: # to transform later down the line:\\n3268: # - we lift all free variables to being inputs.\\n3269: #\\n3270: # If the introspection fails (due to the existence of graph breaks), then\\n3271: # we roll back the current OutputGraph state and graph break on the\\n3272: # HigherOrderOperator.\\n3273: \",\"url\":\"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2880-3273\"}",
  "structuredContent": {
    "content": "2880:         )\n2881:         self.lifted_freevars[proxy] = new_proxy\n2882:         return new_proxy\n2883: \n2884:     def maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(self, arg: Any) -> Any:\n2885:         \"\"\"\n2886:         If arg is a free variable, then lift it to be an input.\n2887:         Returns the new lifted arg (if arg was a freevar), else the\n2888:         original arg.\n2889:         \"\"\"\n2890:         if not isinstance(arg, torch.fx.Proxy):\n2891:             # Note: arg can be a python built-in slice type e.g.\n2892:             # x[:max_seq] is represented as get_item(t, (slice(None, max_seq, None)))\n2893:             # we need to also look into the slice variable itself to lift the\n2894:             # proxies there.\n2895:             if isinstance(arg, slice):\n2896:                 return slice(\n2897:                     *(\n2898:                         self.maybe_lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(sub_arg)\n2899:                         for sub_arg in (arg.start, arg.stop, arg.step)\n2900:                     )\n2901:                 )\n2902:             else:\n2903:                 return arg\n2904:         elif arg.tracer == self:\n2905:             return arg\n2906:         return self.lift_tracked_freevar_to_input(arg)\n2907: \n2908:     # See NOTE: [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input] for overall design\n2909:     # You MUST call this API every time when creating a proxy in wrap_fx_proxy for a call\n2910:     # that produced unbacked symints or tensors with unbacked symint shapes.\n2911:     # This function is used to track the unbacked symints with its proxies created during\n2912:     # dynamo tracing so that subgraph knows how to bind a symbol input with parent's proxy.\n2913:     # LazyProxy are created for tensor shapes that're unbacked so that we don't create proxies\n2914:     # for symbols that're not going to be used.\n2915:     def track_unbacked_symbols(\n2916:         self, example_value: Any, e_proxy: Union[LazyProxy, torch.fx.Proxy]\n2917:     ) -> None:\n2918:         # When binding the symbols in an exmaple_value, we bind the symbols\n2919:         # to the proxy's associated Tracer instead of current tracer.\n2920:         # This is because:\n2921:         # 1. We may be calling wrap_tensors during speculate_subgraph because\n2922:         # the variables are lazily realized. The proxy are top-level phs but\n2923:         # current tracer is a subtracer.\n2924:         # 2. For autograd.Function, we trace the backward graph with a new tracer\n2925:         # whose parent is the forward tracer, but we're using all the proxies created\n2926:         # in forward tracer to trace the backward.\n2927:         # For example, forward calls save_for_backward for a input tensor t.\n2928:         # Backward calls t.tolist(). In this case, all the proxies that backward tracer\n2929:         # sees are from parent tracer (i.e. the forward tracer). (e.g. t[0].item())\n2930:         # See test_validate_outputs_unbacked for repro on 2.\n2931:         tracer = e_proxy.tracer\n2932:         assert isinstance(tracer, SubgraphTracer)\n2933: \n2934:         def need_bind(s: Any) -> bool:\n2935:             from torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import is_symbolic\n2936: \n2937:             return (\n2938:                 is_symbolic(s)\n2939:                 and isinstance(s.node.expr, sympy.Symbol)\n2940:                 and s.node.shape_env.is_unbacked_symint(s.node.expr)\n2941:                 and s.node.expr not in self.bound_symbols\n2942:             )\n2943: \n2944:         def _proxy_with_example_value(\n2945:             example_value: Any, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any\n2946:         ) -> fx.Proxy:\n2947:             proxy = tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\n2948:             set_example_value(proxy.node, example_value)\n2949:             return proxy\n2950: \n2951:         if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n2952:             for i, s in enumerate(example_value.size()):\n2953:                 if need_bind(s):\n2954:                     log.debug(\n2955:                         \"_track_unbacked_symbols %s for %s.size()[%s] at debug_level %s\",\n2956:                         s,\n2957:                         e_proxy,\n2958:                         i,\n2959:                         tracer.debug_level,\n2960:                     )\n2961:                     lazy_proxy = LazyProxy(\n2962:                         tracer,\n2963:                         _proxy_with_example_value,\n2964:                         s,\n2965:                         \"call_function\",\n2966:                         torch.ops.aten.sym_size.int,\n2967:                         (e_proxy, i),\n2968:                         {},\n2969:                         type_expr=type(s),\n2970:                     )\n2971:                     self.track_unbacked_symbols(s, lazy_proxy)\n2972: \n2973:             if example_value.layout is torch.strided:\n2974:                 for i, s in enumerate(example_value.stride()):\n2975:                     if need_bind(s):\n2976:                         log.debug(\n2977:                             \"_track_unbacked_symbols %s for %s.stride()[%s] at debug_level %s\",\n2978:                             s,\n2979:                             e_proxy,\n2980:                             i,\n2981:                             tracer.debug_level,\n2982:                         )\n2983:                         lazy_proxy = LazyProxy(\n2984:                             tracer,\n2985:                             _proxy_with_example_value,\n2986:                             s,\n2987:                             \"call_function\",\n2988:                             torch.ops.aten.sym_stride.int,\n2989:                             (e_proxy, i),\n2990:                             {},\n2991:                             type_expr=type(s),\n2992:                         )\n2993:                         self.track_unbacked_symbols(s, lazy_proxy)\n2994: \n2995:             elif example_value.layout is torch.sparse_coo:\n2996:                 self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value._indices(), e_proxy)\n2997:                 self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value._values(), e_proxy)\n2998:             elif example_value.layout in {torch.sparse_csr, torch.sparse_bsr}:\n2999:                 self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value.crow_indices(), e_proxy)\n3000:                 self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value.col_indices(), e_proxy)\n3001:             elif example_value.layout in {torch.sparse_csc, torch.sparse_bsc}:\n3002:                 self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value.ccol_indices(), e_proxy)\n3003:                 self.track_unbacked_symbols(example_value.row_indices(), e_proxy)\n3004:             if is_traceable_wrapper_subclass(example_value):\n3005:                 attrs, ctx = example_value.__tensor_flatten__()\n3006:                 for attr in attrs:\n3007:                     inner_t = getattr(example_value, attr)\n3008:                     self.track_unbacked_symbols(inner_t, getattr(e_proxy, attr))\n3009:         elif isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt):\n3010:             # Only bind unbacked symbols. backed symbols are lifted as inputs.\n3011:             if need_bind(example_value):\n3012:                 expr = example_value.node.expr\n3013:                 tracer.bound_symbols[expr] = e_proxy\n3014: \n3015:     # See Note [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]\n3016:     def _lift_basic_symbols(\n3017:         self, example_value: Union[torch.SymInt, torch.Tensor], src: Optional[Source]\n3018:     ) -> None:\n3019:         # The before arg is for inserting symints in the sizes/strides of a tensor\n3020:         # before the tensor. This ordering ensures that when we look at the tensor's\n3021:         # symbols, they're already lifted/tracked. E.g. this assumption is used\n3022:         # in insert_deferred_runtime_asserts.\n3023:         def _lift_symbols_in_symint(\n3024:             s: Union[int, torch.SymInt],\n3025:             source: Optional[Source],\n3026:             before: bool = False,\n3027:         ) -> None:\n3028:             if not is_symbolic(s):\n3029:                 return\n3030: \n3031:             assert isinstance(s, torch.SymInt)\n3032:             self_to_be_bound = self.lookup_unbound_symbols(s)\n3033:             if len(self_to_be_bound) == 0:\n3034:                 return\n3035: \n3036:             # For subgraph\n3037:             if self.parent is not None:\n3038:                 # Recursively lift symbols in symint until top-level.\n3039:                 self.parent._lift_basic_symbols(s, source)\n3040:                 for s0 in self_to_be_bound:\n3041:                     parent_proxy = self.parent.bound_symbols[s0]\n3042:                     example_val = parent_proxy.node.meta[\"example_value\"]  # type: ignore[union-attr]\n3043:                     assert isinstance(example_val, torch.SymInt)\n3044:                     ph = self.create_graph_input(\n3045:                         str(s0),\n3046:                         type(example_val),\n3047:                         example_val,\n3048:                         before=before,\n3049:                         source=source,\n3050:                     )\n3051:                     log.debug(\n3052:                         \"_lift_symbols_in_symint %s from %s at debug_level %s\",\n3053:                         s0,\n3054:                         source.name() if source is not None else \"subgraph inputs\",\n3055:                         self.debug_level,\n3056:                     )\n3057:                     self.lifted_freevars[parent_proxy] = ph  # type: ignore[index]\n3058:             # For root_tracer:\n3059:             else:\n3060:                 assert len(self_to_be_bound) == 1, (\n3061:                     f\"For root tracer, we only expect to bind basic symbols (compound symbols \"\n3062:                     f\"should be cached before) but got unbound symbols {self_to_be_bound} in {s}\"\n3063:                 )\n3064:                 assert source is not None, (\n3065:                     f\"Source of '{s}' is None when lifting it to input of top-level. If it's an unbacked symbol, \"\n3066:                     \"this could be because it's not tracked with lazy_bind_unbacked_symbols. \"\n3067:                     f\"Otherwise, should provide a source when create_graph_input for `{s}` at root tracer.\"\n3068:                 )\n3069:                 s0 = next(iter(self_to_be_bound))\n3070:                 ph = self.create_graph_input(\n3071:                     str(s0),\n3072:                     type(s),\n3073:                     s,\n3074:                     before=before,\n3075:                     source=source,\n3076:                 )\n3077:                 log.debug(\n3078:                     \"_lift_symbols_in_symint %s from %s at debug_level %s\",\n3079:                     s,\n3080:                     source.name() if source is not None else \"subgraph inputs\",\n3081:                     self.debug_level,\n3082:                 )\n3083:                 ph.node.meta[\"grapharg\"] = GraphArg(\n3084:                     source,\n3085:                     s,\n3086:                     pass_arg_as_tensor=False,\n3087:                     fake_tensor=None,\n3088:                     is_tensor=False,\n3089:                 )\n3090: \n3091:         if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n3092:             for i, s in enumerate(example_value.size()):\n3093:                 _lift_symbols_in_symint(\n3094:                     s,\n3095:                     (\n3096:                         TensorPropertySource(src, TensorProperty.SIZE, i)\n3097:                         if src is not None\n3098:                         else None\n3099:                     ),\n3100:                     before=True,\n3101:                 )\n3102:             if example_value.layout is torch.strided:\n3103:                 for i, s in enumerate(example_value.stride()):\n3104:                     _lift_symbols_in_symint(\n3105:                         s,\n3106:                         (\n3107:                             TensorPropertySource(src, TensorProperty.STRIDE, i)\n3108:                             if src is not None\n3109:                             else None\n3110:                         ),\n3111:                         before=True,\n3112:                     )\n3113:                 _lift_symbols_in_symint(\n3114:                     example_value.storage_offset(),\n3115:                     (\n3116:                         TensorPropertySource(src, TensorProperty.STORAGE_OFFSET)\n3117:                         if src is not None\n3118:                         else None\n3119:                     ),\n3120:                     before=True,\n3121:                 )\n3122:             elif example_value.layout is torch.sparse_coo:\n3123:                 self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value._indices(), src)\n3124:                 self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value._values(), src)\n3125:             elif example_value.layout in {torch.sparse_csr, torch.sparse_bsr}:\n3126:                 self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value.crow_indices(), src)\n3127:                 self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value.col_indices(), src)\n3128:             elif example_value.layout in {torch.sparse_csc, torch.sparse_bsc}:\n3129:                 self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value.ccol_indices(), src)\n3130:                 self._lift_basic_symbols(example_value.row_indices(), src)\n3131:             if is_traceable_wrapper_subclass(example_value):\n3132:                 attrs, ctx = example_value.__tensor_flatten__()\n3133:                 for attr in attrs:\n3134:                     inner_t = getattr(example_value, attr)\n3135:                     self._lift_basic_symbols(\n3136:                         inner_t, AttrSource(src, attr) if src is not None else None\n3137:                     )\n3138:         elif isinstance(example_value, torch.SymInt):\n3139:             _lift_symbols_in_symint(\n3140:                 example_value,\n3141:                 src,\n3142:             )\n3143: \n3144:     # Lookup the proxy in current tracer for each symbol in expressions of s,\n3145:     # See Note [Auto lift basic free symbols when create_graph_input]\n3146:     def lookup_unbound_symbols(self, s: torch.SymInt) -> list[sympy.Symbol]:\n3147:         free_symbols = s.node.expr.free_symbols\n3148:         if len(free_symbols) == 0:\n3149:             return []\n3150: \n3151:         to_be_bound = []\n3152:         for s0 in free_symbols:\n3153:             if s0 not in self.bound_symbols:\n3154:                 to_be_bound.append(s0)\n3155:                 continue\n3156: \n3157:             proxy = self.bound_symbols[s0]\n3158:             if isinstance(proxy, LazyProxy):\n3159:                 proxy = proxy()\n3160:                 self.bound_symbols[s0] = proxy\n3161:             assert isinstance(proxy, torch.fx.Proxy) and proxy.tracer is self, (\n3162:                 f\"The proxy of symbol {s0} doesn't belong to current tracer.\"\n3163:             )\n3164:         # Sort the symbols so that we can have a deterministic lifting order\n3165:         return sorted(to_be_bound, key=lambda s: s.name)\n3166: \n3167:     def has_input_mutation(self) -> MutationInfo:\n3168:         input_versions_at_beginning = self._input_versions_at_beginning\n3169:         input_nodes = []\n3170: \n3171:         input_versions_at_end = []\n3172:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n3173:             if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n3174:                 example_value = node.meta[\"example_value\"]\n3175:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n3176:                     input_versions_at_end.append(example_value._version)\n3177:                     input_nodes.append(node)\n3178:             else:\n3179:                 break\n3180: \n3181:         mutated_inputs = [\n3182:             i\n3183:             for i, (v1, v2) in enumerate(\n3184:                 zip(input_versions_at_beginning, input_versions_at_end)\n3185:             )\n3186:             if v1 != v2\n3187:         ]\n3188: \n3189:         if len(mutated_inputs):\n3190:             mutated_nodes = [input_nodes[i] for i in mutated_inputs]\n3191:             msg = f\"Input mutation detected at {mutated_nodes}\"\n3192:             return MutationInfo(True, msg)\n3193: \n3194:         return MutationInfo(False, \"\")\n3195: \n3196:     def has_aliasing(self) -> AliasingInfo:\n3197:         from torch._higher_order_ops.utils import _collect_fake_inputs\n3198: \n3199:         input_storages: dict[StorageWeakRef, torch.fx.Node] = dict()\n3200: \n3201:         for node in self.graph.nodes:\n3202:             if node.op == \"placeholder\":\n3203:                 example_value = _collect_fake_inputs([node])[0]\n3204:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n3205:                     storage = StorageWeakRef(example_value._typed_storage())\n3206:                     if storage in input_storages:\n3207:                         # input-input aliasing\n3208:                         msg = f\"Input-to-input aliasing detected at nodes {input_storages[storage]} and {node}\"\n3209:                         return AliasingInfo(True, msg)\n3210:                     input_storages[storage] = node\n3211:             else:\n3212:                 break\n3213: \n3214:         output_storages: dict[StorageWeakRef, torch.fx.Node] = dict()\n3215:         out_nodes = self.graph.find_nodes(op=\"output\")[0]\n3216:         for out_node in pytree.tree_leaves(out_nodes.args[0]):\n3217:             if out_node:\n3218:                 example_value = _collect_fake_inputs([out_node])[0]\n3219:                 assert not isinstance(example_value, list)\n3220:                 if isinstance(example_value, torch.Tensor):\n3221:                     storage = StorageWeakRef(example_value._typed_storage())\n3222:                     if storage in output_storages:\n3223:                         # output-output aliasing\n3224:                         msg = f\"Output-to-output aliasing detected at nodes {output_storages[storage]} and {out_node}\"\n3225:                         return AliasingInfo(True, msg)\n3226:                     output_storages[storage] = out_node\n3227: \n3228:         intersected_storages = input_storages.keys() & output_storages.keys()\n3229:         if len(intersected_storages) > 0:\n3230:             # input-output aliasing\n3231:             aliased = [\n3232:                 (input_storages[s], output_storages[s]) for s in intersected_storages\n3233:             ]\n3234:             aliased = \", \".join([f\"{i} and {o}\" for i, o in aliased])\n3235:             msg = f\"Input-to-output aliasing detected at nodes {aliased}\"\n3236:             return AliasingInfo(True, msg)\n3237: \n3238:         return AliasingInfo(False, \"\")\n3239: \n3240: \n3241: # NOTE: [HigherOrderOperator tracing design]\n3242: # Ignoring HigherOrderOperators for a moment,\n3243: # OutputGraph represents the graph being built by Dynamo that may be compiled\n3244: # and executed. It holds a root SubgraphTracer where the FX graph is built.\n3245: #\n3246: # HigherOrderOperators are operators that take functions as their arguments.\n3247: # When Dynamo encounters a HigherOrderOperator, then it attempts to introspect\n3248: # the function passed to it (call this the \"body function\"), capture it into a\n3249: # GraphModule, and rewrite the call to the HigherOrderOperator to use the\n3250: # GraphModule.\n3251: #\n3252: # The way we handle the capture of body functions is through having\n3253: # (possibly nested) SubgraphTracers, one per body function.\n3254: #\n3255: # Mechanically, we do the introspection by:\n3256: # - Creating a new SubgraphTracer via OutputGraph.subtracer\n3257: # - Executing the body function.\n3258: # This constructs the graph of the body function in the new SubgraphTracer\n3259: # while modifying the state of the OutputGraph. For example:\n3260: # - the OutputGraph can receive new GraphArgs (if we discover any new\n3261: #   untracked Tensors)\n3262: # - side effects from the body function get accumulated into\n3263: #   OutputGraph.side_effects\n3264: # - guards produced by the body function get accumulated into OutputGraph.guards\n3265: #\n3266: # The traced function has some special properties that make it easier for us\n3267: # to transform later down the line:\n3268: # - we lift all free variables to being inputs.\n3269: #\n3270: # If the introspection fails (due to the existence of graph breaks), then\n3271: # we roll back the current OutputGraph state and graph break on the\n3272: # HigherOrderOperator.\n3273: ",
    "url": "https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/pytorch--d18007a1@d18007a1d0b1ea9a6b2bcd74e8b128eab4b434a0/-/blob/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py%3FL2880-3273"
  }
}
183. ToolSearch @ -
Input
{
  "query": "select:Write"
}
Output
{
  "matches": [
    "Write"
  ],
  "query": "select:Write",
  "total_deferred_tools": 33

184. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "\"\"\"\nCore graph building functionality for PyTorch's Dynamo system. This module contains\nthe essential components for constructing and managing FX graphs during compilation:\n\n- OutputGraph: Manages the overall graph construction and compilation process. It owns\n  a SubgraphTracer and handles graph compilation, execution, and state management.\n  OutputGraph also manages features like graph deduplication, symbolic shape handling,\n  and tracking of side effects.\n\n- SubgraphTracer: Handles the actual FX graph construction by tracing Python code.\n  It supports advanced features like higher-order operators through nested tracers,\n  lifting of free variables, and handling of symbolic shapes.\n\nThe module supports key Dynamo features including:\n- Higher-order operators through nested SubgraphTracers\n- Graph deduplication for optimization\n- Symbolic shape handling and propagation\n- Side effect tracking and management\n- Guard insertion and management\n\"\"\"\n\nimport collections\nimport contextlib\nimport copy\nimport functools\nimport inspect\nimport itertools\nimport logging\nimport operator\nimport re\nimport sys\nimport traceback\nimport warnings\nimport weakref\nfrom collections.abc import Generator, Sequence\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field as dc_field\nfrom types import CodeType\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, cast, Optional, TYPE_CHECKING, Union\nfrom typing_extensions import ParamSpec, TypeVar\n\nimport sympy\n\nimport torch._guards\nimport torch._logging\nimport torch.distributed as dist\nimport torch.nn\nimport torch.utils._pytree as pytree\nfrom torch import fx, Tensor\nfrom torch._C._dynamo import guards\nfrom torch._dynamo.exc import ShortenTraceback, TensorifyScalarRestartAnalysis\nfrom torch._guards import (\n    CompileContext,\n    CompileId,\n    GlobalContextCheckpointState,\n    Source,\n    tracing,\n    TracingContext,\n)\nfrom torch._subclasses.fake_tensor import FakeTensor\nfrom torch._utils_internal import signpost_event\nfrom torch.export.dynamic_shapes import _ConstraintTarget\nfrom torch.fx._lazy_graph_module import _make_graph_module  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\nfrom torch.fx.experimental._backward_state import BackwardState\nfrom torch.fx.experimental.symbolic_shapes import (\n    free_symbols,\n    guard_scalar,\n    is_symbolic,\n    ShapeEnv,\n    Specialization,\n)\nfrom torch.fx.node import Target\nfrom torch.fx.passes.runtime_assert import insert_deferred_runtime_asserts\nfrom torch.multiprocessing.reductions import StorageWeakRef\nfrom torch.utils._ordered_set import OrderedSet\nfrom torch.utils._python_dispatch import is_traceable_wrapper_subclass\n\nfrom . import config, exc, logging as torchdynamo_logging, variables\nfrom .backends.registry import CompiledFn, CompilerFn\nfrom .bytecode_transformation import (\n    create_call_function,\n    create_instruction,\n    create_load_const,\n    Instruction,\n    unique_id,\n)\nfrom .code_context import code_context\nfrom .codegen import PyCodegen\nfrom .current_scope_id import enter_new_scope\nfrom .device_interface import get_interface_for_device\nfrom .exc import (\n    BackendCompilerFailed,\n    exceptions_allowed_to_be_fallback,\n    SkipFrame,\n    unimplemented_v2,\n    unimplemented_v2_with_warning,\n)\nfrom .graph_deduplication import apply_graph_deduplication\nfrom .graph_region_tracker import GraphRegionTracker\nfrom .guards import GuardBuilder, install_guard\nfrom .mutation_guard import is_dynamic_nn_module\nfrom .side_effects import AttributeMutationExisting, SideEffects, ValueMutationExisting\nfrom .source import (\n    _get_source_debug_name,\n    AttrSource,\n    BackwardStateSource,\n    ConstantSource,\n    GetItemSource,\n    GlobalStateSource,\n    is_constant_source,\n    is_from_local_source,\n    LocalSource,\n    NumpyTensorSource,\n    ParamBufferSource,\n    ShapeEnvSource,\n    SyntheticLocalSource,\n    TensorProperty,\n    TensorPropertySource,\n)\nfrom .utils import (\n    _extract_tensor_dict,\n    checkpoint_params,\n    CleanupHook,\n    clone_inputs,\n    count_calls,\n    counters,\n    dynamo_timed,\n    get_instruction_source_311,\n    get_locals_to_steal,\n    get_static_address_type,\n    get_unique_name_wrt,\n    graph_break_reasons,\n    increment_op_count,\n    istype,\n    lazy_format_graph_code,\n    LazyString,\n    nn_module_proxy,\n    same,\n    set_example_value,\n)\nfrom .variables.base import VariableTracker\nfrom .variables.builder import (\n    BackwardStateGraphArg,\n    GraphArg,\n    TrackedFake,\n    wrap_fx_proxy,\n)\nfrom .variables.ctx_manager import ContextWrappingVariable\nfrom .variables.lists import BaseListVariable\nfrom .variables.misc import CellVariable, NullVariable\nfrom .variables.nn_module import NNModuleVariable\nfrom .variables.tensor import (\n    NumpyNdarrayVariable,\n    SymNodeVariable,\n    TensorVariable,\n    UnspecializedPythonVariable,\n)\nfrom .variables.torch_function import TensorWithTFOverrideVariable\nfrom .variables.user_defined import UserDefinedDictVariable\n\n\nif TYPE_CHECKING:\n    from torch._dynamo.package import CompilePackage\n    from torch._dynamo.symbolic_convert import InstructionTranslatorBase\n\nlog = logging.getLogger(__name__)\ngraph_tabular_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph\")\ngraph_code_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph_code\")\ngraph_sizes_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"graph_sizes\")\ntrace_call_log = torch._logging.getArtifactLogger(__name__, \"trace_call\")\n\nRootGuardManager = guards.RootGuardManager\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass VariableTrackerCacheKey:\n    vt_id: int\n    # Two different source can point to the same object. However, Dynamo handles\n    # globals and local source differently when it comes to guards and possibly\n    # some other parts as well. So, cache also relies on the source.\n    source: Source\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass AliasingInfo:\n    has_aliasing: bool\n    msg: str\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass MutationInfo:\n    has_mutation: bool\n    msg: str\n\n\nclass VariableTrackerCache:\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self.cache: dict[VariableTrackerCacheKey, VariableTracker] = {}\n\n    def lookup(self, value: Any, source: Source) -> Optional[VariableTracker]:\n        key = VariableTrackerCacheKey(id(value), source)\n        if key not in self.cache:\n            return None\n        return self.cache[key]\n\n    def add(self, value: Any, source: Source, vt: VariableTracker) -> None:\n        key = VariableTrackerCacheKey(id(value), source)\n        self.cache[key] = vt\n\n    def clone(self) -> \"VariableTrackerCache\":\n        # Needed for copy and restore graph state\n        new_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\n        new_cache.cache.update(self.cache)\n        return new_cache\n\n    def clear(self) -> None:\n        self.cache.clear()\n\n\n@functools.cache\ndef _step_logger() -> Any:\n    return torchdynamo_logging.get_step_logger(log)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass GraphCompileReason:\n    \"\"\"Stores why a given output graph was compiled; i.e. what caused the graph break.\"\"\"\n\n    reason: str\n    user_stack: list[traceback.FrameSummary]\n\n    # Indicates if this was a graph break reason due to graph break.\n    graph_break: bool = True\n\n    def __post_init__(self) -> None:\n        if self.graph_break:\n            graph_break_reasons.append(self)\n\n\ndef _get_gen_rand_values_fn(random_calls: Any) -> Callable[[], list[Any]]:\n    def _gen_rand_values() -> list[Any]:\n        return [fn(*args, **kwargs) for fn, args, kwargs in random_calls]\n\n    return _gen_rand_values\n\n\nclass FakeRootModule(torch.nn.Module):\n    \"\"\"Trick the constructor of fx.GraphModule\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]):\n        super().__init__()\n        for k, v in nn_modules.items():\n            setattr(self, k, v)\n\n    def __repr__(self) -> str:\n        return \"FakeRootModule(...)\"\n\n    def add_nn_modules(self, nn_modules: dict[str, torch.nn.Module]) -> None:\n        for k, v in nn_modules.items():\n            setattr(self, k, v)\n\n\nclass WrapperBackend:\n    def __init__(self, backend: CompilerFn) -> None:\n        self.backend: CompilerFn = backend\n\n    def __call__(\n        self, gm: torch.fx.GraphModule, example_inputs: list[torch.Tensor]\n    ) -> CompiledFn:\n        self.restore = checkpoint_params(gm)\n        self.gm = gm\n        copy_gm = copy.deepcopy(self.gm)\n        self.candidate = self.backend(copy_gm, example_inputs)\n\n        if self.candidate is None or self.candidate is self.gm.forward:\n            return self.gm.forward\n\n        if not config.verify_correctness:\n            return self.candidate\n\n        # if verify_correctness=True\n        try:\n            correct = self.gm.forward(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n            result = self.candidate(*clone_inputs(example_inputs))\n\n            # TODO: replace `same` function with the one in testing\n            if same(correct, result):\n                return self.candidate\n\n            raise RuntimeError(f\"incorrect results of backend {self}\")\n\n        except Exception:\n            log.exception(\"error in verify_correctness\")\n            raise\n        finally:\n            self.restore()\n\n\nScope = dict[str, object]\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass OutputGraphGuardsState:\n    \"\"\"\n    A base class containing fields that are considered \"persistent\" when we\n    want to save all the important state for reconstrucing guards in a different\n    process. Normally we don't need to add states here, but we may have to when\n    the information is needed to serialize the guards, so the fields here are\n    supposed to be serializable as a requirement.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    local_scope: Scope\n    global_scope: Scope\n    # This records the initial torch function mode stack for guarding\n    torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode]\n    guard_on_key_order: set[Source]\n    # Map from graph input's `Source` to sizes / strides metadata\n    input_source_to_sizes_strides: dict[Source, dict[str, Any]]\n    dual_level: int\n    functorch_layers: list[torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.FuncTorchInterpreter]\n    current_device: Optional[torch.device]\n    global_state_guard: torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard\n    _guards: torch._guards.GuardsSet\n    _aotautograd_guards: list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]\n\n    export: bool = False\n    export_constraints: bool = False\n    name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: Optional[str] = None\n\n    @property\n    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n        raise AssertionError(f\"shape_env shouldn't be accessed from {type(self)}\")\n\n    @property\n    def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n        return self._guards\n\n    @property\n    def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n        return self._aotautograd_guards\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass StackLocalsMetadata:\n    \"\"\"\n    Stores metadata for a frame's stack and locals for the purposes of building resume functions\n    \"\"\"\n\n    stack_null_idxes: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_null_keys: list[str] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_args: list[tuple[int, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    stack_ctx_idxes_orig: list[int] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n    locals_ctx_args: list[tuple[str, tuple[Any, ...]]] = dc_field(default_factory=list)\n\n\ndef get_builtins_dict(global_scope: Scope) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    # f_globals[\"__builtins__\"] can be a dict or a module. This is an\n    # implementation detail -\n    # https://docs.python.org/3/library/builtins.html.\n\n    # This makes guarding on any builtin messy because the guard check_fn\n    # has to check if the __builtins__ is a module or dict, and then access\n    # by either using getattr or getitem respectively.\n\n    # To solve this problem, we insert a new entry in f_globals which points\n    # to the builtins __dict__ and then we guard any builtin on this dict.\n    # To avoid any collision with the pre-existing keys, we use the\n    # install_global to give us a unique dict key.\n\n    f_builtins = global_scope[\"__builtins__\"]\n    if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n        f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n    return f_builtins\n\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py",
    "numLines": 374,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 374
  },
  "type": "text"
}
185. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "cat > /tmp/chunk1.py << 'PYEOF'\ncontent = r'''\n\nclass OutputGraph(OutputGraphGuardsState):\n    \"\"\"\n    Wrapper class to hold outputs of InstructionTranslator.  Mainly the\n    generated fx.Graph.\n\n    OutputGraph is 1:1 with a frame being processed. Each frame is associated\n    with some root InstructionTranslator. When user code calls a function,\n    we construct a InliningInstructionTranslator that continues to write into\n    the root InstructionTranslator's OutputGraph.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    side_effects: SideEffects\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        code_options: dict[str, Any],\n        compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn],\n        root_tx: \"InstructionTranslatorBase\",\n        export: bool,\n        export_constraints: Sequence[_ConstraintTarget],\n        frame_state: Any,\n        local_scope: Scope,\n        global_scope: Scope,\n        f_code: CodeType,\n        torch_function_mode_stack: list[torch.overrides.TorchFunctionMode],\n        package: Optional[\"CompilePackage\"],\n    ) -> None:\n        super().__init__(\n            local_scope,\n            global_scope,\n            torch_function_mode_stack,\n            guard_on_key_order=set(),\n            input_source_to_sizes_strides={},\n            dual_level=torch.autograd.forward_ad._current_level,\n            functorch_layers=torch._functorch.pyfunctorch.retrieve_all_functorch_interpreters(),\n            current_device=torch.utils._device.CURRENT_DEVICE,\n            # initial_global_state is only None during NopTest.\n            global_state_guard=torch._dynamo.convert_frame.initial_global_state\n            or torch._C._dynamo.guards.GlobalStateGuard(),\n            # These are set by @property instead, just initialize them as blank\n            _guards=torch._guards.GuardsSet(),\n            _aotautograd_guards=[],\n        )\n        self.tracers = [SubgraphTracer(self, is_export=export)]\n        # Map from graph input's `Source` to its `VariableTracker` to\n        # de-duplicate graph inputs by source and reuse the tracker\n        self.input_source_to_var: dict[Source, VariableTracker] = {}\n        self.export = export\n        self.export_constraints = export_constraints  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.frame_state = frame_state\n        self.cleanup_hooks: list[Callable[[], Any]] = []\n        # compile_id is an id number for the current torch.compile\n        self.compile_id: int = next(_compile_id_counter)\n        # Set of globals installed via install_global* APIs\n        self.installed_globals: set[str] = set()\n\n        # TODO: maybe should just pass the entire f_code in here?  Not\n        # sure...\n        self.co_fields = {\n            \"co_name\": f_code.co_name,\n            \"co_filename\": f_code.co_filename,\n            \"co_firstlineno\": f_code.co_firstlineno,\n        }\n\n        self.region_tracker = GraphRegionTracker()\n\n        # tracked_fakes says where any tensor that was wrapped to fake came\n        # from.  It is similar to GraphArg, in that all GraphArgs will get\n        # will get added to TrackedFakes, but TrackedFakes also contains\n        # GraphArgs that got pruned, and things like Tensor attributes which\n        # aren't explicit graph inputs.  Used by shape guard\n        self.tracked_fakes: list[TrackedFake] = []\n\n        shape_env = ShapeEnv(\n            # Reference Cycle!\n            # Share a reference to the list of TrackedFake.\n            #\n            # ShapeEnv needs this in order to be able to reproduce the call\n            # to produce_guards at an arbitrary time point. That is because\n            # TrackedFake instances may have its metadata changed throughout\n            # the program execution.\n            tracked_fakes=self.tracked_fakes,\n            allow_scalar_outputs=config.capture_scalar_outputs,\n            allow_dynamic_output_shape_ops=config.capture_dynamic_output_shape_ops,\n            prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards=config.prefer_deferred_runtime_asserts_over_guards,\n            allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts=config.allow_complex_guards_as_runtime_asserts,\n            co_fields=self.co_fields,\n        )\n\n        # In export mode, we force the shape_env to strictly disallow any constraining\n        # of the user marked dynamic dims\n        import torch._functorch.config as _config\n\n        with _config.patch(fake_tensor_allow_unsafe_data_ptr_access=False):\n            fake_mode = torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode(\n                shape_env=shape_env,\n                # TODO (tmanlaibaatar) Remove this once we always lift params and buffers\n                allow_non_fake_inputs=True if self.export else False,\n                export=self.export,\n            )\n        self.tracing_context: TracingContext = TracingContext(fake_mode)\n        self.tracing_context.traced_code.append(f_code)\n        self.dynamo_compile_id: Optional[CompileId] = (\n            CompileContext.current_compile_id()\n        )\n        self.init_ambient_guards()\n\n        # Map each tensor id to a list of sources. This is necessary because\n        # tensor ids cannot be recovered from tracked fakes (in general).\n        # We use this map to interpret (i.e., check for violations of) constraints,\n        # specifically equality constraints, which have shared tensor ids in them.\n        # This map should also be generally useful, e.g., for (de)serialization.\n        self.tracked_fakes_id_to_source: dict[int, list[Source]] = (\n            collections.defaultdict(list)\n        )\n        # Stores the full fqn of a param or buffer to the relevant source.\n        self.param_name_to_source: Optional[dict[str, Source]] = {}\n        self.side_effects = SideEffects(self)\n        # Cached variable trackers. This makes symbolic analysis of LOAD_GLOBAL\n        # and LOAD_ATTR for same python objects free.\n        self.variable_tracker_cache = VariableTrackerCache()\n        self.unique_var_id = itertools.count()\n        self.code_options: dict[str, Any] = dict(code_options)\n        self.output_instructions: list[Instruction] = []\n        # used to track nodes that are added between calls of copy_graphstate\n        # and restore_graphstate\n        self.timestamp = 0\n\n        # A list of register_finalizer_fns to apply to the output graph module\n        self.register_finalizer_fns: list[Callable[[fx.GraphModule], None]] = []\n\n        # Not checkpointed\n        self.compiler_fn: Optional[CompilerFn] = compiler_fn\n        self.root_tx = root_tx\n\n        self.package = package\n        # Given a source, what are the user stacks of all locations that\n        # accessed it?\n        #\n        # For efficiency, we only populate this:\n        #   - During export, and\n        #   - If the source could potentially lead to a spurious export input\n        #\n        # Feel free to populate this more frequently if other use-cases arise,\n        # but be aware that we have to generate full stacks for each\n        # recording!\n        self.source_to_user_stacks: dict[Source, list[traceback.StackSummary]] = {}\n\n        self._current_tx: list[InstructionTranslatorBase] = []\n        self.cleanups: list[CleanupHook] = []\n        self.should_exit = False\n        self.unspec_variable_map: dict[str, UnspecializedPythonVariable] = {}\n\n        # This returns false if TF Overall (both mode and subclass) is disabled OR that TF Mode stack is empty\n        self.torch_function_mode_enabled = torch._C._is_torch_function_mode_enabled()\n\n        # Tracks if the output graph has a user defined allowed function in the\n        # graph. This is used later to determine if we should fallback to eager\n        # for certain exceptions. THe idea is that if the user has applied\n        # allow_in_graph, they would like to see the error instead of falling\n        # back for backend errors.\n        self.has_user_defined_allowed_in_graph = False\n\n        # Tracks a list of called ops that were not tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n        # This information is useful for logging.\n        self.non_compliant_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n\n        # Tracks a list of called custom ops that were tagged with \"pt2_compliant_tag\".\n        # This information is useful for logging.\n        self.compliant_custom_ops: set[torch._ops.OpOverload] = set({})\n\n        # We save the global torch state here to be restored in case of graph\n        # breaks. The relevant issue is seen here\n        # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/100570#issuecomment-1543427086\n        # where inlining of a function changes the global state (because of the\n        # presence of torch.no_grad) and there is a graph break.\n        self.save_global_state()\n\n        # Tracks the original FQNs of the constant tensors from the original graph,\n        # i.e. buffers and parameters.\n        self.dynamo_flat_name_to_original_fqn: dict[str, str] = {}\n\n        # All calls to random() are replaced with a single call to __gen_rand_values\n        # functions that returns a tuple of random values for each original call.\n        # random_calls tracks calls to random() and random_values_var stores the name of\n        # the variable that stores __gen_rand_values results.\n        self.random_calls: list[\n            tuple[Callable[..., object], tuple[object, ...], dict[str, object]]\n        ] = []\n        self.random_values_var: Any = None\n\n        # Bytecode to insert right before we call the graph\n        self.pregraph_bytecode: list[Instruction] = []\n\n        # Use to pass values to backward hooks when using compiled autograd\n        self.backward_state: dict[str, VariableTracker] = {}\n        self.backward_state_proxy: Optional[torch.fx.Proxy] = None\n        self.backward_state_var: Optional[str] = None\n\n        self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals: str = (\n            self.install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals()\n        )\n\n        self.compiler_trace_stack = contextlib.ExitStack()\n\n        # These are the ambient, currently-global saved_tensor_hooks stashed in autograd,\n        # that are set for the entire duration of the compiled region.\n        # This is an invariant today because we graph break on the saved_tensor_hook\n        # context manager inside a compiled region\n        self.saved_tensors_hooks_subgraph_names: Optional[list[str]] = (\n            self.maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs()\n        )\n\n    def mark_bytecode_tracing_start(self) -> None:\n        self.compiler_trace_stack.enter_context(\n            dynamo_timed(\n                \"bytecode_tracing\",\n                log_pt2_compile_event=True,\n            )\n        )\n\n    def mark_bytecode_tracing_stop(self) -> None:\n        self.compiler_trace_stack.close()\n\n    def install_builtins_dict_in_fglobals(self) -> str:\n        f_builtins = get_builtins_dict(self.global_scope)\n        return self.install_global(\"__builtins_dict__\", f_builtins)\n\n    def add_backward_state_hook(\n        self, hook: VariableTracker, prefix: str = \"hook\"\n    ) -> tuple[str, torch.fx.Proxy]:\n        name = f\"{prefix}{len(self.backward_state)}\"\n        assert name not in self.backward_state\n        self.backward_state[name] = hook\n        return name, self.get_backward_state_proxy()\n\n    def get_backward_state_proxy(self) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n        if self.backward_state_proxy is None:\n            if self.export:\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"backward_state does not support export\",\n                    context=\"\",\n                    explanation=\"Compiled autograd doesn't work with `torch.export`.\",\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n            example_value = BackwardState()\n            self.backward_state_proxy = self.root_tracer.create_graph_input(\n                \"dynamo_backward_state\",\n                type(example_value),\n                example_value,\n                source=BackwardStateSource(),\n            )\n            self.backward_state_proxy.node.meta[\"grapharg\"] = BackwardStateGraphArg()\n            self.backward_state_var = self.new_var()\n        return self.backward_state_proxy\n\n    # This gets its own helper function so guards DEBUG logs are more informative\n    def init_ambient_guards(self) -> None:\n        # Register a SHAPE_ENV guard to make sure we setup shape guards\n        # that show up in ShapeEnv\n        self.guards.add(ShapeEnvSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.SHAPE_ENV))\n\n        self.guards.add(\n            GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DETERMINISTIC_ALGORITHMS)\n        )\n\n        self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.GRAD_MODE))\n\n        self.guards.add(GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.DEFAULT_DEVICE))\n\n        self.guards.add(\n            GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.TORCH_FUNCTION_STATE)\n        )\n\n        ci = torch._C._functorch.peek_interpreter_stack()\n        if ci is not None:\n            self.guards.add(\n                GlobalStateSource().make_guard(GuardBuilder.FUNCTORCH_STACK_MATCH)\n            )\n        if not torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n            self.guards.add(\n                GlobalStateSource().make_guard(\n                    GuardBuilder.AUTOGRAD_SAVED_TENSORS_HOOKS\n                )\n            )\n\n    def maybe_install_saved_tensors_hooks_subgraphs(self) -> Optional[list[str]]:\n        if torch._dynamo.compiled_autograd.in_compiled_autograd_region:\n            return None\n\n        get_hooks = torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.top_saved_tensors_hooks\n        are_inline_hooks = (\n            torch._functorch._aot_autograd.utils.saved_tensors_hooks_are_inlineable\n        )\n        hooks = get_hooks()\n        if not are_inline_hooks(hooks):\n            return None\n\n        # If GraphModule provided by user contains fx.wrap,\n        # We can only rely on user provided cache hash in this case.\n        # If user did not provide cache hash - then we always bypass cache.\n\n        pack_gm, unpack_gm = hooks\n        pack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n            \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack\",\n            torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, pack_gm.graph),\n        )\n        unpack_subgraph_name = self.install_subgraph(\n            \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack\",\n            torch.fx.GraphModule(self.nn_modules, unpack_gm.graph),\n        )\n        assert pack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_pack_0\"\n        assert unpack_subgraph_name == \"saved_tensors_hooks_unpack_0\"\n        return [pack_subgraph_name, unpack_subgraph_name]\n\n    def dump_guards_state(self) -> OutputGraphGuardsState:\n        # Dump a serializable version of self without extras\n        return OutputGraphGuardsState(\n            local_scope=self.local_scope,\n            global_scope=self.global_scope,\n            torch_function_mode_stack=self.torch_function_mode_stack,\n            guard_on_key_order=self.guard_on_key_order,\n            input_source_to_sizes_strides=self.input_source_to_sizes_strides,\n            dual_level=self.dual_level,\n            functorch_layers=self.functorch_layers,\n            current_device=self.current_device,\n            global_state_guard=self.global_state_guard,\n            name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals=self.name_of_builtins_dict_key_in_fglobals,\n            export=self.export,\n            export_constraints=self.export_constraints,\n            _guards=self.guards,\n            _aotautograd_guards=self.aotautograd_guards,\n        )\n\n    def synthetic_graph_input(\n        self, fn: Callable[..., Any], args: tuple[Any, ...]\n    ) -> VariableTracker:\n        \"\"\"\n        call fn(*args) before the graph runs and turn the result into a fake input.\n        \"\"\"\n        example_value = fn(*args)\n        varname = self.new_var()\n        cg = PyCodegen(self.root_tx)\n        cg.add_push_null(\n            lambda: cg.load_import_from(\n                fn.__module__,\n                fn.__name__,\n            )\n        )\n        cg.foreach(map(variables.ConstantVariable.create, args))\n        cg.call_function(len(args), False)\n        cg.store(varname)\n        self.pregraph_bytecode.extend(cg.get_instructions())\n        source = SyntheticLocalSource(varname)\n        result = VariableTracker.build(self.root_tx, example_value, source)\n        # Realize the VT because we will delete the guards on it in the next line.\n        result = result.realize()\n        TracingContext.get().guards_context.dynamo_guards.remove_guards_with_source(\n            source\n        )\n        return result\n\n    def add_cleanup_hook(self, fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> None:\n        self.cleanup_hooks.append(fn)\n\n    def call_cleanup_hooks(self) -> None:\n        for hook in reversed(self.cleanup_hooks):\n            hook()\n        self.cleanup_hooks.clear()\n\n    @property\n    def root_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n        return self.tracers[0]\n\n    @property\n    def current_tracer(self) -> \"SubgraphTracer\":\n        return self.tracers[-1]\n\n    def is_root_tracer(self) -> bool:\n        # Helper to tell if we are inside the higher order operator tracing.\n        return len(self.tracers) == 1\n\n    @property\n    def graph(self) -> torch.fx.Graph:\n        return self.current_tracer.graph\n\n    # TODO(rzou): can delete after we refactor speculate_subgraph to use nested GraphTracer.\n    @graph.setter\n    def graph(self, value: torch.fx.Graph) -> None:\n        self.current_tracer.graph = value\n\n    @property\n    def input_name_to_proxy(self) -> dict[str, fx.Proxy]:\n        return self.current_tracer.input_name_to_proxy\n\n    @property\n    def real_value_cache(self) -> dict[fx.Node, torch.Tensor]:\n        return self.current_tracer.real_value_cache\n\n    @property\n    def bound_symbols(self) -> dict[sympy.Symbol, Union[torch.fx.Proxy, \"LazyProxy\"]]:\n        return self.current_tracer.bound_symbols\n\n    # If you are here, and you're looking for create_graph_input,\n    # to avoid ambiguity, please call one of the following:\n    # - self.current_tracer.create_graph_input\n    # - self.root_tracer.create_graph_input\n    # See NOTE [HigherOrderOperator tracing design] for more context.\n\n    def create_proxy(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Proxy:\n        return self.current_tracer.create_proxy(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    def create_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> torch.fx.Node:\n        return self.current_tracer.create_node(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    def remove_node(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\n        return self.current_tracer.remove_node(*args, **kwargs)\n\n    @contextlib.contextmanager\n    def subtracer(\n        self, source_target: Optional[Target], prior_tracer: \"SubgraphTracer\"\n    ) -> Generator[fx.Tracer, None, None]:\n        new_scope_ctx = enter_new_scope()\n        try:\n            if prior_tracer:\n                # Lineage MUST stay preserved\n                assert prior_tracer.parent is self.current_tracer\n            new_scope_ctx.__enter__()\n            tracer = (\n                prior_tracer\n                if prior_tracer\n                else SubgraphTracer(\n                    self,\n                    parent=self.current_tracer,\n                    source_target=source_target,\n                    is_export=self.current_tracer.is_export,\n                )\n            )\n            self.tracers.append(tracer)\n            yield tracer\n        finally:\n            new_scope_ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n            self.tracers.pop()\n\n    @property\n    def output(self) -> \"OutputGraph\":\n        return self\n\n    @property\n    def fake_mode(self) -> torch._subclasses.FakeTensorMode:\n        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n        return self.tracing_context.fake_mode\n\n    @property\n    def shape_env(self) -> ShapeEnv:\n        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode is not None\n        assert self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env is not None\n        return self.tracing_context.fake_mode.shape_env\n\n    @property\n    def guards(self) -> torch._guards.GuardsSet:\n        return self.tracing_context.guards_context.dynamo_guards\n\n    @property\n    def nn_modules(self) -> dict[str, Any]:\n        return self.tracing_context.module_context.nn_modules\n\n    @property\n    def aotautograd_guards(self) -> list[torch._guards.GuardEnvExpr]:\n        return self.tracing_context.guards_context.aotautograd_guards\n\n    def save_global_state(\n        self, out: Optional[dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]]] = None\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Saves to out if it is provided. Else saves to the tracing context's global_state.\n        \"\"\"\n        global_state = cast(\n            dict[str, tuple[Callable[..., Any], bool]],\n            (\n                out\n                if out is not None\n                else self.tracing_context.global_context.global_state\n            ),\n        )\n\n        global_state[\"grad_enabled\"] = (torch.set_grad_enabled, torch.is_grad_enabled())\n\n        global_state[\"autocast_enabled\"] = (\n            functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \"cuda\"),\n            torch.is_autocast_enabled(\"cuda\"),\n        )\n        global_state[\"autocast_cpu_enabled\"] = (\n            functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_enabled, \"cpu\"),\n            torch.is_autocast_enabled(\"cpu\"),\n        )\n        global_state[\"autocast_gpu_dtype\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\n            functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \"cuda\"),\n            torch.get_autocast_dtype(\"cuda\"),\n        )\n        global_state[\"autocast_cpu_dtype\"] = (  # type:ignore[assignment]\n            functools.partial(torch.set_autocast_dtype, \"cpu\"),\n            torch.get_autocast_dtype(\"cpu\"),\n        )\n        global_state[\"autocast_cache_enabled\"] = (\n            torch.set_autocast_cache_enabled,\n            torch.is_autocast_cache_enabled(),\n        )\n'''\nwith open('/workspace/torch/_dynamo/output_graph.py', 'a') as f:\n    f.write(content)\nprint(\"chunk1 done\")\nPYEOF\npython3 /tmp/chunk1.py",
  "description": "Append chunk 1 (OutputGraph class __init__ and initial methods) to output_graph.py"
}
